        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    1 About This Documentation

    • Purpose

    • Intended Audience

    • Acronyms and Abbreviations


    1.1 Purpose


This document provides an overall description of the VANGO’s V32F20x/ V32H20x application

processor, which describes the overview, features, logical structures, functions and register listings of

each module. The document also describes the interface timings and related parameters in diagrams.

In addition, the document describes the pins, pin usages, performance parameters, and package

dimension of V32F20x/ V32H20x in detail.


    1.2 Intended Audience


    The document is intended for:

    • Design and maintenance personnel for electronics

    • Sales personnel for electronic parts and components


    1.3 Acronyms and Abbreviations


    The table below contains acronyms and abbreviations used in this documentation.

     AES    Advanced Encryption Standard

     ADC    Analog-to-Digital Converter

     AHB    AMBA High-speed Bus

     APB    Advanced Peripheral Bus

     ARM    Advanced RISC Machine

     BDMA | Basic Direct Memory Access

     BPWM | Basic Pulse Width Modulation

     BTMR   Basic Timer

     BSPI   Basic Serial Peripheral Interface

     CAN    Controller Area Network Interface

1.4 Documentation conventions


 List of abbreviations for registers

 The following abbreviations (b) are used in register descriptions:

 Read/write (rw): Software can read and write to this bit.

 Read-only (r): Software can only read this bit.

 Write-only (w): Software can only write to this bit. Reading this bit returns the reset value.

 Read/ clear write0 (rc_w0): Software can read as well as clear this bit by writing 0. Writing 1 has no

 effect on the bit value.

 Read/clear write1 (rc_w1): Software can read as well as clear this bit by writing 1. Writing 0 has no

 effect on the bit value.

 Read/clear write (rc_w): Software can read as well as clear this bit by writing to the register. The value

 written to this bit is not important.

 Read/clear by read (rc_r): Software can read this bit. Reading this bit automatically clears it to 0.

 Writing this bit has no effect on the bit value.

 Read/set by read (rs_r): Software can read this bit. Reading this bit automatically sets it to 1. Writing

 this bit has no effect on the bit value.

 Read/set (rs): Software can read as well as set this bit. Writing 0 has no effect on the bit value.

 Read/write once (rwo): Software can only write once to this bit and can also read it at any time. Only a

 reset can return the bit to its reset value.

 Toggle (t): The software can toggle this bit by writing 1. Writing 0 has no effect.

 Read-only write trigger (rt_w1): Software can read this bit. Writing 1 triggers an event but has no

 effect on the bit value.

 Read/Write 1 clear by hardware (r_w1c): Software can read this bit. Writing 1 triggers an event, and

 hardware will clear the bit automatically.

 Reserved (Rsvd): Reserved bit, must be kept at reset value.

 Glossary

            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        97 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

This section gives a brief definition of acronyms and abbreviations used in this document:

• Word: data of 32-bit length.

• Half-word: data of 16-bit length.

• Byte: data of 8-bit length.

• IAP (in-application programming): IAP is the ability to re-program the Flash memory of a

microcontroller while the user program is running.

• ICP (in-circuit programming): ICP is the ability to program the Flash memory of a microcontroller

using the SWD protocol or the bootloader while the device is mounted on the user application board.

• AHB: Advanced high-performance bus.

• APB: Advanced peripheral bus.

• PPC: Peripheral protection controller.

• MPC: Memory protection controller.

• RAZ: read-as-zero.

• WI: writes ignored.

• RAZ/WI: read-as-zero, writes ignored.










Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    98 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    2 Overview

    This part describes the overview for V32F20x/ V32H20x processor.

    • Introduction

    • Features

    • Block Diagram


    2.1 Introduction


V32F20x/ V32H20x is an ARM Cortex-M33 microcontroller for embedded applications, which can be

equipped with an ARM Cortex-M0 coprocessor. These devices include up to 1024 + 80 KB of on-chip

SRAM, up to 2048+256 KB on-chip flash, high-speed and full-speed USB host and device interface with

crystal-less operation for full-speed, two Ethernet 10/100 MACs with RMII, two CAN interfaces, two

SD/MMC/SDIO interfaces.

The Arm Cortex CM33 provides a security foundation, offering isolation to protect valuable IP and data

with TrustZone technology. It simplifies the design and software development of digital signal control

systems with the integrated digital signal processing (DSP) instructions. To support security requirements,

the V32F20x/ V32H20x also offers support for RNG, SHA, HMAC, AES, SM2, SM3, SM4, CRC, ECC, RSA,

UUID, dynamic encrypt and decrypt, debug authentication.


    2.2 Features


    The Key feature is as follows:

    • Three types of Architecture

      – CM33 (V32F205x/ V32H205x/ V32F207x/ V32H207x)

      – Dual CM33 (V32F208x/ V32H208x)

      – Dual CM33 + CM0 (V32F209x/ V32H209x)

    • ARM Cortex-M33 core (Core0)

      – Running at a frequency

       V32F20x up to 180 MHz (Typical: 144 MHz/ 180MHz)

       V32H20x up to 220 MHz (Typical: 144 MHz/ 180MHz/ 192MHz/ 220MHz)

      – Secure extension, TrustZone supported

      – Floating Point Unit (FPU) supported
    §  dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        99 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

  – Memory Protection Unit (MPU) supported

  – DSP Instruction supported

  – ARM Cortex M33 build-in Nested Vectored Interrupt Controller (NVIC)

  – Non-Mask-able Interrupt (NMI) input with a selection of sources

  – System tick timer

  – Crypto co-processor is provided to enable hardware acceleration for various functions required for

  certain asymmetric cryptographic algorithms, such as, Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), RSA etc.

• ARM Cortex-M33 core (Core 1)

  – Running at a frequency as Core 0

  – The Configuration of this instance does not include MPU, FPU, DSP, ETM and TrustZone

  – Sys-Tick and WIC supported

• ARM Cortex-M0 core

  – Running at a frequency of up to 64 MHz

  – Debug logic included, 4 breakpoints and comparators supported

  – Up to 2 Debug Watch Points supported

  – Single Cycle Multiply Logic

  – Sys-Tick and WIC supported

• On-chip Memory

  – Up to 2MB (support 2 bank) + 256KB on-chip flash, both with cache

  – Up to 1024KB total contiguous SRAM dedicate for CM33 on System Bus. Another 80 KB sharable for

  CM33 and CM0. All SRAM protect with ECC (1-bit error correct/ 2-bit error detect)

  – All SRAM with ECC protection

  – 64KB ROM with boot code in CM33

• External Interface

  – Up to Flexcomm (I2C/ UART/ SPI) x 14

  – Up to UART x 6

  – Up to BSPI x 6

  – Up to OSPI (QSPI) x 2

  – Up to SDIO x 2

  – Up to I2S x 2

  – Up to I2C x 1

  – Up to CAN 2.0 x 2
§ dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                              100 / 1446

                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

  – Up to DCMI x 1

  – USB 2.0 full-speed host/ device controller with on-chip PHY

  – USB 2.0 high-speed host/device controller with on-chip PHY

  – Up to ISO7816 x 4

  – Up to Ethernet 10/100 MAC with RMII x 2

  – Support RTC

• Other Digital Peripherals:

  – Up to DMA controller with 8 channels x 2

  – Up to 141 General Purpose IOs

  – Up to 32 GPIOs can be selected as Pin interrupts

  – Support CRC16/ CRC32

  – FFT with 128 point

• Security features:

  – ARM TrustZone enabled.

  – AES-128/ 196/ 256, SM4 encryption/ decryption engine

  – Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA1/ SHA256/ SM3) module supports secure boot

  – Random Number Generator

• Timers:

  – Up to BTMR x 8

  – Up to GTMR x 4

  – Up to ETMR x 2

  – Up to LPBTMR x 2

  – UP to Watchdog Timer x 3

• Analog Peripherals

  – 12-bit x 16 Channels SAR-ADC x 2

  – 12-bit DAC x 2

  – 8 Channels EADC x 1

  – COMP x 2 + RTC_COMP x 2

• WAVER + FFT

• Support IAP/ ISP

• Support crystal free applications

• On chip network for IO pad functional re-assignment.
§ dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                 101 / 1446

  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
  2.3 Block Diagram


  The follow figure shows the block diagram of the V32F20x/ V32H20x processor.



                                 | |
  !  SWD              MPU         [   I                  !

3 System

This part details the V32F20x/ V32H20x system construction from following aspects.

• System Architecture

• Memory Map

• Clock Generator

• Power Supply

• Reset

• Boot ROM

• System IRQ

• Security


3.1 System Architecture


The main system consists of 32-bit multilayer AHB bus matrix that interconnects:

x       Up to ten masters:

     –   C-bus, connecting Cortex®-M33 with TrustZone and FPU core C-bus through the ART (instruction

         cache)

     –   Cortex®-M33 with TrustZone and FPU core S-bus

     –   C-bus, connecting Cortex®-M33 core C-bus to the external memories through the ART

         (instruction cache)

     –   Cortex®-M33 core S-bus

     –   Cortex®-M0 core

     –   DMA0/1

     –   BDMA

     –   SDIO0/1

x       Up to thirteen slaves:

     –   Internal Flash A memory

     –   Internal Flash B memory

     –   Internal Flash 0 memory
i            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        103 / 1446

             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

     –   Internal SRAM0

     –   Internal SRAM1

     –   Internal SRAM2

     –   Internal SRAM3

     –   Internal SRAM4

     –   Internal SRAM5

     –   Internal SRAM6

     –   AHB0 peripherals including AHB0 to APB bridges and APB0 peripherals

     –   AHB1 peripherals including AHB1 to APB bridges and APB1 peripherals

     –   OSPI controller

    The bus matrix provides access from a master to a slave, enabling concurrent access and efficient

    operation even when several high-speed peripherals work simultaneously.
    These architectures are shown in Figure 3-1~ Figure 3-3:

    3.1.1 C-bus

    This bus connects the C-bus of the Cortex®-M33 core to the BusMatrix via the instruction cache. This
    i                                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    105 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

bus is used for instruction fetch and data access to the internal memories mapped in code region. The

target of this bus are the internal Flash and internal SRAMs.

3.1.2 S-bus

This bus connects the system bus of the Cortex®-M33 core to the BusMatrix. This bus is used by the

core to access data located in a peripheral or SRAM area. The targets of this bus are the internal SRAMs,

the AHB0 peripherals and the external memories through OSPI controller

The SRAM0~3 is also accessible on this bus to allow continuous mapping with each other..

3.1.3 DMA-bus

This bus connects the AHB master interface of the DMA to the BusMatrix.The targets of this bus are the

SRAM0 ~ SRAM3, the AHB0 peripherals and the external memories through the OSPI.

3.1.4 BDMA-bus

This bus connects the AHB1 master interface of the BDMA to the BusMatrix.The targets of this bus are

the SRAM4 ~ SRAM6 and the AHB1 peripherals.

3.1.5 SDIO controller bus

This bus connects the SDIO interface to the BusMatrix. This bus is used only by the SDIO to load/store

data from/to memory. The targets of this bus are data memories: internal SRAMs (SRAM0~3), internal

Flash memory.

3.1.6 BusMatrix

The BusMatrix manages the access arbitration between masters. The arbitration uses a Round Robin

algorithm. The BusMatrix is composed of up to ten masters (CPU AHB, system bus, C-bus, DMA0/1,

BDMA, SDIO) and up to thirteen slaves (FLASHA, FLASHB, FLASH0, SRAM0~3, SRAM4~6, AHB0, AHB1,

OSPI).










    i    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    106 / 1446

                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

3.2 Memory Map

3.2.1 Multilayer Matrix

V32F20x/ V32H20x uses multilayer AHB matrix to connect the CPU buses and other bus masters to

peripherals in a flexible manner that optimizes performance by allowing peripherals that are on

different slave ports of the matrix to be accessed simultaneously by different bus masters. As shown in

the arch figure.

3.2.2 Memory Protection Unit

The primary CM33 Core has a embed memory protection unit (MPU) that provides fine grain memory

control, enabling application to implement security privilege levels, separating code, data and stack on

a task-by-task basis. Such requirements are critical in many embedded applications.

3.2.3 TrustZone and system mapping on CM33 View

The implementation of ARM TrustZone for Core involves using address bit 28 to divide the address

space into memory into potential secure and non-secure regions. Address bit 28 is not decoded in Type

hardware, so each physical location appears in two places on whatever bus they are located on. Other

hardware determines which kinds of accesses (including non-secure callable) are actually allowed for

any particular address.

The following table shows the overall mapping of the code and data buses for secure and non-secure

accesses to various device resources.

Table 3-1 IDAU mapping from CM33 view

Start Address   End Address          TrustZone   CPU Bus     V32F20x/ V32H20x usage

0x0000_0000     0x0FFF_FFFF          No-Secure   Code/Data   Flash Memory, Boot ROM

0x1000_0000     0x1FFF_FFFF          Secure      Code/Data   Same as above

0x2000_0000     0x2FFF_FFFF          No-Secure   Data        SRAM

0x3000_0000     0x3FFF_FFFF          Secure      Data        Same as above

0x4000_0000     0x4FFF_FFFF          No-Secure   Data        AHB and APB Peripherals

0x5000_0000     0x5FFF_FFFF          Secure      Data        Same as above

3.2.4 Memory, APB and AHB peripherals

The following table details of the addresses arrangement for flash, RAM, APB peripherals and AHB

peripherals. They all have both secure and non-secure access possibilities.

3.3    Clock Generator

3.3.1    Functional description

Provides a wide choice of clock generators:

 • RCH (high-speed internal oscillator) clock: 48 MHz

 • XOH (high-speed external oscillator) clock: 12 MHz

 • XCL (low-speed external oscillator) clock: 32 kHz

 • RCL (low-speed internal oscillator) clock: 32 kHz

The Clock generator offers a high flexibility for the application to select the appropriate clock for CPU and

peripherals, in particular for peripherals that require a specific clock such as USB_FS, USB_HS, SPI, I2S

and SDIO.

To optimize the power consumption, each clock source can be switched ON or OFF independently.

The Clock generator provides up to 2 PLLs; each of them can be configured with integer or fractional

ratios.

The Clock generator offers four clock outputs (MCO1 ~ MCO4), with a great flexibility on the clock

selection and frequency adjustment.

3.3.2    Top Level Clocking Diagram

     OSCOUT  12 MHz  = MUX DIV     PLL1

     RCL
             32KHz

  OSC32K_IN   XOL
 OSC32K_OUT  32 KHz

    3.3.3 Clock output generation

    Four microcontroller clock output pins (MCOx), MCO1, MCO2, MCO3 and MCO4 are available. A clock

    source can be selected for each output.

    MCOx outputs are controlled via SYSCFGLP_CLKOUT1, SYSCFGLP_CLKOUT2, SYSCFGLP_CLKOUT3 and

    SYSCFGLP_CLKOUT4 located in the SYSCFGLP registers.

    The GPIO port corresponding to each MCO pin must be programmed in alternate function mode.

    The clock provided to the MCOs outputs must not exceed the maximum pin speed.

    Table 3-3 MCOx Pin Mux

   3.4 Power Supply


The device requires a 2.3-to-3.6 V operating voltage supply (VDD). An embedded linear voltage regulator

is used to supply the internal 1.1 V digital power. Only single power supply is supported. V32F20x/

V32H20x can enter the ultra-low power consumption mode by entering the RTCOnly mode, at this time,

most modules enter the power down state except RTC module.

3.5 Reset

3.5.1 System reset

A system reset is generated when one of the following events occurs:

1. A low level on the NRST pin (external reset)

2. Window watchdog end of count condition (WWDG reset)

3. Independent watchdog end of count condition (IWDG reset)

4. A software reset (SW reset)


3.6 Boot ROM


64kB on-chip boot ROM with bootloader that allows various options, that based on BOOT0 pin or setting

in FlashA information region:

 • Boot from any address in internal Flash and SRAM

 • Support UART, USB peripheral interfaces ISP mode, automatic detection of the active peripheral

3.6.1  Overview

The internal ROM memory is used to store the boot code. After a reset, CM33 core0 starts its code

execution from this memory. The bootloader code is executed every time the part is power-on reset, is

system reset, or wakes up from a deep sleep mode.

Based on based on BOOT0 pin or setting in FlashA information region, the bootloader decides whether

to boot from internal memory or run into ISP mode. Refer to following table for more details:

Table 3-4 Boot mode and ISP download modes

 nBOOT0 bit in BOOT0 pin nSWBOOT0 bit in  nTZMEN bit in Boot mode or ISP mode

 OPT_WORD         PI12       OPT_WORD        OPT_WORD

  -                0            1               1   Passive mode, boot from address

  1                -            0               1   defined by NSBOOTADDR

  -                0            1               0   Passive mode, boot from address

  1                -            0               1   defined by SECBOOTADDR

  -                1            1               -   ISP mode, automatic detection of

  0                -            0               -   the active peripheral

Following table show the ISP pin and boot pin assignments and is the default pin assignment used by

                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

the ROM code that cannot be changed.

Table 3-5 Boot and ISP pin assignments

Boot and ISP Pin           Port pin assignment

BOOT0                      PI12, can be used as GPIO when nSWBOOT0=0

UART ISP mode

FC7_TX                     PA2

FC7_RX                     PA3

USB ISP mode

USBFS_DP                   IOA12

USBFS_DM                   IOA11

The boot starts after reset is released. The CM33 CORE0 AHB/ APB clock and system clock is switched

to 48 MHz based on 48 MHz RCH at the start of bootloader code. After a power-on/ external/ watchdog

timer reset, the SWD access is disable and therefore, the debugger is unable to connect the chip

during this period of time.

 3.8 Security

3.8.1 Introduction

The security system on V32F20x/ V32H20x has a set of hardware blocks and ROM code to implement

the security features of the device. The hardware consists of an AES engine, a SM3 engine, a SM4 engine,

a SHA engine, a random number generator, and a key storage and management block. The following

figure shows an overview of the V32F20x/ V32H20x security system. All components of the system can

be accessed by the processor or the DMA engine to encrypt or decrypt data and for hashing. The ROM is

responsible for secure boot.

3.8.2 Key storage/management

A critical feature of any security system is how keys are stored and managed. Keys can be used for boot

loading and handling of critical user data. The FlashA INFO block 1 is used as the persistent storage

for key. It starts at offset address 0x2000 of FlashA INFO region. The software could not read it

out once it is set to read protect in INFO configuration. The ENC_ENG have a dedicate path to the key

management block. So when the key read protect is enabled, only intended rom code can make use of

its key. There is no other mechanism by which keys can be observed. But there is in-direct to verify the

correctness of the key during boot up security check or manufactory testing.

3.8.3 AES Engine

V32F20x/ V32H20x provide an on-chip hardware AES encryption and decryption engine to protect the

image content and to accelerate processing for data encryption or decryption, data integrity, and proof

of origin. Data can be encrypted or decrypted by the AES engine using a key from the Key management

module or a software supplied key.

3.8.4 SHA

V32F20x/ V32H20x provides on-chip Hash support to perform SHA-1 and SHA-2 with 256-bit digest

(SHA-256). Hashing is a way to reduce arbitrarily large messages or code images to a relatively small

fixed size ”unique” number called a digest. The SHA-1 Hash produces a 160 bit digest (five words), and

the SHA-256 hash produces a 256 bit digest (eight words).

For the SHA hardware:

  • Even a small change to the input message will cause a major change in the digest output.

  Therefore, for a given input message or image there is only one digest.
§ dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 128 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

  • There is no predictable way to modify one input to result in a specific digest. A message cannot be

  added, inserted, or modified to get the same Hash in any direct way.

  • These two properties make it useful for verifying a message is valid, or corrupted intentionally or

  unintentionally.

Hashing is used for four primary purposes:

  • Core of a digital signature model, including certificates, for example and for secure update.

  • Support a challenge/response or to validate a message when used with a Hash-based Message

  Authentication Code (HMAC)

  • In a secure boot model, which verifies code integrity.

  • Verify external memory that has not been compromised.

3.8.5 Digital signatures

A digital signature combines public/private keys such as RSA, ECC or SM2 with SHA Hashing Signature

is formed in the following way:

  • The message or image is hashed using SHA1, SHA2, SM3 or other.

  • The digest is formed into a buffer along with a header and padding. The header indicates what

  hashing was used. The padding fits the buffer to the size of the public key, for example, 256-bits,

  1024-bits, and 2048-bits.

  • The buffer is signed (encrypted) using the private key. Note: That signing is a reverse of normal

  public or private key where anyone can encrypt using the public key and therefore, only the private key

  holder can decrypt. In this case, the model is reversed so that only a private key holder can sign, and

  anyone can verify it if it is from the private key holder.

To verify the signature, the following steps are used:

  • The message or image is hashed using SHA1, SHA2, SM3 or other. The output of the hash must match

  the signature.

  • The signature is decrypted using the public key.

  • The hash digest is compared against the stored digest in the buffer after decryption.

The advantage of the signature model is that the public key and the signature can be public. Therefore,

the signature can be stored in an external flash file along with the image and then verified using a stored

copy of the public key.

3.8.6 Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC)

An HMAC can be achieved on V32F20x/ V32H20x using a pre-shared key and hashing. It is a way of

§ dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 129 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

verifying a message (encrypted or not) and can also be used to challenge or response.

Both sides must have a pre-shared key that is just a shared secret value and not an encryption key. The

HMAC is formed using three steps:

  • Hashing the message or image.

  • Taking the resultant digest and combine with key and some padding.

  • Hashing the combination of digest, key, and padding. The resultant digest is send

On the other side, the same procedure is followed to verify and get the same digest. Only those parties

that know the key can get the correct digest and therefore, can trust the date that was hashed.

HMACs are significantly faster than signatures, but work only with pre-shared keys, which must not be

leaked or lost (unlike a public key). The HMAC key can be shared dynamically using trust models like

Diffie-Hellman or maybe a board-unique key shared by two devices.

3.8.7 RNG

Random Number Generators (RNG) are used for cryptographic, modeling, and simulation applications,

which employ keys that must be generated in a random fashion.










    § dango  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    130 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
4 System Configure (SYSCFG)

4.1 Overview


The SYS_CFG module is a chip general configuration and status module. SYSCFG0 is for AHB0 to APB0

bridges and APB0 peripherals , SYSCFG1 is for AHB1 to APB1 bridges and APB1 peripherals, SYSCFGLP

is for low-power system domain.

    4.2.1     SYSCFG0_SRAMINIT

    Offset: 0x0004

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 4-2 SYSCFG0_SRAMINIT Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:12Rsvd     -    Reserved

11 INIT3 rw SRAM 3 initial control, initial done signal will clear it automatically.

                       0x1: Doing initial

                       0x0: Initial done

10 INIT2 rw SRAM 2 initial control, initial done signal will clear it automatically.

                       0x1: Doing initial

                       0x0: Initial done

    9     INIT1   rw   SRAM 1 initial control, initial  done signal will clear it automatically.

                       0x1: Doing initial

                       0x0: Initial done

    8     INIT0   rw   SRAM 0 initial control, initial  done signal will clear it automatically.

                       0x1: Doing initial

                       0x0: Initial done

    7     ECCNE3 rw    SRAM3 ECC error NMI enable register.

    6     ECCNE2 rw    SRAM2 ECC error NMI enable register.

    5     ECCNE1 rw    SRAM1 ECC error NMI enable register.

    4     ECCNE0 rw    SRAM0 ECC error NMI enable register.

    3     ECCEN3 rw    SRAM3 ECC check (2-bit error detect) enable register. No matter whether ECCEN is

                       1 or not, as long as SRAM write occurs, ECC code will always be generated and

                       written to SRAM together. The function of configuring ECCEN as 1 is only whether

                       to check ECC error when reading SRAM. So, if CPU or DMA access some un-initialize

                       region, error may happen because the ECC code information didn’t exist in those

                       non-initialize area. At this moment, programmer can use INIT bit to do SRAM

                       initialize automatically.

                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        135 / 1446

                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    2     ECCEN2 rw    SRAM2 ECC check (2-bit error detect) enable register.

                       0x1: Enable

                       0x0: Disable

    1     ECCEN1 rw    SRAM1 ECC check (2-bit error detect) enable register.

                       0x1: Enable

                       0x0: Disable

    0     ECCEN0 rw    SRAM0 ECC check (2-bit error detect) enable register.

                       0x1: Enable

                       0x0: Disable

    4.2.2     SYSCFG0_FLASHAC

    Offset: 0x0010

    Reset: 0x0000_05A3

    Table 4-3 SYSCFG0_FLASHAC Register Description

    Bit   Symbol   TypeDescription

    31:19Rsvd      -    Reserved

18:4 1USCYCLE rw This register is used for flashA controller to calculate 1us tick from AHB clock.

                        1ustick=(AHB clock period)*(1USCYCLE+1)

    3:2   Rsvd     -    Reserved

    1     ECCDIS   rw   Flash ECC disable control. When 1 bit error occurs, disable hardware error

                        correction.

                        0x1: Disable

                        0x0: Enable

    0     CECCDIS  rw   Flash Cache ECC disable control. When 1 bit error occurs, disable hardware error

                        correction.

                        0x1: Disable

                        0x0: Enable

    4.2.3     SYSCFG0_FLASHBANKSWT

    Offset: 0x0014

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 4-4 SYSCFG0_FLASHBANKSWT Register Description

    Bit      Symbol          Type   Description

    31:1     Rsvd            -      Reserved

    0        SWT             rw     Flash dual bank switch

                                    0x0: Flash A as the main working flash

                                    0x1: Flash B as the main working flash

Table 4-8 SYSCFG0_CORE1RST Register Description

Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31:1Rsvd    -      Reserved

0     RST   rw     CM33 CORE1 system reset signal control.

                   Default CM33 CORE1 is in reset and stays disabled until CM33 CORE0 decides to

                   release it from reset.

                   0x1: Hold the reset signal

                   0x0: Release the reset signal

    4.2.8     SYSCFG0_CM0SAC

    Offset: 0x0040

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 4-9 SYSCFG0_CM0SAC Register Description

    Bit   Symbol    Type Description

    31:18Rsvd       -    Reserved

17 UNPRIV rw Configure CM0 master transfer to un-privilege mode.

                         Set 1 to switch CM0 to un-privilege mode. Before setting, users need clear this bit.

16 NONSEC rw Configure CM0 master transfer mode to non-secure mode.

                         Set 1 to switch CM0 to non-secure mode. Before setting, users need clear this bit.

    15:2  Rsvd      -    Reserved

    1     PRIV      rw   Configure CM0 master transfer mode to privilege mode.

                         Set 1 to switch CM0 to privilege mode. Before setting, users need clear this bit.

    0     SEC       rw   Configure CM0 master transfer mode to secure mode.

                         Set 1 to switch CM0 to secure mode. Before setting, users need clear this bit.

    I                   0x0: Enable
3 CFGSECEXT0 rw CM33 CORE0 Armv8.M Security Extension enable control

                        0x1: Enable

                        0x0: Disable

2 CFGDSP0 rw CM33 CORE0 Armv8.M DSP extension enable control

                        0x1: Enable

                        0x0: Disable

1 CFGFPU0 rw CM33 CORE0 hardware floating-point enable control

                        0x1: Enable

                        0x0: Disable

0 CFGBIGEND0rw CM33 CORE0 static endianness selection for data accesses

                        0x0: Little-endian data.

                        0x1: BE8 big-endian data.

                        This signal affects all data side memory interfaces identically except for the PPB

                        region, which is always little-endian.

                        Instructions fetches are always performed as little-endian.

4.2.22 SYSCFG0_OSPIC

Offset: 0x007C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 4-23 SYSCFG0_OSPIC Register Description

Bit     Symbol  TypeDescription

31:20   Rsvd    -      Reserved

19      SLEEP1  r      OSPI1 enable flag

                       This signal is asserted when the OSPI is enabled. It can be used by the system

                       clock generator/controller module in order to disable the OSPI HCLK. Disabling

                       the OSPI HCLK will reduce power consumption in the system.

                       0x0: Not safe to disable OSPI1 HCLK when OSPI1 is in use or no need to disable

                             Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                             150 / 1446

                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     OSPI1 HCLK after system reset

                     0x1: Safe to remove OSPI1 HCLK when OSPI1 is not in use

18:17   MODE1   r    OSPI1 mode status

                     0x0: Standard SPI mode

                     0x1: SPI Dual Mode

                     0x2: SPI Quad mode

                     0x3: SPI Octal mode

16      XIPEN1  rw   OSPI1 XIP mode enable control:

                     0x1: Enable

                     0x0: Disable

15:4    Rsvd    -    Reserved

3       SLEEP0  r    OSPI0 enable flag

                     This signal is asserted when the OSPI is enabled. It can be used by the system

                     clock generator/controller module in order to disable the OSPI HCLK. Disabling

                     the OSPI HCLK will reduce power consumption in the system.

                     0x0: Not safe to disable OSPI0 HCLK when OSPI0 is in use or no need to disable

                     OSPI0 HCLK after system reset

                     0x1: Safe to remove OSPI0 HCLK when OSPI0 is not in use

2:1     MODE0   r    OSPI0 mode status

                     0x0: Standard SPI mode

                     0x1: SPI Dual Mode

                     0x2: SPI Quad mode

                     0x3: SPI Octal mode

0       XIPEN0  rw   OSPI0 XIP mode enable control

                     0x1: Enable

                     0x0: Disable

    4.2.69 SYSCFG0_MBINT0

    Offset: 0x017c

    Table 4-70 SYSCFG0_MBINT0 Register Description
                              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                       189 / 1446

                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:2Rsvd   -   Reserved

1 S2M w When CM33 CORE1 will connect with CM33 CORE0, CORE1 will write 1 to this bit, and

        interrupt will generate to CORE0. When write 1 to interrupt clear register, this bit

                   will be cleared.

0 M2S w When CM33 CORE0 will connect with CM33 CORE1, CORE0 will write 1 to this bit, and

        interrupt will generate to CORE1. When write 1 to interrupt clear register, this bit

                   will be cleared.

    4.2.70 SYSCFG0_MBINTCLR0

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:2Rsvd   -   Reserved

1 S2M w When write 1 to this bit, the interrupt CORE1 generate to CORE0 will be cleared.

                   This bit will be cleared after interrupt be cleared.

0 M2S w When write 1 to this bit, the interrupt CORE0 generate to CORE1 will be cleared.

                   This bit will be cleared after interrupt be cleared.

                   cleared.

4.2.77 SYSCFG0_PASSWD

Offset: 0x01a0

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 4-78 SYSCFG0_PASSWD Register Description

Bit   Symbol  TypeDescription

31:0PASSWDw          Write 0xBEEF_BEEF to this register to unlock the write access to some password

                     protect register. The unlock state will be invalid after one write access to any register

                     in SYSCFG0.

                     Read bit[0] can know now SYSCFG0 is lock or not.

                     SYSCFG0_SRAMINIT, SYSCFG0_DSLEEPEN, SYSCFG0_SLEEPEN, SYSCFG0_CLKS,

                     SYSCFG0_HCLKD, SYSCFG0_PCLKD, SYSCFG0_CLKEN0, SYSCFG0_CLKEN1,

                     SYSCFG0_TMRCLKEN, SYSCFG0_RST0, SYSCFG0_RST1, SYSCFG0_SYSCFGLPC,

                     SYSCFG0_FLASHBANKSWT, SYSCFG0_CM0SAC registers all needs password

                     protects.

     Register Name    Offset TypeReset Value Description

SYSCFG1_SRAMINIT 0x0004 rw 0x0000_0000 SRAM initialize and ECC configure register.

     SYSCFG1_IREN     0x0008 rw     0x0000_0000 UART IrDA enable configure register.

SYSCFG1_UARTEN 0x000Crw 0x0000_0000 UART enable configure register.

SYSCFG1_EGYCFEN 0x0010 rw 0x0000_0000 EGY and CF enable configure register.

SYSCFG1_PASSWD 0x0018 rw 0x0000_0000 Write access protection password register.

SYSCFG1_SRAMC0 0x001Crw 0x0A00_0A00 SRAM control register 0.

SYSCFG1_SRAMC1 0x0020 rw 0x0A00_0A00 SRAM control register 1.

SYSCFG1_FLASHSC 0x0024 rw 0x0A00_0A00 Flash and SRAM control register.

SYSCFG1_INTMASK 0x0028 rw 0x0000_0000 CM0 interrupt mask register.

SYSCFG1_UARTCD1 0x0038 rw 0x0000_0000 UART clock divider register 1.

SYSCFG1_FLASH0C 0x0040 rw 0x0000_03C3 Flash 0 control register.

     SYSCFG1_HCLKD    0x0048 rw     0x0000_0000 AHB clock divider control register.

     SYSCFG1_PCLKD    0x004Crw      0x0000_0000 APB clock divider control register.

SYSCFG1_UARTCD0 0x0050 rw 0x0000_0000 UART clock divider register 0.

     SYSCFG1_PCLKEN   0x005Crw      0x0800_0000 APB bus peripheral clock enable control register.

     SYSCFG1_RST      0x0060 rw     0x0000_0000 Peripheral reset control register 0.

     SYSCFG1_VTORADDR 0x0064 rw     0x0000_0000 CM0 VTOR base address register.

     SYSCFG1_CFG      0x006Crw      0x0000_0000 CM0 configure register.

     SYSCFG1_I2CCD    0x0074 rw     0x0000_0000 I2C clock divider register.

     SYSCFG1_SEC      0x0078 rw     0x0000_0000 CM0 security configure register.

     SYSCFG1_IRDUTY   0x007Crw      0x0000_0000 UART IrDA duty configure register.

     SYSCFG1_MBINT    0x0080 rw     0x0000_0000 MAILBOX CM0 to CM33 interrupt set register.

SYSCFG1_MBINTCLR 0x0084 rw 0x0000_0000 MAILBOX CM33 to CM0 interrupt clear register.

    4.3.1     SYSCFG1_SRAMINIT

    Offset: 0x0004

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 4-84 SYSCFG1_SRAMINIT Register Description

    Bit   Symbol      Type Description

    31:12 Rsvd        -    Reserved

11 MBSRAM_INIT rw Mailbox SRAM initial control, initial done signal will clear it automatically

                           1: Doing initial

                           0: Initial done

    10    INIT6       rw   SRAM6 initial control, initial done signal will clear it automatically

                           1: Doing initial

                           0: Initial done

    9     INIT5       rw   SRAM5 initial control, initial done signal will clear it automatically

                           1: Doing initial

                           0: Initial done

    8     INIT4       rw   SRAM4 initial control, initial done signal will clear it automatically

                           1: Doing initial

                           0: Initial done

    7     ECCNEMB     rw   Mailbox SRAM ECC error NMI enable register.

    6     ECCNE6      rw   SRAM6 ECC error NMI enable register.

    5     ECCNE5      rw   SRAM5 ECC error NMI enable register.

                           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                           201 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

4   ECCNE4        rw    SRAM4 ECC error NMI enable register.

3   ECCENMB       rw    Mailbox SRAM ECC check (2-bit error detect) enable register. No matter

                        whether ECCEN is 1 or not, as long as SRAM write occurs, ECC code will

                        always be generated and written to SRAM together. The function of

                        configuring ECCEN as 1 is only whether to check ECC error when reading

                        SRAM. So, if CPU or DMA access some un-initialize region, error may happen

                        because the ECC code information didn’t exist in those non-initialize area. At

                        this moment, programmer can use INIT bit to do SRAM initialize

                        automatically.

2   ECCEN6        rw    SRAM6 ECC check (2-bit error detect) enable register.

1   ECCEN5        rw    SRAM5 ECC check (2-bit error detect) enable register.                       |
0   ECCEN4        rw    SRAM4 ECC check (2-bit error detect) enable register.

4.3.2      SYSCFG1_IREN

Offset: 0x0008

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 4-85 SYSCFG1_IREN Register Description

Bit Symbol TypeDescription

31:6Rsvd   -    Reserved

5:0 EN     rw   UART14~19 IrDA enable control register

                Each bit in this register corresponded to one UART TX channel. When EN[x] is set to

                1, it means UART TX[x] will be modulated with IR pulse to output.

4.3.3      SYSCFG1_UARTEN

Offset: 0x000C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 4-86 SYSCFG1_UARTEN Register Description

Bit Symbol TypeDescription

31:6Rsvd   -    Reserved

5:0 EN     rw   UART14~19 enable control register. Each bit in this register corresponded to the UART

                TX/RX channel. When EN[x] is set to 1, it means those GPIOs for UART TX/RX[x] will be

                used for UART, and those GPIOs will be disable.



                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    202 / 1446

                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    4.3.4             SYSCFG1_EGYCFEN

    Offset: 0x0010

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 4-87 SYSCFG1_EGYCFEN Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

    31:3Rsvd   -     Reserved

    2:0 EN     rw    EGY CF pulse output enable

                     Each bit in this register corresponded to the EGY CF outputting. When EN[x] is set to 1,

                     it means those GPIOs for will be used for EGY CF[x] outputting, and those GPIOs will be

                     disable.

                     1: Enable

                     0: Disable

    4.3.5              SYSCFG1_PASSWD

    Offset: 0x0018

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 4-88 SYSCFG1_PASSWD Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

    31:1Rsvd      -   Reserved

0 UNLOCKrw Write 0xBEEFBEEF to this register to unlock the write access to some password

                      protest register. The unlock state will be invalid after one write access to any

                      register in SYSCFG1.

                      Read bit[0] can know now SYSCFG1 is lock or not.

                      SYSCFG1_SRAMINIT, SYSCFG1_CLKS, SYSCFG1_HCLKD, SYSCFG1_PCLKD,

                      SYSCFG1_CLKEN0, SYSCFG1_CLKEN1, SYSCFG1_RST, SYSCFG1_SEC registers all

                      needs password protects.

    4.3.8     SYSCFG1_FLASH0C

    Offset: 0x0040

    Reset: 0x0000_03c3

    Table 4-91 SYSCFG1_FLASH0C Register Description

    Bit   Symbol   TypeDescription

    31:19Rsvd      -    Reserved

18:4 1USCYCLE rw This register is used for flash0 controller to calculate 1us tick from AHB1 clock.

                        1ustick=(AHB clock period)*(1USCYCLE+1)

    3:2   Rsvd     -    Reserved

    1     ECCDIS   rw   Flash0 ECC check disable control:

                        1: Disable

                        0: Enable

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 4-93 SYSCFG1_HCLKD Register Description

Bit    Symbol   Type   Description

31:8   Rsvd     -      Reserved

7:0    DIV      rw     This register is used to control AHB clock divider.

                       0: Clock source divided by 1

                       1: Clock source divided by 2.

                       2: Clock source divided by 3.

                       ...

                       255: Clock source divided by 256.

                       Note: HCLK1 max frequency is 64 MHz

    4.3.21 SYSCFG1_MBINT

    Offset: 0x0080

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 4-104 SYSCFG1_MBINT Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:2Rsvd -      Reserved

1 CM02S w When CM0 will connect with CM33 CORE1, CM0 will write 1 to this bit, and interrupt

                    will generate to CM33 CORE1. When write 1 to interrupt clear register, this bit will be

                    cleared.

0 CM02M w When CM0 will connect with CM33 CORE0, CM0 will write 1 to this bit, and interrupt

                    will generate to CM33 CORE0. When write 1 to interrupt clear register, this bit will be

                    cleared.

    4.3.22 SYSCFG1_MBINTCLR

    Offset: 0x0084

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 4-105 SYSCFG1_MBINTCLR Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:2Rsvd -      Reserved

1 S2CM0 w When write 1 to this bit, the interrupt CM33 CORE1 to CM0 will be cleared. This bit

                    will be cleared after interrupt be cleared.

0 M2CM0 w When write 1 to this bit, the interrupt CM33 CORE0 to CM0 will be cleared. This bit

                    will be cleared after interrupt be cleared.

Bit    Symbol  TypeDescription

31:4   Rsvd    -    Reserved

3      Rsvd    -    Reserved

2      BYP     rw   External active crystal input selectable signal, high active

                    Only effective while XTAL_EN_AON= 0 and X0 as external active crystal input.

                    XTAL_BYP =1 means : If XTAL_EN = 0 and X0 as external active crystal input, the

                    PAD can support active crystal input;(default value) XTAL_BYP = 0 means:

                    Cannot support active crystal input

4.4.4      SYSCFGLP_PLL0C0

Offset: 0x001C

Reset: 0x0000_0110

Table 4-119 SYSCFGLP_PLL0C0 Register Description

Bit    Symbol       Type Description

31     LOCK         r    PLL0 lock indication signal, high active.

30     STABLE       r    1: PLL0 clock stable and not be gated

                         0: PLL0 still be gated

29     UNLOCK_INT   rw   PLL0 unlock interrupt:

                         1: Active

                         0: Inactive

28     INTEN        rw   PLL0 interrupt enable signal:

                         1: Enable

                         0: Disable

27:13 Rsvd          -    Reserved

                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                              230 / 1446

                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    12:5   SWTIME       rw   PLL0 clock output to chip wait time by software configure

                             0: Forbidden

                             Not 0: wait SWTIME cycles

    4      FORCEEN      rw   Software force PLL0 wait stable gate cell enable, high active.

    3      FLOW_SEL     rw   Select software or hardware flow control PLL0 clock output to chip.

                             1: Software flow control PLL0 clock output to chip

                             0: Hardware counter control PLL0 clock output to chip

    2:1    REFCLK_SEL   rw   PLL0 reference clock select:

                             00: RCH divided by 4 (default 12M)

                             01: XOH (default 12M)

                             10: RCH (default 48M)

                             11: Reserved

    0      EN           rw   Enable signal of PLL0, high active.

    4.4.5       SYSCFGLP_PLL0C1

    Offset: 0x0020

    Reset: 0x3072_4302

    Table 4-120 SYSCFGLP_PLL0C1 Register Description

    Bit     Symbol   TypeDescription

    31:16   Rsvd     -   Reserved

15:14 DIV_INTH rw This field which are used to set the first stage loop divider. The default values

                         are 01.

                         00: the first stage loop divider is 1

                         01: the first stage loop divider is 2

                         1x: the first stage loop divider is 4

                         Note: CLKOUT_LOOP is the internal clock that generate from first-stage

                         frequency dividing of VCO.

13:5 DIV_INTL rw This field used to set the second stage loop divider.

                         0 to 9: wrong configuration

                         10: the second stage loop divider is 10

                         …

                         511: the second stage loop divider is 511


                             Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    231 / 1446

                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    4:0   DIV_PRE   rw     Pre-divider of the reference clock

                           0: wrong configuration

                           1: the pre-divider rate is 1

                           …

                           31: the pre-divider rate is 31

                           DIV_INTH default value is 1, the first stage loop is 2.

                           DIV_INTL default value is 24, the second stage loop is 24.

                           DIV_PRE value is 2, the pre-divider rate is 2.

                           The VCO input frequency default value is 12MHz that is from RCH divided by 4.

                           So the PLL0 VCO output frequency = VCO input frequency * first stage loop *

                           second stage loop / pre-divider rate = 12MHz * 2 *24 / 2 = 288MHz.

    Offset: 0x0028

    Reset: 0x0400_0003

    22    FORCEEN       rw   Software force PLL1 wait stable gate cell enable, high active.

    21    FLOW_SEL      rw   Select software or hardware flow control PLL1 clock output to chip

                             1: Software control PLL1 clock output to chip

                             0: Hardware counter control PLL1 clock output to chip

    20:13 Rsvd          -    Reserved

    12    STABLE        r    1: PLL1 clock stable and not be gated

                             0: PLL1 still be gated

                         8h1: Divided by 2

                         …

3:1    REFCLK_SEL   rw   PLL1 reference clock select:

                         000: XOL

                         001: RCL

                         010: RCH divided by 4 (default 12MHz)

                         011: XOH (default 12MHz)

                         100: RCH (default 48MHz)

                         101: PLL0

                         110: PLL0 divider

                         111: RCH divided by 48 (default 1MHz)

0     PWRDN         rw   PLL1 power-down signal

                         1: Power-down

                         0: Power-on

                      1: Bypass mode

22:19CLKOD rw A 4 bit bus that selects the values 1 to 16 for the post VCO divider (OD) default is 0,

                      the PLL1 VCO output frequency = 245.76MHz.

                      Default is d7499, the PLL1 VCO output frequency = 245.76MHz.

                      Default is 0, the PLL1 VCO output frequency = 245.76MHz.

    4.4.17 SYSCFGLP_ANALOCK

    Offset: 0x0054

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 4-132 SYSCFGLP_ANALOCK Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

    31:1Rsvd   -     Reserved

0 UNLOCKrw Analog lock register, write 0xBEEFBEEF to lock analog control register, write

                     0xFEEBFEEB to unlock.

                     The following register is under control: SYSCFGLP_XOHC0, SYSCFGLP_RCHC0,

                     SYSCFGLP_PLL0C0, SYSCFGLP_PLL0C1, SYSCFGLP_PLL1C0, SYSCFGLP_PLL1C1,

                     SYSCFGLP_PLL1C2, SYSCFGLP_PLL0C3, SYSCFGLP_PLL0C4,

                     SYSCFGLP_PVSENS_MAIN, SYSCFGLP_MPMU_CFG1, SYSCFGLP_MPMU_CFG2,

                     SYSCFGLP_MPMU_CFG3, SYSCFGLP_MPMU_AON_UPDATE, SYSCFGLP_MPMU_TEST

                               1: LDOABS is OK

                               1: VCC_PMU is floating

                               PMUB_PVDB=0 means Reset state, the 3.3V voltage power is

                               below the threshold value;PMUB_PVDB=1 means Normal state,

                               the 3.3V voltage power is above the threshold value.

                               PMUB_BORB change from 1 to 0, interrupt generated.

5    PMUB_RSTN_BORB_INTSTSr    PMUB_BORB interrupt status.

                               PMUB_BORB=0 means reset state, the VCC_PMU power is below

                               the threshold value; PMUB_BORB=1 means normal state, the

                               VCC_PMU power is above the threshold value. PMUB_BORB

                               change from1 to0, interrupt generated.

4    VDDRTC_PD_INTSTS   r      VDDRTCPD interrupt status

                               When it changed from 0 to 1, interrupt is generated.

                               1: Interrupt is active

                               0: Interrupt is inactive

3    INT_RSTN_LPORA     r      RSTN_LPORA interrupt status.

                               When RST_LPORA if changed from 1 to 0, this interrupt will

                                1: Interrupt is active

                                0: Interrupt is inactive

0     INT_PORAIOS           r   VCC_PMU interrupt status, VCC_PMU=1 is floating, VCC_PMU=0

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
5 FLASH Controller (FLASH)

5.1 Introduction


The FLASH controller is used to controller the read/write program of embedded FLASH. It supports

program and sector/chip erase. Flash power can be controlled. Programmable timing can be controlled.

It will always provide correct data with correct access timing. The setting in FLASH controller will be reset

after wake-up from sleep or deep-sleep mode, programmer should restore the setting manually after

wake-up from these two states.

FLASH CTRL is the top-level design of flash controller. It is composed of flash cache, flash controller. It

support AHB slave interface and APB slave interface. AHB interface is only used for reading flash and

APB interface is used for register configuration and writing flash indirectly.

Flash Memory which is partitioned into two memory blocks. One is the main memory block, the other is

the information block .The main memory block is organized as a 65536 word by 144 bit. The

information block is organized as a 2048 word by 144 bit. The information block can be used for the

storage of device information. The page erase operation erases all bytes within a page. Page size is

8192 byte.


   5.2 Feature


    • Support cache prefetching to improve performance when executing a sequence of code that has not

    been read before.

    • Support 32-bits AHB slave interface to read flash.

    • Support 32-bits APB slave interface to write flash and configure register.

    • Support ECC error checking.

    • Support cache

    • Support configurable timing parameter of Flash.

    • Support interrupt generation.

    • Support information 0 block read access protection.


    5.3 FLASH Registers


    Table 5-1 FLASH Registers Map
    i        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    257 / 1446

                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Register Name        Offset TypeReset Value Description

    FLASH_CACHE_CTRL     0x0000 rw   0x0000_0040 Flash cache Control register.

    FLASH_CACHE_STATUS   0x0004 r    0x0000_0000 Flash cache status Register.

    FLASH_CACHE_INTEN    0x0008 rw   0x0000_0000 Flash cache interrupt enable control register.

    FLASH_CACHE_INTSTS   0x000Crw    0x0000_0000 Flash cache interrupt status control register.

FLASH_CACHE_HWPARAMS 0x0010 r 0x0000_3554 Flash cache hardware parameters register.

    FLASH_CACHE_CSHR     0x0014 rw   0x0000_0000 Flash cache statistic Hit Register.

    FLASH_CACHE_CSMR     0x0018 rw   0x0000_0000 Flash cache statistic Miss Register.

    FLASH_SFC_CTRL       0x1000 rw   0x0000_0000 Control Registers

    FLASH_SFC_STATE      0x100Crw    0x0000_0000 Status Register mainly for debugging

    FLASH_SFC_TAS        0x1010 rw   0x0000_0001 Tas time configure.

                                         Take 1 clock     cycle as unit.

    FLASH_SFC_TNVH         0x104Crw    0x0000_0006 Tnvh time configure.

                                        Take 1us as unit.

    FLASH_SFC_TNVH1        0x1050 rw   0x0000_0065 Tnvh1 time configure.

                                        Take 1us as unit.

    FLASH_SFC_TWK          0x1054 rw   0x0000_0006 Twk time configure.

                                        Take 1us as unit.

FLASH_SFC_INFO_STATUS 0x108Crw 0x0000_0000 INFO0 and INFO1 status.

    FLASH_SFC_POWER_CFG    0x1090 rw   0x0000_0003 Flash pdm_io and pwrrdy_io configuration

                                        controls power supply.

    FLASH_GFC_INT_SET      0x2000 rw   0x0000_0000 GFC interrupt enable set register for enabling

                                        generation of interrupts.

    FLASH_GFC_INT_CLR      0x2004 rw   0x0000_0000 GFC interrupt enable clear register for

                                        disabling generation of interrupt.

    FLASH_GFC_STATUS_SET   0x2008 rw   0x0000_0020 GFC interrupt status set register for

                                        activating different interrupt sources.

    FLASH_GFC_STATUS_CLR   0x200Crw    0x0000_0020 GFC interrupt status clear register for

                                        acknowledging different interrupt sources.

    FLASH_GFC_MASKED_STATUS0x2010 rw   0x0000_0000 GFC interrupt status register that shows if

                                        each interrupt is pending and is the cause of

                                        the interrupt line being asserted.

    FLASH_GFC_CTRL         0x2014 rw   0x0000_0000 GFC control register for initiating Flash

                                        accesses.

    FLASH_GFC_STATUS       0x2018 r    0x0000_0000 GFC status register for showing the state of

                                        the Flash accesses.

    FLASH_GFC_ADDR         0x201Crw    0x0000_0000 GFC address register for the Flash access.


                           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    259 / 1446

                                                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

FLASH_GFC_DATA0          0x2020 rw   0x0000_0000 GFC data register for the Flash access

                                         containing read and write data bits[31:0].

FLASH_GFC_DATA1          0x2024 rw   0x0000_0000 GFC data register for the Flash access

                                         containing read data bits[63:32].

FLASH_GFC_DATA2          0x2028 rw   0x0000_0000 GFC data register for the flash access

                                         containing read data bits[95:64].

FLASH_GFC_DATA3          0x202Crw    0x0000_0000 GFC data register for the Flash access has

                                         read data bits[127:96].

5.3.1     FLASH_CACHE_CTRL

Offset: 0x0000

Reset: 0x0000_0040

Table 5-2 FLASH_CACHE_CTRL Register Description

Bit   Symbol       TypeDescription

31:8Rsvd           -    Reserved

7     OPT_EN       rw   Cache Performance Optimization enable control

                        0: Disable

                        1: Enable

6     STATISTIC_EN rw   Statistics logic enable control

                        0: Disabled. Counters are stalled

                        1: Enable. Counters are running

5     SET_PROFETCHrw    Cache Pre-fetch enable control

                        0: Disable pre-fetch

                        1: Enable pre-fetch

4     SET_MAN_INV  rw   Cache Invalidate control

                        0: Automatic cache invalidate when cache enabled

                        1: Manual cache invalidate mode

3     SET_MAN_POW rw    Power control

                        0: Automatic

                        1: Manual

2     POW_REQ      rw   Manual SRAM power request.

1     INV_REQ      rw   Manual invalidate request

                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                       260 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                               Functional only when SET_MAN_INV is set. Automatically cleared when

                               invalidation is finished or power or invalidation error occurs. Cannot be

                               cleared manually.

                               Manual invalidation request must be set only when the cache is disabled,

                               otherwise it causes an invalidation error interrupt.

    0    EN            rw      Cache enable control

                               0: Disable cache function

                               1: Enable cache function

    5.3.2     FLASH_CACHE_STATUS

    Offset: 0x0004

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 5-3 FLASH_CACHE_STATUS Register Description

    Bit   Symbol           Type  Description

    31:5  Rsvd             -     Reserved
    4     POW_STAT         r     SRAM power acknowledges        |
    3     Rsvd             -     Reserved

    2     INV_STAT         r     Invalidate Status

                                 Indicate if invalidation process is ongoing.

    1:0   CS               r     Cache Status

                                 0: Cache disabled

                                 1: Cache enabling

                                 2: Cache enabled

                                 3: Cache disabling

    Offset: 0x0008

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

1 MAN_INV_ERRrw Mask interrupt request on Manual Invalidation Error indication

                              (FLASH_CACHE_INTSTS.MAN_INV_ERR is set).

                                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            261 / 1446

                                                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    0     POW_ERR   rw   Masking interrupt request on Power Error indication

                         (FLASH_CACHE_INTSTS.POW_ERR is set).

                         This bit will always be 0 due to SRAM is always power on.

    5.3.4      FLASH_CACHE_INTSTS

    Offset: 0x000C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 5-5 FLASH_CACHE_INTSTS Register Description

    Bit   Symbol    TypeDescription

    31:2Rsvd        -    Reserved

1 MAN_INV_ERRrw Manual invalidation error status. Set when manual invalidation is requested

                         meanwhile the cache is not disabled.

                         Write 1 to clear

    0     POW_ERR   rw   SRAM power error

                         Write 1 to clear.

                         Power acknowledge de-asserted during operation.

                         Manual power request de-asserted while cache is enabled and operating in

                         manual power mode.

    Table 5-9 FLASH_SFC_CTRL Register Description

    Bit Symbol         TypeDescription
    {dango                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                      263 / 1446

    31:4Rsvd             -      Reserved

3:2 WAIT_TIME_TO_SLEEP rw The time waiting to enter sleep mode when flash is idle

                                2’h0: 50us.

                                2’h1: 100us.

                                2’h2: 250us.

                                2’h3: 500us.

    1     LP_MODE_SEL    rw     Flash low power mode select

                                0: Standby mode.

                                1: Sleep mode.

                                When Flash is idle, it will enter sleep mode automatically after the time

                                configured by WAIT_TIME_TO_SLEEP.

                                But this mode is not suggested to use because it may influence CPU

                                performance.

    0     LOW_VDD_MODE   rw     Low VDD mode

                                1: Low VDD mode;

                                0: Normal VDD mode.

                                    10-13: Flash is in erasing state.

                                    14: Flash is in sleeping state.

    Table 5-29 FLASH_SFC_INFO_STATUS Register Description

    Bit   Symbol              TypeDescription

    31:15Rsvd                 -   Reserved

14:4 INFO_ERROR_CODErw INFO0 and INFO1 access error type

                                  bit0 1: INFO0 page0 write access error due to writing (program or erase)

                                  page0 while page0 is prohibited writing access.

                                  bit1: INFO0 page0 read access error due to reading page0 while page0 is

                                  prohibited reading access.

                                  bit2:INFO0 page1 write access error due to writing (program or erase)

                                  page1 while page1 is prohibited writing access.

                                  bit3: INFO0 page1 read access error due to reading page1 while page1 is

                                  prohibited reading access.

                                  bit4: INFO0 page2 write access error due to writing (program or erase)

                                  page2 while page2 is prohibited writing access.

                                  bit5: INFO0 page2 read access error due to reading page2 while page2 is

                                  prohibited reading access.


                                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    270 / 1446

                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                                bit6:INFO0 page3 write access error due to writing (program or erase)

                                page3 while page3 is prohibited writing access.

                                bit7: INFO0 page3 read access error due to reading page3 while page3 is

                                prohibited reading access. bit8: Access info0 address exceed the max

                                address of info0 block. The total size of info0 block is 32KByte.

                                bit9: INFO1 access error due to programing or erasing info0 block.

                                bit10: Access INFO1 address exceed the max address of INFO1 block.

                                The total size of info0 block is 8KByte.

    3:1    Rsvd            -    Reserved

    0      INFO_BUSY       rw   INFO0 or INFO1 is busy due to reading or writing or erasing operation.

                                1: Busy.

                                0: Idle.

    5.3.29 FLASH_SFC_POWER_CFG

    Offset: 0x1090

    Reset: 0x0000_0003

    Table 5-30 FLASH_SFC_POWER_CFG Register Description

    Bit   Symbol       TypeDescription

    31:2Rsvd           -      Reserved

1 PWRRDY_IO_CFGrw Flash power ready configuration.

                              When power down or power off, the bit should be written to 0.

                              When flash is in function mode with reading, programing and erasing, this

                              bit should be written to 1.

                              1: Power ready.

                              0: Power not ready.

    0     PDM_IO_CFG   rw     Flash configuration controls power down mode.

                              User should write this register to 0 when power supply stable. Reading or

                              writing or erasing flash could be operate after at least 5us from writing this

                              register to 0.

                              1: Power down mode.

                              0: Normal working mode.


                                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    271 / 1446

                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
5.3.30 FLASH_GFC_INT_SET

Offset: 0x2000

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 5-31 FLASH_GFC_INT_SET Register Description

Bit   Symbol                 TypeDescription

31:4Rsvd                     -    Reserved

3     CMD_REJECT_INT_SET     rw   Enables CMD_REJECT_IRQ bit to generate interrupts.

                                  Write to       1 sets the interrupt  enable.

                                  Write to       0 has no effect.

2     CMD_FAIL_INT_SET       rw   Enables CMD_FAIL_IRQ bit to generate interrupts.

                                  Write to       1 sets the interrupt  enable.

                                  Write to       0 has no effect.

1     CMD_SUCCESS_INT_SET    rw   Enables CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ bit to generate interrupts.

                                  Write to       1 sets the interrupt  enable.

                                  Write to       0 has no effect.

0     CMD_ACCEPT_INT_SET     rw   Enables CMD_ACCEPT_IRQ bit to generate interrupts.

                                  Write to       1 sets the interrupt  enable.

                                  Write to       0 has no effect.

5.3.31 FLASH_GFC_INT_CLR

Offset: 0x2004

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 5-32 FLASH_GFC_INT_CLR Register Description

Bit   Symbol                 TypeDescription

31:4Rsvd                     -    Reserved

3     CMD_REJECT_INT_CLR     rw   Disables CMD_REJECT_IRQ bit

                                  Write to       1 clears the interrupt enable.

                                  Write to       0 has no effect.

2     CMD_FAIL_INT_CLR       rw   Disables CMD_FAIL_IRQ bit

                                  Write to       1 clears the interrupt enable.

                                  Write to       0 has no effect.

1     CMD_SUCCESS_INT_CLR    rw   Disables CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ bit

          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                272 / 1446

                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                                     Write to  1 clears the interrupt enable.

                                     Write to  0 has no effect.

0     CMD_ACCEPT_INT_CLR        rw   Disables CMD_ACCEPT_IRQ bit

                                     Write to  1 clears the interrupt enable.

                                     Write to  0 has no effect.

5.3.32 FLASH_GFC_STATUS_SET

Offset: 0x2008

Reset: 0x0000_0020

Table 5-33 FLASH_GFC_STATUS_SET Register Description

Bit   Symbol                TypeDescription

31:4Rsvd                    -    Reserved

3     CMD_REJECT_STS_SET    rw   Sets the status of the CMD_REJECT_IRQ bit.

                                 Write to  1 sets the interrupt. Write to 0 has no effect.

                                 Reading this bit shows the current status of the interrupt bit

                                 regardless of the interrupt enable setting.

2     CMD_FAIL_STS_SET      rw   Sets the status of the CMD_FAIL_IRQ bit.

                                 Write to  1sets the interrupt. Write to 0 has no effect.

                                 Reading this bit shows the current status of the interrupt bit

                                 regardless of the interrupt enable setting.

                                 Write to  1 also sets         FLASH_GFC_STATUS.CMD_FAIL.

1     CMD_SUCCESS_STS_SET   rw   Sets the status of the CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ bit.

                                 Write to  1sets the interrupt. Write to 0 has no effect.

                                 Reading this bit shows the current status of the interrupt bit

                                 regardless of the interrupt enable setting. Write to 1 also

                                 sets FLASH_GFC_STATUS.CMD_SUCCESS.

0     CMD_ACCEPT_STS_SET    rw   Sets the status of the CMD_ACCEPT_IRQ bit.

                                 Write to  1 sets the interrupt. Write to 0 has no effect.

                                 Reading this bit shows the current status of the interrupt

                                 bit regardless of the interrupt enable setting.

5.3.33 FLASH_GFC_STATUS_CLR

Offset: 0x200C

          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        273 / 1446

                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Reset: 0x0000_0020

Table 5-34 FLASH_GFC_STATUS_CLR Register Description

Bit   Symbol                    Type Description

31:4 Rsvd                       -    Reserved

3     CMD_REJECT_STS_CLR        rw   Clears the status of the CMD_REJECT_IRQ bit.

                                     Write to 1 clears the interrupt.Write to 0 has no effect.

2     CCMD_FAIL_STS_CLR         rw   Clears the status of the CMD_FAIL_IRQ bit.

                                     Write to 1 clears the interrupt.Write to 0 has no effect.

1     CMD_SUCCESS_STS_CLR       rw   Clears the status of the CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ bit.

                                     Write to 1 clears the interrupt.Write to 0 has no effect.

0     CMD_ACCEPT_STS_CLR        rw   Clears the status of the CMD_ACCEPT_IRQ bit.

                                     Write to 1 clears the interrupt.Write to 0 has no effect.

5.3.34 FLASH_GFC_MASKED_STATUS

Offset: 0x2010

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 5-35 FLASH_GFC_MASKED_STATUS Register Description

Bit   Symbol               TypeDescription

31:4Rsvd                   -   Reserved

3     CMD_REJECT_IRQ       r   When this bit reads 1, it means that CMD_REJECT_IRQ is the

                               cause of the interrupt line being asserted.

2     CMD_FAIL_IRQ         r   When this bit reads 1, it means that CMD_FAIL_IRQ is the cause

                               of the interrupt line being asserted.

1     CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ      r   When this bit reads 1, it means that CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ is the

                               cause of the interrupt line being asserted.

0     CMD_ACCEPT_IRQ       r   When this bit reads 1, it means that CMD_ACCEPT_IRQ is the

                               cause of the interrupt line being asserted.

5.3.35 FLASH_GFC_CTRL

Offset: 0x2014

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 5-36 FLASH_GFC_CTRL Register Description


      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    274 / 1446

                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:5Rsvd        -   Reserved

4 ABORT rw Abort currently ongoing command that is initiated towards the Flash from the APB

                        interface. When ABORT is written, the CMD field is ignored. ABORT is effective only

                        when STATUS.CMD_ACCEPT is set. Reading back ABORT field shows the status of the

                        GFB interface abort signal.

    3     Rsvd      -   Reserved

2:0 CMD rw Initiate a command to the Flash for the address that is stored in the ADDR register

                        and with the data that are stored in DATA0 register. For WRITE and ROW WRITE

                        commands, software must ensure that the state of the addressed word is cleared.

                        The commands are:

                        0b001: READ

                        0b010: WRITE

                        0b011: ROW WRITE

                        0b100: ERASE

                        0b111: MASS ERASE

                        0b000: Reserved

                        0b101: Reserved

                        0b110: Reserved

                        Writing reserved commands has no effect. Reading back the CMD value shows the

                        currently pending CMD. When the CMD is accepted, the CMD field is cleared to 0b000.

    5.3.36 FLASH_GFC_STATUS

    Offset: 0x2018

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 5-37 FLASH_GFC_STATUS Register Description

    Bit   Symbol                TypeDescription

    31:6Rsvd                    -   Reserved

    5     ARBITRATION_LOCKEDr       Arbitration locked

                                    When this bit reads 1, it means that the other interface keeps the

                                    arbitration in locked state and no commands are serviced until the

                                    locked state is removed. Software can use this bit to detect why the


                                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    275 / 1446

                                                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
I                      request is being held up.
4    CMD_FINISH    r   Command finish

                       When this bit reads 1, it means that the previously accepted command

                       is finished but the result cannot be updated in the STATUS register

                       because the interrupt bits are still set. When the result interrupt bits

                       are cleared, the status can be updated and this bit is automatically

                       cleared.

3    CMD_FAIL      r   Command fail

                       When this bit reads 1, it means that the previously accepted

                       command has finished with a failure. Either this bit or the

                       CMD_SUCCESS bit is set for the finished command. Updated when

                       CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ or CMD_FAIL_IRQ is set. Valid until the

                       CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ or CMD_FAIL_IRQ bit is cleared.

2    CMD_SUCCESS   r   Command success

                       When this bit reads 1, it means that the previously accepted

                       command has finished successfully.

                       Either this bit or the CMD_FAIL bit is set for the finished command.

                       Updated when CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ or CMD_FAIL_IRQ is set. Valid

                       until the CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ or CMD_FAIL_IRQ bit is cleared.

1    CMD_ACCEPT    r   Command accept

                       Selecting CMD_PENDING to be executed towards the embedded Flash

                       sets this bit to 1 and means that the command is being forwarded to

                       the embedded Flash. Set to 1 when the CMD_PENDING bit is cleared.

                       Cleared when the command has finished and the CMD_SUCCESS_IRQ

                       or CMD_FAIL_IRQ status bits are cleared for the previous transfer. If

                       the status results of the previous command are not cleared, the

                       command might still be finished for the embedded Flash, but the

                       results cannot update until the previous results are cleared. If an

                       already pending command is accepted when the current command

                       has finished, this bit stays asserted.

0    CMD_PENDING  r    Command pening

                       When the CTRL register is written, the command goes into the
& "dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                              276 / 1446

                                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                                  arbitration queue and waits to be arbitrated towards the embedded

                                  Flash. This bit is set when the command is initiated, but still pending

                                  in the queue, and is cleared when the embedded Flash arbitrates and

                                  accepts the command. This bit is also set when ABORT is written to

                                  the CTRL register, and is cleared when the aborted command has

                                  finished.
