    10.3.1 DMA_CxSAR

    Offset: 0x0000+x*0x58

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 10-3 DMA_CxSAR Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

31:0SAR rw Updated after each source transfer. The SINC field in the CTLx register determines

                    whether the address increments, decrements, or is left unchanged on every source

                    transfer through the block transfer.

    10.3.2 DMA_CxDAR

    Offset: 0x0008+x*0x58

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 10-4 DMA_CxDAR Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

31:0DAR rw Updated after each destination transfer. The DINC field in the CTLx register

                    determines whether the address increments, decrements, or is left unchanged on

                    every destination transfer throughout the block transfer

    10.3.3 DMA_CxLLP

    Offset: 0x0010+x*0x58

    Reset: 0x000_0000

    Table 10-5 DMA_CxLLP Register Description

    {dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 364 / 1446

                                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Bit Symbol    TypeDescription

    31:2LOC       rw   Starting Address In Memory of next LLI if block chaining is enabled. Note that the

                       two LSBs of the starting address are not stored because the address is assumed to

                       be aligned to a 32-bit boundary. LLI accesses are always 32-bit accesses (Hsize=2)

                       aligned to 32-bit boundaries and cannot be changed or programmed to anything

                       other than 32-bit.

    1:0 Reserved r     Reserved

    10.3.4 DMA_CxCTL0

    Offset: 0x0018+x*0x58

    Reset: 0x0000_4800

    Table 10-6 DMA_CxCTL0 Register Description

    Bit  Symbol          TypeDescription

    31:22Rsvd            -     Reserved

    21:20TT_FC           rw    Flow control can be assigned to the DMA, the source peripheral, or the

                               destination peripheral. For more information on transfer types and flow

                               control, refer to ”Setup/Operation of the DMA Transfers”.

                               0x0: Transfer type is Memory to Memory and Flow Controller is DMA

                               0x1: Transfer type is Memory to Peripheral and Flow Controller is DMA

                               0x2: Transfer type is Peripheral to Memory and Flow Controller is DMA

                               0x3: Transfer type is Peripheral to Peripheral and Flow Controller is DMA

    19   Rsvd            -     Reserved

18 DST_SCATTER_ENrw Scatter on the destination side is applicable only when the CTLx.DINC bit

                               indicates an incrementing or decrementing address control.

                               0x0: Destination Scatter is disabled

                               0x1: Destination Scatter is enabled

17 SRC_GATHER_EN rw Gather on the source side is applicable only when the CTLx.SINC bit

                               indicates an incrementing or decrementing address control.

                               0x0: Source gather is disabled

                               0x1: Source gather is enabled

    16:14SRC_MSIZE       rw    Number of data items, each of width CTLx.SRC_TRWIDTH, to be read from

                               the source every time a burst transferred request is made from either the

    {dango                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                             365 / 1446

                                                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     corresponding hardware or software handshaking interface.

                     0x0: Number of data items to be transferred is 1

                     0x1: Number of data items to be transferred is 4

                     0x2: Number of data items to be transferred is 8

                     0x3: Number of data items to be transferred is 16

                     0x4: Number of data items to be transferred is 32

                     0x5: Number of data items to be transferred is 64

                     0x6: Number of data items to be transferred is 128

                     0x7: Number of data items to be transferred is 256

13:11DEST_MSIZE rw   Number of data items, each of width CTLx.DST_TRWIDTH, to be written

                     to the destination every time a destination burst transaction request is

                     made from either the corresponding hardware or software handshaking

                     interface.

                     0x0: Number of data items to be transferred is 1

                     0x1: Number of data items to be transferred is 4

                     0x2: Number of data items to be transferred is 8

                     0x3: Number of data items to be transferred is 16

                     0x4: Number of data items to be transferred is 32

                     0x5: Number of data items to be transferred is 64

                     0x6: Number of data items to be transferred is 128

                     0x7: Number of data items to be transferred is 256

10:9  SINC      rw   Indicates whether to increment or decrement the source address on

                     every source transfer. If the device is fetching data from a source

                     peripheral FIFO with a fixed address, then set this field to ”No change”.

                     0x0: Increments the source address

                     0x1: Decrements the source address

                     0x2: No change in the source address

                     0x3: No change in the source address

8:7   DINC      rw   Indicates whether to increment or decrement the destination address on

                     every destination transfer. If your device is writing data to a destination

                     peripheral FIFO with a fixed address, then set this field to ”NoChange”.

                     0x0: Increments the destination address
{dango               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                366 / 1446

                                                                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                              0x1: Decrements the destination address

                              0x2: No change in the destination address

                              0x3: No change in the destination address

6:4 SRC_TRWIDTH rw Mapped to AHB bus hsize. For a non-memory peripheral, typically the

                              peripheral (source) FIFO width.

                              0x0: Source transfer width is 8 bits

                              0x1: Source transfer width is 16 bits

                              0x2: Source transfer width is 32 bits

3:1 DST_TRWIDTH rw Mapped to AHB bus hsize. For a non-memory peripheral, typically the

                              peripheral (destination) FIFO width.

                              0x0: Destination transfer width is 8 bits

                              0x1: Destination transfer width is 16 bits

                              0x2: Destination transfer width is 32 bits

    0     INT_EN         rw   If set, then all interrupt-generating sources are enabled. Functions as a

                              global mask bit for all interrupts for the channel;

                              0x0: Interrupt is disabled

                              0x1: Interrupt is enabled

    10.3.5 DMA_CxCTL1

    Offset: 0x001C+x*0x58

    Reset: 0x00000002

    Table 10-7 DMA_CxCTL1 Register Description

    Bit   Symbol   TypeDescription

    31:13Rsvd      -    Reserved

    12    DONE     rw   If status write-back is enabled, the upper word of the control register,

                        CTL1[31:0], is written to the control register location of the Linked List Item (LLI)

                        in system memory at the end of the block transfer with the done bit set.

                        Software can poll the LLI CTL1.DONE bit to see when a block transfer is

                        complete. The LLI CTL1.DONE bit should be cleared when the linked lists are set

                        up in memory prior to enabling the channel.

                        LLI accesses are always 32-bit accesses (Hsize=2) aligned to 32-bit boundaries

                        and cannot be changed or programmed to anything other than 32-bit. For more

                        information, refer to ”Multi-Block Transfers”.
    & "dango                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  367 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      0x0: DONE bit is de-asserted the end of block transfer

                      0x1: SET the DONE bit at the end of block transfer

11:0 BLOCK_TS rw When the DMA is the flow controller, the user writes this field before the channel

                      is enabled in order to indicate the block size. The number programmed into

                      BLOCK_TS indicates the total number of single transactions to perform for every

                      block transfer; a single transaction is mapped to a single AMBA beat.

    10.3.6 DMA_CxCFG0

    Offset: 0x0040+x*0x58

    Reset: 0x000_00E00

    Table 10-8 DMA_CxCFG0 Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31 RELOAD_DSTrw The DARx register can be automatically reloaded from its initial value at the

                         end of every block for multi-block transfers. A new block transfer is then

                         initiated.

                         0x0: Destination Reload Disabled.

                         0x1: Destination Reload Enabled

30 RELOAD_SRCrw The SARx register can be automatically reloaded from its initial value at the

                         end of every block for multi-block transfers. A new block transfer is then

                         initiated.

                         0x0: Source Reload Disabled

                         0x1: Source Reload Enabled

29:20MAX_ABRST rw Maximum AMBA burst length that is used for DMA transfers on this channel.

                         A value of 0 indicates that software is not limiting the maximum AMBA burst

                         length for DMA transfers on this channel.

19 SRC_HS_POL rw 0x0: Source Handshaking Interface Polarity is Active high

                         0x1: Source Handshaking Interface Polarity is Active low

18 DST_HS_POL rw Destination Handshaking Interface Polarity.

                         0x0: Destination Handshaking Interface Polarity is Active high

                         0x1: Destination Handshaking Interface Polarity is Active low

    17:12Rsvd    -       Reserved

11 HS_SEL_SRC rw This register selects which of the handshaking interfaces

    {dango               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 368 / 1446

                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                         - hardware or software - is active for source requests on this channel. If the

                         source peripheral is memory, then this bit is ignored.

                         0x0: Hardware handshaking interface.

                         Software initiated transaction requests are ignored.

                         0x1: Software handshaking interface.

                         Hardware initiated transaction requests are ignored.

10 HS_SEL_DST rw This register selects which of the handshaking interfaces

                         - hardware or software - is active for destination requests on this channel. If

                         the destination peripheral is memory, then this bit is ignored.

                         0x0: Hardware handshaking interface. Software initiated transaction requests

                         are ignored.

                         0x1: Software handshaking interface. Hardware initiated transaction requests

                         are ignored.

    9     FIFO_EMPTY r   Indicates if there is data left in the channel FIFO. Can be used in conjunction

                         with CFGx.CH_SUSP to cleanly disable a channel.

                         0x0: Channel FIFO is not empty

                         0x1: Channel FIFO is empty

    8     CH_SUSP   rw   Suspends all DMA data transfers from the source until this bit is cleared. There

                         is no guarantee that the current transaction will complete. Can also be used in

                         conjunction with CFGx.FIFO_EMPTY to cleanly disable a channel without losing

                         any data.

                         0x0: DMA transfer from the source is not suspended

                         0x1: Suspend DMA transfer from the source

7:5 CH_PRIOR rw A priority of 7 is the highest priority, and 0 is the lowest. This field must be

                         programmed within the range 0 to 7. A programmed value outside this range

                         will cause erroneous behavior.

                         0x0: Channel priority is 0 0x1: Channel priority is 1

                         0x2: Channel priority is 2 0x3: Channel priority is 3

                         0x4: Channel priority is 4 0x5: Channel priority is 5

                         0x6: Channel priority is 6 0x7: Channel priority is 7

    4:0   Rsvd      -    Reserved


    {dango               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    369 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
10.3.7 DMA_CxCFG1

Offset: 0x0044+x*0x58

Reset: 0x00000004

Table 10-9 DMA_CxCFG1 Register Description

Bit  Symbol     TypeDescription

31:15Rsvd       -    Reserved

14:11DEST_PER   rw   Destination hardware interface.

                     Assigns a hardware handshaking interface (0: DMAH_NUM_HS_INT-1) to the

                     destination of channel x if the CFGx.HS_SEL_DST field is 0; otherwise, this field

                     is ignored. The channel can then communicate with the destination peripheral

                     connected to that interface through the assigned hardware handshaking

                     interface.

                     NOTE1: For correct DMA operation, only one peripheral (source or destination)

                     should be assigned to the same handshaking interface.

10:7  SRC_PER   rw   Source Hardware Interface

                     Assigns a hardware handshaking interface (0:15) to the source of channel x if

                     the CFGx.HS_SEL_SRC field is 0; otherwise, this field is ignored. The channel

                     can then communicate with the source peripheral connected to that interface

                     through the assigned hardware handshaking interface.

                     NOTE1: For correct DMA operation, only one peripheral (source or destination)

                     should be assigned to the same handshaking interface.

6:2   Rsvd      -    Reserved

1     FIFO_MODErw    Determines how much space or data needs to be available in the FIFO before a

                     burst transaction request is serviced.

                     0x0: Space/data available for single AHB transfer of the specified transfer width

                     0x1: Data available is greater than or equal to half the FIFO depth for destination

                     transfers and space available is greater than half the FIFO depth for source

                     transfers. The exceptions are at the end of a burst transaction request or at the

                     end of a block transfer.

0     FCMODE    rw   Determines when source transaction requests are serviced when the Destination

                     Peripheral is the flow controller.

                     0x0: Source transaction requests are serviced when they occur. Data prefetching
    dango            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                        370 / 1446

                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  is  enabled

                  0x1: Source transaction requests are not serviced until a destination transaction

                  request occurs. In this mode, the amount of data transferred from the source is

                  limited so that it is guaranteed to be transferred to the destination prior to

                  block termination by the destination. Data pre-fetching is disabled.

11 Encrypt & Hash Engine (ENCENG)

11.1 Introduction


    The Encrypt & Hash Engine is an engine combined module, consist of AES, SM4, SHA and SM3. It contain

    an embedded DMA to fetch the source data automatically by hardware self. The processor and DMA could

    also take the move of source data in and result data out.

    The main architecture as shown below.


    11.2 Features


    •  The Encrypt engine support 128-bit, 192-bit or 256-bit keys for encryption and decryption operation.

    •  Use the secure key from Key Manager, which cannot be read.

    •  Use the direct key configured by software.

    •  AES/SM4 support:

    –  Electronic Code Book (ECB) Mode

    –  Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) Mode

    –  Counter (CTR) Mode

    •  The AES engine is compliant with the FIPS-197, Advanced Encryption Standard.

    •  AES offer program ability to select little-endian or big-endian mode for operation.

    •  Performance SHA-1, SHA-2(256) and SM3 based hashing.

    •  Used with HMAC to support a challenge/response or to validate a message.

11.4.1 ENCENG_CFG

Offset: 0x0004

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 11-2 ENCENG_CFG Register Description

Bit Symbol        TypeDescription

31:20Rsvd         - Reserved

{dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                383 / 1446

                                                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

19:16HMAC_KEY_SIZErw     The key size of HMAC, unit in word:

                         ’b0000: 1 word, 32-bit

                         ’b0001: 2 words, 64-bit

                         ’b0010: 3 words, 96-bit

                         ’b0011: 4 words, 128-bit

                         ....

                         ’b1111: 16 words, 512-bit

                         Note: The actual HAMC key could be in any size, just padding the unused

                         bits with zero.

15    KEY_SWAP      rw   If 1, will swap the key input(bytes in each word) , The default is byte order

                         ( so, LSB is 1st order) but allows swapping to MSB is first.

14    DATA_SWAP     rw   If 1, will swap bytes in the word for hash and encryption, The default is

                         byte order ( so, LSB is 1st order) but allows swapping to MSB is first.

13:12HASH_SEL       rw   Hash algorithm select:

                         ’b00: SHA-1

                         ’b01: SHA-256

                         ’b10: SM3

11    NEW           rw   When this bit is set, indicate there will issue a new encryption or hash in

                         following operation. Any history will be cleared automatically through this

                         bit set. This bit will be self-cleared.

10    KPAD_EN       rw   Whether to enable the IPAD and OPAD in HMAC

                         calculation:

                         0: Disable

                         1: Enable

9:8   ENC_KEY_SIZE  rw   The size of Encryption Key:

                         ’b00: 128-bit

                         ’b01: 192-bit

                         ’b10: 256-bit

7     ENC_SEL       rw   Select the encryption algorithm:

                         0: AES

                         1: SM4(ENC_KEY_SIZE must be 2’b00)

6     ENC_DIR       rw   Encrypt or decrypt select:

                             Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            384 / 1446

                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                            0: Encryption

                            1: Decryption

5:4   ENC_MODE         rw   Encryption or decryption mode:

                            ’b00: ECB

                            ’b01: CBC

                            ’b10: CTR

3:2   KEY_INDEX        rw   The key index:

                            ’b00: Use the pre-define key 0

                            ’b01: Use the pre-define key 1

                            ’b10: Use the pre-define key 2

                            ’b11: Use the pre-define key 3

1     KEY_INDEX_EN     rw   Using the key through the index:

                            0: Disable

                            1: Enable

0     ALGO_SEL         rw   Encryption or hash algorithm select:

                            1: Select encryption algorithm

                            0: Select Hash algorithm

    11.5 SM4 Engine

    11.5.1 Overview

    The SM4 Engine supports 128bit key expansion and 128bit data encryption and decryption.

    11.5.2 Algorithm Structure

    SM4 is a block cipher algorithm. Its block length and cipher key length are both of 128bits, SM4 iterates

its round functions for 32 times in both encryption and key expansion algorithm. The structure of

    decryption is the same as the encryption. But the decryption round keys are in the reverse order of the

    encryption round keys.

    Suppose its input 128bit data is (X0, X1, X2, X3), and the round keys are 32bit rki(i=0 31),then the process

    of the encryption algorithm is as follows:

    Xi+4 = F (Xi, Xi+1, Xi+2, Xi+3, rki), i = 0, 1, , ..., 31.       (3)

    Out128bit = R(X₃₂, X₃₃, X₃₄, X₃₅) = (X₃₅, X₃₄, X₃₃, X     ).     (4)
                                 32

    In decryption. the round keys are used in the order of (rk_31,rk_31,...,rk_0).

    where F() is round function F,

    {dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                393 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

F (Xi, Xi+1, Xi+2, Xi+3, rki) = Xi ⊕T (Xi+1 ⊕Xi+2 ⊕Xi+3 ⊕rki). (5)

where T() is permutation T, Suppose 32bit input for function T() is A =(a0,a1,a2,a3).

T (.) = L(Sbox(a₀), Sbox(a₀), Sbox(a₁), Sbox(a₂), Sbox(a₃)). (6)

The S-box is nonlinear byte substitution, The L() is linear transformation.




    Input 128bit    Key 128bit




RndCtrl    ny
  0~31        KeyExpand

   S
   Vy
    YY


    LR]




        Output 128bit

        Figure 11-6 SM4 ENC/DEC Structure

The round keys in this algorithm are generated from the 128bit cipher key via the key expansion.

Suppose the cipher key is MK = (MK0, MK1, MK2, MK3).then the round keys are generated as follows.

(K₀, K₁.K₂.K₃) = (MK₀ ⊕FK₀, MK₁ ⊕FK₁, MK₂ ⊕FK₂, MK₃ ⊕FK₃), (7)

rki = Ki+4 = Ki ⊕T ′(Ki+1 ⊕Ki+2 ⊕Ki+3 ⊕CKi), i = 0, 1, , ..., 31. (8)

The key expansion algorithm structure is similar to encryption process, the differences are the fixed

parameter CK/FK and the linear transformation L.

11.5.3 Application Note

If the crypto key is updated, Before Encryption or Decryption, The user must configure key_vld pulse

firstly to generate round keys. keyexp_done pulse is valid after key expansion is finished







    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    394 / 1446

V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

   11.7 DMA


    The DMA take the response of moving the source data from SRAM and write the result back to SRAM, as

    configured in register.

    The DMA logic will parse the Key, IV & IPAD/OPAD from the source data stream as defined in data

    structure.










    fis    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    396 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
12 Random Number Generator (RNG)

The RNG enables generation and collection of truly random bit stream from digital logics. The typical usage

of RNG is key generation with seeding approved deterministic random numbers.


   12.1 Features


• Produces 5K bits/second of entropy when running at 100MHz.

• Includes an internal entropy source that is based on a chain of digital inverters.

• Built-in hardware tests for auto correlation and continuous random number generation testing.

• AMBA APB2 slave interface.


   12.2 RNG Registers


    Table 397: RNG Registers Map

    Register Name     Offset    Type   Reset Value   Description

    RNG_IMR           0x0100    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG interrupt mask register.

    RNG_ISR           0x0104    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG interrupt status register.

    RNG_ICR           0x0108    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG interrupt clear register.

    RNG_CFG           0x010c    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG configure register.

    RNG_DATA0         0x0114    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG data register 0.

    RNG_DATA1         0x0118    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG data register 1.

    RNG_DATA2         0x011c    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG data register 2.

    RNG_DATA3         0x0120    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG data register 3.

    RNG_DATA4         0x0124    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG data register 4.

    RNG_DATA5         0x0128    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG data register 5.

    RNG_CMD           0x012c    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG source enable register.

    RNG_CNT           0x0130    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG sample counter register.

    RNG_BYPASS        0x0138    rw     0x0000_0000   RNG testing bypass register.

    12.2.1 RNG_IMR

    Offset: 0x0100

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 398: RNG_IMR Register Description
    {dango                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        397 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Bit   Symbol            TypeDescription

    31:4Rsvd                -    Reserved

    3     VN_ERR_MASK       rw   This bit masks the VN_ERR interrupt signal from RNG_ISR register.

                                 0: VN_ERR interrupt is unmasked.

                                 1: VN_ERR interrupt is masked.

    2     CRNGT_ERR_MASK    rw   This bit masks the CRNGT_ERR interrupt signal from RNG_ISR

                                 register.

                                 0: CRNGT_ERR interrupt is unmasked.

                                 1: CRNGT_ERR interrupt is masked.

1 AUTOCORR_ERR_MASKrw This bit masks the AUTOCORR_ERR interrupt signal from RNG_ISR

                                 register.

                                 0: AUTOCORR_ERR interrupt is unmasked.

                                 1: AUTOCORR_ERR interrupt is masked.

0 DATA_VALID_MASK rw This bit masks the DATA_VALID interrupt signal from RNG_ISR

                                 register.

                                 0: DATA_VALID interrupt is unmasked.

                                 1: DATA_VALID interrupt is masked.

    12.2.2 RNG_ISR

    Offset: 0x0104

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 399: RNG_ISR Register Description

    Bit   Symbol       TypeDescription

    31:4Rsvd           -   Reserved

    3     VN_ERR       r   A Von Neumann error occurs if 32 consecutive collected bits are identical,

                           that is, 32 zeros or 32 ones.

                           1: A Von Neumann error occurs.

                           0: No Von Neumann error occurs.

2 CRNGT_ERR r When set to 1, it indicates a Continuous Random Number Generation Testing

                           (CRNGT) error in the TRNG test failed.Failure occurs when two consecutive

                           blocks of 16 collected bits are equal.

                           1: A Continuous Random Number Generation Testing error occurs.

    {dango                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                          398 / 1446

                                                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    I                       0: No Continuous Random Number Generation Testing error occurs.
1 AUTOCORR_ERR r When set to 1, it indicates that the Autocorrelation test failed four times in a

                            row.

                            When set, the TRNG stops functioning until the next reset.

                            1: A Autocorrelation error occurs.

                            0: No Autocorrelation error occurs.

0 DATA_VALID r Set to 1 when 192 bits have been collected in the RNG, and the DATA0~5

                            registers are ready to be read.

                            1: Data in DATA0~5 registers are ready.

                            0: Data in DATA0~5 registers are not ready.

    12.2.3 RNG_ICR

    0   Rsvd        -    Reserved


    12.3 Internal Random Source


The RNG module includes an internal ring oscillator circuit, implemented as an inverter chain with 4 length

options, serving as the entropy source and responsible for generating a single random bit. The four

inverter chain options must be implemented, because each single ring oscillator tends to have large

variance between chip lots and environmental conditions (temperature, voltage, system clock oscillator,

and device age). At runtime, the RNG module can select each of the chain length optionally and identify

which inverter chain produces the best results.










    fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    402 / 1446

 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
13 External ADC Interface Controller
 (EADC)

 13.1 Introduction


 The External ADC Interface Controller is a dedicated application function interface used for capturing the

 8 channels of external DSM ADC, converting from serial to parallel for further analysis.


 13.2 Features

 • At most support 8 channels to capture data at the same time.
 • Support SYN mode and normal mode.
 • Support adc_clk input or output.
 • Synchronization length (syn_step, syn_sets_num) can be configured by individual.
 • Each channel can be enable and disable individual.
 • The capture point could be configured for each channel by individual.

   14.2 Features


     The ETH features are divided into the following categories:

     ■ MAC

     ■ DMA Block

     ■ PTP

     14.2.1 MAC Core Features

    x Supports 10/100 Mbit/s data transfer rates with external PHY interfaces

    x IEEE 802.3-compliant RMII interface to communicate with an external Fast Ethernet PHY

    x Supports both full-duplex and half-duplex operations

      – Supports CSMA/CD Protocol for half-duplex operation

      – Supports IEEE 802.3x flow control for full-duplex operation

      – Optional forwarding of received pause control frames to the user application in full- duplex

      "dlango    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        409 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

  operation

  – Back-pressure support for half-duplex operation

  – Automatic transmission of zero-quanta pause frame on deassertion of flow control input in full-

  duplex operation

x Preamble and start-of-frame data (SFD) insertion in Transmit, and deletion in Receive paths

x Automatic CRC and pad generation controllable on a per-frame basis

x Options for automatic pad/CRC stripping on receive frames

x Programmable frame length to support Standard frames with sizes up to 16 KB

x Programmable inter frame gap (40-96 bit times in steps of 8)

x Supports a variety of flexible address filtering modes:

 – Up to four 48-bit perfect (DA) address filters with masks for each byte

 – Up to three 48-bit SA address comparison check with masks for each byte

 – 64-bit Hash filter (optional) for multicast and unicast (DA) addresses

 – Option to pass all multicast addressed frames

 – Promiscuous mode support to pass all frames without any filtering for network monitoring

 – Passes all incoming packets (as per filter) with a status report

x Separate 32-bit status returned for transmission and reception packets

x Supports IEEE 802.1Q VLAN tag detection for reception frames

x Separate transmission, reception, and control interfaces to the Application

x Supports mandatory network statistics with RMON/MIB counters (RFC2819/RFC2665)

x MDIO interface for PHY device configuration and management

x Detection of LAN wakeup frames and AMD Magic Packet™ frames

x Receive feature for checksum off-load for received IPv4 and TCP packets encapsulated by the Ethernet

  frame

x Enhanced receive feature for checking IPv4 header checksum and TCP, UDP, or ICMP checksum

  encapsulated in IPv4 or IPv6 datagrams

x Support Ethernet frame time stamping as described in IEEE 1588-2008. Sixty-four-bit time stamps are

  given in each frame’s transmit or receive status

x Two sets of FIFOs: a 2-KB Transmit FIFO with programmable threshold capability, and a 2-KB Receive

  FIFO with a configurable threshold (default of 64 bytes)

x Receive Status vectors inserted into the Receive FIFO after the EOF transfer enables multiple-frame

  storage in the Receive FIFO without requiring another FIFO to store those frames’ Receive Status

                                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                          410 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

x Option to filter all error frames on reception and not forward them to the application in Store-and-

Forward mode

x Option to forward under-sized good frames

x Supports Store and Forward mechanism for transmission to the MAC core

x Handles automatic retransmission of Collision frames for transmission

x Discards frames on late collision, excessive collisions, excessive deferral and underrun conditions

x Software control to flush Tx FIFO

x Calculates and inserts IPv4 header checksum and TCP, UDP, or ICMP checksum in frames transmitted

in Store-and-Forward mode



14.2.2 DMA features

x Supports all AHB burst types in the AHB Slave Interface

x Software can select the type of AHB burst (fixed or indefinite burst) in the AHB Master interface.

x Option to select address-aligned bursts from AHB master port

x Optimization for packet-oriented DMA transfers with frame delimiters

x Byte-aligned addressing for data buffer support

x Dual-buffer (ring) or linked-list (chained) descriptor chaining

x Descriptor architecture, allowing large blocks of data transfer with minimum CPU intervention

x each descriptor can transfer up to 8 KB of data

x Comprehensive status reporting for normal operation and transfers with errors

x Individual programmable burst size for Transmit and Receive DMA Engines for optimal host bus

utilization

x Programmable interrupt options for different operational conditions

x Per-frame Transmit/Receive complete interrupt control

x Round-robin or fixed-priority arbitration between Receive and Transmit engines

x Start/Stop modes

x Current Tx/Rx buffer pointer as status registers

x Current Tx/Rx descriptor pointer as status registers



14.2.3 PTP features

x Received and transmitted frames time stamping

x Coarse and fine correction methods
Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        411 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

x Trigger interrupt when system time becomes greater than target time

x Pulse per second output (product alternate function output)


    14.3 Functional description: SMI and RMII


The Mac peripheral consists of a MAC 802.3 (media access control) with a dedicated DMA controller. It

supports reduced media-independent interface (RMII).

The Transmit FIFO (Tx FIFO) buffers data read from system memory by the DMA before transmission

by the MAC Core. Similarly, the Receive FIFO (Rx FIFO) stores the Ethernet frames received from the

line until they are transferred to system memory by the DMA.

The Mac peripheral also includes an SMI to communicate with external PHY. A set of configuration

registers permit the user to select the desired mode and features for the MAC and the DMA controller.

14.3.1 Station management interface: SMI

The station management interface (SMI) allows the application to access any PHY registers through a

2-wire clock and data lines. The interface supports accessing up to 32 PHYs.

The application can select one of the 32 PHYs and one of the 32 registers within any PHY and send

control data or receive status information. Only one register in one PHY can be addressed at any given

time.

Both the MDC clock line and the MDIO data line are implemented as alternate function I/O in the

microcontroller:

x MDC: a periodic clock that provides the timing reference for the data transfer at the maximum

    frequency of 2.5 MHz. The minimum high and low times for MDC must be

x 160 ns each, and the minimum period for MDC must be 400 ns. In idle state the SMI management

    interface drives the MDC clock signal low.

x MDIO: data input/output bitstream to transfer status information to/from the PHY device

    synchronously with the MDC clock signal




    =
    «









    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    412 / 1446

                                                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
 14.3.1.1     SMI frame format

 The frame structure related to a read or write operation is shown in Table 14-1, the order of bit

 transmission must be from left to right.

 Table 14-1 Management frame format

          Management frame fields

          Preamble (32 bits)   Start     Operation PADDR  RADDR           TA  Data (16 bits)        Idle

 Read             1... 1          01     10        ppppp   rrrrr          Z0  ddddddddddddddd          Z

 Write            1... 1          01     01        ppppp   rrrrr          10  ddddddddddddddd          Z

 The management frame consists of eight fields:

x Preamble: each transaction (read or write) can be initiated with the preamble field that corresponds

 to 32 contiguous logic one bits on the MDIO line with 32 corresponding cycles on MDC. This field is

 used to establish synchronization with the PHY device.

x Start: the start of frame is defined by a <01> pattern to verify transitions on the line from the

 default logic one state to zero and back to one.

x Operation: defines the type of transaction (read or write) in progress.

x PADDR: the PHY address is 5 bits, allowing 32 unique PHY addresses. The MSB bit of the address is

 the first transmitted and received.

x RADDR: the register address is 5 bits, allowing 32 individual registers to be addressed within the

 selected PHY device. The MSB bit of the address is the first transmitted and received.

x TA: the turn-around field defines a 2-bit pattern between the RADDR and DATA fields to avoid

 contention during a read transaction. For a read transaction the MAC controller drives high-

 impedance on the MDIO line for the 2 bits of TA. The PHY device must drive a high-impedance

 state on the first bit of TA, a zero bit on the second one. For a write transaction, the MAC controller

 drives a <10> pattern during the TA field. The PHY device must drive a high-impedance state for

 the 2 bits of TA.

x Data: the data field is 16-bit. The first bit transmitted and received must be bit 15 of the ETH_MIID

 register.

x Idle: the MDIO line is driven in high-impedance state. All three-state drivers must be disabled and

 the PHY’s pull-up resistor keeps the line at logic one.





 {dango           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                           413 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
14.3.1.2 SMI write operation

When the application sets the MII Write and Busy bits (in Ethernet MAC MII address register

(ETH_MACMIIAR)), the SMI initiates a write operation into the PHY registers by transferring the PHY

address, the register address in PHY, and the write data (in Ethernet MAC MII data register

(ETH_MACMIIDR). The application should not change the MII Address register contents or the MII Data

register while the transaction is ongoing. Write operations to the MII Address register or the MII Data

register during this period are ignored (the Busy bit is high), and the transaction is completed without

any error. After the Write operation has completed, the SMI indicates this by resetting the busy bit.

Figure 14-3 Shows the frame format for the write operation.



    MDC      '        '    '                 '                         '   '

                                                                                      |
     wo                    |                 \                         \   \       OX|i
    Preamble!⁺  Start  OFpo!                 |                         [   '    data  |
                of     code     PHY address  'Register address                        !
                "frame"    ,                 H                                        |

                    Data to PHY

                Figure 14-3 MDIO timing and frame structure - Write cycle

    14.3.1.3 SMI read operation

    When the user sets the MII Busy bit in the Ethernet MAC MII address register (ETH_MACMIIAR) with

    the MII Write bit at 0, the SMI initiates a read operation in the PHY registers by transferring the PHY

    address and the register address in PHY. The application should not change the MII Address register

    contents or the MII Data register while the transaction is ongoing. Write operations to the MII Address

    register or MII Data register during this period are ignored (the Busy bit is high) and the transaction is

    completed without any error. After the read operation has completed, the SMI resets the Busy bit and

    then updates the MII Data register with the data read from the PHY.

    Figure 14-4 Shows the frame format for the read operation.









    |           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                          414 / 1446
