   15.5 USB_HS registers


    By reading from and writing to the control and status registers (CSRs) through the AHB slave interface,

    the application controls the USB_HS controller. These registers are 32 bits wide, and the addresses are

    32-bit block aligned. The USB_HS registers must be accessed by words (32 bits). CSRs are classified as

    follows:

    • Core global registers

    • Host-mode registers

    • Host global registers

    • Host port CSRs

    • Host channel-specific registers

    • Device-mode registers

    • Device global registers

    • Device endpoint-specific registers

    i        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    551 / 1446

                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• Power and clock-gating registers

• PHY CSRs

Only the Core global, Power and clock-gating, PHY CSRs, and host port control and status registers can

be accessed in both host and peripheral modes. When the USB_HS controller is operating in one mode,

either peripheral or host, the application must not access registers from the other mode. If an illegal

access occurs, a mode mismatch interrupt is generated and reflected in the Core interrupt register (MMIS

bit in the USB_HS_GINTSTS register). When the core switches from one mode to the other, the registers

in the new mode of operation must be reprogrammed as they would be after a power-on reset.

The host and device mode registers occupy different addresses. All registers are implemented in the AHB

clock domain.

    15.5.1 USB_HS_GAHBCFG

    Description: AHB Configuration Register

    This register can be used to configure the core after power-on or a change in mode. This register mainly

    contains AHB system-related configuration parameters. Do not change this register after the initial

    programming. The application must program this register before starting any transactions on either the

    AHB or the USB.

    Offset: 0x0008

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-2 USB_HS_GAHBCFG Register Description

     Bit   Symbol     Type  Description
     31:9  Rsvd       -     Reserved                                                                     |

                            Periodic TxFIFO Empty Level

                            Mode: Host only

                            Indicates when the Periodic TxFIFO Empty Interrupt bit in the Core Interrupt

     8     PTXFELVL   rw    register (PTXFE bit in USB_HS_GINTSTS) is triggered. This bit is used only

                            in Slave mode.

                            0: PTXFE interrupt indicates that the Periodic TxFIFO is half empty

                            1: PTXFE interrupt indicates that the Periodic TxFIFO is completely empty



    fi                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    555 / 1446

                                                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     Non-Periodic TxFIFO Empty Level

                     Mode: Host and device

                     This bit is used only in Slave mode. In host mode and with Shared FIFO with

    device mode, this bit indicates when the Non-Periodic TxFIFO Empty

                     Interrupt bit in the Core Interrupt register (TXFE in USB_HS_DIEPINTx.) is

                     triggered.

                     With dedicated FIFO in device mode, this bit indicates when IN endpoint

7   TXFELVL | rw     Transmit FIFO empty interrupt (TXFE in USB_HS_DIEPINTx.) is triggered.

                     Host mode and with Shared FIFO with device mode:

                     0: TXFE interrupt indicates that the Non-Periodic TxFIFO is half empty

                     1: TXFE interrupt indicates that the Non-Periodic TxFIFO is completely empty

                     Dedicated FIFO in device mode:

                     0: TXFE interrupt indicates that the IN Endpoint TxFIFO is half empty

                     1: TXFE interrupt indicates that the IN Endpoint TxFIFO is completely empty

6   Rsvd    -        Reserved

                     DMA Enable

                     Mode: Host and device

5   DMAEN   rw       This bit is always 0 when Slave-Only mode has been selected.

                     0: Core operates in Slave mode

                     1: Core operates in a DMA mode

                     Burst Length/Type

                     Mode: Host and device

4:1 | HBSTLEN | rw   Internal DMA Mode AHB Master burst type:

                     0x0: 1 word or single

                     0x1: 4 words or INCR

                     0x2: 8 words

                     0x3: 16 words or INCR4

                     0x4: 32 words

                     0x5: 64 words or INCR8

                     0x6: 128 words

                     0x7: 256 words or INCR16

                     0x8: Others reserved

                     Global Interrupt Mask

                     Mode: Host and device

                     The application uses this bit to mask or unmask the interrupt line assertion

0     GINTMSK | rw   to itself. Irrespective of this bit's setting, the interrupt status registers are

                     updated by the controller.

                     0: Mask the interrupt assertion to the application.

                     1: Unmask the interrupt assertion to the application.

15.5.2 USB_HS_GUSBCFG

Description: USB Configuration Register

This register can be used to configure the core after power-on or when changing to Host mode or Device

mode. It contains USB and USB-PHY related configuration parameters. The application must program

this register before starting any transactions on either the AHB or the USB. If you are using the HSIC

interface, HSIC PHY must be in reset while programming this register. Do not make changes to this

register after the initial programming.

Offset: 0x000C

Reset: 0x0000_1408

 Bit   Symbol         Type  Description

                            Corrupt Tx packet
 31    CTXPKT         w
                            Mode: Host and device

                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      This bit is for debug purposes only. Never Set this bit to 1.

                      The application should always write 1'b0 to this bit.

                      0:Normal Mode

                      1: Debug Mode

                      Force Device Mode

                      Mode:Host and device

                      Writing a 1 to this bit forces the controller to device mode.

30    FDMOD      rw   After setting the force bit, the application must wait at least 25 ms
                      before the change to take effect. When the simulation is in scale down

                      mode, waiting for 500 micro sec is sufficient.

                      0: Normal Mode

                      1: Force Device Mode

                      Force Host Mode

                      Mode: Host and device

                      Writing a 1 to this bit forces the core to host mode.

29     FHMOD     rw   After setting the force bit, the application must wait at least 25 ms
                      before the change to take effect. When the simulation is in scale down

                      mode, waiting for 500 micro sec is sufficient.

                      0: Normal Mode

                      1: Force Host Mode

28:16  Rsvd      -    Reserved

                      PHY Low-Power Clock Select

                      Mode: Host and Device
15     PHYLPCS   rw
                      Selects either 480-MHz or 48-MHz (low-power) PHY mode.

                      In FS and LS modes, the PHY can usually operate on a 48-MHz clock

fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            558 / 1446

                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   to save power.

                   In 480 MHz mode, the UTMI interface operates at either 60 or 30-

                   MHz, depending upon whether 8- or 16-bit data width is selected. In

                   48-MHz mode, the UTMI interface operates at 48 MHz in FS mode

                   and at either 48 or 6 MHz in LS mode (depending on the PHY vendor).

                   This bit drives the utmi_fsls_low_power core output signal, and is

                   valid only for UTMI+ PHYs.

                   0: 480-MHz Internal PLL clock

                   1: 48-MHz External Clock

14 Rsvd      -     Reserved

                   USB Turnaround Time

                   Mode: Device only

                   Sets the turnaround time in PHY clocks. Specifies the response time

                   for a MAC request to the Packet FIFO Controller (PFC) to fetch data

                   from the DFIFO (SPRAM).

13:10 TRDT    rw   Note: The    previous values  are  calculated for the minimum   AHB
                   frequency of 30 MHz. USB turnaround time is critical for certification

                   where long cables and 5-Hubs are used. If you need the AHB to run

                   at less than 30 MHz, and if USB turnaround time is not critical, these

                   bits can be programmed to a larger value.

                   0x9: MAC interface is 8-bit UTMI+.

                   0x5: MAC interface is 16-bit UTMI+.

                   HNP-Capable

                   Mode: Host and Device

9     HNPCAP r     The application uses    this  bit  to control the  controller's HNP

                   capabilities.

                   If HNP functionality is disabled by the software, the USB signals on

    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.     AA A A     A                A        559 / 1446

                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  the PHY domain must be tied to the appropriate values.

                  0:HNP capability is not enabled.

                  1: HNP capability is enabled

                  SRP-Capable

                  Mode: Host and Device

    The application uses this bit to control the controller's SRP

                  capabilities. If the core operates as a non-SRP-capable Bdevice, it

                  cannot request the connected A-device (host) to activate VBUS and
8   SRPCAP   r    start a session.

                  If SRP functionality is disabled by the software, the USB signals on

                  the PHY domain must be tied to the appropriate values.

                  0x0: SRP capability is not enabled

                  0x1: SRP capability is enabled

7   Rsvd     -    Reserved

                  USB 2.0 High-Speed PHY or USB 1.1 Full-Speed Serial Transceiver

                  Select

                  Mode: Host and Device

                  The application uses this bit to select either a high-speed UTMI+ or

                  ULPI PHY, or a full-speed transceiver.

                  If a USB 1.1 Full-Speed Serial Transceiver interface was not selected

6  PHSEL     rw   in, this bit is always 0, with Write Only access.

                  If a high-speed PHY interface was not selected in, this bit is always

                  1, with Write Only access.

    If both interface types were selected (parameters have nonzero

    values), the application uses this bit to select which interface is

                  active, and access is Read and Write.

                  0: USB 2.0 high-speed UTMI+ PHY is selected

                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                      560 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                           1: USB 1.1 full-speed serial transceiver is selected

5     Rsvd     -           Reserved

                           ULPI or UTMI+ Select

                           Mode: Host and Device

                           The application uses this bit to select either a UTMI+ interface or ULPI
4     ULPI_UTMI_SEL | rw   Interface.

                           0: UTMI+ Interface

                           1: ULPI Interface

                           PHY Interface

                           Mode: Host and Device

                           The application uses this bit to configure the core to support a UTMI+

    PHY with an 8- or 16-bit interface. When a ULPI PHY is chosen, this

                           must be Set to 8-bit mode.
3     PHYIF    rw
                           This bit is writable only If UTMI+ and ULPI were selected.

    Otherwise, this bit returns the value for the power-on interface

                           selected during configuration.

                           0: PHY 8bit Mode

                           1: PHY 16bit Mode

                           HS/FS Timeout Calibration

                           Mode: Host and Device

                           The number of PHY clocks that the application programs in this field

2:0   TOCAL    rw          is added to the high-speed/full-speed interpacket timeout duration in
                           the core to account for any additional delays introduced by the PHY.

                           This can be required, because the delay introduced by the PHY in

                           generating the linestate condition can vary from one PHY to another.

                           The USB standard timeout value for high-speed operation is 736 to

fi                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                             561 / 1446

                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                           816 (inclusive) bit times. The USB standard timeout value for full-

                           speed operation is 16 to 18 (inclusive) bit times.

    The application must program this field based on the speed of

                           enumeration. The number of bit times added per PHY clock are as

                           follows:

                           High-speed operation:

                           ■ One 30-MHz PHY clock = 16 bit times

                           ■ One 60-MHz PHY clock = 8 bit times

                           Full-speed operation:

                           ■ One 30-MHz PHY clock = 0.4 bit times

                           ■ One 60-MHz PHY clock = 0.2 bit times

                           ■ One 48-MHz PHY clock = 0.25 bit times

        -Periodic TxFIFO 10 flush in device mode when in shared FIFO operation

        -Periodic TxFIFO 11 flush in device mode when in shared FIFO operation

        -Periodic TxFIFO 12 flush in device mode when in shared FIFO operation
{dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 564 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

        -TXFIFO 12 flush in device mode when in dedicated FIFO mode

        0xD:

        -Periodic TxFIFO 13 flush in Device mode when in shared FIFO operation

        -TXFIFO 13 flush in device mode when in dedicated FIFO mode

        0xE:

        -Periodic TxFIFO 14 flush in Device mode when in shared FIFO operation

        -TXFIFO 14 flush in device mode when in dedicated FIFO mode

        0xF:

        -Periodic TxFIFO 15 flush in Device mode when in shared FIFO operation

        - TXFIFO 15 flush in device mode when in dedicated FIFO mode

        0x10: Flush all the transmit FIFOs in device or host mode

        TxFIFO Flush

        Mode: Host and Device

        This bit selectively flushes a single or all transmit FIFOs, but cannot do so If

        the core is in the midst of a transaction.

        The application must write this bit only after checking that the core is neither

        writing to the TxFIFO nor reading from the TxFIFO.

        Verify using these registers:
5 TXFFLSH | rw ■ ReadNAK Effective Interrupt ensures the core is not reading from the FIFO

        ■ Write USB_HS_GRSTCTL.AHBIDL ensures the core is not writing anything

        to the FIFO.

        Flushing is normally recommended when FIFOs are reconfigured or when

        switching between Shared FIFO and Dedicated Transmit FIFO operation.

        FIFO flushing is also recommended during device endpoint disable. The

        application must wait until the core clears this bit before performing any

        operations. This bit takes eight clocks to clear, using the slower clock of

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 565 / 1446

                                                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   phy_clk or hclk.

                   0x0: No Flush

                   0x1: Selectively flushes a single or all transmit FIFOs

                   RxFIFO Flush

                   Mode: Host and Device

                   The application can flush the entire RxFIFO using this bit, but must first

                   ensure that the core is not in the middle of a transaction. The application

                   must only write to this bit after checking that the controller is neither reading

4   RXFFLSH | rw   from the RxFIFO nor writing to the RxFIFO.

                   The application must wait until the bit is cleared before performing any other

                   operations. This bit requires eight clocks (slowest of PHY or AHB clock) to

                   clear.

                   0: Does not flush the entire RxFIFO

                   1: Flushes the entire RxFIFO

3   Rsvd    -      Reserved

                   Host Frame Counter Reset

                   Mode: Host only

                   The application writes this bit to reset the (micro) Frame number counter

                   inside the core. When the (micro) Frame counter is reset, the subsequent

2   FCRST   rw     SOF sent out by the core has a (micro)Frame number of 0.

    When application writes 1 to the bit, it might not be able to read back the

                   value as it will get cleared by the core in a few clock cycles.

                   0: No reset

                   1: Host Frame Counter Reset

                   PIU FS Dedicated Controller Soft Reset
1   HSRST   rw
                   Mode: Host and Device

        —_— Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                             566 / 1446

                                                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

             Resets the PIU FS Dedicated Controller

             All module state machines in FS Dedicated Controller of PIU are reset to the

             IDLE state. Used to reset the FS Dedicated controller in PIU in case of any

             PHY Errors like Loss of activity or Babble Error resulting in the PHY remaining

             in RX state for more than one frame boundary.

             This is a self clearing bit and core clears this bit after all the necessary logic

             is reset in the core.

             0: No Reset

             1: PIU FS Dedicated Controller Soft Reset

             Core Soft Reset

             Mode: Host and Device

             Resets the hclk and phy_clock domains as follows:

             ■ Clears the interrupts and all the CSR registers except the following register

             bits:

             - USB_HS_PCGCCTL.RSTPDMODL

             - USB_HS_PCGCCTL.GAYEHCLK

             - USB_HS_PCGCCTL.PWRCLMP

0 CSRST rw   - USB_HS_PCGCCTL.STPPCLK

             - USB_HS_HCFG.FSLSPCS

             - USB_HS_DCFG.DSPD

             ■ All module state machines

             ■ All module state machines (except the AHB Slave Unit) are reset to the

             IDLE state, and all the transmit FIFOs and the receive FIFO are flushed.

             ■ Any transactions on the AHB Master are terminated as soon as possible,

             after gracefully completing the last data phase of an AHB transfer. Any

             transactions on the USB are terminated immediately.

fi               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   567 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

        ■ When Hibernation or ADP feature is enabled, the PMU module is not reset

        by the Core Soft Reset.

        The application can write to this bit any time it wants to reset the core. This

        is a self-clearing bit and the core clears this bit after all the necessary logic

        is reset in the core, which can take several clocks, depending on the current

        state of the core. Once this bit is cleared software must wait at least 3 PHY

        clocks before doing any access to the PHY domain (synchronization delay).

        Software must also must check that bit 31 of this register is 1 (AHB Master

        is IDLE) before starting any operation.

        Typically software reset is used during software development and also when

        you dynamically change the PHY selection bits in the USB configuration

        registers listed above. When you change the PHY, the corresponding clock

        for the PHY is selected and used in the PHY domain. Once a new clock is

        selected, the PHY domain has to be reset for proper operation.

        0: No reset

        1: Resets hclk and phy_clock domains

15.5.4 USB_HS_GINTSTS

Description: Interrupt Register

This register interrupts the application for system-level events in the current mode (Device mode or Host

mode).

Some of the bits in this register are valid only in Host mode, while others are valid in Device mode only.

This register also indicates the current mode. To clear the interrupt status bits of type rw, the application

must write 1'b1 to the bit.

The FIFO status interrupts are read only; once software reads from or writes to the FIFO while servicing

these interrupts, FIFO interrupt conditions are cleared automatically.

The application must clear the USB_HS_GINTSTS register at initialization before unmasking the interrupt

bit to avoid any interrupts generated prior to initialization.

Offset: 0x0014

Reset: 0x1400_0022

                        Data Fetch Suspended

                        Mode: Device only

                        This interrupt is valid only in DMA mode. This interrupt indicates that

    the core has stopped fetching data. For IN endpoints due to the
22   DATAFSUSP | r_w1c  unavailability of TxFIFO space or Request Queue space. This interrupt is

                        used by the pplication for an endpoint mismatch algorithm.

                        For example, after detecting an endpoint mismatch, the application:

                        ■ Sets a Global non-periodic IN NAK handshake

                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                             571 / 1446

                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

             ■ Disables IN endpoints

             ■ Flushes the FIFO

             ■ Determines the token sequence from the IN Token Sequence Learning

             Queue

             ■ Re-enables the endpoints

             ■ Clears the Global non-periodic IN NAK handshake

             If the Global non-periodic IN NAK is cleared, the core has not yet fetched

        data for the IN endpoint, and the IN token is received. The core

             generates an 'IN token received when FIFO empty' interrupt. It then

             sends the host a NAK response. To avoid this scenario, the application

             can check the USB_FS_GINTSTS.FetSusp interrupt, which ensures that

             the FIFO is full before clearing a Global NAK handshake.

             Alternatively, the application can mask the IN token received when FIFO

             empty interrupt when clearing a Global IN NAK handshake.

             0x0: Not active

             0x1: Data Fetch Suspended

             Incomplete Periodic Transfer

             Mode: Host only

             In Host mode, the core sets this interrupt bit when there are incomplete

             periodic transactions still pending which are scheduled for the current

             microframe.

    21 IPXFR r_w1c | Incomplete Isochronous OUT Transfer

        least one isochronous OUT endpoint on which the transfer is not

        with the End of Periodic Frame Interrupt (EOPF) bit in this register.

17:16 Rsvd -      Reserved

                  End of Periodic Frame Interrupt

                  Mode: Device only

                  Indicates that the period specified in the Periodic Frame Interval field of

                  the Device Configuration register (PFIVL bit in USB_HS_DCFG) has been

    reached in the current microframe. In case of Non-Ignore Frame

                  Number Scatter/Gather (Descriptor DMA) mode, the controller internally

                  handles the following scenarios based on EOPF:

                  Read Flush: At the EOPF, the controller checks if there are any pending

                  packets in the FIFO corresponding to the current (micro)Frame.

                  ■ If there are any pending packets, then the controller initiates read

15    EOPF r_w1c  flush, due to which the read pointer is updated to the starting location
                  of the next micro-frame packet.

    ■ If there are no pending packets corresponding to the current

                  (micro)Frame, the controller does not take any action.

                  Write Flush: At the EOPF, if the controller is still fetching the current

    micro-frame data, then the controller stops pushing data into the

    TXFIFO but keeps fetching the complete packet from the System

                  Memory. After completing the scheduled packet size fetch, the controller

                  updates the Status Quadlet Fields (Transmit Status to BUFFLUSH) and

                  closes the Descriptor. During the descriptor close, the controller initiates

                  write flush, due to which the write pointer is updated to the starting

                  location of the next micro-frame packet.

|                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.             574 / 1446

                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                Because the controller stops pushing the packet to the TxFIFO after

                EOPF, to bring the write pointer to the starting location of the next micro-

                frame, write flush is done.

                0x0: Not active

                0x1: End of Periodic Frame Interrupt

                Isochronous OUT Packet Dropped Interrupt

                Mode: Device only

                The controller sets this bit when it fails to write an isochronous OUT

                packet into the RxFIFO because the
14 ISOODRP r_w1c
    RxFIFO does not have enough space to accommodate a maximum

                packet size packet for the isochronous OUT endpoint.

                0x0: Not active

                0x1: Isochronous OUT Packet Dropped Interrupt

                Mode: Device only

                The core sets this bit to indicate that speed enumeration is complete.

                to obtain the enumerated speed.

                0x1: Enumeration Done

                The controller sets this bit to indicate that a reset is detected on the

3   SOF      r_w1c  Start of (micro) Frame

                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.     ri AA AAA A              577 / 1446

                                                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    Mode: Host and Device

                    In Host mode, the core sets this bit to indicate that an SOF (FS), micro-

                    SOF (HS), or Keep-Alive (LS) is transmitted on the USB. The application

                    must write a 1 to this bit to clear the interrupt.

                    In Device mode, the controller sets this bit to indicate that an SOF token

                    has been received on the USB. The application can read the Device

                    Status register to get the current (micro) Frame number. This interrupt

                    is seen only when the core is operating at either HS or FS. This bit can

                    be set only by the core and the application must write 1 to clear it.

                    Note: This register may return 1'b1 if read immediately after power-on

                    reset. If the register bit reads 1'b1 immediately after power-on reset, it

                    does not indicate that an SOF has been sent (in case of host mode) or

                    SOF has been received (in case of device mode). The read value of this

                    interrupt is valid only after a valid connection between host and device

                    is established. If the bit is set after power on reset the application can

                    clear the bit.

                    0x0: No Start of Frame

                    0x1: Start of Frame

                    USB Interrupt

                    Mode: Host and Device

                    The controller sets this bit to indicate an USB protocol event.

    The application must read the USB Interrupt Status
2   USBINT   r      (USB_HS_GUSBINT) register to determine the exact event that caused

                    this interrupt. The application must clear the appropriate status bit in

                    the USB_HS_GUSBINT register to clear this bit.

                    0x0: No Interrupt

                    0x1: USB Interrupt

1   MMIS     r_w1c  Mode Mismatch Interrupt

                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               578 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        Mode: Host and Device

                        The core sets this bit when the application is trying to access:

                        ■ A Host mode register, when the controller is operating in Device mode

                        ■ A Device mode register, when the controller is operating in Host mode

                        The register access is completed on the AHB with an OKAY response,

        but is ignored by the controller internally and does not affect the

                        operation of the controller.

                        This bit can be set only by the core and the application should write 1

                        to clear it.

                        0x0: No Mode Mismatch Interrupt

                        0x1: Mode Mismatch Interrupt

                        Current Mode of Operation

                        Mode: Host and Device

    0      CMOD     r   Indicates the current mode.

                        0x0: Device mode

                        0x1: Host mode

    15.5.5 USB_HS_GINTMSK

    Description: Interrupt Mask Register

    This register works with the Interrupt Register (USB_HS_GINTSTS) to interrupt the application. When

an interrupt bit is masked, the interrupt associated with that bit is not generated. However, the

    USB_HS_GINTSTS register bit corresponding to that interrupt is still set.

    Note: The fields of this register change depending on host or device mode.

    Offset: 0x0018

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

15.5.6 USB_HS_GRXSTSR

Description: Receive Status Debug Read Register

fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.       584 / 1446

                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

A read to the Receive Status Debug Read register returns the contents of the top of the Receive FIFO.

The receive status contents must be interpreted differently in Host and Device modes. The core ignores

the receive status read when the receive FIFO is empty and returns a value of 32'h0000_0000.

Note:

❑ Use of these fields vary based on whether the core is functioning as a host or a device.

❑ Do not read this register's reset value before configuring the core because the read value is 'X' in the

simulation.

Offset: 0x001C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 15-7 USB_HS_GRXSTSR Register Description

 Bit    Symbol   Type  Description

 31:21  Rsvd     -     Reserved

                       Packet Status indicates the status of the received packet.

                       In host mode,

                       4'b0010: IN data packet received

                       4'b0011: IN transfer completed (triggers an interrupt)

                       4'b0101: Data toggle error (triggers an interrupt)

                       4'b0111: Channel halted (triggers an interrupt)

                       Others: Reserved
20:17  PKTSTS   r
                       In device mode,

                       4'b0001: Global OUT NAK (triggers an interrupt)

                       4'b0010: OUT data packet received

                       4'b0011: OUT transfer completed (triggers an interrupt)

                       4'b0100: SETUP transaction completed (triggers an interrupt)

                       4'b0110: SETUP data packet received

                       Others: Reserved

15.5.7 USB_HS_GRXSTSP

Description: Receive Status Read/Pop Register

A read to the Receive Status Read and Pop register returns the contents of the top of the Receive FIFO

and additionally pops the top data entry out of the RxFIFO.

The receive status contents must be interpreted differently in Host and Device modes. The core ignores

the receive status pop/read when the receive FIFO is empty and returns a value of 32'h0000_0000. The

application must only pop the Receive Status FIFO when the Receive FIFO Non-Empty bit of the Core

Interrupt register (RXFLVL bit in USB_HS_GINTSTS) is asserted.

Note:

❑ Use of these fields vary based on whether the core is functioning as a host or a device.

❑ Do not read this register's reset value before configuring the core because the read value is 'X' in the

                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                     586 / 1446

                                                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

simulation.

Offset: 0x0020

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 15-8 USB_HS_GRXSTSP Register Description

 Bit    Symbol   Type  Description

                       Packet Status indicates the status of the received packet. In host mode,

                       4'b0010: IN data packet received

                       4'b0011: IN transfer completed (triggers an interrupt)

                       4'b0101: Data toggle error (triggers an interrupt)

                       4'b0111: Channel halted (triggers an interrupt)

                       Others: Reserved

                       4'b0001: Global OUT NAK (triggers an interrupt)

                       4'b0100: SETUP transaction completed (triggers an interrupt)

                       4'b0110: SETUP data packet received

                       Others: Reserved

                       Data PID

                       In host mode, indicates the Data PID of the received packet.

                       In device mode, indicates the Data PID of the received OUT data packet.
16:15  DPID     r

15.5.8 USB_HS_GRXFSIZ

Description: Receive FIFO Size Register

The application can program the RAM size that must be allocated to the RxFIFO.

Offset: 0x0024

Reset: 0x0000_0400

Table 15-9 USB_HS_GRXFSIZ Register Description

 Bit    Symbol  Type  Description

 31:11  Rsvd    -     Reserved

                      RxFIFO Depth

                      Mode: Host and Device

                      This value is in terms of 32-bit words.

                      ■ Minimum value is 16

                      ■ Maximum value is 32,768

10:0   | RXFD  rw     The power-on reset value of this register is specified as the Largest Rx Data

                      FIFO Depth during configuration.

                      If Enable Dynamic FIFO Sizing is selected in coreConsultant, these flops are

                      optimized, and reads return the power-on value.

                      If Enable Dynamic FIFO Sizing is selected in coreConsultant, you can write a

                      new value in this field. Programmed values must not exceed the power-on

                      value.

    15.5.10       USB_HS_GNPTXSTS

    Description: Non-periodic Transmit FIFO/Queue Status Register

    In Device mode, this register is valid only in Shared FIFO operation.

This read-only register contains the free space information for the Non-periodic TxFIFO and the

    Nonperiodic

    Transmit Request Queue.

    Offset: 0x002c

    Reset: 0x0008_0100

    Table 15-12 USB_HS_GNPTXSTS Register Description

     Bit   Symbol     Type  Description

     31    Rsvd       -     Reserved

                            Top of the Non-periodic Transmit Request Queue

        Entry in the Non-periodic Tx Request Queue that is currently being

                            processed by the MAC.

                            ■ Bits [30:27]: Channel/endpoint number

                            ■ Bits [26:25]:

    30:24 NPTXQTOP    r    | ■ 2'b00: IN/OUT token

                            - 2'b01: Zero-length transmit packet (device IN/host OUT)

                            - 2'b10: PING/CSPLIT token

                            - 2'b11: Channel halt command

                            ■ Bit [24]: Terminate (last Entry for selected channel/endpoint)

                            Reset: 7'h0

                            Non-periodic Transmit Request Queue Space Available

    23:16 NPTQXSAV | r      Indicates the amount of free space available in the Nonperiodic Transmit

                            Request Queue. This queue holds both IN and OUT requests in Host mode.

    fi                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                             591 / 1446

                                                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        Device mode has only IN requests.

                        ■ 8'h0: Non-periodic Transmit Request Queue is full

                        ■ 8'h1: 1 location available

                        ■ 8'h2: 2 locations available

                        ■ n: n locations available (0 <= n <= 8)

                        ■ Others: Reserved

                        Non-periodic TxFIFO Space Avail

                        Indicates the amount of free space available in the Nonperiodic TxFIFO.

                        Values are in terms of 32-bit words.

                        ■ 16'h0: Non-periodic TxFIFO is full

15:0   NPTXFSAV     r   ■ 16'h1: 1 word available

                        ■ 16'h2: 2 words available

                        ■ 16'hn: n words available (where 0 <= n <= 32,768)

                        ■ 16'h8000: 32,768 words available

                        ■ Others: Reserved

15.5.13     USB_HS_HCFG

Description: Host Configuration Register

Offset: 0x0400

Reset: 0x0012_0000

Table 15-15 USB_HS_HCFG Register Description

 Bit   Symbol   Type   Description
 31:3  Rsvd     -      Reserved                                                                    |

                       FS- and LS-Only Support

                       The application uses this bit to control the core's enumeration speed. Using

                       this bit, the application can make the core enumerate as a FS host, even If

                       the connected device supports HS traffic. Do not make changes to this field

                       after initial programming.
 2     FSLSS    rw
                       0x0 :

                       HS/FS/LS, based on the maximum speed supported by the connected device

                       0x1 :

                       FS/LS-only, even if the connected device can support HS

                       FS/LS PHY Clock Select

                       When the core is in FS Host mode

                       ■ 2'b00: PHY clock is running at 30/60 MHz

1:0   | FSLSPCS | rw   ■ 2'b01: PHY clock is running at 48 MHz

                       ■ Others: Reserved

                       When the core is in LS Host mode

                       ■ 2'b00: PHY clock is running at 30/60 MHz. When the UTMI+/ULPI PHY Low


fi                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    594 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      Power mode is not selected, use 30/60 MHz.

                      ■ 2'b01: PHY clock is running at 48 MHz. When the UTMI+ PHY Low Power

                      mode is selected, use 48MHz If the PHY supplies a 48 MHz clock during LS

                      mode.

                      ■ 2'b10: PHY clock is running at 6 MHz. In USB 1.1 FS mode, use 6 MHz when

                      the UTMI+ PHY Low Power mode is selected and the PHY supplies a 6 MHz

                      clock during LS mode. If you select a 6 MHz clock during LS mode, you must

                      do a soft reset.

                      ■ 2'b11: Reserved

15.5.14     USB_HS_HFIR

Description: Host Frame Interval Register

Offset: 0x0404

Reset: 0x0000_0B8F

Table 15-16 USB_HS_HFIR Register Description

 Bit    Symbol  Type  Description

 31:16  Rsvd    -     Reserved

                      Frame Interval

                      The value that the application programs to this field specifies the interval

                      between two consecutive SOFs (FS) or micro-SOFs (HS) or Keep-Alive tokens

                      (HS). This field contains the number of PHY clocks that constitute the required

                      frame interval. The Default value set in this field is for FS operation when the

                      PHY clock frequency is 60 MHz. The application can    write a value to this

15:0   | FRIVL rw     register only after the Port Enable bit of the Host Port Control and Status

                      register (PENA bit in USB_HS_HPRT) has been Set. If no value is programmed,

                      the core calculates the value based on the PHY clock specified in the FS/LS

    PHY Clock Select field of the Host Configuration register (FSLSPCS in

    USB_HS_HCFG). Do not change the value of this field after the initial

                      configuration.

                      ■ 125 s * (PHY clock frequency for HS)

fi                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                     595 / 1446

                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                       ■ 1 ms * (PHY clock frequency for FS/LS)

15.5.15     USB_HS_HFNUM

Description: Host Frame Number/Frame Time Remaining Register

This register indicates the current frame number. It also indicates the time remaining (in terms of the

number of PHY clocks) in the current (micro) frame.

Offset: 0x0408

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 15-17 USB_HS_HFNUM Register Description

 Bit    Symbol   Type  Description

                       Frame Time Remaining

                       Indicates the amount of time remaining in the current microframe (HS) or

                       Frame (FS/LS), in terms of PHY clocks.
 31:16  FTREM    r
                       This field decrements on each PHY clock. When it reaches zero, this field is

                       reloaded with the value in the Frame Interval register and a new SOF is

                       transmitted on the USB.

                       Frame Number

                       This field increments when a new SOF is transmitted on the USB, and is reset

 15:0   FRNUM | r      to 0 when it reaches 16'h3FFF.

                       0x0: No SOF is transmitted

                       0x1: SOF is transmitted

23:16 PTXQSAV r

15:0  PTXFSAVL r

    15.5.17      USB_HS_HAINT

    Description: Host All Channels Interrupt Register

    When a significant event occurs on a channel, the Host All Channels Interrupt register interrupts the

application using the Host Channels Interrupt bit of the Core Interrupt register (HCINT bit in

    USB_HS_GINTSTS).

    There is one interrupt bit per channel, up to a maximum of 16 bits. Bits in this register are set and

    cleared when the application sets and clears bits in the corresponding Host Channel-n Interrupt register.

    Offset: 0x0414

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-19 USB_HS_HAINT Register Description

     Bit     Symbol   Type     Description

     31:12   Rsvd     -        Reserved

                               Channel Interrupt for channel no.

     11:0    HAINT    r        0x0: Not active

                               0x1: Host Channel Interrupt

    15.5.18      USB_HS_HAINTMSK

    Description: Host All Channels Interrupt Mask Register

    The Host All Channel Interrupt Mask register works with the Host All Channel Interrupt register to

    interrupt the application when an event occurs on a channel. There is one interrupt mask bit per channel,

    up to a maximum of 16 bits.

    Offset: 0x0418

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    fi                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 598 / 1446

                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Table 15-20 USB_HS_HAINTMSK Register Description

 Bit    Symbol    Type   Description

 31:12  Rsvd      -      Reserved

                         Channel Interrupt Mask

                         One bit per channel: Bit 0 for channel 0, bit 11 for channel 11
 11:0   HAINTM    rw
                         0x0: Unmask Channel interrupt

                         0x1: Mask Channel interrupt

15.5.19     USB_HS_HPRT

Description: Host Port Control and Status Register

This register is available only in Host mode. Currently, the USB Host supports only one port. A single

register holds USB port-related information such as USB reset, enable, suspend, resume, connect status,

and test mode for each port. It is shown in the "Interrupt Hierarchy" figure. The rw bits in this register

can trigger an interrupt to the application through the Host Port Interrupt bit of the Core Interrupt register

(HPRTINT bit in USB_HS_GINTSTS). On a Port Interrupt, the application must read this register and clear

the bit that caused the interrupt. For the rw bits, the application must write a 1 to the bit to clear the

interrupt.

Offset: 0x0440

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 15-21 USB_HS_HPRT Register Description

 Bit    Symbol     Type   Description

 31:19  Rsvd       -      Reserved

                          Port Speed

                          Indicates the speed of the device attached to this port.

                          2'b00: High speed
 18:17  PSPD       r
                          2'b01: Full speed

                          2'b10: Low speed

                          2'b11: Reserved

fi                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                   599 / 1446

                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     Port Test Control

                     The application writes a nonzero value to this field to put the port into a

                     Test mode, and the corresponding pattern is signaled on the port.

                     4'b0000: Test mode disabled

                     4'b0001: Test_J mode
16:13 PTCTL    rw
                     4'b0010: Test_K mode

                     4'b0011: Test_SE0_NAK mode

                     4'b0100: Test_Packet mode

                     4'b0101: Test_Force_Enable

                     Others: Reserved

                     Port Power

                     The application uses this field to control power to this port (write 1'b1 to

                     set to 1'b1 and write 1'b0 to set to 1'b0), and the core can clear this bit
12     PPWR     rw   on an over current condition.

                     0: Power off

                     1: Power on

                     Port Line Status

                     Indicates the current logic level USB data lines
11:10 | PLSTS   r
                     Bit [10]: Logic level of D+

                     Bit [11]: Logic level of D-

9     Rsvd      -    Reserved

                     Port Reset

8     PRST      rw   When the application sets this bit, a reset sequence is started on this port.
                     The application must time the reset period and clear this bit after the reset

                     sequence is complete.


fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    600 / 1446

                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

             The application must leave this bit set for at least a minimum duration

             mentioned below to start a reset on the port. The application can leave it

             set for another 10 ms in         addition to the required minimum duration,

             before clearing the bit, even though there is no maximum limit Set by the

             USB standard.This bit is cleared by the core even if there is no device

             connected to the Host.

             ■ High speed: 50 ms

             ■ Full speed/Low speed: 10 ms

             0: Port not in reset

             1: Port in reset

             Port Suspend

             The application sets this bit to put this port in Suspend mode.

             The core only stops sending SOFs when this is Set. To stop the PHY clock,

    the application must Set the Port Clock Stop bit, which asserts the

             suspend input pin of the PHY.

             The read value of this bit reflects the current suspend status of the port.

7 PSUSP rw   This bit is cleared by the core after a remote wakeup signal is detected or
             the application sets the Port Reset bit or Port Resume bit in this register

    or the Resume/Remote Wakeup Detected Interrupt bit or Disconnect

             Detected Interrupt bit in the Core Interrupt register (WKUINT or DISCINT

             in USB_HS_GINTSTS, respectively).This bit is cleared by the core even if

             there is no device connected to the Host.

             0: Port not in Suspend mode

             1: Port in Suspend mode

             Port Resume

6 PRES  rw   The application sets this bit to drive resume signaling on the port. The
             core continues to drive the resume signal until the application clears this

             bit.

                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        601 / 1446

                                                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     If the core detects a USB remote wakeup sequence, as indicated by the

                     Port Resume/Remote Wakeup Detected Interrupt bit of the Core Interrupt

    register (WKUINT bit in USB_HS_GINTSTS), the core starts driving

                     resume signaling without application intervention and clears this bit when

                     it detects a disconnect condition. The read value of this bit indicates

                     whether the core is currently driving resume signaling.

                     0: No resume driven

                     1: Resume driven

                     Port Overcurrent Change

                     The core sets this bit when the status of the Port Overcurrent Active bit

                     (bit 4) in this register changes.This bit can be set only by the core and
5   POCCHNG  r_w1c   the application should write 1 to clear it

                     0: Status of port overcurrent status is not changed

                     1: Status of port overcurrent changed

                     Port Overcurrent Active

                     Indicates the overcurrent condition of the port.
4   POCA     r
                     0: No overcurrent condition

                     1: Overcurrent condition

                     Port Enable/Disable Change

                     The core sets this bit when the status of the Port Enable bit [2] of this

                     register changes.This bit can be set only by the core and the application
3   PENCHNG | r_w1c  should write 1 to clear it.

                     0: Port Enable bit 2 has not changed

                     1: Port Enable bit 2 changed

                     Port Enable
2   PENA    r_w1c
                     A port is enabled only by the core after a reset sequence, and is disabled

                     —_— Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                           602 / 1446

                                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                           by an overcurrent condition, a disconnect condition, or by the application

                           clearing this bit. The application cannot Set this bit by a register write. It

                           can only clear it to disable the port by writing 1. This bit does not trigger

                           any interrupt to the application.

                           0: Port disabled

                           1: Port enabled

                           Port Connect Detected

                           The core sets this bit when a device connection is detected to trigger an

                           interrupt to the application using the Host Port Interrupt bit of the Core

                           Interrupt register

    1     PCDET       r_w1c | (HPRTINT bit in USB_HS_GINTSTS).This bit can be set only by the core

                           and the application should write 1 to clear it.The application must write a

                           1 to this bit to clear the interrupt.

                           1: Device connection detected

                           0: No device connection detected

                           Port Connect Status

    0     PCSTS       r    0: No device is attached to the port

                           1: A device is attached to the port

15.5.20 USB_HS_HCCHARx (x = 0..11, where x =
Channel_number)

    Description: Host Channel x Characteristics Register

    Offset: 0x0500 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-22 USB_HS_HCCHARx Register Description (x = 0..11, where x = Channel_number)

     Bit   Symbol   Type  Description

                          Channel Enable
     31    CHENA    rw
                          This field is set by the application and cleared by the USB host.

    fi                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   603 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    ■ 1'b0: Channel disabled

                    ■ 1'b1: Channel enabled

                    Channel Disable

                    The application sets this bit to stop transmitting/receiving data on a channel,

                    even before the transfer for that channel is complete. The application must

30     CHDIS  rw    wait  for the    Channel Disabled interrupt before treating  the channel as
                    disabled.

                    0x0: Transmit/Recieve normal

                    0x1: Stop transmitting/receiving data on channel

                    Odd Frame

                    This field is set (reset) by the application to indicate that the USB host must

                    perform a transfer in an odd (micro)Frame.

29     ODDFRM  rw   This  field   is applicable for only  periodic (isochronous  and interrupt)

                    transactions.

                    0x0: Even Frame Transfer

                    0x1: Odd Frame Transfer

                    Device Address
28:22  DAD     rw
                    This field selects the specific device serving as the data source or sink.

                    Multi Count      / Error Count

    When the Split Enable bit of the Host Channel-n Split Control register

                    (USB_HS_HCSPLTx.SPLITEN) is reset (1'b0), this field indicates to the host

                    the number of transactions that must be executed per microframe for this

21:20  MC      rw   periodic endpoint. For non periodic transfers, this field is used only in DMA

                    mode, and specifies the number packets to be fetched for this channel before

                    the internal DMA engine changes arbitration.

    When USB_HS_HCSPLTx.SPLITEN is Set (1'b1), this field indicates the

                    number of immediate retries to be performed for a periodic split transactions

fis                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                              604 / 1446

                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    on transaction errors. This field must be Set to at least 2'b01.

                    0x0: Reserved. This field yields undefined result

                    0x1: 1 transaction

                    0x2 :2 transactions to be issued for this endpoint per microframe

                    0x3 :3 transactions to be issued for this endpoint per microframe

                    Endpoint Type

                    Indicates the transfer type selected.

                    0x0: Control
19:18  EPTYP   rw
                    0x1: Isochronous

                    0x2: Bulk

                    0x3: Interrupt

                    Low-Speed Device

    This field is Set by the application to indicate that this channel is

17     LSDEV   rw   communicating to a low-speed device.

                    0x0: Not Communicating with low speed device

                    0x1: Communicating with low speed device

16     Rsvd    -    Reserved

                    Endpoint Direction

                    Indicates whether the transaction is IN or OUT.
15     EPDIR   rw
                    0x0: OUT Direction

                    0x1: IN Direction

                    Endpoint Number

                    Indicates the endpoint number on the device serving as the data source or
14:11  EPNUM   rw
                    sink.

                    0x0: End point 0

fi                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               605 / 1446

                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                             0x1: End point 1

                             0x2: End point 2

                             0x3: End point 3

                             0x4: End point 4

                             0x5: End point 5

                             0x6: End point 6

                             0x7: End point 7

                             0x8: End point 8

                             0x9: End point 9

                             0xC: End point 12

                             0xD: End point 13

                             Indicates the maximum packet size of the associated endpoint.

15.5.21 USB_HS_HCSPLTx (x = 0..11, where x =
Channel_number)

    Description: Host Channel x Split Control Register

    Offset: 0x0504 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-23 USB_HS_HCSPLTx Register Description (x = 0..11, where x = Channel_number)

     Bit   Symbol        Type  Description

     31    SPLITEN       rw    Split Enable

                               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                606 / 1446

                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        The application sets this field to indicate that this channel is enabled to

                        perform split transactions.

                        0x0: Split not enabled

                        0x1: Split enabled

30:17  Rsvd     -       Reserved

                        Do Complete Split

    The application sets this field to request the USB host to perform a

16     COMPLSPLT | rw   complete split transaction.

                        0x0: No complete split transaction

                        0x1: Complete Split transaction

                        Transaction Position

                        This field is used to determine whether to send all, first, middle, or last

                        payloads with each OUT transaction.

                        ■ 2'b11: All. This is the entire data payload is of this transaction (which

                        is less than or equal to 188 bytes).

15:14 | XACTPOS rw      ■ 2'b10: Begin. This is the first data payload of this transaction (which is

                        larger than 188 bytes).

                        ■ 2'b00: Mid. This is the middle payload of this transaction (which is larger

                        than 188 bytes).

                        ■ 2'b01: End. This is the last payload of this transaction (which is larger

                        than 188 bytes).

                        Hub Address
13:7   HUBADDR   rw
                        This field holds the device address of the transaction translator's hub.

                        Port Address
6:0    PRTADDR   rw
                        This field is the port number of the recipient transaction translator.



fi                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    607 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
15.5.22 USB_HS_HCINTx (x = 0..11, where x =
Channel_number)

    Description: "Host Channel x Interrupt Register"

This register indicates the status of a channel with respect to USB- and AHB-related events. The

    application must read this register when the Host Channels Interrupt bit of the Core Interrupt register

    (HCINT bit in USB_HS_GINTSTS) is set. Before the application can read this register, it must first read

    the Host All Channels Interrupt (USB_HS_HAINT) register to get the exact channel number for the Host

    Channel-n Interrupt register. The application must clear the appropriate bit in this register to clear the

    corresponding bits in the USB_HS_HAINT and USB_HS_GINTSTS registers.

    Offset: 0x0508 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-24 USB_HS_HCINTx Register Description (x = 0..11, where x = Channel_number)

     Bit    Symbol   Type   Description

     31:11  Rsvd     -      Reserved

                            Data Toggle Error .

                            This bit can be set only by the core and the application should write 1 to

     10     DTERR    r_w1c  clear it.

                            0x0: No Data Toggle Error

                            0x1: Data Toggle Error

                            Frame Overrun .

                            This bit can be set only by the core and the application should write 1 to

     9      FRMOR | r_w1c | clear it.

                            0x0: No Frame Overrun

                            0x1: Frame Overrun

                            Babble Error

     8      BBERR           | r_w1c | This bit can be set only by the core and the application should write 1 to

                            clear it.


    fi                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    608 / 1446

                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  0x0: No Babble Error

                  0x1: Babble Error

                  Transaction Error

                  Indicates one of the following errors occurred on the USB.

                  ■ CRC check failure

                  ■ Timeout

                  ■ Bit stuff error
7  TXERR r_w1c
                  ■ False EOP

                  This bit can be set only by the core and the application should write 1 to

                  clear it.

                  0x0: No Transaction Error

                  0x1: Transaction Error

                  NYET Response Received Interrupt

                  This bit can be set only by the core and the application should write 1 to

6   NYET | r_w1c  clear it.

                  0x0: No NYET Response Received Interrupt

                  0x1: NYET Response Received Interrupt

                  ACK Response Received/Transmitted Interrupt

                  This bit can be set only by the core and the application should write 1 to

5   ACK   r_w1c | clear it.

                  0x0: No ACK Response Received or Transmitted Interrupt

                  0x1: ACK Response Received or Transmitted Interrupt

                  NAK Response Received Interrupt

4   NAK   r_w1c | This bit can be set only by the core and the application should write 1 to

                  clear it.
{dango            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               609 / 1446

                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     0x0: No NAK Response Received Interrupt

                     0x1: NAK Response Received Interrupt

                     STALL Response Received Interrupt

                     This bit can be set only by the core and the application should write 1 to

3   STALL   r_w1c |  clear it.

                     0x0: No Stall Response Received Interrupt

                     0x1: Stall Response Received Interrupt

                     AHB Error

                     This error is generated only in Internal DMA mode when an AHB error occurs

    during an AHB ead/write operation. The application can read the
2   AHBERR  r_w1c    corresponding channel's DMA address register to get the error address.

                     0x0: No AHB error

                     0x1: AHB error during AHB read/write

                     Channel Halted

    Idicates the transfer completed abnormally either because of any USB

                     transaction error or in response to disable request by the application or
1   CHH     r_w1c    because of a completed transfer.

                     0x0: Channel not halted

                     0x1: Channel Halted

                     Transfer Completed

                     Transfer completed normally without any errors.This bit can be set only by

0   XFRC    r_w1c | the core and the application should write 1 to clear it.

                     0x0: Transfer in progress or No Active Transfer

                     0x1: Transfer completed normally without any errors
