15.5.24 USB_HS_HCTSIZx (x = 0..11, where x =
Channel_number)

    Description: Host Channel x Transfer Size Register

    Offset: 0x0510 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-26 USB_HS_HCTSIZx Register Description (x = 0..11, where x = Channel_number)

    Bit Symbol   Type    Description
    {dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                 612 / 1446

                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   Do Ping

                   This bit is used only for OUT transfers. Setting this field to 1 directs the host

                   to do PING protocol.

31    DOPING rw    Note: Do not set this bit for IN transfers. If this bit is set for for IN transfers

                   it disables the channel.

                   0x0: No ping protocol

                   0x1: Ping protocol

                   Data PID

                   The application programs this field with the type of PID to use for the initial

                   transaction. The host maintains this field for the rest of the transfer.

30:29 DPID   rw    ■ 2'b00: DATA0

                   ■ 2'b01: DATA2

                   ■ 2'b10: DATA1

                   ■ 2'b11: MDATA (non-control)/SETUP (control)

                   Packet Count

                   This field is programmed by the application with the expected number of

                   packets to be transmitted (OUT) or received (IN).
28:19 | PKTCNT | rw
                   The host decrements this count on every successful transmission or reception

    of an OUT/IN packet. Once this count reaches zero, the application is

                   interrupted to indicate normal completion.

                   Transfer Size

                   For an OUT, this field is the number of data bytes the host sends during the

                   transfer.
18:0  | XFRSIZ | rw
                   For an IN, this field is the buffer size that the application has Reserved for

                   the transfer. The application is expected to program this field as an integer

                   multiple of the maximum packet size for IN transactions (periodic and non-


fi                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    613 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                          periodic).

15.5.25 USB_HS_HCDMAx (x = 0..11, where x =
Channel_number)

    Description: "Host Channel x DMA Address Register"

    This register is used by the USB host in the internal DMA mode to maintain the current buffer pointer for

    IN/OUT transactions. The starting DMA address must be DWORD-aligned.

    Offset: 0x0514 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-27 USB_HS_HCDMAx Register Description (x = 0..11, where x = Channel_number)

     Bit   Symbol   Type   Description

                           This field holds the start address in the external memory from which the

 31:0 DMAADDR rw data for the endpoint must be fetched or to which it must be stored. This

                           register is incremented on every AHB transaction.

    15.5.26     USB_HS_DCFG

    Description: Device Configuration Register

    This register configures the core in Device mode after power-on or after certain control commands or

    enumeration. Do not make changes to this register after initial programming.

    Offset: 0x0800

    Reset: 0x0812_0000

    Table 15-28 USB_HS_DCFG Register Description

     Bit    Symbol    Type  Description

     31:26  Rsvd      -     Reserved

                            Periodic Scheduling Interval

                            This field specifies the amount of time the Internal DMA engine must

                            allocate for fetching periodic IN endpoint data.
     25:24 | PERSCHIVL | rw
                            Based on the number of periodic endpoints, this value must be specified

                            as 25,50 or 75% of (micro)Frame.

        ■ When any periodic endpoints are active, the internal DMA engine

    fi                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                           614 / 1446

                                                                  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      allocates the specified amount      of time in fetching periodic IN endpoint

                      data .

                      ■ When no periodic endpoints are active, Then the internal DMA engine

                      services non-periodic endpoints, ignoring this field.

                      ■ After the specified time within a (micro)Frame, the DMA switches to

                      fetching for non-periodic endpoints.

                      - 2'b00: 25% of (micro) Frame.

                      - 2'b01: 50% of (micro) Frame.

                      - 2'b10: 75% of (micro) Frame.

                      - 2'b11: Reserved.

23:13 Rsvd      -     Reserved

                      Periodic Frame Interval

                      Indicates the time within a (micro) Frame at which the application must

                      be notified using the End Of Periodic Frame Interrupt. This can be used to

                      determine If all the isochronous traffic for that (micro) Frame is complete.

12:11 | PFIVL   rw    ■ 2'b00: 80% of the (micro) Frame interval

                      ■ 2'b01: 85% of the (micro) Frame interval

                      ■ 2'b10: 90% of the (micro) Frame interval

                      ■ 2'b11: 95% of the (micro) Frame interval

                      Device Address

10:4  | DAD     rw    The application must program this field after every       SetAddress control

                      command.

3     Rsvd      -     Reserved

                      Non-Zero-Length Status OUT Handshake

2     NZLSOHSK | rw   The application can use this field to select the handshake the core sends

                      on receiving a nonzero-length data packet during the OUT transaction of

fi                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 615 / 1446

                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        a control transfer's Status stage.

    ■ 1'b1: Send a STALL handshake on a nonzero-length status OUT

                        transaction and do not send the received OUT packet to the application.

                        ■ 1'b0: Send the received OUT packet to the application (zerolength or

                        nonzero-length) and send a handshake based on the NAK and STALL bits

                        for the endpoint in the Device Endpoint Control register.

                        Device Speed

    Indicates the speed at which the application requires the core to

                        enumerate, or the maximum speed the application can support. However,

                        the actual bus speed is determined only after the connect sequence is

                        completed, and is based on the speed of the USB host to which the core

1:0    DSPD       rw    is connected.

                        0x0: High speed USB 2.0 PHY clock is 30 MHz or 60 MHz

                        0x1: Full speed USB 2.0 PHY clock is 30 MHz or 60 MHz

                        0x2: Low speed USB 1.1 transceiver clock is 6 MHz

                        0x3: Full speed USB 1.1 transceiver clock is 48 MHz

15.5.27     USB_HS_DCTL

Description: Device Control Register

Offset: 0x0804

Reset: 0x0000_0002

Table 15-29 USB_HS_DCTL Register Description

 Bit    Symbol    Type  Description

 31:12  Rsvd      -     Reserved

                        Power-On Programming Done

    The application uses this bit to indicate that register programming is
 11     POPRGDNE  rw
                        completed after a wake-up from Power Down mode.

                        0x0: Power-On Programming not done


fi                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    616 / 1446

                                                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    0x1: Power-On Programming Done

                    Clear Global OUT NAK

                    A write to this field clears the Global OUT NAK.
10    CGONAK   w
                    0x0: Disable Clear Global OUT NAK

                    0x1: Clear Global OUT NAK

                    Set Global OUT NAK

                    A write to this field sets the Global OUT NAK. The application uses this bit

                    to send a NAK handshake on all OUT endpoints. The application must set

9     SGONAK   w    this bit only after making sure that the Global OUT NAK Effective bit in the
                    Core Interrupt Register (GONAKEFF bit in USB_HS_GINTSTS) is cleared.

                    0x0: Disable Global OUT NAK

                    0x1: Set Global OUT NAK

                    Clear Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                    A write to this field clears the Global Non-periodic IN NAK.
8     CGINAK   w
                    0x0: Disable Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                    0x1: Clear Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                    Set Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                    A write to this field sets the Global Non-periodic IN NAK.The application

                    uses this bit to send a NAK handshake on all non-periodic IN endpoints.

                    The core can also Set this bit when a timeout condition is detected on a

7     SGINAK   w    non-periodic endpoint in shared FIFO operation. The application must Set
                    this bit only after making sure that the Global IN NAK Effective bit in the

                    Core Interrupt Register (GINAKEFF bit in USB_HS_GINTSTS) is cleared

                    0x0: Disable Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                    0x1: Set Global Non-periodic IN NAK

6:4   TCTL     rw   Test Control

fi                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 617 / 1446

                                                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 ■ 3'b000: Test mode disabled

                 ■ 3'b001: Test_J mode

                 ■ 3'b010: Test_K mode

                 ■ 3'b011: Test_SE0_NAK mode

                 ■ 3'b100: Test_Packet mode

                 ■ 3'b101: Test_Force_Enable

                 ■ Others: Reserved

                 Global OUT NAK Status

                 ■ 1'b0: A handshake is sent based on the FIFO Status and the NAK and

                 STALL bit settings.
3   GONSTS   r
                 ■ 1'b1: No data is written to the RxFIFO, irrespective of space availability.

                 Sends a NAK handshake on all packets, except on SETUP transactions. All

                 isochronous OUT packets are dropped.

                 Global Non-periodic IN NAK Status

                 ■ 1'b0: A handshake is   sent out based on the data availability in the

2   GINSTS   r   transmit FIFO.

                 ■ 1'b1: A NAK handshake is sent out on all non-periodic IN endpoints,

                 irrespective of the data availability in the transmit FIFO.

                 Soft Disconnect

                 The application uses this bit to signal the controller to do a soft disconnect.

    As long as this bit is set, the host does not see that the device is

                 connected, and the device does not receive signals on the USB. The core
1  SDIS     rw
                 stays in the disconnected state until the application clears this bit.

                 ■ 1'b0: Normal operation. When this bit is cleared after a soft disconnect,

                 the core generates a device connect event to the USB host. When the

                 device is reconnected, the USB host restarts device enumeration.

        —_—      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                    618 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                          ■ 1'b1: The core generates a device disconnect event to the USB host.

                          Remote Wakeup Signaling

                          When the application sets this bit, the core initiates remote signaling to

                          wake up the USB host. The application must Set this bit to instruct the

0     RWUSIG         rw   core to exit the Suspend state. As specified in the USB 2.0 specification,
                          the application must clear this bit 1-15 ms after setting it.

                          0x0: Core does not send Remote Wakeup Signaling

                          0x1: Core sends Remote Wakeup Signaling

15.5.28     USB_HS_DSTS

Description: Device Status Register

This register indicates the status of the core with respect to USB-related events. It must be read on

interrupts from Device All Interrupts (USB_HS_DAINT) register.

Offset: 0x0808

Reset: 0x0000_0002

Table 15-30 USB_HS_DSTS Register Description

 Bit    Symbol    Type   Description

 31:22  Rsvd      -      Reserved

                         Frame or Microframe Number of the Received SOF

                         When the core is operating at high speed, this field contains a microframe

                         number. When the core is operating at full or low speed, this field contains

                         a Frame number.

 21:8  | SOFFN    r      Note: This register may return a non-zero value if read immediately after

                         power-on reset. In case the register bit reads non-zero immediately after

                         power-on reset, it does not indicate that SOF has been received from the

                         host. The read value of this interrupt is valid only after a valid connection

                         between host and device is established.
 7:4    Rsvd      -      Reserved                                                                    |

 3      EERR      r      Erratic Error
fi      ¥dango            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 619 / 1446

                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    The core sets this bit to report any erratic errors

                (phy_rxvalid_i/phy_rxvldh_i or phy_rxactive_i is asserted for at least 2 ms,

                due to PHY error) seen on the UTMI+. Due to erratic errors, the core goes

                into Suspended state and an interrupt is generated to the application with

    Early Suspend bit of the Core Interrupt register (ESUSP bit in

                USB_HS_GINTSTS). If the early suspend is asserted due to an erratic error,

                the application can only perform a soft disconnect recover.

                0x0: No Erratic Error

                0x1: Erratic Error

                Enumerated Speed

    Indicates the speed at which the controller has come up after speed

                detection through a connect or reset sequence.

                ■ 2'b00: High speed (PHY clock is running at 30 or 60 MHz)
2:1 ENUMSPD r
                ■ 2'b01: Full speed (PHY clock is running at 30 or 60 MHz)

                ■ 2'b10: Low speed (PHY clock is running at 6 MHz)

                ■ 2'b11: Full speed (PHY clock is running at 48 MHz)

                Low speed is not supported for devices using a UTMI+ PHY.

                Suspend Status

                In Device mode, this bit is set as long as a Suspend condition is detected

                on the USB. The core enters the Suspend state when there is no activity on

                the phy_line_state_i signal for an extended period of time. The core comes

                out of the suspend under the following conditions :

0   SUSPSTS r   ■ If there is any activity on the phy_line_state_i signal, or

                ■ If the application writes to the Remote Wakeup Signaling bit in the Device

                Control register (RWUSIG bit in USB_HS_DCTL).

                When the core comes out of the suspend, this bit is set to 1'b0.

                0x0: No suspend state

                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 620 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                         0x1: Suspend state

15.5.29      USB_HS_DIEPMSK

Description: Device IN Endpoint Common Interrupt Mask Register

This register works with each of the Device IN Endpoint Interrupt (USB_HS_DIEPINTx) registers for all

endpoints to generate an interrupt per IN endpoint. The IN endpoint interrupt for a specific status in the

USB_HS_DIEPINTx register can be masked by writing to the corresponding bit in this register. Status

bits are masked by default.

15.5.31       USB_HS_DAINT

Description: Device All Endpoints Interrupt Register

When a significant event occurs on an endpoint, a Device All Endpoints Interrupt register interrupts the

application using the Device OUT Endpoints Interrupt bit or Device IN Endpoints Interrupt bit of the Core

Interrupt register (OEPINT or IEPINT in USB_HS_GINTSTS, respectively). There is one interrupt bit per

endpoint, up to a maximum of 6 bits for OUT endpoints and 6 bits for IN endpoints. For a bidirectional

endpoint, the corresponding IN and OUT interrupt bits are used. Bits in this register are set and cleared

when the application sets and clears bits in the corresponding Device Endpoint-n Interrupt register

(USB_HS_DIEPINTx/USB_HS_DOEPINTx).

Offset: 0x0818

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 15-33 USB_HS_DAINT Register Description

 Bit          Symbol      Type    Description

 31:22        Rsvd        -       Reserved

 21           OEPINT5     r       OUT Endpoint 5 Interrupt Bit

 20           OEPINT4     r       OUT Endpoint 4 Interrupt Bit

 19           OEPINT3     r       OUT Endpoint 3 Interrupt Bit

 18           OEPINT2     r       OUT Endpoint 2 Interrupt Bit

 17           OEPINT1     r       OUT Endpoint 1 Interrupt Bit

 16           OEPINT0     r       OUT Endpoint 0 Interrupt Bit
 15:6         Rsvd        -       Reserved                   |

 5            IEPINT5     r       IN Endpoint 5 Interrupt Bit

 4            IEPINT4     r       IN Endpoint 4 Interrupt Bit

 3            IEPINT3     r       IN Endpoint 3 Interrupt Bit

 2            IEPINT2     r       IN Endpoint 2 Interrupt Bit

 1            IEPINT1     r       IN Endpoint 1 Interrupt Bit



fi            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    624 / 1446

                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    0            IEPINT0        r                 IN Endpoint 0 Interrupt Bit

    15.5.32      USB_HS_DAINTMSK

    Description: Device All Endpoints Interrupt Mask Register

The Device Endpoint Interrupt Mask register works with the Device Endpoint Interrupt register to

    interrupt the application when an event occurs on a device endpoint. However, the Device All Endpoints

    Interrupt (USB_HS_DAINT) register bit corresponding to that interrupt is still set.

    Offset: 0x081c

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-34 USB_HS_DAINTMSK Register Description

     18      OEPM2          rw     OUT Endpoint 2 Interrupt mask Bit

     17      OEPM1          rw     OUT Endpoint 1 Interrupt mask Bit

     16      OEPM0          rw     OUT Endpoint 0 Interrupt mask Bit
     15:6    Rsvd           -      Reserved                                           |

     5       IEPM5          rw     IN Endpoint 5 Interrupt mask Bit

     4       IEPM4          rw     IN Endpoint 4 Interrupt mask Bit

     3       IEPM3          rw     IN Endpoint 3 Interrupt mask Bit

     2       IEPM2          rw     IN Endpoint 2 Interrupt mask Bit

     1       IEPM1          rw     IN Endpoint 1 Interrupt mask Bit

     0       IEPM0          rw     IN Endpoint 0 Interrupt mask Bit

    15.5.33      USB_HS_DTHRCTL

    Description: Device Threshold Control Register

    fi                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                625 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Offset: 0x0830

Reset: 0x0C10_0020

Table 15-35 USB_HS_DTHRCTL Register Description

 Bit    Symbol     Type  Description

 31:28  Rsvd       -     Reserved

                         Arbiter Parking Enable

    This bit controls internal DMA arbiter parking for IN endpoints. If

                         thresholding is enabled and this bit is set to one, then the arbiter parks

                         on the IN endpoint for which there is a token received on the USB.
 27     ARPEN      rw    This is done to avoid getting into underrun conditions. By default,

                         arbiter parking is enabled.

                         0x0: Disable DMA arbiter parking

                         0x1: Enable DMA arbiter parking for IN endpoints
 26     Rsvd       -     Reserved        |

                         Receive Threshold Length

                         This field specifies Receive thresholding size in DWORDS.

                         This field also specifies the amount of data received on the USB before
 25:17  RXTHRLEN   rw    the core can start transmitting on the AHB. The threshold length has

                         to be at least eight DWORDS. The recommended value for RXTHRLEN

                         is to be the same as the programmed AHB Burst Length (HBSTLEN bit

                         in USB_HS_GAHBCFG).

                         Receive Threshold Enable

                         When this bit is set, the core enables thresholding in the receive

 16   | RXTHREN    rw  | direction.

                         0x0: Disable thresholding

                         0x1: Enable thresholding in the receive direction

 15:11 Rsvd        -     Reserved

fi                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 626 / 1446

                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                               Transmit Threshold Length

                               This field specifies the transmit thresholding size in DWORDS. This

                               field specifies the amount of data in bytes to be in the corresponding

     10:2   TXTHRLEN     rw    endpoint transmit FIFO, before the core can start transmitting on the
                               USB. The threshold length has to be at least eight DWORDS. This field

                               controls both isochronous and nonisochronous IN endpoint thresholds.

                               The recommended value for TXTHRLEN is to be the same as the

                               programmed AHB burst length (HBSTLEN bit in USB_HS_GAHBCFG).

                               ISO IN Endpoints Threshold Enable.

     1      ISOTHREN     rw    When this bit is Set, the core enables thresholding for isochronous IN

                               endpoints.

                               Non-ISO IN Endpoints Threshold Enable.

        When this bit is Set, the core enables thresholding for Non-

     0      NONISOTHREN | rw   Isochronous IN endpoints.

                               0x0: No thresholding

                               0x1: Enable thresholding for non-isochronous IN endpoints

    15.5.34     USB_HS_DIEPEMPMSK

    Description: Device IN Endpoint FIFO Empty Interrupt Mask Register This register is valid only in

Dedicated FIFO operation. This register is used to control the IN endpoint FIFO empty interrupt

    generation (TXFE_USB_HS_DIEPINTx).

    Offset: 0x0834

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-36 USB_HS_DIEPEMPMSK Register Description

     Bit    Symbol    Type    Description

     31:16  Rsvd      -       Reserved

                              IN EP Tx FIFO Empty Interrupt Mask Bits

 15:0 | INEPTXFEM | rw | These bits acts as mask bits for USB_HS_DIEPINTx.TXFE interrupt, one

                              bit per IN Endpoint:
    fi                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.      627 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                            Bit 0 for IN EP 0, bit 5 for IN EP 5

                            0x1: Mask IN EP0 Tx FIFO Empty Interrupt

                            0x2: Mask IN EP1 Tx FIFO Empty Interrupt

                            0x4: Mask IN EP2 Tx FIFO Empty Interrupt

                            0x8: Mask IN EP3 Tx FIFO Empty Interrupt

                            0x10: Mask IN EP4 Tx FIFO Empty Interrupt

                            0x20: Mask IN EP5 Tx FIFO Empty Interrupt

    15.5.35     USB_HS_DIEPTSIZ0

    Description: Device IN Endpoint 0 Transfer Size Register

    The application must modify this register before enabling endpoint 0. Once endpoint 0 is enabled using

Endpoint Enable bit of the Device Control Endpoint 0 Control registers (EPENA in USB_HS_DIEPCTL0),

    the core modifies this register. The application can only read this register once the core has cleared the

    Endpoint Enable bit. Nonzero endpoints use the registers for endpoints.

    Offset: 0x0910

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-37 USB_HS_DIEPTSIZ0 Register Description

     Bit    Symbol   Type  Description

     31:21  Rsvd     -     Reserved

                           Packet Count

                           Indicates the total number of USB packets that constitute the Transfer Size

                           amount of data for endpoint 0.

     20:19 | PKTCNT | rw   ■ IN Endpoints : This field is decremented every time a packet (maximum

                           size or short packet) is read from the TxFIFO.

                           ■ OUT Endpoints: This field is decremented every time a packet (maximum

                           size or short packet) is written to the RxFIFO.
     18:7   Rsvd     -     Reserved                                                                  |

     6:0    XFRSIZ   rw    Transfer Size
    fi                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               628 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                          This field contains the transfer size in bytes for the current endpoint. The

                          transfer size = Sum of buffer sizes across all descriptors in the list for the

                          endpoint. In Buffer DMA, the core only interrupts the application after it has

                          exhausted the transfer size amount of data. The transfer size can be set to

                          the maximum packet size of the endpoint, to be interrupted at the end of

                          each packet.

                          ■ IN Endpoints: The core decrements this field every time a packet from the

                          external memory is written to the TxFIFO.

                          ■ OUT Endpoints: The core decrements this field every time a packet is read

                          from the RxFIFO and written to the external memory.

15.5.36 USB_HS_DIEPCTLx (x = 0..5, where x =
Endpoint_number)

    Description: Device Control IN Endpoint x Control Register

    Offset: 0x0900 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-38 USB_HS_DIEPCTLx Register Description (x = 0..5, where x = Endpoint_number)

     Bit   Symbol        Type  Description

                               Endpoint Enable

                               Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.

                               ■ The core clears this bit before setting any of the following interrupts

                               on this endpoint:

                               - SETUP Phase Done
     31    EPENA         rw
                               - Endpoint Disabled

                               - Transfer Completed

                               Note: For control endpoints in DMA mode, this bit must be set to be able

                               to transfer SETUP data packets in memory.

                               0x0: No Action


    fi                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    629 / 1446

                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
HEE 0x1: Enable Endpoint

            Endpoint Disable

            Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.

            The application sets this bit to stop transmitting/receiving data on an

            endpoint, even before the transfer for that endpoint is complete. The

            application must wait for the Endpoint Disabled interrupt before treating
30 EPDIS    rw
            the endpoint as disabled. The core clears this bit before setting the

            Endpoint Disabled interrupt. The application must set this bit only if

            Endpoint Enable is already set for this endpoint.

            0x0: No Action

            0x1: Disable Endpoint

            Set DATA1 PID

            - Applies to interrupt and bulk IN and OUT endpoints only.

            - Writing to this field sets the Endpoint Data PID (DPID) field in this

            register to DATA1.

            Set odd (micro)Frame
29 SODDFRM w
            - Applies to isochronous IN and OUT endpoints only.

            - Writing to this field sets the even and odd (micro)Frame (EONUM) field

            to odd (micro)Frame.

            0x0: Disables Set DATA1 PID

            0x1: Enables Set DATA1 PID

            Set DATA0 PID

            - Applies to interrupt/bulk IN and OUT endpoints only.

28 SEVNFRM  w | - Writing to this field sets the Endpoint Data PID (DPID) field in this

            register to DATA0.

            Set Even (micro) Frame

fi          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                630 / 1446

                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     -- Applies to isochronous IN and OUT endpoints only.

                     -- Writing to this field sets the Even/Odd (micro) Frame (EONUM) field

                     to even (micro) Frame.

                     The frame number in which to send data is in the transmit descriptor

                     structure. The frame in which to receive data is updated in receive

                     descriptor structure.

                     0x0: Disables Set DATA0 PID

                     0x1: Endpoint Data PID to DATA0

                     Set NAK

                     A write to this bit sets the NAK bit for the endpoint.

    Using this bit, the application can control the transmission of NAK

27     SNAK     w    handshakes on an     endpoint. The core can also Set    this bit for an

                     endpoint after a SETUP packet is received on that endpoint.

                     0x0: No Set NAK

                     0x1: Set NAK

                     Clear NAK

                     A write to this bit clears the NAK bit for the endpoint.
26     CNAK     w
                     0x0: No Clear NAK

                     0x1: Clear NAK

                     TxFIFO Number

                     Shared FIFO Operation non-periodic endpoints must set this bit to zero.

                     Periodic endpoints must map this to the corresponding Periodic TxFIFO

25:22  TXFNUM   rw   number.

                     ■ 4'h0: Non-Periodic TxFIFO

                     ■ Others: Specified Periodic TxFIFO.number

    Note: An interrupt IN endpoint can be configured as a nonperiodic

fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            631 / 1446

                                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

           endpoint for applications such as mass storage. The core treats an IN

           endpoint as a non-periodic endpoint if the TxFNum field is set to 0.

           Otherwise, a separate periodic FIFO must be allocated for an interrupt

           IN endpoint, and the number of this FIFO must be programmed into the

           TxFNum field. Configuring an interrupt IN endpoint as a non-periodic

           endpoint saves the extra periodic FIFO area.

    Dedicated FIFO Operation: These bits specify the FIFO number

    associated with this endpoint. Each active IN endpoint must be

           programmed to a separate FIFO number.

           This field is valid only for IN endpoints.

           0x0: Tx FIFO 0

           0x1: Tx FIFO 1

           0x2: Tx FIFO 2

           0x3: Tx FIFO 3

           0x4: Tx FIFO 4

           0x5: Tx FIFO 5

           0x6: Tx FIFO 6

           0x7: Tx FIFO 7

           0x8: Tx FIFO 8

           0x9: Tx FIFO 9

           0xA: Tx FIFO 10

           0xB: Tx FIFO 11

           0xC: Tx FIFO 12

           0xD: Tx FIFO 13

           0xE: Tx FIFO 14

           0xF: Tx FIFO 15

"dlango    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                           632 / 1446

                                                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    STALL Handshake

                    Applies to non-control, non-isochronous IN and OUT endpoints only.

                    The application sets this bit to stall all tokens from the USB host to this

                    endpoint. If a NAK bit, Global Non-periodic IN NAK, or Global OUT NAK

    is set along with this bit, the STALL bit takes priority. Only the

                    application can clear this bit, never the core.

                    Applies to control endpoints only.
21    STALL     rw
                    The application can only set this bit, and the core clears it, when a

                    SETUP token is received for this endpoint. If a NAK bit, Global Non-

                    periodic IN NAK, or Global OUT NAK is set along with this bit, the STALL

    bit takes priority. Irrespective of this bit's setting, the core always

                    responds to SETUP data packets with an ACK handshake.

                    0x0: STALL All non-active tokens

                    0x1: STALL All Active Tokens

20     Rsvd     -   Reserved Field

                    Endpoint Type

                    This is the transfer type supported by this logical endpoint.

                    ■ 2'b00: Control
19:18  EPTYP    rw
                    ■ 2'b01: Isochronous

                    ■ 2'b10: Bulk

                    ■ 2'b11: Interrupt

                    NAK Status

                    When either the application or the core sets this bit:

17     NAKSTS   r   ■ The core stops receiving any data on an OUT endpoint, even if there

                    is space in the RxFIFO to accommodate the incoming packet.

                    ■ For non-isochronous IN endpoints: The core stops transmitting any

                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               633 / 1446

                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  data on an IN endpoint, even if there data is available in the TxFIFO.

                  ■ For isochronous IN endpoints: The core sends out a zerolength data

                  packet, even if there data is available in the TxFIFO.

                  Irrespective of this bit's setting, the core always responds to SETUP data

                  packets with an ACK handshake.

                  0x0: The core is transmitting non-NAK handshakes based on the FIFO

                  status

                  0x1: The core is transmitting NAK handshakes on this endpoin

                  Endpoint Data PID

                  Applies to interrupt/bulk IN and OUT endpoints only.

                  Contains the PID of the packet to be received or transmitted on this

                  endpoint. The application must program the PID of the first packet to

    be received or transmitted on this endpoint, after the endpoint is

                  activated. The applications use the SD0PID fields of this register to

                  program either DATA0 or DATA1 PID.

                  ■ 1'b0: DATA0

                  ■ 1'b1: DATA1
16 EONUMDPID r
                  Even/Odd (Micro)Frame

                  Applies to isochronous IN and OUT endpoints only.

    Indicates the (micro) frame number in which the core

                  transmits/receives isochronous data for this endpoint. The application

                  must program the even/odd (micro) frame number in which it intends

    to transmit/receive isochronous data for this endpoint using the

                  SEVNFRM and SODDFRM fields in this register.

                  ■ 1'b0: Even (micro) frame

                  ■ 1'b1: Odd (micro) frame

15 USBAEP    rw   USB Active Endpoint
   —_— Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                          634 / 1446

                                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                               Indicates whether this endpoint is active in the current configuration

                               and interface. The core clears this bit for all endpoints (other than EP 0)

                               after detecting a USB reset.

                               After receiving the SetConfiguration and SetInterface commands, the

                               application must program endpoint registers accordingly and set this

                               bit.

                               0x0: Not Active

                               0x1: USB Active Endpoint

     14:11 Rsvd          -     Reserved

                               Maximum Packet Size

     10:0  | MPSIZ       rw    The application must program this field with the maximum packet size

                               for the current logical endpoint. This value is in bytes.

15.5.37 USB_HS_DIEPINTx (x = 0..5, where x =
Endpoint_number)

    Description: Device IN Endpoint x Interrupt Register

    Offset: 0x0908 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-39 USB_HS_DIEPINTx Register Description (x = 0..5, where x = Endpoint_number)

     Bit    Symbol       Type   Description

     31:14  Rsvd         -      Reserved

                                NAK Interrupt

        The core generates this interrupt when a NAK is transmitted or

        received by the device. In case of isochronous IN endpoints the

     13     NAK          r_w1c | interrupt gets generated when a zero length packet is transmitted due
                                to un-availability of data in the TXFifo.

                                0x0: No NAK interrupt

                                0x1: NAK Interrupt

    fi                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                635 / 1446

                                                                  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        NAK Interrupt

    The core generates this interrupt when babble is received for the

12  | BERR  r_w1c | endpoint.

                        0x0: No interrupt

                        0x1: BbleErr interrupt

                        Packet Drop Status

                        This bit indicates to the application that an ISOC OUT packet has been

                        dropped. This bit does not have an associated mask bit and does not
11   PKTDRPSTS | r_w1c  generate an interrupt.

                        0x0: No interrupt

                        0x1: Packet Drop Status interrupt
10   Rsvd   | -         Reserved

                        Buffer Not Available Interrupt

                        The core generates this interrupt when the descriptor accessed is not

9    BNA     r_w1c      ready for the Core to process, such as Host busy or DMA done.

                        0x0: No BNA interrupt

                        0x1: BNA interrupt

                        Fifo Underrun

                        Applies to IN endpoints only.

                        This bit is valid only if thresholding is enabled. The core generates this

8    TXFIFOUDRN | r_w1c | interrupt when it detects a transmit FIFO underrun condition for this

                        endpoint.

                        0x0: No Tx FIFO Underrun interrupt

                        0x1: TxFIFO Underrun interrupt

7    TXFE     r         Transmit FIFO Empty

{dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                           636 / 1446

                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    This bit is valid only for IN endpoints

                    This interrupt is asserted when the TxFIFO for this endpoint is either

    half or completely empty. The half or completely empty status is

    determined by the TxFIFO Empty Level bit in the Core AHB

                    Configuration register (TXFELVL bit in USB_HS_GAHBCFG).

                    0x0: No Transmit FIFO Empty interrupt

                    0x1: Transmit FIFO Empty interrupt

                    IN Endpoint NAK Effective

                    Applies to periodic IN endpoints only.

                    This bit can be cleared when the application clears the IN endpoint NAK

                    by writing to CNAK bit in USB_HS_DIEPCTLx.

                    This interrupt indicates that the core has sampled the NAK bit Set

                    (either by the application or by the core). The interrupt indicates that
6  INEPNE    r_w1c
                    the IN endpoint NAK bit Set by the application has taken effect in the

                    core.

                    This interrupt does not guarantee that a NAK handshake is sent on the

                    USB. A STALL bit takes priority over a NAK bit.

                    0x0: No Endpoint NAK Effective interrupt

                    0x1: IN Endpoint NAK Effective interrupt

5   Rsvd     -      Reserved

                    IN Token Received When TxFIFO is Empty

                    Applies to non-periodic IN endpoints only.

4   ITTXFE   r_w1c  Indicates that an IN token was received when the associated TxFIFO
                    (periodic/non-periodic) was empty. This interrupt is     asserted on the

                    endpoint for which the IN token was received.

                    0x0: No IN Token Received interrupt



             Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    637 / 1446

                                                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                       0x1: IN Token Received Interrupt

                       Timeout Condition

                       ■ In shared TX FIFO mode, applies to non-isochronous IN endpoints

                       only.

                       ■ In dedicated FIFO mode, applies only to Control IN endpoints.
     3   TOC      r_w1c
                       Indicates that the core has detected a timeout condition on the USB

                       for the last IN token on this endpoint.

                       0x0: No Timeout interrupt

                       0x1: Timeout interrupt

     2   Rsvd     -    Reserved

                       Endpoint Disabled Interrupt

                       Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.

                       This bit indicates that the endpoint is disabled per the application's
     1   EPDISD   r_w1c
                       request.

                       0x0: No Endpoint Disabled Interrupt

                       0x1: Endpoint Disabled Interrupt

                       Transfer Completed Interrupt

                       Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.

                       This field indicates that the programmed transfer is complete on the
    0   XFRC      r_w1c
                       AHB as well as on the USB, for this endpoint.

                       0x0: No Transfer Complete Interrupt

                       0x1: Transfer Complete Interrupt

15.5.38 USB_HS_DIEPTSIZx (x = 1..5, where x =
Endpoint_number)

    Description: Device IN Endpoint x Transfer Size Register


    fi            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    638 / 1446

Offset: 0x0910 + x*20

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 15-40 USB_HS_DIEPTSIZx Register Description (x = 1..5, where x = Endpoint_number)

 Bit    Symbol   Type  Description

 31     Rsvd     -     Reserved

                       Applies to IN endpoints only.

                       For periodic IN endpoints, this field indicates the number of packets that must

    be transmitted per microframe on the USB. The core uses this field to

                       calculate the data PID for isochronous IN endpoints.

                       2'b01: 1 packet

 30:29  MCNT     rw   | 2'b10: 2 packets

                       2'b11: 3 packets

                       For non-periodic IN endpoints, this field is valid only in Internal DMA mode.

                       It specifies the number of packets the core must fetchfor an IN endpoint

                       before it switches to the endpoint pointed to by the Next Endpoint field of the

                       Device Endpoint-n Control register.

                       Packet Count

                       Indicates the total number of USB packets that constitute the Transfer Size

 28:19 | PKTCNT | rw   amount of data for endpoint 0.

                       This field is decremented every time a packet (maximum size or short packet)

                       is read from the TxFIFO.

                       Transfer Size

                       Indicates the transfer size in bytes for endpoint 0. The core interrupts the

                       application only after it has exhausted the transfer size amount of data. The
 6:0   XFRSIZ | rw     transfer size can be Set to the maximum packet size of the endpoint, to be

                       interrupted at the end of each packet.

                       The core decrements this field every time a packet from the external memory


fi                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    639 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                          is written to the TxFIFO.

15.5.39 USB_HS_DIEPDMAx (x = 1..5, where x =
Endpoint_number)

    Description: Device IN Endpoint x DMA Address Register

    Offset: 0x0914 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 15-41 USB_HS_DIEPDMAx Register Description (x = 1..5, where x = Endpoint_number)

     Bit    Symbol   Type    Description

        Holds the start address of the external memory for storing or fetching

                             endpoint data.

                             Note: For control endpoints, this field stores control OUT data packets as

                             well as SETUP transaction data packets.
     31:0   DMAADDR  rw
                             When more than three SETUP packets are received back-toback, the SETUP

                             data packet in the memory is overwritten.

                             This register is incremented on every AHB transaction. The application can

                             give only a DWORD-aligned address.

Endpoint_number)

                      Values are in terms of 32-bit words.

Description: Device Control OUT Endpoint 0 Control Register

Reset: 0x0000_8000

   Bit  Symbol Type  Description

                     this bit indicates that the application has allocated the memory to start

                     receiving data from the USB.

                     ■ The core clears this bit before setting any of the following interrupts on this

                     endpoint:

                     - SETUP Phase Done

                     - Endpoint Disabled
31      EPENA  rw
                     - Transfer Completed

                     Note: In DMA mode, this bit must be set for the core to transfer SETUP data

    packets into memory and it will not be cleared on Transfer Completed

                     interrupt of SETUP packet.

                     0x1: Enable Endpoint

    30  EPDIS  r     Endpoint Disable

fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.             641 / 1446

                                                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    The application cannot disable control OUT endpoint 0.

29:28  Rsvd    -    Reserved

                    Set NAK

                    A write to this bit sets the NAK bit for the endpoint. Using this bit, the

                    application can control the transmission of NAK handshakes on an endpoint.

27     SNAK    w    The core can also set bit on a Transfer Completed interrupt, or after a SETUP
                    is received on the endpoint.

                    A write to this bit clears the NAK bit for the endpoint.
26     CNAK    w
                    0x0: No action

25:22  Rsvd    -    Reserved

                    STALL Handshake

                    The application can only set this bit, and the core clears it, when a SETUP

                    along with this bit, the STALL bit takes priority. Irrespective of this bit's
21     STALL   rw   setting, the core always responds to SETUP data     packets with an ACK

                    handshake.

                    does not check the correctness of OUT packets before transferring them to

fi                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  642 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     application memory.

 19:18 | EPTYP  r    Hardcoded to 2'b00 for control.

                     data, even If there is space in the RxFIFO to accommodate the incoming

 17     NAKSTS | r   packet. Irrespective of this bit's setting, the core always responds to SETUP
                     data packets with an ACK handshake.

 15     USBAEP  r
                     in all configurations and interfaces.

                     0x1: USB Active Endpoint 0
 14:2   Rsvd    -    Reserved        |

                            interrupted at the end of each packet.

                            The core decrements this field every time a packet is read from the RxFIFO

                            and written to the external memory.

16 USB full-speed (USB_FS)

16.1 Overview


    This section presents the architecture and the programming model of the USB_FS controller.


    16.2 Features

    • Support for the following speeds:
    – Full-Speed (FS, 48-Mbps),
    – Low-Speed (LS, 1.5-Mbps) modes
    • Low speed is not supported as a device.
    • Supports System DMA access
    • Internal USB1.1 FS PHY
    • Supports up to 4 bidirectional endpoints, including control endpoint 0.
    • Supports up to 8 host channels.
    • Includes automatic ping capabilities
    • A dedicated 1.25-Kbyte data RAM with advanced FIFO management:
    – Supports endpoint FIFO sizes that are not powers of 2
    – Supports packet-based, Dynamic FIFO memory allocation for endpoints for small FIFOs and flexible
    – Supports shared FIFO mode, all non-periodic IN endpoints share a common TX FIFO and periodic IN

    endpoints have separate individual FIFOs.

    • Supports the Keep-Alive in Low-Speed mode and SOFs in Full-Speed modes
    • Power-saving features
    – clock gating
    – partial power gating
    – Switching to lower frequency 32-KHz clock support for both Device and Host modes during USB

    Suspend and Session-Off modes










    {dango  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    656 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    16.3 USB_FS functional description






        Cortex core
    |
    2

    T

    Power&                     '
    Clock                      |    mars   FS      OP

        USB Clock at 48 MHz  an





    1.25 Kbytes
    USB data
    FIFOs


        Figure 16-1 USB full-speed block diagram

16.3.1 USB full-speed core

The USB FS receives the 48 MHz ±0.25% clock from the system. The USB clock is used for driving the

48 MHz domain at fullspeed (12 Mbit/s) and must be enabled prior to configuring the USB FS core.

The CPU reads and writes from/to the USB FS core registers through the AHB peripheral bus. It is

informed of USB events through the single USB interrupt line.

The CPU submits data over the USB by writing 32-bit words to dedicated USB_FS locations (push

registers). The data are then automatically stored into Tx-data FIFOs configured within the USB data

RAM. There is one Tx-FIFO push register for each in-endpoint (peripheral mode) or out-channel (host

mode).

The CPU receives the data from the USB by reading 32-bit words from dedicated USB_FS addresses (pop

registers). The data are then automatically retrieved from a shared Rx-FIFO configured within the 1.25

KB USB data RAM. There is one Rx-FIFO pop register for each out-endpoint or in-channel.

The USB protocol layer is driven by the serial interface engine (SIE) and serialized over the USB by the

full-/low-speed transceiver module within the on-chip physical layer (PHY).

& "dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 657 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
16.3.2 Full-speed USB PHY

The embedded full-speed USB PHY is controlled by the USB FS core and conveys USB control & data

signals through the full-speed subset of the UTMI+ Bus (UTMIFS). It provides the physical support to

USB connectivity.

The full-speed USB PHY includes the following components:

• FS/LS transceiver module used by both host and device. It directly drives transmission and reception

on the single-ended USB lines.

• DP/DM integrated pull-up and pull-down resistors controlled by the USB_FS core.

Caution: To guarantee a correct operation for the USB USB FS peripheral, the AHB frequency should be

higher than 14.2 MHz.


    16.4 USB peripheral


This section gives the functional description of the USB_FS in the USB device mode.

– The force device mode bit in the Global USB configuration register (FDMOD in USB_FS_GUSBCFG) is

set to 1, forcing the USB_FS core to work as a USB peripheral-only.

16.4.1 Peripheral states

Soft disconnect

The DP pullup resistor is removed by setting the soft disconnect bit in the device control register (SDIS

bit in USB_FS_DCTL), causing a device disconnect detection interrupt on the host side even though the

USB cable was not really removed from the host port.

Default state

In the Default state the USB_FS expects to receive a SET_ADDRESS command from the host. No other

USB operation is possible. When a valid SET_ADDRESS command is decoded on the USB, the application

writes the corresponding number into the device address field in the device configuration register (DAD

bit in USB_FS_DCFG). The USB_FS then enters the address state and is ready to answer host transactions

at the configured USB address.

Suspended state

The USB_FS peripheral constantly monitors the USB activity. After counting 3 ms of USB idleness, the

early suspend interrupt (ESUSP bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS) is issued, and confirmed 3 ms later, if

appropriate, by the suspend interrupt (USBSUSP bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS). The device suspend bit is

then automatically set in the device status register (SUSPSTS bit in USB_FS_DSTS) and the USB_FS

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 658 / 1446

                                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    enters the suspended state.

    The suspended state may optionally be exited by the device itself. In this case the application sets the

    remote wakeup signaling bit in the device control register (RWUSIG bit in USB_FS_DCTL) and clears it

    after 1 to 15 ms.

When a resume signaling is detected from the host, the resume interrupt (WKUPINT bit in

    USB_FS_GINTSTS) is generated and the device suspend bit is automatically cleared.

    16.4.2 Peripheral endpoints

    The USB_FS core instantiates the following USB endpoints:

    • Control endpoint 0:

    – Bidirectional and handles control messages only

    – Separate set of registers to handle in and out transactions

    – Proper control (USB_FS_DIEPCTL0/ USB_FS_DOEPCTL0), transfer configuration

    (USB_FS_DIEPTSIZ0/ USB_FS_DIEPTSIZ0), and status-interrupt (USB_FS_DIEPINTx/

    USB_FS_DOEPINT0) registers. The available set of bits inside the control and transfer size registers

    slightly differs from that of other endpoints

    • 3 IN endpoints

    – Each of them can be configured to support the isochronous, bulk or interrupt transfer type

    – Each of them has proper control (USB_FS_DIEPCTLx), transfer configuration (USB_FS_DIEPTSIZx),

    and status-interrupt (USB_FS_DIEPINTx) registers

– The Device IN endpoints common interrupt mask register (USB_FS_DIEPMSK) is available to

    enable/disable a single kind of endpoint interrupt source on all of the IN endpoints (EP0 included)

    – Support for incomplete isochronous IN transfer interrupt (IISOIXFR bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS), asserted

    when there is at least one isochronous IN endpoint on which the transfer is not completed in the current

    frame. This interrupt is asserted along with the end of periodic frame interrupt (USB_FS_GINTSTS/EOPF).

    • 3 OUT endpoints

    – Each of them can be configured to support the isochronous, bulk or interrupt transfer type

    – Each of them has a proper control (USB_FS_DOEPCTLx), transfer configuration (USB_FS_DOEPTSIZx)

    and status-interrupt (USB_FS_DOEPINTx) register

– Device Out endpoints common interrupt mask register (USB_FS_DOEPMSK) is available to

    enable/disable a single kind of endpoint interrupt source on all of the OUT endpoints (EP0 included)

    – Support for incomplete isochronous OUT transfer interrupt (INCOMPISOOUT bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS),

    asserted when there is at least one isochronous OUT endpoint on which the transfer is not completed in

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                                         659 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

the current frame. This interrupt is asserted along with the end of periodic frame interrupt

(USB_FS_GINTSTS/EOPF).

Endpoint control

• The following endpoint controls are available to the application through the device endpoint-x IN/OUT

control register (USB_FS_DIEPCTLx/ USB_FS_DOEPCTLx):

– Endpoint enable/disable

– Endpoint activate in current configuration

– Program USB transfer type (isochronous, bulk, interrupt)

– Program supported packet size

– Program Tx-FIFO number associated with the IN endpoint

– Program the expected or transmitted data0/data1 PID (bulk/interrupt only)

– Program the even/odd frame during which the transaction is received or transmitted (isochronous only)

– Optionally program the NAK bit to always negative-acknowledge the host regardless of the FIFO status

– Optionally program the STALL bit to always stall host tokens to that endpoint

– Optionally program the SNOOP mode for OUT endpoint not to check the CRC field of received data

Endpoint transfer

The device endpoint-x transfer size registers (USB_FS_DIEPTSIZx/ USB_FS_DOEPTSIZx) allow the

application to program the transfer size parameters and read the transfer status. Programming must be

done before setting the endpoint enable bit in the endpoint control register. Once the endpoint is enabled,

these fields are read-only as the USB FS core updates them with the current transfer status.

The following transfer parameters can be programmed:

• Transfer size in bytes

• Number of packets that constitute the overall transfer size

Endpoint status/interrupt

The device endpoint-x interrupt registers (USB_FS_DIEPINTx/ USB_FS_DOPEPINTx) indicate the status

of an endpoint with respect to USB- and AHB-related events. The application must read these registers

when the OUT endpoint interrupt bit or the IN endpoint interrupt bit in the core interrupt register (OEPINT

bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS or IEPINT bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS, respectively) is set. Before the application

can read these registers, it must first read the device all endpoints interrupt (USB_FS_DAINT) register

to get the exact endpoint number for the device endpoint-x interrupt register. The application must clear

the appropriate bit in this register to clear the corresponding bits in the DAINT and GINTSTS registers.

The peripheral core provides the following status checks and interrupt generation:

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 660 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    • Transfer completed interrupt, indicating that data transfer was completed on both the application (AHB)

    and USB sides

    • Setup stage has been done (control-out only)

    • Associated transmit FIFO is half or completely empty (in endpoints)

    • NAK acknowledge has been transmitted to the host (isochronous-in only)

    • IN token received when Tx-FIFO was empty (bulk-in/interrupt-in only)

    • Out token received when endpoint was not yet enabled

    • Babble error condition has been detected

    • Endpoint disable by application is effective

    • Endpoint NAK by application is effective (isochronous-in only)

    • More than 3 back-to-back setup packets were received (control-out only)

    • Timeout condition detected (control-in only)

    • Isochronous out packet has been dropped, without generating an interrupt


    16.5 USB host


    This section gives the functional description of the USB_FS in the USB host mode.

    – The force host mode bit in the global USB configuration register (FHMOD bit in USB_FS_GUSBCFG)

    forces the USB_FS core to work as a USB host-only.

    Note: On-chip 5 V VBUS generation is not supported. For this reason, a charge pump or, if 5 V are available

    on the application board, a basic power switch must be added externally to drive the 5 V VBUS line. The

    external charge pump can be driven by any GPIO output.

    16.5.1 USB host states

    Host detection of a peripheral connection

    The USB_FS core issues a host port interrupt triggered by the device connected bit in the host port

    control and status (PCDET bit in USB_FS_HPRT).

    Host detection of peripheral a disconnection

The peripheral disconnection event triggers the disconnect detected interrupt (DISCINT bit in

    USB_FS_GINTSTS).

    Host enumeration

    After detecting a peripheral connection the host must start the enumeration process by sending USB

    reset and configuration commands to the new peripheral.

    {dango          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                        661 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Before starting to drive a USB reset, the application waits for the USB interrupt triggered by the debounce

done bit (DBCDNE bit in USB_FS_GUSBINT), which indicates that the bus is stable again after the

electrical debounce caused by the attachment of a pull-up resistor on DP (FS) or DM (LS).

The application drives a USB reset signaling (single-ended zero) over the USB by keeping the port reset

bit set in the host port control and status register (PRST bit in USB_FS_HPRT) for a minimum of 10 ms

and a maximum of 20 ms. The application takes care of the timing count and then of clearing the port

reset bit.

Once the USB reset sequence has completed, the host port interrupt is triggered by the port

enable/disable change bit (PENCHNG bit in USB_FS_HPRT). This informs the application that the speed

of the enumerated peripheral can be read from the port speed field in the host port control and status

register (PSPD bit in USB_FS_HPRT) and that the host is starting to drive SOFs (FS) or Keep alives (LS).

The host is now ready to complete the peripheral enumeration by sending peripheral configuration

commands.

Host suspend

The application decides to suspend the USB activity by setting the port suspend bit in the host port

control and status register (PSUSP bit in USB_FS_HPRT). The USB_FS core stops sending SOFs and

enters the suspended state.

The suspended state can be optionally exited on the remote device’s initiative (remote wakeup). In this

case the remote wakeup interrupt (WKUPINT bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS) is generated upon detection of a

remote wakeup signaling, the port resume bit in the host port control and status register (PRES bit in

USB_FS_HPRT) self-sets, and resume signaling is automatically driven over the USB. The application

must time the resume window and then clear the port resume bit to exit the suspended state and restart

the SOF.

If the suspended state is exited on the host initiative, the application must set the port resume bit to

start resume signaling on the host port, time the resume window and finally clear the port resume bit.

16.5.2 Host channels

The USB_FS core instantiates 8 host channels. Each host channel supports an USB host transfer (USB

pipe). The host is not able to support more than 8 transfer requests at the same time. If more than 8

transfer requests are pending from the application, the host controller driver (HCD) must re-allocate

channels when they become available from previous duty, that is, after receiving the transfer completed

and channel halted interrupts.

Each host channel can be configured to support in/out and any type of periodic/nonperiodic transaction.

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 662 / 1446

                                                                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Each host channel makes us of proper control (USB_FS_HCCHARx), transfer configuration

(USB_FS_HCTSIZx) and status/interrupt (USB_FS_HCINTx) registers with associated mask

    (USB_FS_HCINTMSKx) registers.

    Host channel control

• The following host channel controls are available to the application through the host channel-x

    characteristics register (USB_FS_HCCHARx):

    – Channel enable/disable

    – Program the FS/LS speed of target USB peripheral

    – Program the address of target USB peripheral

    – Program the endpoint number of target USB peripheral

    – Program the transfer IN/OUT direction

    – Program the USB transfer type (control, bulk, interrupt, isochronous)

    – Program the maximum packet size (MPS)

    – Program the periodic transfer to be executed during odd/even frames

    Host channel transfer

    The host channel transfer size registers (USB_FS_HCTSIZx) allow the application to program the transfer

    size parameters, and read the transfer status. Programming must be done before setting the channel

    enable bit in the host channel characteristics register. Once the endpoint is enabled the packet count

    field is read-only as the USB FS core updates it according to the current transfer status.

    • The following transfer parameters can be programmed:

    – transfer size in bytes

    – number of packets making up the overall transfer size

    – initial data PID

    Host channel status/interrupt

    The host channel-x interrupt register (USB_FS_HCINTx) indicates the status of an endpoint with respect

    to USB- and AHB-related events. The application must read these register when the host channels

    interrupt bit in the core interrupt register (HCINT bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS) is set. Before the application

    can read these registers, it must first read the host all channels interrupt (USB_FS_HCAINT) register to

    get the exact channel number for the host channel-x interrupt register. The application must clear the

    appropriate bit in this register to clear the corresponding bits in the HAINT and GINTSTS registers. The

    mask bits for each interrupt source of each channel are also available in the USB_FS_HCINTMSK-x

    register.

                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                      663 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• The host core provides the following status checks and interrupt generation:

– Transfer completed interrupt, indicating that the data transfer is complete on both the application (AHB)

and USB sides

– Channel has stopped due to transfer completed, USB transaction error or disable command from the

application

– Associated transmit FIFO is half or completely empty (IN endpoints)

– ACK response received

– NAK response received

– STALL response received

– USB transaction error due to CRC failure, timeout, bit stuff error, false EOP

– Babble error

– Frame overrun

– Data toggle error

16.5.3 Host scheduler

The host core features a built-in hardware scheduler which is able to autonomously re-order and manage

the USB transaction requests posted by the application. At the beginning of each frame the host executes

the periodic (isochronous and interrupt) transactions first, followed by the nonperiodic (control and bulk)

transactions to achieve the higher level of priority granted to the isochronous and interrupt transfer types

by the USB specification.

The host processes the USB transactions through request queues (one for periodic and one for

nonperiodic). Each request queue can hold up to 8 entries. Each entry represents a pending transaction

request from the application, and holds the IN or OUT channel number along with other information to

perform a transaction on the USB. The order in which the requests are written to the queue determines

the sequence of the transactions on the USB interface.

At the beginning of each frame, the host processes the periodic request queue first, followed by the

nonperiodic request queue. The host issues an incomplete periodic transfer interrupt (IPXFR bit in

USB_FS_GINTSTS) if an isochronous or interrupt transaction scheduled for the current frame is still

pending at the end of the current frame. The USB FS core is fully responsible for the management of the

periodic and nonperiodic request queues.The periodic transmit FIFO and queue status register

(USB_FS_HPTXSTS) and nonperiodic transmit FIFO and queue status register (USB_FS_HNPTXSTS) are

read-only registers which can be used by the application to read the status of each request queue. They

contain:

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 664 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• The number of free entries currently available in the periodic (nonperiodic) request queue (8 max)

• Free space currently available in the periodic (nonperiodic) Tx-FIFO (out-transactions)

• IN/OUT token, host channel number and other status information.

As request queues can hold a maximum of 8 entries each, the application can push to schedule host

transactions in advance with respect to the moment they physically reach the SB for a maximum of 8

pending periodic transactions plus 8 pending nonperiodic transactions.

To post a transaction request to the host scheduler (queue) the application must check that there is at

least 1 entry available in the periodic (nonperiodic) request queue by reading the PTXQSAV bits in the

USB_FS_HNPTXSTS register or NPTQXSAV bits in the USB_FS_HNPTXSTS register.


    16.6 SOFs

    16.6.1 Host SOFs

    In host mode the number of PHY clocks occurring between the generation of two consecutive SOF (FS)

    or Keep-alive (LS) tokens is programmable in the host frame interval register (HFIR), thus providing

    application control over the SOF framing period. An interrupt is generated at any start of frame (SOF bit

    in OTH_FS_GINTSTS). The current frame number and the time remaining until the next SOF are tracked

    in the host frame number register (HFNUM).

    16.6.2 Peripheral SOFs

    In device mode, the start of frame interrupt is generated each time an SOF token is received on the USB

    (SOF bit in OTH_FS_GINTSTS). The corresponding frame number can be read from the device status

    register (FNSOF bit in USB_FS_DSTS).

    The end of periodic frame interrupt (GINTSTS/EOPF) is used to notify the application when 80%, 85%,

    90% or 95% of the time frame interval elapsed depending on the periodic frame interval field in the

    device configuration register (PFIVL bit in USB_FS_DCFG). This feature can be used to determine if all

    of the isochronous traffic for that frame is complete.


    16.7 USB data FIFOs


The USB system features 1.25 Kbyte of dedicated RAM with a sophisticated FIFO control mechanism.

The packet FIFO controller module in the USB_FS core organizes RAM space into Tx-FIFOs into which the

application pushes the data to be temporarily stored before the USB transmission, and into a single Rx

{dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 665 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

FIFO where the data received from the USB are temporarily stored before retrieval (popped) by the

application. The number of instructed FIFOs and how these are organized inside the RAM depends on the

device’s role. In peripheral mode an additional Tx-FIFO is instructed for each active IN endpoint. Any

FIFO size is software configured to better meet the application requirements.

        Figure 16-2 Device-mode FIFO address mapping and AHB FIFO access mapping

16.8.1 Peripheral Rx FIFO

The USB peripheral uses a single receive FIFO that receives the data directed to all OUT endpoints.

Received packets are stacked back-to-back until free space is available in the Rx-FIFO. The status of the

received packet (which contains the OUT endpoint destination number, the byte count, the data PID and

the validity of the received data) is also stored by the core on top of the data payload. When no more

space is available, host transactions are NACKed and an interrupt is received on the addressed endpoint.

The size of the receive FIFO is configured in the receive FIFO Size register (USB_FS_GRXFSIZ).

The single receive FIFO architecture makes it more efficient for the USB peripheral to fill in the receive

RAM buffer:

• All OUT endpoints share the same RAM buffer (shared FIFO)

• The USB FS core can fill in the receive FIFO up to the limit for any host sequence of OUT tokens


    W dango          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                         666 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

The application keeps receiving the Rx-FIFO non-empty interrupt (RXFLVL bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS) as

long as there is at least one packet available for download. It reads the packet information from the

receive status read and pop register (USB_FS_GRXSTSP) and finally pops data off the receive FIFO by

reading from the endpoint-related pop address.

16.8.2 Peripheral Tx FIFOs

The core has a dedicated FIFO for each IN endpoint. The application configures FIFO sizes by writing the

non periodic transmit FIFO size register (USB_FS_TX0FSIZ) for IN endpoint0 and the device IN endpoint

transmit FIFOx registers (USB_FS_DIEPTXFx) for IN endpoint-x.
