8      CGINAK    w
                      0x0: Disable Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                      0x1: Clear Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                      Set Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                      A write to this field sets the Global Non-periodic IN NAK.The application

7      SGINAK    w    uses this bit to send a NAK handshake on all non-periodic IN endpoints.
                      The core can also Set this bit when a timeout condition is detected on a

                      non-periodic endpoint in shared FIFO operation. The application must Set

                      this bit only after making sure that the Global IN NAK Effective bit in the

fis                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 734 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    Core Interrupt Register (GINAKEFF bit in USB_FS_GINTSTS) is cleared

                    0x0: Disable Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                    0x1: Set Global Non-periodic IN NAK

                    Test Control

                    ■ 3'b000: Test mode disabled

                    ■ 3'b001: Test_J mode

                    ■ 3'b010: Test_K mode
6:4   TCTL    rw
                    ■ 3'b011: Test_SE0_NAK mode

                    ■ 3'b100: Test_Packet mode

                    ■ 3'b101: Test_Force_Enable

                    ■ Others: Reserved

                    Global OUT NAK Status

                    ■ 1'b0: A handshake is sent based on the FIFO Status and the NAK and

                    STALL bit settings.
3     GONSTS   r
                    ■ 1'b1: No data is written to the RxFIFO, irrespective of space availability.

                    Sends a NAK handshake on all packets, except on SETUP transactions. All

                    isochronous OUT packets are dropped.

                    Global Non-periodic IN NAK Status

                    ■ 1'b0: A handshake is   sent out based on the data availability in the

2     GINSTS   r    transmit FIFO.

                    ■ 1'b1: A NAK handshake is sent out on all non-periodic IN endpoints,

                    irrespective of the data availability in the transmit FIFO.

                    Soft Disconnect

1     SDIS     rw   The application uses this bit to signal the controller to do a soft disconnect.
    As long as this bit is set, the host does not see that the device is

                    connected, and the device does not receive signals on the USB. The core

fi                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                    735 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                          stays in the disconnected state until the application clears this bit.

                          ■ 1'b0: Normal operation. When this bit is cleared after a soft disconnect,

                          the core generates a device connect event to the USB host. When the

                          device is reconnected, the USB host restarts device enumeration.

                          ■ 1'b1: The core generates a device disconnect event to the USB host.

                          Remote Wakeup Signaling

                          When the application sets this bit, the core initiates remote signaling to

                          wake up the USB host. The application must Set this bit to instruct the

0     RWUSIG         rw   core to exit the Suspend state. As specified in the USB 2.0 specification,
                          the application must clear this bit 1-15 ms after setting it.

                          0x0: Core does not send Remote Wakeup Signaling

                          0x1: Core sends Remote Wakeup Signaling

16.13.27 USB_FS_DSTS

Description: Device Status Register

This register indicates the status of the core with respect to USB-related events. It must be read on

interrupts from Device All Interrupts (USB_FS_DAINT) register.

Offset: 0x0808

Reset: 0x00C7_FF02

Table 16-29 USB_FS_DSTS Register Description

 Bit    Symbol    Type   Description

 31:22  Rsvd      -      Reserved

                         Frame or Microframe Number of the Received SOF

                         When the core is operating at high speed, this field contains a microframe

                         number. When the core is operating at full or low speed, this field contains

 21:8  | SOFFN    r      a Frame number.

                         Note: This register may return a non-zero value if read immediately after

                         power-on reset. In case the register bit reads non-zero immediately after

                         power-on reset, it does not indicate that SOF has been received from the
a                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                736 / 1446

                                                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 host. The read value of this interrupt is valid only after a valid connection

                 between host and device is established.
7:4   Rsvd   -   Reserved                    |

                 Erratic Error

    The core sets this bit to report any erratic errors

                 (phy_rxvalid_i/phy_rxvldh_i or phy_rxactive_i is asserted for at least 2 ms,

                 due to PHY error) seen on the UTMI+. Due to erratic errors, the core goes

                 into Suspended state and an interrupt is generated to the application with
3     EERR   r   Early   Suspend   bit   of the Core Interrupt register     (ESUSP  bit in

                 USB_FS_GINTSTS). If the early suspend is asserted due to an erratic error,

                 the application can only perform a soft disconnect recover.

                 0x0: No Erratic Error

                 0x1: Erratic Error

                 Enumerated Speed

    Indicates the speed at which the controller has come up after speed

                 detection through a connect or reset sequence.

                 ■ 2'b00: Reserved
2:1   ENUMSPD r
                 ■ 2'b01: Reserved

                 ■ 2'b10: Reserved

                 ■ 2'b11: Full speed (PHY clock is running at 48 MHz)

                 Low speed is not supported for devices using a UTMI+ PHY.

                 Suspend Status

                 In Device mode, this bit is set as long as a Suspend condition is detected

                 on the USB. The core enters the Suspend state when there is no activity on
0     SUSPSTS r
                 the phy_line_state_i signal for an extended period of time. The core comes

                 out of the suspend under the following conditions :

                 ■ If there is any activity on the phy_line_state_i signal, or
          —_— Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                     737 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                          ■ If the application writes to the Remote Wakeup Signaling bit in the Device

                          Control register (RWUSIG bit in USB_FS_DCTL).

                          When the core comes out of the suspend, this bit is set to 1'b0.

                          0x0: No suspend state

                          0x1: Suspend state

16.13.28 USB_FS_DIEPMSK

Description: Device IN Endpoint Common Interrupt Mask Register

This register works with each of the Device IN Endpoint Interrupt (USB_FS_DIEPINTx) registers for all

endpoints to generate an interrupt per IN endpoint. The IN endpoint interrupt for a specific status in the

USB_FS_DIEPINTx register can be masked by writing to the corresponding bit in this register. Status bits

are masked by default.

Offset: 0x0810

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 16-30 USB_FS_DIEPMSK Register Description

 Bit    Symbol        Type   Description
 31:7   Rsvd          -      Reserved                                                                  |

                             IN Endpoint NAK Effective Mask

 6      INEPNEM       rw     0x0: Mask IN Endpoint NAK Effective Interrupt

                             0x1: No IN Endpoint NAK Effective Interrupt Mask

                             IN Token received with EP Mismatch Mask

                             0x1: No Mask IN Token received with EP Mismatch Interrupt

                             IN Token Received When TxFIFO Empty Mask

 4      ITTXFEMSK     rw     0x0: Mask IN Token Received When TxFIFO Empty Interrupt

                             0x1 :No IN Token Received When TxFIFO Empty Interrupt

 3      TOM           rw     Timeout Condition Mask (Non-isochronous endpoints)

fi                          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 738 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                            0x0: Mask Timeout Condition Interrupt

                            0x1: No Timeout Condition Interrupt Mask

 2       Rsvd          -    Reserved

                            Endpoint Disabled Interrupt Mask

 1       EPDM          rw   0x0: Mask Endpoint Disabled Interrupt

                            0x1: No Endpoint Disabled Interrupt Mask

                            Transfer Completed Interrupt Mask

 0       XFRCM         rw   0x0: Mask Transfer Completed Interrupt

                            0x1: No Transfer Completed Interrupt Mask

Description: Device OUT Endpoint Common Interrupt Mask Register

This register works with each of the Device OUT Endpoint Interrupt (USB_FS_DOEPINTx) registers for

all endpoints to generate an interrupt per OUT endpoint. The OUT endpoint interrupt for a specific status

in the USB_FS_DOEPINTx register can be masked by writing into the corresponding bit in this register.

Status bits are masked by default.

Reset: 0x0000_0000

                           SETUP Phase Done Mask

                           Applies to control endpoints only.

                         Endpoint Disabled Interrupt Mask

                         0x1: No Endpoint Disabled Interrupt Mask

                         0x1: No Transfer Completed Interrupt Mask

16.13.30 USB_FS_DAINT

Description: Device All Endpoints Interrupt Register

When a significant event occurs on an endpoint, a Device All Endpoints Interrupt register interrupts the

application using the Device OUT Endpoints Interrupt bit or Device IN Endpoints Interrupt bit of the Core

Interrupt register (OEPINT or IEPINT in USB_FS_GINTSTS, respectively). There is one interrupt bit per

endpoint, up to a maximum of 4 bits for OUT endpoints and 4 bits for IN endpoints. For a bidirectional

endpoint, the corresponding IN and OUT interrupt bits are used. Bits in this register are set and cleared

when the application sets and clears bits in the corresponding Device Endpoint-n Interrupt register

(USB_FS_DIEPINTx/USB_FS_DOEPINTx).

Offset: 0x0818

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 16-32 USB_FS_DAINT Register Description

 Bit           Symbol     Type    Description

 31:20         -          -       Reserved

 19            OEPINT3    r       OUT Endpoint 3 Interrupt Bit

 18            OEPINT2    r       OUT Endpoint 2 Interrupt Bit

 17            OEPINT1    r       OUT Endpoint 1 Interrupt Bit



fi                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    740 / 1446

                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

     16           OEPINT0          r          OUT Endpoint 0 Interrupt Bit

     15           -                -          Reserved

     3            IEPINT3          r          IN Endpoint 3 Interrupt Bit

     2            IEPINT2          r          IN Endpoint 2 Interrupt Bit

     1            IEPINT1          r          IN Endpoint 1 Interrupt Bit

     0            IEPINT0          r          IN Endpoint 0 Interrupt Bit

    16.13.31 USB_FS_DAINTMSK

    Description: Device All Endpoints Interrupt Mask Register

The Device Endpoint Interrupt Mask register works with the Device Endpoint Interrupt register to

    interrupt the application when an event occurs on a device endpoint. However, the Device All Endpoints

    Interrupt (USB_FS_DAINT) register bit corresponding to that interrupt is still set.

    Offset: 0x081c

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 16-33 USB_FS_DAINTMSK Register Description

     Bit   Symbol            Type     Description

     31    -                 -        Reserved

     19    OEPM3             rw       OUT Endpoint 3 Interrupt mask Bit

     18    OEPM2             rw       OUT Endpoint 2 Interrupt mask Bit

     17    OEPM1             rw       OUT Endpoint 1 Interrupt mask Bit
     16    OEPM0             rw    | OUT Endpoint 0 Interrupt mask Bit

     15    -                 -        Reserved

     3     IEPM3             rw       IN Endpoint 3 Interrupt mask Bit

     2     IEPM2             rw       IN Endpoint 2 Interrupt mask Bit

     1     IEPM1             rw       IN Endpoint 1 Interrupt mask Bit

     0     IEPM0             rw       IN Endpoint 0 Interrupt mask Bit



    fi                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    741 / 1446

                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
16.13.32 USB_FS_DTHRCTL

Description: Device Threshold Control Register

Offset: 0x0830

Reset: 0x0C10_0020

Table 16-34 USB_FS_DTHRCTL Register Description

 Bit    Symbol      Type  Description

 31:28  -           -     Reserved

                          Arbiter Parking Enable

    This bit controls internal DMA arbiter parking for IN endpoints. If

                          thresholding is enabled and this bit is set to one, then the arbiter parks

                          on the IN endpoint for which there is a token received on the USB.
 27     ARPEN       rw    This is done to avoid getting into underrun conditions. By default,

                          arbiter parking is enabled.

                          0x0: Disable DMA arbiter parking

                          0x1: Enable DMA arbiter parking for IN endpoints
 26     -        | -      Reserved

                          Receive Threshold Length

                          This field specifies Receive thresholding size in DWORDS.

                          This field also specifies the amount of data received on the USB before
 25:17 | RXTHRLEN   rw    the core can start transmitting on the AHB. The threshold length has

                          to be at least eight DWORDS. The recommended value for RXTHRLEN

                          is to be the same as the programmed AHB Burst Length (HBSTLEN bit

                          in USB_FS_GAHBCFG).

                          Receive Threshold Enable

                          When this bit is set, the core enables thresholding in the receive
 16    RXTHREN      rw
                          direction.

                          0x0: Disable thresholding


fi                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    742 / 1446

                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                               0x1: Enable thresholding in the receive direction

     15:11  -            -     Reserved

                               Transmit Threshold Length

                               This field specifies the transmit thresholding size in DWORDS. This

                               field specifies the amount of data in bytes to be in the corresponding

     10:2   TXTHRLEN     rw    endpoint transmit FIFO, before the core can start transmitting on the
                               USB. The threshold length has to be at least eight DWORDS. This field

                               controls both isochronous and nonisochronous IN endpoint thresholds.

                               The recommended value for TXTHRLEN is to be the same as the

                               programmed AHB burst length (HBSTLEN bit in USB_FS_GAHBCFG).

                               ISO IN Endpoints Threshold Enable.

     1      ISOTHREN     rw    When this bit is Set, the core enables thresholding for isochronous IN

                               endpoints.

                               Non-ISO IN Endpoints Threshold Enable.

        When this bit is Set, the core enables thresholding for Non-

     0      NONISOTHREN | rw   Isochronous IN endpoints.

                               0x0: No thresholding

                               0x1: Enable thresholding for non-isochronous IN endpoints

    16.13.33 USB_FS_DIEPEMPMSK

    Description: Device IN Endpoint FIFO Empty Interrupt Mask Register This register is valid only in

Dedicated FIFO operation. This register is used to control the IN endpoint FIFO empty interrupt

    generation (TXFE_USB_FS_DIEPINTx).

    Offset: 0x0834

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 16-35 USB_FS_DIEPEMPMSK Register Description

     Bit    Symbol    Type    Description

     31:16  -         -       Reserved

                             IN EP Tx FIFO Empty Interrupt Mask Bits

                             These bits acts as mask bits for USB_FS_DIEPINTx.TXFE interrupt, one

                             Bit 0 for IN EP 0, bit 3 for IN EP 3
    15:0     INEPTXFEM    rw
                             0x1: Mask IN EP0 Tx FIFO Empty Interrupt

    16.13.34 USB_FS_DIEPTSIZ0

    Description: Device IN Endpoint 0 Transfer Size Register

    The application must modify this register before enabling endpoint 0. Once endpoint 0 is enabled using

Endpoint Enable bit of the Device Control Endpoint 0 Control registers (EPENA in USB_FS_DIEPCTL0),

    the core modifies this register. The application can only read this register once the core has cleared the

    Endpoint Enable bit. Nonzero endpoints use the registers for endpoints.

    Offset: 0x0910

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 16-36 USB_FS_DIEPTSIZ0 Register Description

     Bit    Symbol   Type   Description

     31:21  -        -      Reserved

                            Packet Count

                            Indicates the total number of USB packets that constitute the Transfer Size

                            amount of data for endpoint 0.

     20:19 | PKTCNT | rw    ■ IN Endpoints : This field is decremented every time a packet (maximum

                            size or short packet) is read from the TxFIFO.

                            ■ OUT Endpoints: This field is decremented every time a packet (maximum

                            size or short packet) is written to the RxFIFO.
     18:7  -     |   -      Reserved
    fi                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               744 / 1446

                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    Transfer Size

                    This field contains the transfer size in bytes for the current endpoint. The

                    transfer size = Sum of buffer sizes across all descriptors in the list for the

                    endpoint. In Buffer DMA, the core only interrupts the application after it has

                    exhausted the transfer size amount of data. The transfer size can be set to

6:0 XFRSIZ | rw     the maximum packet size of the endpoint, to be interrupted at the end of
                    each packet.

                    ■ IN Endpoints: The core decrements this field every time a packet from the

                    external memory is written to the TxFIFO.

                    ■ OUT Endpoints: The core decrements this field every time a packet is read

                    from the RxFIFO and written to the external memory.

16.13.35 USB_FS_DIEPCTL0

Description: Device Control IN Endpoint i Control Register

Offset: 0x0900

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 16-37 USB_FS_DIEPCTL0 Register Description

Bit   Symbol   Type  Description

                     Endpoint Enable

                     Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.

                     ■ The core clears this bit before setting any of the following interrupts on this

                     endpoint:

                     - SETUP Phase Done
 31   EPENA    rw
                     - Endpoint Disabled

                     - Transfer Completed

                     Note: For control endpoints in DMA mode, this bit must be set to be able to

                     transfer SETUP data packets in memory.

                     0x0: No Action


fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    745 / 1446

                                                                  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     0x1: Enable Endpoint

                     Endpoint Disable

                     Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.

    The application sets this bit to stop transmitting/receiving data on an

    endpoint, even before the transfer for that endpoint is complete. The

                     application must wait for the Endpoint Disabled interrupt before treating the
30     EPDIS   rw
                     endpoint as disabled. The core clears this bit before setting the Endpoint

                     Disabled interrupt. The application must set this bit only if Endpoint Enable

                     is already set for this endpoint.

                     0x0: No Action

                     0x1: Disable Endpoint

29:28  -       -     Reserved

                     Set NAK

                     A write to this bit sets the NAK bit for the endpoint.

    Using this bit, the application can control the transmission of NAK

27     SNAK    w     handshakes on an endpoint. The core can also Set this bit for an endpoint

                     after a SETUP packet is received on that endpoint.

                     0x0: No Set NAK

                     0x1: Set NAK

                     Clear NAK

                     A write to this bit clears the NAK bit for the endpoint.
26     CNAK    w
                     0x0: No Clear NAK

                     0x1: Clear NAK

                     TxFIFO Number

25:22  TXFNUM | rw   Shared FIFO Operation non-periodic endpoints must set this bit to zero.

    Periodic endpoints must map this to the corresponding Periodic TxFIFO

fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  746 / 1446

                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

           number.

           ■ 4'h0: Non-Periodic TxFIFO

           ■ Others: Specified Periodic TxFIFO.number

           Note: An interrupt IN endpoint can be configured as a nonperiodic endpoint

           for applications such as mass storage. The core treats an IN endpoint as a

           non-periodic endpoint if the TxFNum field is set to 0. Otherwise, a separate

           periodic FIFO must be allocated for an interrupt IN endpoint, and the number

           of this FIFO must be programmed into the TxFNum field. Configuring an

           interrupt IN endpoint as a non-periodic endpoint saves the extra periodic

           FIFO area.

           Dedicated FIFO Operation: These bits specify the FIFO number associated

    with this endpoint. Each active IN endpoint must be programmed to a

           separate FIFO number.

           This field is valid only for IN endpoints.

           STALL Handshake

           Applies to non-control, non-isochronous IN and OUT endpoints only.

           The application sets this bit to stall all  tokens from the USB host to this

           endpoint. If a NAK bit, Global Non-periodic IN NAK, or Global OUT NAK is set

           along with this bit, the STALL bit takes priority. Only the application can clear

           this bit, never the core.

           Applies to control endpoints only.
21 STALL rw
           The application can only set this bit, and the core clears it, when a SETUP

           token is received for this endpoint. If a NAK bit, Global Non-periodic IN NAK,

           or Global OUT NAK is set along with this bit, the STALL bit takes priority.

           Irrespective of this bit's setting, the core always responds to SETUP data

           packets with an ACK handshake.

           0x0: STALL All non-active tokens

           0x1: STALL All Active Tokens

           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                      747 / 1446

                                                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
20    |  -       -     Reserved Field

                       Endpoint Type

                       This is the transfer type supported by this logical endpoint.

                       ■ 2'b00: Control
19:18    EPTYP   rw
                       ■ 2'b01: Isochronous

                       ■ 2'b10: Bulk

                       ■ 2'b11: Interrupt

                       NAK Status

                       When either the application or the core sets this bit:

                       ■ The core stops receiving any data on an OUT endpoint, even if there is

                       space in the RxFIFO to accommodate the incoming packet.

                       ■ For non-isochronous IN endpoints: The core stops transmitting any data on

                       an IN endpoint, even if there data is available in the TxFIFO.
17     NAKSTS    r
                       ■ For isochronous IN endpoints: The core sends out a zerolength data packet,

                       even if there data is available in the TxFIFO.

                       Irrespective of this bit's setting, the core always responds to SETUP data

                       packets with an ACK handshake.

                       0x0: The core is transmitting non-NAK handshakes based on the FIFO status

                       0x1: The core is transmitting NAK handshakes on this endpoin
16       -     | -     Reserved

                       USB Active Endpoint

                       Indicates whether this endpoint is active in the current configuration and

                       interface. The core clears this bit for all endpoints (other than EP 0) after
15       USBAEP | rw   detecting a USB reset.

    After receiving the SetConfiguration and SetInterface commands, the

                       application must program endpoint registers accordingly and set this bit.

fi                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  748 / 1446

                                                                  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                          0x0: Not Active

                          0x1: USB Active Endpoint

     14:11  -         -   Reserved

                          Maximum Packet Size

 10:0 MPSIZ rw | The application must program this field with the maximum packet size for
        the current logical endpoint. This value is in bytes.

                     0x0: Disables Set DATA0 PID

                     A write to this bit sets the NAK bit for the endpoint.

                     Using this bit, the application can control the transmission of NAK

                  ■ 2'b01: Isochronous

                  ■ 2'b10: Bulk

                  ■ 2'b11: Interrupt

                  NAK Status

                  When either the application or the core sets this bit:

                  ■ The core stops receiving any data on an OUT endpoint, even if

                  there is space in the RxFIFO to accommodate the incoming packet.

                  ■ For non-isochronous IN endpoints: The core stops transmitting

                  any data on an IN endpoint, even if there data is available in the

                  TxFIFO.
17 NAKSTS    r
                  ■ For isochronous IN endpoints: The core sends out a zerolength

                  data packet, even if there data is available in the TxFIFO.

    Irrespective of this bit's setting, the core always responds to

                  SETUP data packets with an ACK handshake.

                  0x0: The core is transmitting non-NAK handshakes based on the

                  FIFO status

                  0x1: The core is transmitting NAK handshakes on this endpoin

                  Endpoint Data PID

                  Applies to interrupt/bulk IN and OUT endpoints only.

                  Contains the PID of the packet to be received or transmitted on

                  this endpoint. The application must program the PID of the first

16 EONUM_DPID r   packet to be received or transmitted on this endpoint, after the

                  endpoint is activated. The applications use the SD0PID fields of

                  this register to program either DATA0 or DATA1 PID.

                  ■ 1'b0: DATA0

                  ■ 1'b1: DATA1



Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    753 / 1446

                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                           Even/Odd Frame

                           Applies to isochronous IN and OUT endpoints only.

                           Indicates the frame number in which the core transmits/receives

                           isochronous data for this endpoint. The application must program

                           the even/odd frame number in which it intends to transmit/receive

        isochronous data for this endpoint using the SEVNFRM and

                           SODDFRM fields in this register.

                           ■ 1'b0: Even frame

                           ■ 1'b1: Odd frame

                           USB Active Endpoint

        Indicates whether this endpoint is active in the current

        configuration and interface. The core clears this bit for all

                           endpoints (other than EP 0) after detecting a USB reset.

    15     USBAEP     rw   After receiving the SetConfiguration and SetInterface commands,

                           the application must program endpoint registers accordingly and

                           set this bit.

                           0x0: Not Active

                           0x1: USB Active Endpoint

    14:11  -          -    Reserved

                           Maximum Packet Size

    10:0  | MPSIZ     rw   | The application must program this field with the maximum packet

                           size for the current logical endpoint. This value is in bytes.



16.13.37 USB_FS_DIEPINTx (x = 0..3, where x =
Endpoint_number)

    Description: Device IN Endpoint x Interrupt Register

    Offset: 0x0908 + x*20

    fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    754 / 1446

                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 16-39 USB_FS_DIEPINTx Register Description (x = 0..3, where x = Endpoint_number)

 Bit   Symbol   Type   Description
 31:8  -     |  -      Reserved

                       Transmit FIFO Empty

                       This bit is valid only for IN endpoints

                       This interrupt is asserted when the TxFIFO for this endpoint is either half or

                       completely empty. The half or completely empty status is determined by the
 7     TXFE     r
                       TxFIFO Empty Level bit in the Core AHB Configuration register (TXFELVL bit

                       in USB_FS_GAHBCFG).

                       0x0: No Transmit FIFO Empty interrupt

                       0x1: Transmit FIFO Empty interrupt

                       IN Endpoint NAK Effective

                       Applies to periodic IN endpoints only.

                       This bit can be cleared when the application clears the IN endpoint NAK by

                       writing to CNAK bit in USB_FS_DIEPCTLx.

                       This interrupt indicates that the core has sampled the NAK bit Set (either by

6      INEPNE   r_w1c | the application or by the core). The interrupt indicates that the IN endpoint

                       NAK bit Set by the application has taken effect in the core.

                       This interrupt does not guarantee that a NAK handshake is sent on the USB.

                       A STALL bit takes priority over a NAK bit.

                       0x0: No Endpoint NAK Effective interrupt

                       0x1: IN Endpoint NAK Effective interrupt

 5     -        -      Reserved

                       IN Token Received When TxFIFO is Empty
 4     ITTXFE  | r_w1c
                       Applies to non-periodic IN endpoints only.


fi                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    755 / 1446

                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Indicates that an IN token was received when the associated TxFIFO

               (periodic/non-periodic) was empty. This interrupt is asserted on the endpoint

               for which the IN token was received.

               0x0: No IN Token Received interrupt

               0x1: IN Token Received Interrupt

               Timeout Condition

               ■ In shared TX FIFO mode, applies to non-isochronous IN endpoints only.

               ■ In dedicated FIFO mode, applies only to Control IN endpoints.

3   TOC   r_w1c | Indicates that the core has detected a timeout condition on the USB for the

               last IN token on this endpoint.

               0x0: No Timeout interrupt

               0x1: Timeout interrupt

2   -     -    Reserved

               Endpoint Disabled Interrupt

               Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.

1   EPDISD | r_w1c | This bit indicates that the endpoint is disabled per the application's request.

               0x0: No Endpoint Disabled Interrupt

               0x1: Endpoint Disabled Interrupt

               Transfer Completed Interrupt

               Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.

               This field indicates that the programmed transfer is complete on the AHB as
0   XFRC  r_w1c
               well as on the USB, for this endpoint.

               0x0: No Transfer Complete Interrupt

               0x1: Transfer Complete Interrupt



{dango         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    756 / 1446

16.13.38 USB_FS_DIEPTSIZx (x = 1..3, where x =
Endpoint_number)

    Description: Device IN Endpoint x Transfer Size Register

    Offset: 0x0910 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 16-40 USB_FS_DIEPTSIZx Register Description (x = 1..3, where x = Endpoint_number)

     Bit    Symbol Type    Description
     31     -     | -      Reserved

                           Applies to IN endpoints only.

                           For periodic IN endpoints, this field indicates the number of packets that must

        be transmitted per microframe on the USB. The core uses this field to

                           calculate the data PID for isochronous IN endpoints.

                           ■ 2'b01: 1 packet

     30:29  MCNT   | rw    ■ 2'b10: 2 packets

                           ■ 2'b11: 3 packets

                           For non-periodic IN endpoints, this field is valid only in Internal DMA mode.

                           It specifies the number of packets the core must fetchfor an IN endpoint

                           before it switches to the endpoint pointed to by the Next Endpoint field of the

                           Device Endpoint-n Control register.

                           Packet Count

                           Indicates the total number of USB packets that constitute the Transfer Size

     28:19 | PKTCNT | rw   amount of data for endpoint 0.

                           This field is decremented every time a packet (maximum size or short packet)

                           is read from the TxFIFO.

                           Transfer Size

     18:0  | XFRSIZ | rw   Indicates the transfer size in bytes for endpoint 0. The core interrupts the

                           application only after it has exhausted the transfer size amount of data. The

    fi                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                    757 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                          transfer size can be Set to the maximum packet size of the endpoint, to be

                          interrupted at the end of each packet.

                          The core decrements this field every time a packet from the external memory

                          is written to the TxFIFO.

16.13.39 USB_FS_DIEPDMAx (x = 1..3, where x =
Endpoint_number)

    Description: Device IN Endpoint x DMA Address Register

    Offset: 0x0914 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 16-41 USB_FS_DIEPDMAx Register Description (x = 1..3, where x = Endpoint_number)

     Bit    Symbol   Type    Description

        Holds the start address of the external memory for storing or fetching

                             endpoint data.

                             Note: For control endpoints, this field stores control OUT data packets as

                             well as SETUP transaction data packets.
     31:0   DMAADDR  rw
                             When more than three SETUP packets are received back-toback, the SETUP

                             data packet in the memory is overwritten.

                             This register is incremented on every AHB transaction. The application can

                             give only a DWORD-aligned address.

16.13.40 USB_FS_DTXFSTSx (x = 0..3, where x =
Endpoint_number)

    Description: Device IN Endpoint Transmit FIFO Status Register x

    Offset: 0x0918 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0140

    Table 16-42 USB_FS_DTXFSTSx Register Description (x = 0..3, where x = Endpoint_number)

     Bit     Symbol       Type   Description

     31:16   Rsvd         -      Reserved

     15:0    INEPTFSAV    r      IN Endpoint TxFIFO Space Avail
    fi ¥dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            758 / 1446

                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      Indicates the amount of free space available in the Endpoint TxFIFO.

                      Values are in terms of 32-bit words.

                      ■ 16'h0: Endpoint TxFIFO is full

                      ■ 16'h1: 1 word available

                      ■ 16'h2: 2 words available

                      ■ 16'hn: n words available (where 0 n 32,768)

                      ■ 16'h8000: 32,768 words available

                      ■ Others: Reserved

16.13.41 USB_FS_DOEPCTL0

Description: Device Control OUT Endpoint 0 Control Register

Offset: 0x0b00

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 16-43 USB_FS_DOEPCTL0 Register Description

   Bit  Symbol Type  Description

                     this bit indicates that the application has allocated the memory to start

                     receiving data from the USB.

                     ■ The core clears this bit before setting any of the following interrupts on this

                     endpoint:

                     - SETUP Phase Done

                     - Endpoint Disabled
    31  EPENA rw
                     - Transfer Completed

                     Note: In DMA mode, this bit must be set for the core to transfer SETUP data

    packets into memory and it will not be cleared on Transfer Completed

                     interrupt of SETUP packet.

                     0x0: No action

                     0x1: Enable Endpoint

fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.             759 / 1446

                                                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    Endpoint Disable

30     EPDIS   r    The application cannot disable control OUT endpoint 0.

                    0x0: No Endpoint disable

29:28  Rsvd    -    Reserved

                    Set NAK

                    A write to this bit sets the NAK bit for the endpoint. Using this bit, the

                    application can control the transmission of NAK handshakes on an endpoint.

27     SNAK    w    The core can also set bit on a Transfer Completed interrupt, or after a SETUP
                    is received on the endpoint.

                    0x0: No action

                    0x1: Set NAK

                    Clear NAK

                    A write to this bit clears the NAK bit for the endpoint.
26     CNAK    w
                    0x0: No action

                    0x1: Clear NAK

25:22  Rsvd    -    Reserved

                    STALL Handshake

                    The application can only set this bit, and the core clears it, when a SETUP

                    token is received for this endpoint. If a NAK bit or Global OUT NAK is Set

                    along with this bit, the STALL bit takes priority. Irrespective of this bit's
21     STALL   rw   setting, the core always responds to SETUP data     packets with   an     ACK

                    handshake.

                    0x0: No Stall

                    0x1: Stall Handshake

                    Snoop mode
20     SNPM    rw
                    This bit configures the endpoint to Snoop mode. In Snoop mode, the core
fi                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  760 / 1446

                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    does not check the correctness of OUT packets before transferring them to

                    application memory.

                    Endpoint Type

19:18 | EPTYP   r   Hardcoded to 2'b00 for control.

                    0x0: Endpoint Control 0

                    NAK Status

                    When either the application or the core sets this bit, the core stops receiving

                    data, even If there is space in the RxFIFO to accommodate the incoming

17     NAKSTS   r   packet. Irrespective of this bit's setting, the core always responds to SETUP
                    data packets with an ACK handshake.

                    0x0 :The core is transmitting non-NAK handshakes based on the FIFO status

                    0x1 :The core is transmitting NAK handshakes on this endpoint

16     Rsvd     -   Reserved

                    USB Active Endpoint

                    This bit is always set to 1, indicating that a control endpoint 0 is always active
15     USBAEP   r
                    in all configurations and interfaces.

                    0x1: USB Active Endpoint 0

14:2   Rsvd     -   Reserved

                    Maximum Packet Size

                    The maximum packet size for control OUT endpoint 0 is the same as what is

                    programmed in control IN Endpoint 0.

1:0    MPSIZ    r   ■ 2'b00: 64 bytes

                    ■ 2'b01: 32 bytes

                    ■ 2'b10: 16 bytes

                    ■ 2'b11: 8 bytes

16.13.43 USB_FS_DOEPCTLx (x = 1..3, where x =
Endpoint_number)

    Description: Device Control OUT Endpoint x Control Register

    Offset: 0x0B00 + x*20

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 16-45 USB_FS_DOEPCTLx Register Description (x = 1..3, where x = Endpoint_number)

    Bit  Symbol        Type  Description

                             Endpoint Enable

                             Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.

                             ■ For IN endpoints, this bit indicates that data is ready to be

                             transmitted on the endpoint.

                             ■ For OUT endpoints, this bit indicates that the application has

                             allocated the memory to start receiving data from the USB.

        The core clears this bit before setting any of the following

                             interrupts on this endpoint:

    31   EPENA        rw     ■ SETUP Phase Done

                             ■ Endpoint Disabled

                             ■ Transfer Completed

                             Note: For control endpoints in DMA mode, this bit must be set for

                             the controller to transfer SETUP data packets to the memory. This

        bit will not be cleared on Transfer Completed interrupt of the

                             SETUP packet.

                             0x0: No Action

                             0x1: Enable Endpoint

                             Endpoint Disable
     30  EPDIS        rw
                             Applies to IN and OUT endpoints.



    fi    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    763 / 1446

               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

               The application sets this bit to stop transmitting/receiving data on

    an endpoint, even before the transfer for that endpoint is

               complete. The application must wait for the Endpoint Disabled

               interrupt before treating the endpoint as disabled. The core clears

    this bit before setting the Endpoint Disabled interrupt. The

               application must set this bit only if Endpoint Enable is already set

               for this endpoint.

               0x0: No Action

               0x1: Disable Endpoint

               Set DATA1 PID

               ■ Applies to interrupt and bulk IN and OUT endpoints only.

               ■ Writing to this field sets the Endpoint Data PID (DPID) field in

               this register to DATA1.

               Set Odd frame

29 SODDFRM w   ■ Applies to isochronous IN and OUT endpoints only.

               ■ Writing to this field sets the even and odd frame (EONUM) field

               to odd frame.

               0x0: Disables Set DATA1 PID or Do not force Odd Frame

               0x1: Set Endpoint Data PID to DATA1 or Sets EONUM field to odd

               Frame

               Set DATA0 PID

               ■ Applies to interrupt/bulk IN and OUT endpoints only.

               ■ Writing to this field sets the Endpoint Data PID (DPID) field in
28 SEVNFRM  w
               this register to DATA0.

               Set Even frame

               ■ Applies to isochronous IN and OUT endpoints only.



fi    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    764 / 1446

                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      ■ Writing to this field sets the Even/Odd frame (EONUM) field to

                      even frame.

                      0x0: Disables Set DATA0 PID or Do not force Even Frame

                      0x1: Set Endpoint Data PID to DATA0 or Sets EONUM field to odd

                      Frame

                      Set NAK

                      A write to this bit sets the NAK bit for the endpoint.

                      Using this bit, the application can control the transmission of NAK

27     SNAK      w    handshakes on an endpoint. The core can also set this bit for an

                      endpoint after a SETUP packet is received on that endpoint.

                      0x0: No Set NAK

                      0x1: Set NAK

                      Clear NAK

                      A write to this bit clears the NAK bit for the endpoint.
26     CNAK      w
                      0x0: No Clear NAK

                      0x1: Clear NAK

25:22  Rsvd      -    Reserved Field

                      STALL Handshake

                      Applies to non-control, non-isochronous IN and OUT endpoints

                      only.

                      The application sets this bit to stall all tokens from the USB host

21     STALL     rw   to this endpoint. If a NAK bit, Global Non-periodic IN NAK, or

                      Global OUT NAK is set along with this bit, the STALL bit takes

                      priority. Only the application can clear this bit, never the core.

                      Applies to control endpoints only.

                      The application can only set this bit, and the core clears it, when

fi                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                          765 / 1446

                                                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      a SETUP token is received for this endpoint. If a NAK bit, Global

                      Non-periodic IN NAK, or Global OUT NAK is set along with this bit,

                      the STALL bit takes priority. Irrespective of this bit's setting, the

    core always responds to SETUP data packets with an ACK

                      handshake.

                      0x0: STALL All non-active tokens

                      0x1: STALL All Active Tokens

                      Snoop mode

20     SNPM      rw   This bit configures the endpoint to Snoop mode. In Snoop mode,
                      the core does not check the correctness of OUT packets before

                      transferring them to application memory.

                      Endpoint Type

                      This is the transfer type supported by this logical endpoint.

                      ■ 2'b00: Control
19:18  EPTYP     rw
                      ■ 2'b01: Isochronous

                      ■ 2'b10: Bulk

                      ■ 2'b11: Interrupt

                      NAK Status

                      When either the application or the core sets this bit:

                      ■ The core stops receiving any data on an OUT endpoint, even if

                      there is space in the RxFIFO to accommodate the incoming packet.

17    NAKSTS     r    ■ For non-isochronous IN endpoints: The core stops transmitting
                      any data on an IN endpoint, even if there data is available in the

                      TxFIFO.

                      ■ For isochronous IN endpoints: The core sends out a zerolength

                      data packet, even if there data is available in the TxFIFO.

    Irrespective of this bit's setting, the core always responds to

                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            766 / 1446

                  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  SETUP data packets with an ACK handshake.

                  0x0 :The core is transmitting non-NAK handshakes based on the

                  FIFO status

                  0x1 :The core is transmitting NAK handshakes on this endpoint

                  Endpoint Data PID

                  Applies to interrupt/bulk IN and OUT endpoints only.

                  Contains the PID of the packet to be received or transmitted on

                  this endpoint. The application must program the PID of the first

                  packet to be received or transmitted on this endpoint, after the

                  endpoint is activated. The applications use the SD0PID fields of

                  this register to program either DATA0 or DATA1 PID.

                  ■ 1'b0: DATA0

                  ■ 1'b1: DATA1
16 DPIDEONUM r
                  Even/Odd Frame

                  ■ Applies to isochronous IN and OUT endpoints only.

                  ■ Indicates the frame number in which the core transmits/receives

                  isochronous data for this endpoint.

    The application must program the even/odd frame number in

    which it intends to transmit/receive isochronous data for this

                  endpoint using the SEVNFRM and SODDFRM fields in this register.

                  - 1'b0: Even frame

                  - 1'b1: Odd frame

                  USB Active Endpoint

    Indicates whether this endpoint is active in the current

15 USBAEP    rw   configuration and interface. The core clears  this  bit for all

                  endpoints (other than EP 0) after detecting a USB reset.

                  After receiving the SetConfiguration and SetInterface commands,

                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                   767 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                                         the application must program endpoint registers accordingly and

                                         set this bit.

                                         0x0: Not Active

                                         0x1: USB Active Endpoint

     14:11 Rsvd               -          Reserved Field

                                         Maximum Packet Size

     10:0  | MPSIZ            rw | The application must program this field with the maximum packet
                                         size for the current logical endpoint. This value is in bytes.

                  well as on the USB, for this endpoint.

    16.13.47 USB_FS_PCGCCTL

    Description: Power and Clock Gating Control Register

    Offset: 0x0E00

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 16-49 USB_FS_PCGCCTL Register Description


    fi                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    771 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

 Bit   Symbol    Type    Description
 31:5  Rsvd      -       Reserved                                                                   |

                         Gate Hclk

                         ■ The application sets this bit to gate hclk to modules other than the AHB

                         Slave and Master and wakeup logic when the USB is suspended or the

                         session is not valid.

 1     GATEHCLK  rw      ■ The application clears this bit when the USB is resumed or a new session

                         starts.

                         0x0: Clears this bit when the USB is resumed or a new session starts

                         0x1: Sets this bit to gate hclk to modules when the USB is suspended or the

                         session is not valid

                         Stop Pclk

                         ■ The application sets this bit to stop the PHY clock (phy_clk) when the USB

                         is suspended, the session is not valid, or the device is disconnected.

0      STPPCLK  rw       ■ The application clears this bit when the USB is resumed or a new session

                         starts.

                         0x0: Disable Stop Pclk

                         0x1: Enable Stop Pclk

16.13.48 USB_FS_PHYCTL0

Offset: 0x0F000

Reset: 0x0000_0000

V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
17 CRC Engine(CRC)

The CRC Engine is available in V32F20x/ V32H20x .


17.1 Features


The CRC Engine has the following key features:

• Supports three common polynomials CRC-CCITT, CRC-16 and CRC-32.
– CRC-CCITT:x¹⁶ + x¹² + x⁵ + 1
– CRC-16:x16 + x15 + x2 + 1
– CRC-32:x32 + x26 + x23 + x22 + x16 + x12 + x11 + x10 + x8 + x7 + x5 + x4 + x2 + x + 1
• Bit order reverse and 1’s complement programmable setting for input data and CRC sum.
• Programmable seed number setting.
• Supports CPU PIO back-to-back transfer.
• Accept any size of data width per write:8 , 16 or 32-bit.
– 8-bit write: 1-cycle operation.
– 16-bit write: 2-cycle operation (8-bit x 2-cycle).
– 32-bit write: 4-cycle operation (8-bit x 4-cycle).

17.2 CRC Registers


Table 17-1 CRC Registers Map

Register Name     Offset    Type  Reset Value   Description

CRC_CTRL          0x0000    rw    0x0000_0000   Control register.

CRC_SEED          0x0004    w     0x0000_FFFF   Seed register.

CRC_SUM           0x0008    r     0x0000_FFFF   Sum register.

CRC_DATA          0x000C    w     0x0000_0000   DATA register.

CRC_STATUS        0x0010    r     0x0000_0000   Status register.

CRC_FASTIO        0x001C    rw    0x0000_0000   Fast GPIO output data register.

17.2.1 CRC_CTRL

Offset: 0x0000

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 17-2 CRC_CTRL Register Description
{dango                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        774 / 1446

                                                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Bit    Symbol   Type        Description

    31:6   Rsvd     -           Reserved

    5      SC       rw          CRC sum complement

                                1: 1’s complement for SUM.

                                0: No 1’s complement for SUM.

    4      SBO      rw          CRC sum bit order

                                1: Bit order reverse for SUM.

                                0: No bit order reverse for SUM.

    3      CMPL     rw          Data complement

                                1: 1’s complement for DATA.

                                0: No 1’s complement for DATA.

    2      BITRVS   rw          Data bit order

                                1: Bit order reverse for DATA (per byte).

                                0: No bit order reverse for DATA (per byte).

    1:0    POLY     rw          CRC polynomial:

                                1X: CRC-32 polynomial.

                                01: CRC-16 polynomial.

                                00: CRC-CCITT polynomial.

    17.2.2 CRC_SEED

    Offset: 0x0004

    Reset: 0x0000_FFFF

    Table 17-3 CRC_SEED Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31:0SEED w A write access to this register will load CRC seed value to SUM register with select bit

                   order and 1’s complement pre-processes.

                   Remark: A write access to this register will overrule the CRC calculation in

                   progresses.

    17.2.3 CRC_SUM

31:0DATA w Data written to this register will be taken to perform CRC calculation with selected bit

    17.3       CRC-CCITT setup

    • Polynomial= x¹⁶ + x¹² + x⁵ + 1
    • Seed value = 0xFFFF
    • Bit order reverse for data input: No

    {dango                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    776 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    • 1’s complement for data input: No
    • Bit order reverse for CRC sum: No
    • 1’s complement for CRC sum: No
    • CRC_MODE = 0x0000_0000
    • CRC_SEED = 0x0000 FFFF

    17.4 CRC-16 setup

    • Polynomial= x¹⁶ + x¹⁵ + x² + 1

• Seed value = 0xFFFF FFFF
• Bit order reverse for data input: Yes

• Bit order reverse for CRC sum: Yes
