19.4.5 GTMR_SR

Offset: 0x0010

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 19-7 GTMR_SR Register Description

Bits   Symbol TypeDescription

15:13Rsvd     -     Reserved

12     CC4OF rw     refer to CC1OF description

11     CC3OF rw     refer to CC1OF description

10     CC2OF rw     refer to CC1OF description

9      CC1OF rw     This flag is set by hardware only when the corresponding channel is configured in

                    input capture mode. It is cleared by software by writing it to ’0’.

                    0: No overcapture has been detected.

                    1: The counter value has been captured in GTMR_CCR1 register while CC1IF flag

                    was already set

8:7    Rsvd    -    Reserved

6      TIF     rw   This flag is set by hardware on trigger event (active edge detected on TRGI input

                    when the slave mode controller is enabled in all modes but gated mode, both edges

                    in case gated mode is selected). It is cleared by software.

                    0: No trigger event occurred.

                    1: Trigger interrupt pending.

5      Rsvd    -    Reserved

4      CC4IF   rw   refer to CC1IF description

3      CC3IF   rw   refer to CC1IF description

2      CC2IF   rw   refer to CC1IF description

1      CC1IF   rw   If channel CC1 is configured as output:

                    This flag is set by hardware when the counter matches the compare value, with

                    some exception in center-aligned mode (refer to the CMS bits in the GTMR_CR1

                    register description). It is cleared by software.

{dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 849 / 1446

                                                                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   0: No match.

                   1: The content of the counter GTMR_CNT matches the content of the GTMR_CCR1

                   register.

                   When the contents of GTMR_CCR1 are greater than the contents of GTMR_ARR, the

                   CC1IF bit goes high on the counter overflow (in upcounting and up/down-counting

                   modes) or underflow (in downcounting mode)

                   If channel CC1 is configured as input:

                   This bit is set by hardware on a capture. It is cleared by software or by reading the

                   GTMR_CCR1 register.

                   0: No input capture occurred

                   1: The counter value has been captured in GTMR_CCR1 register (An edge has been

                   detected on IC1 which matches the selected polarity)

0     UIF     rw   This bit is set by hardware on an update event. It is cleared by software.

                   0: No update occurred.

                   1: Update interrupt pending. This bit is set by hardware when the registers are

                   updated:

                   -   At overflow or underflow regarding the repetition counter value (update if

                   repetition counter = 0) and if the UDIS=0 in the GTMR_CR1 register.

                   -   When CNT is reinitialized by software using the UG bit in GTMR_EGR register, if

                   URS=0 and UDIS=0 in the GTMR_CR1 register.

                   -   When CNT is reinitialized by a trigger event, if URS=0 and UDIS=0 in the

                   GTMR_CR1 register.

19.4.6 GTMR_EGR

Offset: 0x0014

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 19-8 GTMR_EGR Register Description

Bits Symbol TypeDescription

15:7Rsvd   -      Reserved

6     TG   rw     This bit is set by software in order to generate an event, it is automatically cleared by

                  hardware.

                  0: No action

                  1: The TIF flag is set in GTMR_SR register. Related interrupt or DMA transfer can

{dango                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                     850 / 1446

                                                                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        occur if enabled.

    5     Rsvd   -      Reserved

    4     CC4G   rw     refer to CC1G description

    3     CC3G   rw     refer to CC1G description

    2     CC2G   rw     refer to CC1G description

1 CC1G rw This bit is set by software in order to generate an event, it is automatically cleared by

                        hardware.

                        0: No action

                        1: A capture/compare event is generated on channel 1:

                        If channel CC1 is configured as output:

                        CC1IF flag is set, Corresponding interrupt or DMA request is sent if enabled.

                        If channel CC1 is configured as input:

                        The current value of the counter is captured in GTMR_CCR1 register. The CC1IF flag

                        is set, the corresponding interrupt or DMA request is sent if enabled. The CC1OF flag

                        is set if the CC1IF flag was already high.

0 UG rw This bit can be set by software, it is automatically cleared by hardware.

                        0: No action

                        1: Reinitialize the counter and generates an update of the registers. Note that the

                        prescaler counter is cleared too (anyway the prescaler ratio is not affected). The

                        counter is cleared if the center-aligned mode is selected or if DIR=0 (up-counting),

                        else it takes the auto-reload value (GTMR_ARR) if DIR=1 (downcounting).

    19.4.7 GTMR_CCMR1

    Offset: 0x0018

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Output compare mode:

    Table 19-9 GTMR_CCMR1 Register Description

    Bits   Symbol TypeDescription

    15     OC2CE rw      Output compare 2 clear enable

    14:12OC2M       rw   Output compare 2 mode

    11     OC2PE rw      Output compare 2 preload enable

    10     OC2FE rw      Output compare 2 fast enable

    {dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                     851 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

9:8 CC2S rw This bit-field defines the direction of the channel (input/output) as well as the used

                   input.

                   00: CC2 channel is configured as output

                   01: CC2 channel is configured as input, IC2 is mapped on TI2

                   10: CC2 channel is configured as input, IC2 is mapped on TI1

                   11: CC2 channel is configured as input, IC2 is mapped on TRC. This mode is working

                   only if an internal trigger input is selected through the TS bit (GTMR_SMCR register)

    7  OC1CE rw    0: OC1Ref is not affected by the ETRF Input

                   1: OC1Ref is cleared as soon as a High level is detected on ETRF input

6:4 OC1M rw These bits define the behavior of the output reference signal OC1REF from which

                   OC1 and OC1N are derived. OC1REF is active high whereas OC1 and OC1N active

                   level depends on CC1P and CC1NP bits.

                   000: Frozen - The comparison between the output compare register GTMR_CCR1

                   and the counter GTMR_CNT has no effect on the outputs.(this mode is used to

                   generate a timing base).

                   001: Set channel 1 to active level on match. OC1REF signal is forced high when the

                   counter GTMR_CNT matches the capture/compare register 1 (GTMR_CCR1).

                   010: Set channel 1 to inactive level on match. OC1REF signal is forced low when the

                   counter GTMR_CNT matches the capture/compare register 1 (GTMR_CCR1).

                   011:Toggle-OC1REF toggles when GTMR_CNT=GTMR_CCR1.

                   100: Force inactive level - OC1REF is forced low.

                   101: Force active level - OC1REF is forced high.

                   110: PWM mode 1 - In upcounting, channel 1 is active as long as

                   GTMR_CNT<GTMR_CCR1 else inactive. In downcounting, channel 1 is inactive

                   (OC1REF=’0’) as long as

                   GTMR_CNT>GTMR_CCR1 else active (OC1REF=’1’).

                   111: PWM mode 2 - In upcounting, channel 1 is inactive as long as

                   GTMR_CNT<GTMR_CCR1 else active. In downcounting, channel 1 is active as long as

                   GTMR_CNT>GTMR_CCR1 else inactive.

    3  OC1PE rw    0: Preload register on GTMR_CCR1 disabled. GTMR_CCR1 can be written at anytime,

                   the new value is taken in account immediately.

                   1:PreloadregisteronGTMR_CCR1enabled.
         {dango        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                      852 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

        Read/Write operations access the preload register. GTMR_CCR1 preload value is

        loaded in the active register at each update event.

2 OC1FE rw This bit is used to accelerate the effect of an event on the trigger in input on the CC

        output.

        0: CC1 behaves normally depending on counter and CCR1 values even when the

        trigger is ON. The minimum delay to activate CC1 output when an edge occurs on

        the trigger input is 5 clock cycles.

        1: An active edge on the trigger input acts like a compare match on CC1 output. Then,

        OC is set to the compare level independently from the result of the comparison.

        Delay to sample the trigger input and to activate CC1 output is reduced to 3 clock

        cycles. OCFE acts only if the channel is configured in PWM1 or PWM2 mode.

1:0 CC1S rw This bit-field defines the direction of the channel (input/output) as well as the used

        input.

        00: CC1 channel is configured as output

        01: CC1 channel is configured as input, IC1 is mapped on TI1

        10: CC1 channel is configured as input, IC1 is mapped on TI2

        11: CC1 channel is configured as input, IC1 is mapped on TRC. This mode is working

        only if an internal trigger input is selected through TS bit (GTMR_SMCR register)




    Input capture mode:

    Table 19-10 GTMR_CCMR1 Register Description

    Bit Symbol AttrDescription

    15:12IC2F  rw   Intput capture 2 filter

    11:10IC2PSC rw  Intput capture 2 prescaler

    9:8 CC2S   rw   Capture/compare 2 selection

                    This bit-field defines the direction of the channel (in- put/output) as well as the used

                    input.

                    00: CC2 channel is configured as output

                    01: CC2 channel is configured as input, IC2 is mapped on TI1

                    10: CC2 channel is configured as input, IC2 is mapped on TI2

                    11: CC2 channel is configured as input, IC2 is mapped on TRC. This mode is working

    {dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        853 / 1446

                                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 only if

                 an internal trigger input is selected through TS bit (GTMR_SMCR register)

                 Note: CC2S bits are writable only when the channel is OFF (CC2E = ‘0’ in

                 GTMR_CCER).

7:4  IC1F  rw    Intput capture 1 filter

                 This bit-field defines the frequency used to sample TI1 input and the length of the

                 digital filter applied to TI1. The digital filter is made of an event counter in which N

                 consecutive events are needed to validate a transition on the output:

                 0000: No filter, sampling is done at fDTS

                 0001: fSAMPLING=fCK_INT, N=2

                 0010: fSAMPLING=fCK_INT, N=4

                 0011: fSAMPLING=fCK_INT, N=8

                 0100: fSAMPLING=fDTS/2, N=6

                 0101: fSAMPLING=fDTS/2, N=8

                 0110: fSAMPLING=fDTS/4, N=6

                 0111: fSAMPLING=fDTS/4, N=8

                 1000: fSAMPLING=fDTS/8, N=6

                 1001: fSAMPLING=fDTS/8, N=8

                 1010: fSAMPLING=fDTS/16, N=5

                 1011: fSAMPLING=fDTS/16, N=6

                 1100: fSAMPLING=fDTS/16, N=8

                 1101: fSAMPLING=fDT S/32, N=5

                 1110: fSAMPLING=fDTS/32, N=6

                 1111: fSAMPLING=fDTS/32, N=8

3:2   IC1PSC rw  Intput capture 1 prescaler

                 This bit-field defines the ratio of the prescaler acting on CC1 input (IC1).

                 The prescaler is reset as soon as CC1E=’0’ (GTMR_CCER register).

                 00: No prescaler, capture is done each time an edge is detected on the capture input

                 01: Capture is done once every 2 events

                 10: Capture is done once every 4 events

                 11: Capture is done once every 8 events

1:0   CC1S rw    Capture/compare 1 selection
       {dango        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                         854 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

This bit-field defines the direction of the channel (input/ output) as well as the used

input.

00: CC1 channel is configured as output

01: CC1 channel is configured as input, IC1 is mapped on TI1

10: CC1 channel is configured as input, IC1 is mapped on TI2

11: CC1 channel is configured as input, IC1 is mapped on TRC. This mode is working

only if an internal trigger input is selected through TS bit (GTMR_SMCR register)

Note: CC1S bits are writable only when the channel is OFF (CC1E = ‘0’ in

GTMR_CCER).

19.4.9 GTMR_CCER

Offset: 0x0020

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 19-13 GTMR_CCER Register Description

Bits   Symbol TypeDescription

15:14Rsvd     -        Reserved

13     CC4P   rw       refer to CC1P description

12     CC4E   rw       refer to CC1E description

11:10Rsvd     -        Reserved

9      CC3P   rw       refer to CC1P description

8      CC3E   rw       refer to CC1E description

7:6    Rsvd   -        Reserved

5      CC2P   rw       refer to CC1P description

4      CC2E   rw       refer to CC1E description

3:2    Rsvd   -        Reserved

1      CC1P   rw       CC1 channel configured as output:

                       0: OC1 active high

                       1: OC1 active low

                       CC1 channel configured as input:

                       This bit selects whether IC1 or IC1 is used for trigger or capture operations.

                       0: Non-inverted: Capture is done on a rising edge of IC1.

                       When used as external trigger, IC1 is non-inverted.

                       1: Inverted: Capture is done on a falling edge of IC1.

                       When used as external trigger, IC1 is inverted.

0      CC1E     rw     CC1 channel configured as output:

                       0: Off - OC1 is not active. OC1 level is then function of MOE, OSSI, OSSR, OIS1,

                       OIS1N and CC1NE bits.

            {dango         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  857 / 1446

                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        1: On - OC1 signal is output on the corresponding output pin depending on MOE,

                        OSSI, OSSR, OIS1, OIS1N and CC1NE bits.

                        CC1 channel configured as input:

                        This bit determines if a capture of the counter value can actually be done into the

                        input capture/compare register 1 (GTMR_CCR1) or not.

                        0: Capture disabled.

                        1: Capture enabled.

    Bits Symbol TypeDescription

15:0PSC rw The counter clock frequency (CK_CNT) is equal to fCK_PSC / (PSC[15:0] + 1).

                     PSC contains the value to be loaded in the active prescaler register at each update

                     event (including when the counter is cleared through UG bit of GTMR_EGR register or

                     through trigger controller when configured in ’reset mode’).

    19.4.12         GTMR_ARR

    Offset: 0x002c

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 19-16 GTMR_ARR Register Description

    Bits    Symbol  Type   Description

    19.4.13         GTMR_CCR1

    Offset: 0x0034
    §                          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        858 / 1446

                                                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 19-17 GTMR_CCR1 Register Description

Bits Symbol TypeDescription

15:0CCR1 rw   If channel CC1 is configured as output:

              CCR1 is the value to be loaded in the actual capture/compare 1 register (preload

              value).

              It is loaded permanently if the preload feature is not selected in the GTMR_CCMR1

              register

              (bit OC1PE). Else the preload value is copied in the active capture/compare 1 register

              when an update event occurs.

              The active capture/compare register contains the value to be compared to the

              counter GTMR_CNT and signaled on OC1 output. If channel CC1 is configured as

              input:

              CCR1 is the counter value transferred by the last input capture 1 event (IC1). The

              GTMR_CCR1 register is read-only and cannot be programmed.

19.4.14     GTMR_CCR2

Offset: 0x0038

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 19-18 GTMR_CCR2 Register Description

Bits Symbol TypeDescription

15:0CCR2 rw   If channel CC2 is configured as output:

              CCR2 is the value to be loaded in the actual capture/compare 2 register (preload

              value).

              It is loaded permanently if the preload feature is not selected in the GTMR_CCMR1

              register (bit OC2PE). Else the preload value is copied in the active capture/compare 2

              register when an update event occurs.

              The active capture/compare register contains the value to be compared to the

              counter GTMR_CNT and signalled on OC2 output. If channel CC2 is configured as

              input:

              CCR2 is the counter value transferred by the last input capture 2 event (IC2). The

              GTMR_CCR2 register is read-only and cannot be programmed.

  20 Basic Timer Controller
   (BTMR/LPBTMR)

  20.1 Introduction


   The 32 bits general purpose timers could be used to generate regulate interrupt for CPU.


  20.2 Features


    32 bits general purpose timer in each Timer group


  20.3 BTMR Registers


   Table 20-1 BTMR Registers Map

   Register Name      Offset    Type     Reset Value   Description

   BTMR_CTRL          0x0000    rw       0x00          Timer0 control register.

   BTMR_VALUE         0x0004    rw       0x00          Timer0 real time counting register.

   BTMR_RELOAD        0x0008    rw       0x00          Timer0 reloads register.

   BTMR_INTSTS        0x000C    rw       0x00          Timer0 interrupt status register.

   20.3.1 BTMR_CTRL Register

   Table 20-2 BTMR_CTRL Register Description

   Bit   Symbol Type Description

   31:4 Rsvd    -    Reserved.

   3     INTEN  rw   Timerx interrupt enable register.

2 EXTCLK rw Select the external clock signal as the clock source.

                     The rising edge of external clock will be used as the enable signal of the internal

                     counter. Every time when a rising edge of external clock arrives, the internal

                     counter subtracts 1.

                     In this mode, the frequency of the external clock signal should be lower than

                     PCLK/6.

1 EXTEN rw Select Timer external clock pin as the clock enable IO port.

                     When EXTEN is 1 and external clock pin is 1, the internal counter will subtract 1 as

   fi                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        862 / 1446

                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   PCLK clock frequency. When EXTEN is 1 and external clock pin is 0, the internal

                   counter will not change. There are 2 simultaneously cycle clock in order to avoid

                   the glitch es on the external clock pin.

                   When EXTCLK and EXTEN enable at the same time, EXTCLK priority is higher than

                   EXTEN.

0     EN      rw   Timerx enable control register.

                   0: Disable

                   1: Enable

20.3.2 BTMR_VALUE Register

Table 20-3 BTMR_VALUE Register Description

Bit       Symbol       Type     Description

31:0      VALUE        rw       Timerx real time counter register.

20.3.3 BTMR_RELOAD Register

Table 20-4 BTMR_RELOAD Register Description

Bit   Name     Type Description

31:0 RELOAD rw     Timerx reloads register. Each time timer counts down to 0, RELOAD value will be

                   overloaded to TMRx_VALUE timer, and restart count down again; meanwhile,

                   INTSTS bit will be set 1.

                   The calculation formula RELOAD timer:

                   RELOAD = Period * APBCLK – 1.

20.3.4 BTMR_INTSTS Register

Table 20-5 BTMR_INTSTS Register Description

Bit      Symbol     Type       Description

31:1     Rsvd       -          Reserved.

0        INTSTS     rc_w1      Timerx interrupt status register.

                               When timer count down to 0, the bit will set 1.

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
21 Basic PWM Controller (BPWM)

21.1 Introduction


The 16bits timers has the PWM and capture function. Each timer can generate individual interrupt to

CPU. Each PWM can output 3 outputs with different output waveform, and one of 4 input can be

selected to be the capture signal.


   21.2 Features


     16 bits PWM/Capture timer.

     Each PWM timer can have at most 3 outputs and one input.

     Totally 8 output mode can be selected.

21.4 BPWM Registers


 Table 21-1 BPWM Registers Map

 Register Name          Offset   Type Reset Value Description

 21.4.1 BPWM_CTL Register

 The BPWM_CTL is the 16 bits PWM timer x’s control register.

 Table 21-2 BPWM_CTL Register Description

 Bit   Symbol Type  Description

 31:8 Rsvd    -     Reserved.

 7:6   ID     rw    PWM Timerx input clock divide control.

                    0: Input clock 1/2 division

                    1: Input clock 1/4 division

                    2: Input clock 1/8 division

                    3: Input clock 1/16 division

 5:4   MC     rw    PWM Timer control.

                    0: Stop counting

                    1: Count up: Count up to CCR0, and from 0 to start it.

                    2: Continue counting: Count up to 0xFFFF, and from 0 to start it.

                    3: Count up/down: Count up to CCR0, and from CCR0 count down to 0.

 3     TSEL   rw    Clock source selected

                    0: APB clock/128

                    1: APB clock

 2     CLR    rw    TAR clear register

                    When the bit set 1, TAR will clear 0. After TAR cleared 0, the bit also will be

                    automatic cleared 0.


 {dango                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    865 / 1446

                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    1   IE    rw   PWM Timerx interrupt enable register.

                   0: Disable

                   1: Enable

    0   IFG   rc_w1 PWM Timerx interrupt status flag bit. Write 1 to clear the bit.

                   Count up: From CCR0 to 0, the bit set 1.

                   Continue counting: From 0xFFFF to 0, the bit set 1.

                   Count up/ count down: From 0x0001 to 0, the bit set 1.





                   0xFFFF






        Continuous Mode
Stop Mode Timer will count to 0xFFFF, and restart from
Timer stop. 0 and count to 0xFFFF.




    0xFFFF     0xFFFF

    CCR0       CCR0




        Up Mode Up/Down Mode
        Timer will count to CCR0, and restart from 0 Timer will count to CCR0, and then down count
        to  CCR0. to 0x0, and then up_count to CCR0 again.

        Figure 21-2 PWM Timer Mode

When MC is set to a non-zero value, the timer will start to work. When the PWM Timer working mode is

up-counting mode and up/down-counting mode, if PWMx_CCR0 is set to 0, timer x will stop counting.

If a non-zero value is written to PWMx_CCR0 again, the timer will restart counting.

In the up-counting mode, the value of CCR0 is changed during the counting period and the new value

of CCR0 is greater than the current count value, the timer will count to the new CCR0 and then return

to 0. If the new value of CCR0 is less than the current count value, the timer will reset the counter

value to 0 after counting one beat and restart counting.

In the up/down counting mode, during the up counting period, if the value of CCR0 is changed during

the counting period and the new value of CCR0 is greater than the current count value, the timer will

count to the new CCR0 and then count down to 0. If the new CCR0 is less than the current count

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 866 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

value, the timer will count down to 0 after counting one beat. During the down counting period, if the

value of CCR0 is changed during the counting period, the timer will continuously complete the down

counting process.

In continuous mode, user can dynamically set the value of CCR0~CCR2 to control the output

frequency. The figure below shows an example of this application.

    CCR0b                      |
0xFFFF EEE
    CCR1b                  CCR0c
  CCR1a|      ]  |{  CCR1d i|  i  CCR1e  CCR0d

CCR0a        CCR1c
    CCR1f






t0    t0    t0


t1  t1  t1  t1  t1


                         Figure 21-3 Continue Mode

In the figure above, CCR0a and CCR1a are the values of CCR0 and CCR1 at time Ta0 and Ta1,

respectively, and CCR0b and CCR1b are the values of CCR0 and CCR1 at time Tb0 and Tb1

(Tb0=Ta0+t0, Tb1=Ta1+t1).

The figure above shows that in the continuous mode, user can set CCIFG (bit 0 of CCTLx) and CCIE (bit

4 of CCTLx), and set CCR0 and CCR1, then an interrupt signal can be generated when TAR=CCR0a or

TAR=CCR1a. When TAR=CCR0b or TAR=CCR1b, another interrupt with a different cycle can be

generated. T0 and T1 are completely independent, so a PWM timer can set up to 3 completely

independent interrupts. In this mode, when the timer counts from 0xFFFF to 0x0, the IFG flag will be

set to 1.

21.4.2 BPWM_TAR Register

The BPWM_TAR is the 16 bits PWM timer x’s current count register.

Table 21-3 BPWM_TAR Register Description

Bit      Symbol   Type   Description

31:0     TAR      rw     PWM Timerx real time counting register.


{dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    867 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
21.4.3 BPWM_CCTLx Register

The BPWM_CCTLx is the 16 bits PWM timer x’s compare/capture control register 0.

Table 21-4 BPWM_CCTLx Register Description

Bit  Symbol  Type  Description

31:16 Rsvd   -     Reserved.

15:14 CM     rw    Capture edge selection.

                   0: Prohibit the capture function

                   1: Rising-edge capture

                   2: Falling-edge capture

                   3: Both-edge capture

13:11 Rsvd   -     Reserved.

10    SCCI   r     When TAR is equal to CCRx, the electricity level status of current channel input

                   pins.

9     OUTEN  rw    OUTx output enable control register.

                   0: OUTx output disable

                   1: OUTx output enable

8     CAP    rw    Capture/compare mode selection.

                   0: Compare mode. It is used to produce PWM output.

                   1: Capture mode. It is used to measure or count the external signal frequency.

7:5   OUTMOD rw    Output mode selection.

                   0: Constant mode, the value of OUTx output OUT bit.

                   1: Set mode. When TAR=CCRx(x=0~2), OUTx will set 1.

                   2: Reverse/reset mode. When TAR=CCRx(x=1~2), OUTx reverse; when

                   TAR=CCR0, OUTx reset is 0. This mode cannot be used in OUT0.

                   3: Set/ reset mode. When TAR=CCRx(x=1~2), OUTx set 1; when TAR=CCR0,

                   OUTx reset to 0. This mode cannot be used in OUT0.

                   4: Reverse mode. When TAR=CCRx(x=0~2), OUTx will be reversed.

                   5: Resert mode. When TAR=CCRx(x=0~2), OUTx will reset to 0.

                   6: Reserved/ set mode. When TAR=CCRx(x=1~2), OUTx was reversed; when

                   TAR=CCR0, OUTx will set 1. This mode cannot be used in OUT0.

                   7: Reset / set mode. When TAR=CCRx(x=1~2), OUTx reset is 0; when

                   TAR=CCR0, OUTxwill set 1. This mode cannot be used in OUT0.
& "dango               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 868 / 1446

                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

4   CCIE    rw   Capture /compare interrupt enable register.

3   Rsvd    -    Reserved.

2   OUT     rw   When OUTMOD set 0, the bit is used to control the output value of OUTx.

1   COV     rc_w1 Capture overflows flag bit. When CCIFG set 1 but happened another capture

                 event, the bit will be set 1.

0   CCIFG   rc_w1 Under the capture mode, when capture event happened, the bit will set 1, and

                 TAR will be locked in CCRx.

                 Under the comparsion mode, when TAR=CCRx, the bit will be set 1.

The BPWM_CCRx is the 16 bits PWM timer x’s compare/capture data register 0.

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
22 Digital-to-analog Converter (DAC)

22.1 Introduction


The DAC module is a 12-bit, voltage output digital-to-analog converter. The DAC can be configured in 8-

or 12-bit mode and may be used in conjunction with the DMA controller. In 12-bit mode, the data could

be left- or right-aligned. The DAC has two output channels, each with its own converter. In dual DAC

channel mode, conversions could be done independently or simultaneously when both channels are

grouped together for synchronous update operation. An input reference pin VREF+ (shared with ADC) is

available for better resolution.


    22.2 Features

    • Two DAC converters: one output channel each
    • Left or right data alignment in 12-bit mode
    • Synchronized update capability
    • Noise-wave generation
    • Triangular-wave generation
    • Dual DAC channel independent or simultaneous conversions
    • DMA capability for each channel
    • External triggers for conversion
    • Input voltage reference VREF+


        bis

    Tia
    Time


                       er
    — 7    logic  OMA request
        rE

converter



    por     Figure 22-1 DAC channel block diagram
            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    875 / 1446

         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    22.3  Functional Description

    22.3.1 DAC channel enable

    Each DAC channel can be powered on by setting its corresponding ENx bit in the DAC_CR register. The DAC

    channel is then enabled after a startup time twakeup.

    22.3.2 DAC output buffer enable

    The DAC integrates two output buffers that can be used to reduce the output impedance, and to drive

    external loads directly without having to add an external operational amplifier. Each DAC channel output

    buffer can be enabled and disabled using the corresponding BOFFx bit in the DAC_CR register.

    22.3.3 DAC data format

    Depending on the selected configuration mode, the data has to be written in the specified register as

    described below:

    • Single DAC channelx, there are three possibilities:
     – 8-bit right alignment: user has to load data into DAC_DHR8Rx [7:0] bits (stored into DHRx[11:4]

     bits)

     – 12-bit left alignment: user has to load data into DAC_DHR12Lx [15:4] bits (stored into DHRx[11:0]

     bits)

     – 12-bit right alignment: user has to load data into DAC_DHR12Rx [11:0] bits (stored into

     DHRx[11:0] bits)

    Depending on the loaded DAC_DHRyyyx register, the data written by the user will be shifted and stored

    into the DAC_DHRx (Data Holding registers, these are internal non-memory-mapped registers). The

    DHRx register will then be loaded into the DAC_DORx register either automatically, by software trigger

    or by an external event trigger.

         31      24      15      7      0

                                                         8-bit right aligned

                                                         12-bit left aligned

                                                         12-bit right aligned

         Figure 22-2 Data registers in single DAC channel mode



• Dual DAC channels, there are three possibilities:
– 8-bit right alignment: Data for DAC channel1 to be loaded into DAC_DHR8RD [7:0] bits (stored into
        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 876 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

DHR1[11:4] bits) and data for DAC channel2 to be loaded into DAC_DHR8RD [15:8] bits (stored into

DHR2[11:4] bits)

– 12-bit left alignment: Data for DAC channel1 to be loaded into DAC_DHR12LD [15:4] bits (stored into

DHR1[11:0] bits) and data for DAC channel2 to be loaded into DAC_DHR12LD [31:20] bits (stored into

DHR2[11:0] bits)

– 12-bit right alignment: Data for DAC channel1 to be loaded into DAC_DHR12RD [11:0] bits (stored into

DHR1[11:0] bits) and data for DAC channel2 to be loaded into DAC_DHR12RD [27:16] bits (stored into

DHR2[11:0] bits)

Depending on the loaded DAC_DHRyyyD register, the data written by the user will be shifted and stored

into the DHR1 and DHR2 (Data Holding registers, these are internal nonmemory-mapped registers). The

DHR1 and DHR2 registers will then be loaded into the DOR1 and DOR2 registers, respectively, either

automatically, by software trigger or by an external event trigger.


    31  24 15  7  0

                   8-bit right aligned

                   12-bit left aligned

                   12-bit right aligned


        Figure 22-3 Data registers in dual DAC channel mode

22.3.4 DAC Conversion

The DAC_DORx cannot be written directly and any data transfer to the DAC channelx must be performed

by loading the DAC_DHRx register (write on DAC_DHR8Rx, DAC_DHR12Lx, DAC_DHR12Rx,

DAC_DHR8RD, DAC_DHR12LD or DAC_DHR12LD).

Data stored into the DAC_DHRx register are automatically transferred to the DAC_DORx register after one

APB1 clock cycle, if no hardware trigger is selected (TENx bit in DAC_CR register is reset). However, when

a hardware trigger is selected (TENx bit in DAC_CR register is set) and a trigger occurs, the transfer is

performed three APB1 clock cycles later. When DAC_DORx is loaded with the DAC_DHRx contents, the

analog output voltage becomes available after a time of tSETTLING that depends on the power supply

voltage and the analog output load.

22.3.5 DAC Output Voltage

Digital inputs are converted to output voltages on a linear conversion between 0 and VREF+. The analog

output voltages on each DAC channel pin are determined by the equation

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 877 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
22.3.6 DAC trigger selection

If the TENx control bit is set, conversion can then be triggered by an external event (timer counter,

external interrupt line). The TSELx[2:0] control bits determine which one, out of 8 possible events, will

trigger conversion.

Each time a DAC interface detects a rising edge on the selected timer TRGO output, or on the selected

external interrupt, the last data stored into the DAC_DHRx register is transferred into the DAC_DORx

register. The DAC_DORx register is updated three APB1 cycles after the trigger occurs.

If the software trigger is selected, the conversion starts once the SWTRIG bit is set. SWTRIG is reset by

hardware once the DAC_DORx register has been loaded with the DAC_DHRx register contents.

22.3.7 DMA request

DAC channel has a DMA capability. Two DMA channels are used to service DAC channel DMA requests.

A DAC DMA request is generated when an external trigger (but not a software trigger) occurs while the

DMAENx bit is set. The value of the DAC_DHRx register is then transferred to the DAC_DORx register.

In dual mode, if both DMAENx bits are set, two DMA requests are generated. If only one DMA request is

needed, you should set only the corresponding DMAENx bit. In this way, the application can manage both

DAC channels in dual mode by using one DMA request and a unique DMA channel.

The DAC DMA request is not queued so that if a second external trigger arrives before the

acknowledgement of the last request, then the new request will not be serviced and no error is reported.

22.3.8 Noise Generation

In order to generate a variable-amplitude pseudonoise, a Linear Feedback Shift register is available. The

DAC noise generation is selected by setting WAVEx[1:0] to “01”. The preloaded value in the LFSR is 0xAAA.

This register is updated, three APB1 clock cycles after each trigger event, following a specific calculation

algorithm.

        XOR




    x2




    12




Figure 22-4 DAC LFSR register calculation algorithm

         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    878 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

The LFSR value, that may be masked partially or totally by means of the MAMPx[3:0] bits in the DAC_CR

register, is added up to the DAC_DHRx contents without overflow and this value is then stored into the

DAC_DORx register.

If LFSR is 0x0000, a ‘1’ is injected into it (antilock-up mechanism).

It is possible to reset LFSR wave generation by resetting the WAVEx[1:0] bits.

Note: DAC trigger must be enabled for noise generation, by setting the TENx bit in the DAC_- CR register.

22.3.9 Triangle-wave Generation

It is possible to add a small-amplitude triangular waveform on a DC or slowly varying signal. DAC

triangle-wave generation is selected by setting WAVEx[1:0] to “10”. The amplitude is configured through

the MAMPx[3:0] bits in the DAC_CR register. An internal triangle counter is incremented three APB1 clock

cycles after each trigger event. The value of this counter is then added to the DAC_DHRx register without

overflow and the sum is stored into the DAC_DORx register. The triangle counter is incremented while it

is less than the maximum amplitude defined by the MAMPx[3:0] bits. Once the configured amplitude is

reached, the counter is decremented down to 0, then incremented again and so on.

It is possible to reset triangle wave generation by resetting WAVEx[1:0] bits.

Note: DAC trigger must be enabled for noise generation, by setting the TENx bit in the DAC_CR register.

MAMPx[3:0] bits must be configured before enabling the DAC, otherwise they cannot be changed.


   22.4 Dual DAC channel conversion


To efficiently use the bus bandwidth in applications that require the two DAC channels at the same time,

three dual registers are implemented: DHR8RD, DHR12RD and DHR12LD. A unique register access is

then required to drive both DAC channels at the same time.

Many possible conversion modes are possible using the two DAC channels and these dual registers. All

the conversion modes can nevertheless be obtained using separate DHRx registers if needed.

All modes are described in the paragraphs below.

22.4.1 Independent trigger without wave generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure different trigger sources by setting different values in the TSEL1[2:0] and TSEL2[2:0] bits
• Load the dual DAC channel data into the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD, DAC_DHR12LD or

DAC_DHR8RD)
{dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 879 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

When a DAC channel1 trigger arrives, the DHR1 register is transferred into DAC_DOR1 (three APB1 clock

cycles later).

When a DAC channel2 trigger arrives, the DHR2 register is transferred into DAC_DOR2 (three APB1 clock

cycles later).

22.4.2 Independent trigger with same LFSR generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure different trigger sources by setting different values in the TSEL1[2:0] and TSEL2[2:0] bits
• Configure the two DAC channel WAVEx[1:0] bits as “01” and the same LFSR mask value in the

MAMPx[3:0] bits

• Load the dual DAC channel data into the desired DHR register (DHR12RD, DHR12LD or DHR8RD)

When a DAC channel1 trigger arrives, the LFSR1 counter, with the same mask, is added to the DHR1

register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR1 (three APB1 clock cycles later). Then the LFSR1

counter is updated.

When a DAC channel2 trigger arrives, the LFSR2 counter, with the same mask, is added to the DHR2

register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR2 (three APB1 clock cycles later). Then the LFSR2

counter is updated.

22.4.3 Independent trigger with different LFSR generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure different trigger sources by setting different values in the TSEL1[2:0] and TSEL2[2:0] bits
• Configure the two DAC channel WAVEx[1:0] bits as “01” and set different LFSR masks values in the

MAMP1[3:0] and MAMP2[3:0] bits

• Load the dual DAC channel data into the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD, DAC_DHR12LD or

DAC_DHR8RD)

When a DAC channel1 trigger arrives, the LFSR1 counter, with the mask configured by MAMP1[3:0], is

added to the DHR1 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR1 (three APB1 clock cycles later).

Then the LFSR1 counter is updated.

When a DAC channel2 trigger arrives, the LFSR2 counter, with the mask configured by MAMP2[3:0], is

added to the DHR2 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR2 (three APB1 clock cycles later).

Then the LFSR2 counter is updated.


    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    880 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
22.4.4 Independent trigger with same triangle generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure different trigger sources by setting different values in the TSEL1[2:0] and TSEL2[2:0] bits
• Configure the two DAC channel WAVEx[1:0] bits as “1x” and the same maximum amplitude value in the

MAMPx[3:0] bitsLoad the dual DAC channel data into the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD,

• Load the dual DAC channel data into the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD, DAC_DHR12LD or

DAC_DHR8RD)

When a DAC channel1 trigger arrives, the DAC channel1 triangle counter, with the same triangle

amplitude, is added to the DHR1 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR1 (three APB1 clock

cycles later). The DAC channel1 triangle counter is then updated.

When a DAC channel2 trigger arrives, the DAC channel2 triangle counter, with the same triangle

amplitude, is added to the DHR2 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR2 (three APB1 clock

cycles later). The DAC channel2 triangle counter is then updated.

22.4.5 Independent trigger with different triangle
generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure different trigger sources by setting different values in the TSEL1[2:0] and TSEL2[2:0] bits
• Configure the two DAC channel WAVEx[1:0] bits as “1x” and set different maximum amplitude values in

the MAMP1[3:0] and MAMP2[3:0] bits

• Load the dual DAC channel data into the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD, DAC_DHR12LD or

DAC_DHR8RD)

When a DAC channel1 trigger arrives, the DAC channel1 triangle counter, with a triangle amplitude

configured by MAMP1[3:0], is added to the DHR1 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR1

(three APB1 clock cycles later). The DAC channel1 triangle counter is then updated.

When a DAC channel2 trigger arrives, the DAC channel2 triangle counter, with a triangle amplitude

configured by MAMP2[3:0], is added to the DHR2 register part and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR2

(three APB1 clock cycles later). The DAC channel2 triangle counter is then updated.

22.4.6 Simulaneous software start

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 881 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
• Load the dual DAC channel data to the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD, DAC_DHR12LD or

DAC_DHR8RD)

In this configuration, one APB1 clock cycle later, the DHR1 and DHR2 registers are transferred into

DAC_DOR1 and DAC_DOR2, respectively.

22.4.7 Simultaneous trigger without wave generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure the same trigger source for both DAC channels by setting the same value in the TSEL1[2:0]

and TSEL2[2:0] bits

• Load the dual DAC channel data to the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD, DAC_DHR12LD or

DAC_DHR8RD)

When a trigger arrives, the DHR1 and DHR2 registers are transferred into DAC_DOR1 and DAC_DOR2,

respectively (after three APB1 clock cycles).

22.4.8 Simultaneous trigger with same LFSR generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure the same trigger source for both DAC channels by setting the same value in the TSEL1[2:0]

and TSEL2[2:0] bits

• Configure the two DAC channel WAVEx[1:0] bits as “01” and the same LFSR mask value in the

MAMPx[3:0] bits

• Load the dual DAC channel data to the desired DHR register (DHR12RD, DHR12LD or DHR8RD)

When a trigger arrives, the LFSR1 counter, with the same mask, is added to the DHR1 register and the

sum is transferred into DAC_DOR1 (three APB1 clock cycles later). The LFSR1 counter is then updated.

At the same time, the LFSR2 counter, with the same mask, is added to the DHR2 register and the sum

is transferred into DAC_DOR2 (three APB1 clock cycles later). The LFSR2 counter is then updated.

22.4.9 Simultaneous trigger with different LFSR
generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure the same trigger source for both DAC channels by setting the same value in the TSEL1[2:0]

and TSEL2[2:0] bits
{dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 882 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
• Configure the two DAC channel WAVEx[1:0] bits as “01” and set different LFSR masks values using

the MAMP1[3:0] and MAMP2[3:0] bits

• Load the dual DAC channel data into the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD, DAC_- DHR12LD or

DAC_DHR8RD)

When a trigger arrives, the LFSR1 counter, with the mask configured by MAMP1[3:0], is added to the DHR1

register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR1 (three APB1 clock cycles later). The LFSR1 counter is

then updated. At the same time, the LFSR2 counter, with the mask configured by MAMP2[3:0], is added

to the DHR2 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR2 (three APB1 clock cycles later). The

LFSR2 counter is then updated.

22.4.10 Simultaneous trigger with same triangle
generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure the same trigger source for both DAC channels by setting the same value in the TSEL1[2:0]

and TSEL2[2:0] bits

• Configure the two DAC channel WAVEx[1:0] bits as “1x” and the same maximum amplitude value using

the MAMPx[3:0] bits

• Load the dual DAC channel data into the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD, DAC_DHR12LD or

DAC_DHR8RD)

When a trigger arrives, the DAC channel1 triangle counter, with the same triangle amplitude, is added

to the DHR1 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR1 (three APB1 clock cycles later). The DAC

channel1 triangle counter is then updated.

At the same time, the DAC channel2 triangle counter, with the same triangle amplitude, is added to the

DHR2 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR2 (three APB1 clock cycles later). The DAC

channel2 triangle counter is then updated.

22.4.11 Simultaneous trigger with different triangle
generation

To configure the DAC in this conversion mode, the following sequence is required:

• Set the two DAC channel trigger enable bits TEN1 and TEN2
• Configure the same trigger source for both DAC channels by setting the same value in the TSEL1[2:0]

and TSEL2[2:0] bits

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 883 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
• Configure the two DAC channel WAVEx[1:0] bits as “1x” and set different maximum amplitude values in

the MAMP1[3:0] and MAMP2[3:0] bits

• Load the dual DAC channel data into the desired DHR register (DAC_DHR12RD, DAC_DHR12LD or

DAC_DHR8RD)

When a trigger arrives, the DAC channel1 triangle counter, with a triangle amplitude configured by

MAMP1[3:0], is added to the DHR1 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR1 (three APB1 clock

cycles later). Then the DAC channel1 triangle counter is updated.

At the same time, the DAC channel2 triangle counter, with a triangle amplitude configured by

MAMP2[3:0], is added to the DHR2 register and the sum is transferred into DAC_DOR2 (three APB1 clock

cycles later). Then the DAC channel2 triangle counter is updated.


   22.5 DAC Registers


    Table 22-1 DAC Registers Map

    Offset: 0x0000

    Bit Symbol   TypeDescription

    31:29Rsvd    -    Reserved

    28  DMAEN2rw      DAC channel 2 DMA enable

                      This bit is set and cleared by software.

                      0: DAC channel 2 DMA mode disabled

                      1: DAC channel 2 DMA mode enabled

27:24MAMP2 rw DAC channel 2 mask/ amplitude selector

                      These bits are written by software to select mask in wave generation mode or

                      amplitude in triangle generation mode.

                      0000: Unmask bit0 of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 1

                      0001: Unmask bits[1:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 3

                      0010: Unmask bits[2:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 7

                      0011: Unmask bits[3:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 15

                      0100: Unmask bits[4:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 31

                      0101: Unmask bits[5:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 63

                      0110: Unmask bits[6:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 127

                      0111: Unmask bits[7:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 255

                      1000: Unmask bits[8:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 511

                      1001: Unmask bits[9:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 1023

                      1010: Unmask bits[10:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 2047

                      1011: Unmask bits[11:0] of LFSR/ triangle amplitude equal to 4095

23:22WAVE2 rw DAC channel 2 noise/ triangle wave generation enable

                      These bits are set/ reset by software.

                      00: wave generation disabled

                      01: Noise wave generation enabled

                      1x: Triangle wave generation enabled

                      Note: only used if bit TEN2 = 1 (DAC channel2 trigger enabled)

    21:19TSEL2   rw   DAC channel 2 trigger selection

                      010: ETMR0 TRGO event

                     111: Software trigger

    18  TEN2   rw    DAC channel 2 trigger enable

                     This bit set and cleared by software to enable/ disable DAC channel 2 trigger

                     0: DAC channel 2 trigger disabled and data written into DAC_DHRx register is

                     transferred one APB1 clock cycle later to the DAC_DOR2 register.

23 Cryptographic Co-processor (COP)

23.1 Introduction


    The Cryptographic Accelerator engine provides acceleration of asymmetric cryptographic algorithms.

    Supported crypto functions are implemented in the SDK.


    23.2 Features


    In conventional MCU without Cryptographic Co-processor, the ARM CM33 will perform the computation

    work. The Cryptographic Co-processor will free up the ARM CM33 to do other tasks while the Co-processor

    does the computation.

    Acceleration of RSA, DH and ECC over GF (p) operations, based on the Montgomery method for fast

    modular multiplications. More specifically:

    • RSA modular exponentiation

    • ECC scalar multiplication, point on curve check.

    • ECDSA signature generation and verification.


    23.3 Operation


    The Co-processor use 16kB shared SRAM size for scratch pad.

    The Co-processor consist of:

    • 4 x 32-bit (ABCD) Data register, feeding the two multipliers.

    • A mask register used for creating and XOR mask for unmasking ABCD and masking output for side

    channel protection.

    • 1 x 64-bit multi plies

    • 1 x 128-bit Adders

    • 1 x CIC


    23.4 CIC Coprocessor Instruction Definition


    This section define the function of CIC filter coprocessor instruction.



    fi    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    893 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
23.4.1 Overview

Feature List:

• Two instruction, the cic integrate and the cic comb.
• Support 1 bit or 32 bit input for cic integrate.
• 64 bit input for cic comb
• Support 2, 3, 4, or 5 stage (order).
• Support decimation rate configure of cic integrate.
• Decimation offset configure of cic integrate.
• Input Length is depend on the configured Result length.

For intergrate: Input_length = Result_length * decimation.

For Comb: Input_length = Result_length.

                    1 bit input        32 bit input
                 (x is the value of

Table 23-1 COP Registers Map

Register Name         Offset      Type  Reset Value               Description

COP_CFG               0x0000      rw    0x0000_0000               COP configure register

23.4.3.1       COP_CFG

Offset: 0x0000

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 23-2 COP_CFG Register Description

Bit         Symbol          Type      Description

31:16       CDOFFSET        rw        The offset of CD, unit in DW(32-bit)

15:0        ABOFFSET        rw        The offset of AB, unit in DW(32-bit)

fi                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        895 / 1446

                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
23.4.3.2           COP_CTRL

Offset: 0x0004

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 23-3 COP_CTRL Register Description

Bit  Symbol TypeDescription

31:16RESOFFSETrw    The offset of result, unit in DW(32-bit)

15:8 MODE      rw   Operation mode:

                    0x01: Mul6464NoSum, Walking 1 or more of J loop, doing r[j]=ab*cd[j]

    0x02 : Mul6464Sum, Walking 1 or more of J loop, doing c,r[j]=r[j]+ab*cd[j]

                    0x03 : Mul6464FullSum, Walking 1 or more of J loop, doing

                    c,r[j]=r[j]+ab*cd[j]+c[j-1]

                    0x 08 : ADD64, Walking 1 or more of J loop, doing c[j], r[j] = r[j] + ab + c[j-1]

                    0x09: SUB64, Walking 1 or more of J loop, doing r[j] = r[j] -ab. If ab is larger,

                    final borrow is implicit, but carry bit set if borrowed.

                    0x14 : COPY Walking 1 or more of J loop, copy from AB offset to RESOFF using

                    64 bits at a time

                    0xF0: CIC_Integrate

                    0xF1: CIC_CIC_Comb.

7:0  ITER      rw   Iteration loop counter.

                    0: One cycle only does not iterate.

                    loop_num: Iterate to the number defined in ITER.

23.4.3.3     COP_CIC_CFG

Offset: 0x0008

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 23-4 COP_CIC_CFG Register Description

Bit  Symbol TypeDescription

31:30Rsvd   - Reserved







{dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    896 / 1446

                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

29:27CN     rw    Number of channels.

                  Min value: 0

                  Max value: 7

                  Just for 1 bit input type of cic integrate. It determines where to get the 32*1 bits

                  from SRAM to calculate the integration.

                  If Channel Number is x,

                  the first 32 inputs is at CDOFFSET+0*4*(x+1), the second 32 inputs is at

                  CDOFFSET+1*4*(x+1),

                  the third 32 inputs is at CDOFFSET+2*4*(x+1),etc.

26:17RL      rw   The length of result, (1, 2, 3, 4.)

                  Min value: 1

                  Max value: 1023

                  for integrate : input length = result length * decimation rate;

                  for comb : input length = result length;

16    IT     rw   data type of Integrate input

                  0: 1bit

                  1: 32bit

15:6  DO     rw   Decimation offset of integrate instruction.

                  Its value should be less than the decimation rate

5:2   DR     rw   Decimation rate of integrate instruction x: decimation rate is power(2,x)

                  0: Minimum decimation rate is 1

                  1: Decimation rate is 2

                  …

                  9: Decimation rate is 512

                  10: Maximum decimation rate is 1024

1:0   Order  rw   The stage of the integrate or comb

                  0: 2

                  1: 3

                  2: 4

                  3: 5




{dango            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    897 / 1446

                                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

24 Digital Camera Interface (DCMI)

24.1 Overview


    DCMI is a digital camera synchronous parallel interface able to receive 8/10/12/14 bit data flow from

    external CMOS camera module. It supports different data formats and compressed data (JPEG).


    24.2     Features

    • Supports 8/10/12/14 bit DVP interface.
    • Supports hardware or embedded synchronization.
    • Supports continuous or single frame mode.
    • Supports crop feature.
    • Supports data DMA transfer.

    24.3                         Function Description

    24.3.1 Capture Data

    8-bit data

    When the captured data width is programmed to 8-bit, the camera interface take 4 captured data to get

    one 32-bit word. When one word is captured or the line end (non-JPEG mode) or the frame end is

    detected, one word data will be loaded to FIFO.

    Data storage format is as shown in the table:

                                 Bit:      | 23:16     | 15:8 | 7:0
                                 Data:  | Data4 | Data3 | Data2 | Datal

    10-bit data

    When the captured data width is programmed to 10-bit, the camera interface take 2 captured data to

    get one 32-bit word. When one word is captured or the line end (non-JPEG mode) or the frame end is

    detected, one word data will be loaded to FIFO.

    Data storage format is as shown in the table:

                                  Bit:  31:26 | 25:16    | 15:10 | 9:0
                                  Data: | 6’h0   | Data2 | 6’h0  | Datal

    12-bit data
    fi        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        900 / 1446

                                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

When the captured data width is programmed to 12-bit, the camera interface take 2 captured data to

get one 32-bit word. When one word is captured or the line end (non-JPEG mode) or the frame end is

detected, one word data will be loaded to FIFO.

Data storage format is as shown in the table:

    |                       Bit:   |  31:28    |  27:16  |     15:12 |  11:0 |
                            Data:  | 4h0         | Data2    | 4'h0   | Datal

14-bit data

When the captured data width is programmed to 14-bit, the camera interface take 2 captured data to

get one 32-bit word. When one word is captured or the line end (non-JPEG mode) or the frame end is

detected, one word data will be loaded to FIFO.

Data storage format is as shown in the table:

    |                       Bit:   |  31:30     |  29:16    |  15:14    |  13:0 |
                            Data:  | 2’h0    | Data2        | 2’h0   | Datal

24.3.2 Hardware synchronization mode

In hardware synchronization mode ,DCMI uses Vsync and Hsync signals to capture data signals, when

Vsync and Hsync are both invalid, DCMI receives the transferd data, one line transfer is finished when

Hsync is valid, and one frame transfer is finished when Vsync is valid. The polarity of Vsync /Hsync and

pixel clock is programmable.

24.3.3 JPEG mode

When JPEG mode is enable, the crop feature and embedded code synchronization are forbidden, and

Hsync is only used to enable data, it will not trig the line end.

24.3.4 Embedded code synchronization mode

In embed codes synchronization mode, the Vsync and Hsync function are replaced by the specified codes

in data flow. This mode is supported only in 8-bit data capture mode.

The DCMI_ESC and DCMI_ESC_MASK registers are used to set the feature code. When FF00_00XX is

detected in the data flow and XX is equal to the programmed feature code, trig the synchronization, start

or stop the data captured.

DCMI_ESC_MASK register is used to mask same bits in feature code, the synchronization is trigged only

when the unmask bits of the data are equal to the value in DCMI_ESC register. For example, program

DCMI_ES.FS to AA, and DSMI_ESC_MASK.FSM is F0, the frame synchronization is trigged after the

occurrence of the FF00_00AX(X=0~F).

    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                                          901 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
24.3.5 Window crop

Limit the capture range in a window area, by setting register DCMI_CROP_START and DCMI_CROP_SIZE.

24.3.6 Single frame capture

In single frame capture mode, DCMI close capture feature automatically after one frame capture is

finished.


24.4 DCMI Registers


Table 24-1 DCMI Registers Map

Name                   Offset  Type  Reset Value   Description

DCMI_CONTROL           0x00    rw    0x0000_0000   DCMI control register

DCMI_STATUS            0x04    r     0x0000_0003   DCMI status register

DCMI_INTRAW            0x08    r     0x0000_0000   DCMI raw interrupt register

    24.5    DCMI clock


DCMI has two clock domain, hclk from chip system and pixel clock from external camera.

The pixel clock will be synchronized in hclk domain, and the posedge of pixel clock is detected after

synchronization. The posedge signal is used to indicate the captured data is stable.

The pixel clock period must be larger than 2.5 hclk period.










    fi                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    908 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
25 Analog-to-digital Converter (ADC)

25.1 Introduction


The 12-bit ADC is a successive approximation analog-to-digital converter. A/D conversion of the various

channels can be perform in single, continuous, scan or discontinuous mode. The result of the ADC is

stored in a left-aligned or right-aligned 16-bit data register.

The analog watchdog feature allows the application to detect if the input voltage goes outside the user-

defined high or low thresholds.


    25.2     Main Features

    • 12-bit resolution

    • Interrupt generation at End of Conversion, End of Injected conversion and Analog watchdog event

    • Single and continuous conversion

    • Scan mode for automatic conversion of channel 0 to channel n

    • Self-calibration

    • Data alignment with in-built data coherency

    • Channel by channel programmable sampling delay time

    • External trigger option for both regular and injected conversion

    • Discontinuous mode

    • Dual mode

    • ADC conversion time : 1us at 48MHz（1.17 us at 72MHz)

    • ADC input range:

    • 0-VREFP(SDIF =0);0-2*VREFP(SDIF =1);

    • DMA request generation during regular channel conversion




    {dango  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    909 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
 25.3 Function description

25.3.1 ADC on-off control

The ADC can be power-on by setting the ADON bit in the ADC_CTRL2 register. When the ADON bit is set

for the first time, it wakes up the ADC from Power Down mode.

The ADC needs about 24 ADC clock cycles to power up.

The conversion can be stopped, and the ADC put in power down mode by resetting the ADON bit.

Note: After ADC is powered up, setting the ADON again can not trigger ADC conversion any longer in this

version.

25.3.2 Channel selection

There are 16 multiplexed channels. It is possible to organize the conversions in two groups: regular and

injected. A group consists of a sequence of conversions which can be done on any channel and in any

order.

• The regular group is composed of up to 16 conversions. The regular channels and their order in the

conversion sequence must be selected in the ADC_RSQx registers. The total number of conversions in the

regular group must be written in the L[3:0] bits in the ADC_RSQ1 register.

• The injected group is composed of up to 4 conversions. The injected channels and their order in the

conversion sequence must be selected in the ADC_JSQ register. The total number of conversions in the

injected group must be written in the JL[1:0] bits in the ADC_JSQ register. When the JL[1:0] =2’b0,

the channel configure in JSQ4 will be converted when triggered. When JL[1:0] = 2’b1, the converted

sequence of injected channel is JSQ3,JSQ4.

Note: If you want to change the ADC_RSQx or ADC_JSQ registers, you should make sure there is no

conversion going on, otherwise, the conversion results maybe not reliable.

25.3.3 Single conversion mode

In single conversion mode, the ADC does one conversion. This mode is started either by setting the

ADON bit in the ADC_CTRL2 register (for a regular channel only) or by external trigger (for a regular or

injected channel), while the CON bit is 0.

Once the conversion of the selected channel is completed:

• If a regular channel was converted:

  – The converted data is stored in the 16-bit ADC_RDOR register

& "dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 910 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
 – The EC flag is set

 – An interrupt is generated if the ECIE is set

• If an injected channel was converted:

 – The converted data is stored in the 16-bit ADC_DRJx register

 – The JEC flag is set

 – An interrupt is generated if the JECIE bit is set.

 The ADC is then stopped.

25.3.4 Continuous conversion mode

In continuous conversion mode, ADC starts another conversion as soon as it finished one. This mode is

started either by external trigger or by setting the ADON bit in the ADC_CTRL2 register, while the CON

bit is 1.

After each conversion:

• If a regular channel was converted:

 – The converted data is stored in the 16-bit ADC_RDOR register

 – The EC flag is set

 – An interrupt is generated if the ECIE is set

• If an injected channel was converted:

 – The converted data is stored in the 16-bit ADC_DRJx register

 – The JEC flag is set

 – An interrupt is generated if the JECIE bit is set.

Note:

In CON mode, the Injected group will also be converted only one time. But the regular group will be

converted continuously.

25.3.5 Analog watchdog

 The AWD analog watchdog status bit is set if the analog voltage converted by the ADC is below a low

threshold or above a high threshold. These thresholds are programmed in the 12 least significant bits of

the ADC_WHTR and ADC_WLTR 16-bit registers. An interrupt can be enabled by using the AWDIE bit in

& "dango        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                        911 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

the ADC_CTRL1 register.

The threshold value is independent of the alignment selected by the DALIGN bit in the ADC_CTRL2

register. The comparison is done before the alignment.

The analog watchdog can be enabled on one or more channels by configuring the ADC_CTRL1 register

as follow.

The single channel mode is selected by AWDCH[4:0] bits.

Table 25-1 shows how the ADC_CR1 register should be configured to enable the analog

watchdog on one or more channels.

                   Analog voltage
                 Higher threshold                      HTR
                  Lower threshold    Guarded area      LTR


    Figure 25-1 Analog watchdog’s guarded area

Table 25-1 Analog watchdog channel selection

Channels to be guarded by analog watchdog            AWDSGE   AWDEN   JAWDEN

None                                                 x        0       0

All injected channels                                0        0       1

All regular channels                                 0        1       0

All regular and injected channels                    0        1       1

Single injected channel                              1        0       1

Single regular channel                               1        1       0

Single injected or regular channel                   1        1       1

25.3.6 Scan mode

This mode is used to scan a group of analog channels.

Scan mode can be selected by setting the SCAN bit in the ADC_CTRL1 register. Once this bit is set, ADC

scans all the channels selected in the ADC_RSQx registers (for regular channels) or in the ADC_JSQ (for

injected channels). A single conversion is performed for each channel of the group. After each end of

conversion, the next channel of group is converted automatically. If the CON bit is set, conversion does


fis    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                              912 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

not stop at the last selected group channel but continues again from the first selected group channel.

When using scan mode, DMA bit must be set and the direct memory access controller is used to transfer

the converted data of regular channels to SRAM after each update of the ADC_RDOR register.

The injected channel converted data is always stored in the ADC_JDORx registers.

25.3.7 Injected channel management

25.3.7.1 Triggered injection

To use triggered injection, the JAUT bit must be cleared in the ADC_CTRL1 register.

1. Start conversion of a group of regular channels either by external trigger or by setting the ADON bit

in the ADC_CTRL2 register.

2. If an external injected trigger occurs during the regular group channel conversion, the current

conversion (will be reset when sampling delay has not finished or will be finished when conversion has

started) and the injected channel sequence is converted in Scan once mode or single mode or

discontinuous mode.

3. Then, the regular group channel conversion is resumed from the last interrupted regular con- version.

If a regular event occurs during an injected conversion, it does not interrupt it but the regular sequence

is executed at the end of the injected sequence.

25.3.7.2 Auto-injection

If the JAUT bit is set, then the injected group channels are automatically converted after the regular group

channels. This can be used to convert a sequence of up to 20 conversions programmed in the ADC_RSQx

and ADC_JSQ registers.

In this mode external trigger on injected channels must be disabled.

If the CONT bit is also set in addition to the JAUT bit, regular channels followed by injected channels are

continuously converted.



    25.3.8 Discontinuous mode

    25.3.8.1    Regular group

    This mode is enabled by setting the RDISEN bit in the ADC_CTRL1 register. It can be used to convert a short

    sequence of n conversions (n <=8) which is a part of the sequence of the conversions selected in the
    & "dango    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    913 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

ADC_RSQx registers. The value of n is specified by writing to the DISN[2:0] bits in the ADC_CTRL1

register.

When an external trigger occurs, it starts the next n conversions selected in the ADC_RSQx registers until

all the conversions in the sequence are done. The total sequence length is defined by the L[3:0] bits in the

ADC_RSQ1 register.

Example:

  n=3, channels to be converted =0,1,2,3,6,7,9,10

  first trigger: sequence converted 0,1,2. An EC event is generated at each conversion.

  second trigger: sequence converted 3,6,7. An EC event is generated at each conversion.

  third trigger: sequence converted 9,10. An EC event is generated at each conversion.

  fourth trigger: sequence converted 0,1,2. An EC event is generated at each conversion.

25.3.8.2 Injected group

This mode is enabled by setting the JDISEN bit in the ADC_CTRL1 register. It can be used to convert the

sequence selected in the ADC_JSQ register, channel by channel, after an external trigger event.

When an external trigger occurs, it starts the next channel conversions selected in the ADC_JSQ registers

until all the conversions in the sequence are done. The total sequence length is defined by the JL[1:0] bits

in the ADC_JSQ register. Note:

It is not possible to use both auto-injected and discontinuous modes simulantaneously.

The user must avoid setting discontinuous mode for both regular and injected groups together.

Discontinuous mode must be enabled only for one group conversion.


   25.4 Calibration


The offset calibration function can compensate inner circuit offset of the sar adc.

Firstly inner offset could refected at output B[11:0] by setting the control signal DISH high and SDIF

high, which makes VIP and VIN short together internally. (B[11:0] is the conversion result of the sar adc )

The difference between output result of B[11:0] and 12’h800 equals to inner circuit offset. During the

calibration period, OFFSET[5:0] must be set all 0, and all the other working condition, setting, and time

sequences are the same with normal ADC conversion.

During normal ADC conversion, DISH should be set back to low, and the input configuration OFFSET[5:0],

in complement code, [5] for sign bit, [4] for MSB, [0] for LSB, could be set according to the offset value

read out during calibration.
        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 914 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

For example, with 17lsb inner circuit offset, B[11:0] would be 12’h811 while DISH=1,SDIF=1,and

OFFSET[5:0] =0, then OFFSET[5:0] could be set as 6’h2f, which means to minus 17lsb during normal

ADC conversion. If the inner circuit offset is -26lsb, B[11:0] would be 12’h7e6 while DISH=1,SDIF=1,and

OFFSET[5:0] =0, then OFFSET[5:0] could be set as 6’h1a, which means to plus 26lsb during normal work.

The ADC controller support auto calibration and manually calibration.

Auto calibration is started by setting the CAL bit in the ADC_CTRL2 register. Once calibration is over, the

CAL bit is reset by hardware and normal conversion can be performed. The calibration codes are stored

in the ADC_CFG (OFFSET[5:0]) as soon as the calibration phase ends.

For manually calibration,

  (1) Configure DISH=1,SDIF=1 and OFFSET[5:0]=0 through ADC_CFG register,

  (2) Start a conversion and get the 12 bit result from the 16 bit result register,

  (3) Calculate the offset value,

  (4) Configure the OFFSET[5:0] and clear the DISH (and SDIF if needed).


   25.5 Data alignment


DALIGN bit in the ADC_CTRL2 register selects the alignment of data stored after conversion. Data can be

left or right aligned.

The injected group channels converted data value is decreased by the user-defined offset written in the

ADC_JOFSx registers so the result can ve a negative value. The SEXT bit is the extended sign value.

For regular group channels no offset is subtracted so only twelve bits are significant.










    {dango  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    915 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
