    Figure 25-2 Alignment of data


   25.6 Channel-by-channel programmable sample
   delay time


    ADC samples the input voltage for a number of ADC_CLK cycles which can be modified using the SMP[2:0]

    bits in the ADC_SMPT1 and ADC_SMPT2 registers. Each channel can be sampled with a different sample

    delay time.

    The totla conversion time is calculated as follow:

    Tconv = Sampling time + 12.5 cycles


    25.7 Conversion on external trigger


Conversion can be triggered by an external event (e.g. timer capture, EXTI line). If the EXTTRIG control

bit is set, then external events are able to trigger a conversion. The EXSEL[2:0] and JEXSEL[2:0] control

bits allow the application to select decide which out of 8 possible events can trigger conversion for the

regular and injected groups.

Note:

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 916 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

When an external trigger is selected for ADC regular or injected conversion, only the rising edge of the

signal can start the conversion. The software source trigger events can be generated by setting a bit in a

register (SWSTR and JSWSTR in ADC_CTRL2).

A regular group conversion can be interrupted by an injected trigger.


   25.8 DMA request


Since converted regular channels value are stored in a unique data register, it is necessary to use DMA

for conversion of more than one regular channel. This avoids the loss of data already stored in the

ADC_RDOR register.

Only the end of conversion of a regular channel generates a DMA request, which allows the transfer of its

converted data from the ADC_RDOR register to the destination location selected by the user.

Note:

ADC1 also has its own DMA request . In dual ADC mode, ADC1-converted data can be transferred in dual

ADC mode using DMA thanks to master ADC0.


   25.9 Dual ADC mode


    In dual ADC mode the start of conversion is triggered alternately or simultaneously by the ADC0 master

    to the ADC1 slave, depending on the mode selected by the DUALMOD[2:0] bits in the ADC0_CR1 register.

    25.9.1 Injected simultaneous mode

    This mode converts an injected channel group. The source of external trigger comes from the injected

    group mux of ADC0 (selected by the JEXSEL[2:0] bits in the ADC0_CR2 register). A simultaneous trigger

    is provided to ADC1.

    Note:

    Do not convert the same channel on the two ADCs (no overlapping times for the two ADCs when

    converting the same channel).

    At the end of conversion event on ADC0 or ADC1:

    • The converted data is stored in the ADC_JDORx registers of each ADC interface.

    • An JEC interrupt is generated (if enabled on one of the two ADC interfaces) when the ADC0/ADC1

    injected channels are all converted.

    Note:

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        917 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

In simultaneous mode, exactly the same sampling delay time should be configured for the two channels

that will be sampled simultaneously by ADC0 and ADC1.



        sampling

    ADC1
    ADCO

        of injected conversion on ADCO and ADC1



        Figure 25-3 Injected simultaneous mode on 4 channels

    25.9.2 Regular simultaneous mode

    This mode is performed on a regular channel group. The source of the external trigger comes from the

    regular group MUX of ADC0 (selected by the EXSEL[2:0] bits in the ADC0_CR2 register). A simultaneously

    trigger is provided to the ADC1.

    Note:

    Do not convert the same channel on the two ADCs (no overlapping times for the two ADCs when

    converting the same channel).

    At the end of conversion event on ADC0 or ADC1:

    • A 32-bit DMA transfer request is generated (if DMAEN bit is set) which transfers to SRAM the

    ADC0_RDOR 32-bit register containing the ADC1 converted data in the upper half-word and the ADC0

    converted data in the lower half-word.

    • An EC interrupt is generated (if enabled on one of the two ADC interfaces) when ADC0/ADC1 regular

    channels are all converted.

    Note: In regular simultaneous mode, exactly the same sampling delay time should be configured for the

    two channels that will be sampled simultaneously by ADC0 and ADC1.



                                      [] sampling
    Anco [Tero [Tort [Tera [ ems]     [conversion





        Figure 25-4 Regular simultaneous mode on 16 channels

    25.9.3 Fast interleaved mode

    This mode can be started only on a regular channel group (usually one channel). The source of external
    §    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        918 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

trigger comes from the regular channel mux of ADC0. After an external trigger occurs:

• ADC1 starts immediately and

• ADC0 starts after a delay of 7 ADC clock cycles.

If CON bit is set on both ADC0 and ADC1, the selected regular channels of both ADCs are continuously

converted.

After an EC interrupt is generated by ADC0 (if enabled through the ECIE bit) a 32-bit DMA transfer

request is generated (if the DMAEN bit is set ) which transfers to SRAM the ADC0_RDOR 32-bit register

containing the ADC1 converted data and the ADC0 converted data. Note:

The maximum sampling delay time allowed is < 7 ADCCLK cycles to avoid the overlap be- tween ADC0

and ADC1 sampling delay phases in the event that they convert the same channel.

        End of conversion on ADC1 0 samping

                  cycles

        Figure 25-5 Fast interleaved mode on 1 channel in continuous conversion mode

    25.9.4 Slow interleaved mode

    This mode can be started only on a regular channel group (only one channel). The source of external trigger

    comes from the regular channel mux of ADC0. After an external trigger occurs:

    • ADC1 starts immediately and

    • ADC0 starts after a delay of 14 ADC clock cycles.

    • ADC1 starts after a second delay of 14 ADC cycles, and so on. Note:

    The maximum sampling delay time allowed is <14 ADCCLK cycles to avoid an overlap with the next

    conversion.

    After an EC interrupt is generated by ADC0 (if enabled through ECIE bit), a 32-bit DMA transfer request

    is generated(if the DMAEN bit is set), which transfers to SRAM the ADC0_RDOR 32-bit register containing

    the ADC1 converted data and the ADC0 converted data.

    A new ADC1 start is automatically generated after 28 ADC clock cycles.

    CON bit can not be set in the mode since it continuously converts the selected regular channel.
                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.     AA A     i A     919 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Note:

The application must ensure that no external trigger for injected channel occurs when inter- leaved mode

in enabled.

25.9.5 Alternate trigger mode

This mode can be started only on an injected channel group. The source of external trigger comes from

the injected group mux of ADC0.

• When the first trigger occurs, all injected group channels in ADC0 are converted.

• When the second trigger arrives, all injected group channels in ADC1 are converted.

• and so on.

A JEC interrupt, if enabled, is generated after all injected group channels of ADC0 are converted. A JEC

interrupt, if enabled, is generated after all injected group channels of ADC1 are converted. If another

external trigger occurs after all injected group channels have been converted then the alternate trigger

process restarts by converting ADC0 injected group channels.

            1%     trigger              3" trigger                   n" trigger
                                   EOC, JEOC       \      EOC, JEOC        \    [Conversion
                                                          on ADCO
                                   7                             7
            ADCO    OJ
            ADC1                                                     —
                                   EOC, JEOC          /          EOC, JEOC      /
                                       on ADC1
               2" trigger                          4" trigger      (n+1)" trigger

               Figure 25-7 Alternate trigger: injected channel group of each ADC

If the injected discontinuous mode is enabled for both ADC0 and ADC1:

• When the first trigger occurs, the first injected channels in ADC0 are converted.

• When the second trigger arrives, the first injected channels in ADC1 are converted.
& "dango                                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        920 / 1446

                                                                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
• and so on ...

        A JEC interrupt, if enabled, is generated after all injected group channels of ADC0 are converted. A JEC

interrupt, if enabled, is generated after all injected group channels of ADC1 are converted. If another

external trigger occurs after all injected group channels have been converted then the al- ternate trigger

process restarts.

                 first trigger  third trigger      5th trigger      7th trigger                  [J] sampling
                                                                                  JEOC on ADCO   [J conversion

               ADCO
               act                       /¹ /          /[T1                            /
                   second trigger     fourth trigger   6th trigger                 8th trigger

                             Figure 25-8 Alternate trigger: 4 injected channels (each ADC) in discontinuous mode

25.9.6 Independent mode

In this mode the dual ADC synchronization is bypassed and each ADC interfaces works independently.

25.9.7 Combined regular/injected simultaneous mode

It is possible to interrupt simultaneous conversion of a regular group to start simultaneous conversion of

an injected group.

Note: In combined regular/injected simultaneous mode, extactly the same sampling time should be

configured for the two channels that will be sampled simultaneously by ADC0 and ADC1.

25.9.8 Combined regular simultaneous + alternate trigger
mode

It is possible to interrupt regular group simultaneous conversion to start alternate trigger conversion of an

injected group.

                                           1% trigger

                                  |    |  oa | [om             |                   [Jom]     [Jem

ADC MASTER inj                    :                                                :            :

                   Tow            |    [om                 [ow |     [ow [Jom]                  [Tom [[omw]
ADC SLAVE inj                     :                        :                       :
                                                                                                Synchro not lost
                                                                                    2™ trigger


por            Figure 25-9 Alternate + regular simultaneous
                                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                             921 / 1446

                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

If a trigger occurs during an injected conversion that has interrupted a regular conversion, it will be

ignored. Figure 25-10 shows the behavior in this case (the second trigger is ignored).

                      1st trigger      3rd trigger

      [Tom  [Jom   [Toe |            [Toe         [Tom  Jom]
      [Lom  [Tom   [Tom     |        [Toe JTow ]  h[Tow     ]
inj   '     '      '      vo                            !


                          2nd trigger                 2nd trigger

 Figure 25-10 Case of trigger occurring during injected conversion

25.9.9 Combined injected simultaneous + interleaved

It is possible to interrupt an interleaved conversion with an injected event. In this case the interleaved

conversion is interrupted and the injected conversion starts, at the end of the injected sequence the

interleaved conversion resumed,

 ADCO                     TT cro  TT     cro |    sampling
                                               [conversion
 act     [J cio           cro     cho |
                                  [cw     ||      TT TT cho |
                                                     cro |

Figure 25-11 Interleaved single channel with injected sequence CH11, CH12


25.10 ADC interrupts


An interrupt can be produced on end of conversion for regular and injected groups and when the analog

watchdog status bit is set. Separate interrupt enable bits are available for flexibility.

Note:

ADC0 and ADC1 interrupts are mapped onto the same interrupt vector. Two other flags are present in

the ADC_STS register, but there is no interrupt associated with them:

• JSTR (Start of conversion for injected group channels)

• RSRT (Start of conversion for regular group channels)

Table 25-2 ADC interrupts

    —_— Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                   922 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

End of conversion regular group EC ECIE

End of conversion injected group JEC JECIE

Analog watchdog status bit set AWD AWDIE

25.11.1     ADC_STS

   Offset: 0x0000

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 25-4 ADC_STS Register Description

Bit   Symbol      TypeDescription

31:10RSV          -    Reserved

9     WKP         rw   This bit is set by hardware when the converted voltage crosses the values

                       programmed in the ADC_WLTR and ADC_WHTR registers and WKP is set.

                       It is cleared by software (write 0 to clear).

                       0: No Analog watchdog wake up event occurred

                       1: Analog watchdog wake up event occurred not used now

8     DMA_MISS    rw   This bit is set when DMA has not read the data when the data is overwritten.

                       It is cleared by software (write 1 to clear).

                       0: DMA does not miss data

                       1: DMA miss data

7     JEC_INTSTS  rw   Interrupt status

                       This bit is set by hardware at the end of all injected group channel conversion.

                       It is cleared by software (write 0 to clear).

                       0: Conversion is not complete

                       1: Conversion complete

{dango                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        924 / 1446

                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

6   AWD_INTSTSrw    Interrupt status

                    This bit is set by hardware when the converted voltage crosses the values

                    programmed in the ADC_WLTR and ADC_WHTR registers. It is cleared by

                    software (write 0 to clear).

                    0: No Analog watchdog event occurred

                    1: Analog watchdog event occurred

5   EC_INTSTS  rw   Interrupt status

                    This bit is set by hardware at the end of a group channel conversion (only

                    regular). It is cleared by software (write 0 to clear).

                    0: Conversion is not complete

                    1: Conversion complete

4   RSRT       rw   This bit is set by hardware when regular channel conversion starts. It is

                    cleared by software (write 0  to clear).

                    0: No regular channel conversion started

                    1: Regular channel conversion has started

3   JSTR       rw   This bit is set by hardware when injected channel group conversion starts. It

                    is cleared by software(write  0 to clear).

                    0: No injected group conversion started

                    1: Injected group conversion has started

2   JEC        rw   This bit is set by hardware at the end of all injected group channel conversion.

                    It is cleared by software (write 0 to clear).

                    0: Conversion is not complete

                    1: Conversion complete

1   EC         rw   This bit is set by hardware at the end of a group channel conversion (only

                    regular). It is cleared by software (write 0 to clear) or by reading the

                    ADC_RDOR (reading before DMA finish will not clear EC, because reading
a                              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        925 / 1446

                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    during request is taken as reading from DMA).

                    0: Conversion is not complete

                    1: Conversion complete

0     AWD     rw    This bit is set by hardware when the converted voltage crosses the values

                    programmed in the ADC_WLTR and ADC_WHTR registers. It is cleared by

                    software (write 0 to clear).

                    0: No Analog watchdog event occurred

                    1: Analog watchdog event occurred

25.11.2     ADC_CTRL1

  Offset: 0x0004

  Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 25-5 ADC_CTRL1 Register Description

Bit    Symbol   Type  Description

31     WKPEN    rw    Wake up enable.

                      0: Analog watchdog wake up disable

                      1: A wake up interrupt is generated when the analog watchdog event

                      occur.

30:24  RSV      -     Reserved

23     AWDEN    rw    This bit is set/reset by software.

                      0: Analog watchdog disabled on regular channels

                      1: Analog watchdog enabled on regular channels

22     JAWDEN   rw    This bit is set/reset by software

                      0: Analog watchdog disabled on injected channels

                      1: Analog watchdog enabled on injected channels

21:20  RSV      -     Reserved



fi                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    926 / 1446

                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

19:16 DUALMOD rw These bits are written by software to select the operating mode.

                        0000: Independent mode.

                        0001: Combined regular simultaneous + injected simultaneous mode

                        0010: Combined regular simultaneous + alternate trigger mode

                        0011: Combined injected simultaneous + fast interleaved mode

                        0100: Combined injected simultaneous + slow Interleaved mode

                        0101: Injected simultaneous mode only

                        0110: Regular simultaneous mode only

                        0111: Fast interleaved mode only

                        1000: Slow interleaved mode only

                        1001: Alternate trigger mode only

                        Note: In dual mode, a change of channel configuration generates a restart

                        that can produce a loss of synchronization. It is recommended to disable

                        dual mode before any configuration change.

    15:13 DISN     rw   These bits are written by software to define the number of regular

                        channels to be converted in discontinuous mode, after receiving an

                        external trigger.

                        000: 1 channel

                        001: 2 channels

                        ........

                        111: 8 channels

    12   JDISEN    rw   This bit set and cleared by software to enable/disable discontinuous mode

                        on injected group channels

                        0: Discontinuous mode on injected channels disabled

                        1: Discontinuous mode on injected channels enabled

         {dango         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        927 / 1446

                                                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

11   RDISEN   rw   This bit set and cleared by software to enable/disable Discontinuous mode

                   on regular channels.

                   0: Discontinuous mode on regular channels disabled

                   1: Discontinuous mode on regular channels enabled

10   JAUT     rw   This bit set and cleared by software to enable/disable automatic injected

                   group conversion after regular group conversion.

                   0: Automatic injected group conversion disabled

                   1: Automatic injected group conversion enabled

9    AWDSGE   rw   This bit set and cleared by software to enable/disable the analog

                   watchdog on the channel identified by the AWDCH[4:0] bits.

                   0: Analog watchdog enabled on all channels

                   1: Analog watchdog enabled on a single channel.

8    SCN      rw   This bit is set and cleared by software to enable/disable Scan mode. In

                   Scan mode, the inputs selected through the ADC_RSQx or ADC_JSQx

                   registers are converted.

                   0: Scan mode disabled

                   1: Scan mode enabled

                   Note: An EC or JEC interrupt is generated only on the end of conversion of

                   the last channel if the corresponding ECIE or JECIE bit is set

7    JECIE    rw   This bit is set and cleared by software to enable/ disable the end of

                   conversion interrupt for injected channels.

                   0: JEC interrupt disabled

                   1: JEC interrupt enabled. An interrupt is generated when the JEC bit is

                   set.

6    AWDIE    rw   This bit is set and cleared by software to enable/disable the analog

                   watchdog interrupt.

                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.     A A     A A A             928 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                             0: Analog watchdog interrupt disabled

                             1: Analog watchdog interrupt enabled

5      ECIE         rw       This bit is set and cleared by software to enable/ disable the end of

                             Conversion interrupt.

                             0: EC interrupt disabled

                             1: EC interrupt enabled. An interrupt is generated when the EC bit is set.

4:0    AWDCH        rw       These bits are set and cleared by software. They select the input channel

                             to be guarded by the Analog watchdog.

                             00000: ADC analog Channel0

                             00001: ADC analog Channel1

                             ......

                             01111: ADC analog Channel15

                             Other values: Reserved.

25.11.3     ADC_CTRL2

      Offset: 0x0008

      Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 25-6 ADC_CTRL2 Register Description

Bit    Symbol           Type  Description

31:23  RSV              -     Reserved

22     SWSTR            rw    This bit is set by software to start conversion and cleared by hardware as

                              soon as conversion starts. It starts a conversion of a group of regular

                              channels if SWSTR is selected as trigger event by the EXSEL[2:0] bits.

                              0: Reset state

                              1: Starts conversion of regular channels

21     JSWSTR           rw    This bit is set by software and cleared by software or by hardware as

                              soon as the conversion starts. It starts a conversion of a group of

fi                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               929 / 1446

                                                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      injected channels (if JSWSTR is selected as trigger event by the

                      JEXSEL[2:0] bits.

                      0: Reset state

                      1: Starts conversion of injected channels

20     EXTTRIG   rw   This bit is set and cleared by software to enable/ disable the external

                      trigger used to start conversion of a regular channel group.

                      0: Conversion on external event disabled

                      1: Conversion on external event enabled

19:17  EXSEL     rw   These bits select the external event used to trigger the start of

                      conversion of a regular group:

                      For ADC0 and ADC1, the assigned triggers are: When BTIME_EN is 0 :

                      000: ETMR0 CC1 event

                      001: ETMR0 CC2 event

                      010: ETMR0 CC3 event

                      011: GTMR0 CC2 event

                      100: GTMR1 TRGO event

                      101: GTMR3 CC4 event

                      110: EXTI line 11/ETMR1_TRGO event

                      111: SWSTR

                      When BTIME_EN is 1:(use basic timer to trigger)

                      000: BTMR0 TRGO event

                      001: BTMR1 TRGO event

                      010: BTMR2 TRGO event

                      011: BTMR3 TRGO event

                      Others: Disable trigger
{dango                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            930 / 1446

                                                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

16     RSV         -    Reserved

15     JEXTTRIG    rw   This bit is set and cleared by software to enable/ disable the external

                        trigger used to start conversion of an injected channel group.

                        0: Conversion on external event disabled

                        1: Conversion on external event enabled

14:12  JEXSEL      rw   These bits select the external event used to trigger the start of

                        conversion of an injected group: For ADC0 and ADC1 the assigned

                        triggers are:

                        When JBTIME_EN is 0 :

                        000: ETMR0 TRGO event

                        001: ETMR0 CC4 event

                        010: GTMR0 TRGO event

                        011: GTMR0 CC1 event

                        100: GTMR1 CC4 event

                        101: GTMR2 TRGO event

                        110: EXTI line15/ ETMR1_CC4 event

                        111: JSWSTR

                        When JBTIME_EN is 1: ( use basic timer to trigger)

                        000: BTMR0 TRGO event

                        001: BTMR1 TRGO event

                        010: BTMR2 TRGO event

                        011: BTMR3 TRGO event

                        Others: Disable trigger

11       DALIGN    rw   This bit is set and cleared by software.

                        0: Right Alignment
          dango            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                         931 / 1446

                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
I                       1: Left Alignment

10:9   RSV         -    Reserved

8      DMAEN       rw   This bit is set and cleared by software.

                        0: DMA mode disabled

                        1: DMA mode enabled

7:6    RSV         -    Reserved

5      JBTIME_EN   rw   This bit combine with JEXSEL to decide the trigger source of injected

4      BTIME_EN    rw   This bit combine with EXSEL to decide the trigger source of regular

3      RSTCAL      rw   This bit is set by software and cleared by hardware.

                        It is cleared after the calibration registers are initialized.

                        0: Calibration register initialized.

                        1: Initialize calibration register

2      CAL         rw   This bit is set by software to start the calibration. It is reset by hardware

                        after calibration is complete.

                        0: Calibration completed

                        1: Enable calibration

1      CON         rw   This bit is set and cleared by software. If set conversion takes place

                        continuously till this bit is reset.

                        0: Single conversion mode

                        1: Continuous conversion mode

0      ADON        rw   This bit is set and cleared by software. If this bit holds a value of zero

                        and a 1 is written to it then it wakes up the ADC from Power Down state.

                        The application should allow a delay of tSTAB between power up and

                        start of conversion.

                        0: Disable ADC conversion/calibration and go to power down mode.

{dango                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        932 / 1446

                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        1: Enable ADC

    Bit    Symbol Type Description

    31:22 RSV    -      Reserved

21:20 JLEN rw These bits are written by software to define the total number of conversions in the

                        injected channel conversion sequence.

                        00: 1 conversion

                        01: 2 conversions

                        10: 3 conversions

                        11: 4 conversions

19:15 JSQ4 rw Fourth conversion in injected sequence (when JL[1:0] = 3).

                        These bits are written by software with the channel number (0..17) assigned as the

                        fourth in the sequence to be converted.

                        note:

                        Unlike a regular conversion sequence, if JL[1:0] length is less than four, the

                        channels are converted in a sequence starting from (4-JL). Example:

                        ADC_JSQ[21:0] = 10 00011 00011 00111 00010 means that a scan conversion

                        will convert the following channel sequence: 7, 3, 3. (not 2, 7, 3)

14:10 JSQ3 rw third conversion in injected sequence (when JL[1:0] = 3)

9:5 JSQ2 rw second conversion in injected sequence (when JL[1:0] = 3)

4:0 JSQ1 rw first conversion in injected sequence (when JL[1:0] = 3)

26 Octo-SPI controller (OSPI)

26.1 Overview


    The OSPI supports most external serial memories such as serial PSRAMs，serial NOR and serial NAND

    FLASH with the following functional modes:

    • Non-XIP mode: all the operations are performed using the OSPI registers.
    • XIP mode: the external memory is memory mapped and it is seen by the system as if it was an internal

    memory, supporting both read and write operations.

    The OSPI supports the following protocols with associated frame formats:

    • the standard frame format with the command, address, wait cycles and data phase.
    • The HyperBus frame format.

    26.2 Features

    • Two OSPIs support serial master or slave operation
    • Support the following standards:
     – Motorola SPI
     – Texas instruments Synchronous Serial Protocol (SSP)
     – National Semiconductors Microwire
    • Standard/Dual/Quad/Octal SPI support
    • Hyperbus by Cypress Semiconductors
     – Dual Data Rate (DDR) support
     – Read Data Strobe support for DDR transfer
     – Data Mask support for DDR transfers
    • Execute In Place (XIP) mode support for read and write transfers
    • Enhanced (Dual/Quad/Octal) SPI features:
     – Programmable address, instruction, wait cycles, and data frame size
     – Continuous transfer mode in XIP operation (for reads)
    • External DMA for indirect mode and XIP mode operations
    • Configurable decryption of software-encypted data read from external FLASH
    • 4 to 32 bits data size selection

         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        948 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    • Interrupt generation on FIFO threshold and error

    26.3.2 OSPI interface

    The OSPI supports two different protocols:

    • Normal command protocol

    • HyperBus protocol

In any of these two protocols, the OSPI uses from 4 to 11 signals to interface with an external memory,

    depending on the functional mode：

    • CS#: chip-select used in all protocols

    • CLK: communication clock used in all protocols

    • DQS: data strobe used mainly in Octal protocol

    • IO[1:0]: data bus LSB used in all protocols

    • IO[7:2]: data bus MSB used in Octal protocols

    The OSPI uses the following GPIO pins as signal pins:

    Table 26-1 GPIO pins for the OSPI

     Signal name             GPIO pin

     OSPI0 CS#/CLK/DQS       IOG5/ IOG2/ IOG3

     OSPI0 IO[7:0]           IOD13/ IOD12/ IOD15/ IOD14/ IOD8/ IOD9/ IOD10/ IOD11

     OSPI1 CS#/CLK/DQS       IOE10/ IOF12/ IOE13

    fi                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.         950 / 1446

                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

OSPI1 IO[7:0]             IOG1/ IOG0/ IOF15/ IOF14/ IOF13/ IOE7/ IOE11/ IOE14

26.3.3 Normal command protocol

When in normal command protocol, the OSPI communicates with the external device using registers.

each operation can be divided to four phases:

  • Instruction phase

  • Address phase

  • Dummy phase(Wait cycles)

  • Data phase

Usually, instruction should be present, and other phases can be skipped.The CS# is driven low before

the start of each operation and goes high again after the operation finishes.

         ese
                          0     1  2         3 4      18    19     20     21
         ew                                      FLT
                      |  CMD     Address         Dummy   ——>»{Byte 1|Byte 2
                      I      I      Yad y——|      ~~     pra,     |      X              out
                                                                                       Date

                          Figure 26-4 Octal SPI read example

26.3.4 Hyperbus protocol

OSPI supports the Hyperbus protocol that works on the principle of the SPI DDR(Dual Data Rate)

interface. The DDR protocol transfers two data bytes per clock cycle on the input/output (I/O) signals.

The HyperBus uses 11 pins:

• CLK

• IO[7:0] as bidirectional data bus

• RWDS for read and write data strobe and latency insertion (mapped on DQS pin)

• CS#

The Hyperbus frame consists of command, address, and data. The command and address information is

conveyed by using 48-bit command-address (CA). Table 26-2 shows the CA frame format:

Table 26-2 CA frame bit assignment

 CA bit   Bit name                           Bit function

                                             Identifies the transaction as a read or write.
 47       R/W#
                                             1 – Indicates Read transaction.


& "dango                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    951 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                                          0 – Indicates a Write Transaction.

                                          Indicates whether the read or write transaction accesses

                                          the memory or register space.
 46      Address Space
                                          0 – Indicates memory space.

                                          1 – Indicates the register space.

                                          Indicates whether the burst is linear or wrapped.

 45      Burst Type                       0 – Indicates a wrapped burst.

                                          1 – Indicates a linear burst.

                                          Row and Upper Column component of the target address:
 44:16   Row&Upper Column Address
                                          System word address bits A31-A3.
 15:3    Reserved                                                                                |]

                                          Lower Column component of the target address: System

 2:0     Lower column address             word address bits A2-0 selecting the starting word within

                                          a half-page.

Read and write operation with initial latency

The HyperBus read and write operations need to care about two timings:

• tRWR: minimal read/write recovery time for the device

• tACC: access time for the device

During the read operation, the RWDS is used by the device in two ways:

• during the command/address phase, to request an additional latency

• during the data phase, for data strobing

    26.3.5 OSPI interrupts

    The following are the registers related to OSPI Interrupts:

    • OSPI_TXEICR

    • OSPI_RXOICR

    • OSPI_RXUICR

    • OSPI_ISR

    • OSPI_RISR

    • OSPI_ICR

    • OSPI_IMR

    OSPI_RISR register stores raw interrupts status prior to interrupt masking, and OSPI_ISR register stores

interrupts status after interrupt masking. Interrupt masking provides more flexibility for OSPI
               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                       954 / 1446

                                                                  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

programming.

26.3.6 Non-XIP mode

In Non-XIP mode, the commands are started by writing to the OSPI registers and the data is transferred

by writing or reading the data register, in a similar way to other peripherals.

Non-XIP Read Operation

A Dual, Quad, or Octal SPI read operation can be divided into four phases:

• Instruction phase

• Address phase

• Wait cycles

• Data phase

The following register fields are used for a read operation:

• OSPI_CTRLR0.SPI_FRF - Specifies the format in which the transmission happens for the frame.

• OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 – Specifies length of instruction, address, and data.

 – OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.INST_L – Specifies length of an instruction

 – OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.ADDR_L – Specifies address length

 – OSPI_CTRLR0.DFS – Specifies data length.

Wait Cycles can be programmed using OSPI_SPIC_CTRLR0.WAIT_CYCLES field. The wait cycles are

introduced for target slave to change their mode from input to output and the wait cycles can vary for

different devices.

For a READ operation, OSPI sends instruction and control data once and waits until it receives NDF

(OSPI_CTRLR1 register) number of data frames

Non-XIP Write Operation

Dual, Quad, or Octal SPI write operations can be divided into three parts:

• Instruction phase

• Address phase

• Data phase

Registers used for a write operation are similar with those used for a read operation.

An instruction takes one FIFO location and address can take more than one FIFO locations. Both the

instruction and address must be programmed in the data register (DR). DWC_ssi waits until both have

been programmed to start the write operation.

The instruction, address and data can be programmed to send in dual mode/quad mode/octal mode,

which can be selected from the OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.TRANS_TYPE and OSPI_CTRLR0.SPI_FRF fields.

     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                                  955 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Some of the devices require instruction and address only transfer (without data phase). This

could be achieved by skipping the address phase, and transmitting the address as data to the

device. For this, OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.ADDR_L should be programmed to 0, and OSPI_CTRLR0.DFS should

be programmed with the required length.

26.3.7 XIP mode

OSPI provides a function to directly perform memory read operation from AHB transaction. This is

called execute in place mode, in which OSPI acts as memory mapped interface to the SPI memory.

XIP Read Operation

XIP operations are supported in only in Dual, Quad, or Octal enhanced SPI modes of operation, and so,

the OSPI_CTRLR0.SPI_FRF bit must not be programmed to 0. Typically, an XIP operation consists of an

address phase and a data phase. The programming flow to set-up an XIP transfer is as follows:

• Set the SPI frame format value in OSPI_CTRLR0 register.

• Set the SPI transfer mode value to Receive only mode in OSPI_CTRLR0 register.

• Set the address length, wait cycles, and transaction type in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.

Note that, the maximum address length is 32.

The instruction phase can also be included in XIP transfer by using XIP_INST_EN field of the

OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register. In this case, the following registers must be set:

• Set length of instruction in the OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register.

• Write the instruction opcodes in the OSPI_XIP_INCR_INST.

• set OSPI_EN bit in OSPI_OSPIEN register.

• Set XIPEN0 bit or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register.

Note that, if OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.XIP_DFS_HC is set to 1 then the XIP address must always be aligned

with the DFS value programmed in the OSPI_CTRLR0.DFS bits.

After the programming is complete, you can initiate a read transaction through the AHB interface that is

transferred to the SPI peripheral using programmed values.
XIP Write Operation
OSPI can write into the SPI memory without software overhead of writing the instruction and address

separately inside the transmit buffer. XIP for write feature executes the write transactions directly without

software intervention. The programming flow to set-up an XIP write transfer is similar with the flow

described in XIP read model.

OSPI ends the transfer on the SPI interface under the following conditions:

• If IDLE transfer is detected in-between an XIP write transfer.

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 956 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• Transmit FIFO goes empty.

Registers Related to eXecute In Place (XIP) Mode

The following are the registers related to eXecute In Place (XIP) Mode:

• OSPI_CTRLR0

• OSPI_XIP_CTRL

• OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0

• OSPI_XIP_INCR_INST

For more information about these registers, see OSPI registers.

26.3.8 Normal command protocol Configuration

Non-XIP mode write operation configuration
When Non-XIP mode write operation is used, the configuration is done in the following way:

• Unset XIPEN0 bit or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

• Configure GPIO pins used for current OSPI

• Set OSPI0 bit or OSPI1 bit in SYSCFG0_RST0 register for OSPI software reset

• Select clock for OSPI clock source if higher clock rate is needed(By default, OSPI use 48Mhz RCH clock)

• Configure SPI frame format, transfer mode, clock polarity, clock phase, frame format and data frame

size in OSPI_CTRLR0 register.

• Configure SCKDV in OSPI_BAUDR for proper OSPI interface clock rate

• Specify length of instruction, address, and data in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Set wait cycles value to 0 in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Specify number of data frames in OSPI_CTRLR1 register

• Enable Specified OSPI interrupts if needed

• Enable OSPI by setting OSPI_EN bit in OSPI_OSPIEN register

• Write instruction, address, and data into OSPI_DRx register if OSPI TX FIFO is not full

• Disable OSPI by setting OSPI_EN bit to 0 in OSPI_OSPIEN register if data transfer is finished

Non-XIP mode read operation configuration

Non-XIP mode read operation configuration is similar with write operation configuration above.

The differences are as follows:

• Set SPI transfer mode to RX mode in OSPI_CTRLR0 register

• Set wait cycles value to dummy cycles in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Write instruction, address into OSPI_DRx register if OSPI TX FIFO is not full

• Read data from OSPI_DRx register if OSPI RX FIFO is not empty

{dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                              957 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Non-XIP mode write operation with DMA configuration

When Non-XIP mode write operation is used, the configuration is done in the following way:

• Unset XIPEN0 bit or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

• Configure GPIO pins used for current OSPI

• Set OSPI0 bit or OSPI1 bit in SYSCFG0_RST0 register for OSPI software reset

• Select clock for OSPI clock source if higher clock rate is needed(By default, OSPI use 48Mhz RCH clock)

• Configure SPI frame format, transfer mode, clock polarity, clock phase, frame format and data frame

size in OSPI_CTRLR0 register.

• Configure SCKDV in OSPI_BAUDR for proper OSPI interface clock rate

• Specify length of instruction, address, and data in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Set wait cycles value to 0 in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Specify number of data frames in OSPI_CTRLR1 register

• Configure DMA channel for data transfer if high speed is needed. When DMA is used,

instruction,address and data should be packed together to be transferred by DMA engine

• Configure OSPI DMA TX FIFO level value in OSPI_DMATDLR register when DMA is used

• Set TDMAE bit in OSPI_DMACR register when DMA is used

• Enable Specified OSPI interrupts if needed

• Enable OSPI by setting OSPI_EN bit in OSPI_OSPIEN register

• Wait for the completion of DMA transfer

• Disable OSPI if OSPI is idle.

Non-XIP mode Read operation with DMA configuration

Non-XIP mode read operation with DMA configuration is similar with write operation with DMA

configuration above.The differences are as follows:

• Set SPI transfer mode to RX mode in OSPI_CTRLR0 register

• Set wait cycles value to dummy cycles in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Write read instruction, read address into OSPI_DRx register if OSPI TX FIFO is not full

XIP mode read operation configuration

When XIP mode read operation is used, the configuration is done in the following way:

• Unset XIPEN0 bit or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

• Configure GPIO pins used for current OSPI

• Set OSPI0 bit or OSPI1 bit in SYSCFG0_RST0 register for OSPI software reset

• Select clock for OSPI clock source if higher clock rate is needed(By default, OSPI use 48Mhz RCH clock)

{dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 958 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• Configure SPI frame format, transfer mode, clock polarity, clock phase, frame format and data frame

size in OSPI_CTRLR0 register.

• Configure SCKDV in OSPI_BAUDR for proper OSPI interface clock rate

• Specify length of instruction, address, and data in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Set wait cycles value to dummy cycles in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Enable Specified OSPI interrupts if needed

• Set XIP_INST_EN bit in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Set XIP_DFS_HC bit in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register if needed(By default, XIP DFS is 32-bit)

• Write the instruction opcodes in the OSPI_XIP_INCR_INST.

• Set OSPI_EN bit in OSPI_OSPIEN register.

• Set XIPEN0 bit or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

• Once XIP read is finished, disable OSPI and unset XIPEN0 it or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

XIP mode read operation with DMA configuration

When XIP mode read operation is used, the configuration is done in the following way:

• Unset XIPEN0 bit or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

• Configure GPIO pins used for current OSPI

• Set OSPI0 bit or OSPI1 bit in SYSCFG0_RST0 register for OSPI software reset

• Select clock for OSPI clock source if higher clock rate is needed(By default, OSPI use 48Mhz RCH clock)

• Configure SPI frame format, transfer mode, clock polarity, clock phase, frame format and data frame

size in OSPI_CTRLR0 register.

• Specify length of instruction, address, and data in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Set wait cycles value to dummy cycles in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Enable Specified OSPI interrupts if needed

• Set XIP_INST_EN bit in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Set XIP_DFS_HC bit in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register if needed(By default, XIP DFS is 32-bit)

• Write the instruction opcodes in the OSPI_XIP_INCR_INST.

• Set OSPI_EN bit in OSPI_OSPIEN register.

• Set XIPEN0 bit or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

• Once XIP read is finished, disable OSPI and unset XIPEN0 it or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

26.3.9 Hyperbus protocol configuration

When the Hyperbus feature is enabled in OSPI, the OSPI_CTRLR0.SPI_HYPERBUS_EN register bit is set

to program OSPI as a Hyperbus master. As the Hyperbus interface has a fixed frame format, the following
{dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 959 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

fields are fixed when OSPI is programmed into Hyperbus mode:

• OSPI_CTRLR0.SPI_FRF – 11b (Octal Frame format)

• OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.TRANS_TYPE – 10b (Address to be sent in Octal format)

• OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.ADDR_L – 1100b (Address length of 48 bits)

• OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.INST_L – 00b (No Instruction phase)

• OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.SPI_DDR_EN – 1 (Address to be sent on Octal-DDR format)

• OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.SPI_RXDS_EN – 1 (Enable read data strobe to sample incoming data)

OSPI provides an option for RXDS signaling in Command-Address (CA) phase as required by the

Hyperbus protocol. You can enable this feature by programming the OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.RXDS_SIG_EN

bit to 1.In this mode, if the rxds signal is detected as HIGH during command and address cycles, then

OSPI sends (2*OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.WAIT_CYCLES-1) Wait cycles after the address phase is complete.

Figure below shows an example of rxds signaling during CA phase. If the

OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0.RXDS_SIG_EN bit is set to 0 or if the rxds level is 0 during CA phase, the number of

Wait cycles is fixed to the programmed value in the OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0. WAIT_CYCLES bits.


    CS# [|
 moro JE) Command-Addess
   RXDS | 2°SPI_CTRLRO.WAIT_CYCLES

 ror I

        Figure 26-9 RXDS in Command-Address (CA) phase

The Hyperbus protocol configuration also consists of Non-XIP mode configuration and XIP mode

configuration, and the programming flow is similar with Normal command protocol configureation. As a

result, we only describe XIP mode read operation configuration in the following.

XIP mode read operation configuration

• Unset XIPEN0 bit or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

• Configure GPIO pins used for Hyperbus

• Set OSPI0 bit or OSPI1 bit in SYSCFG0_RST0 register for OSPI software reset

• Select clock for OSPI clock source if higher clock rate is needed(By default, OSPI use 48Mhz RCH clock)

• Configure SPI frame format, transfer mode, clock polarity, clock phase, frame format and data frame

size in OSPI_CTRLR0 register.

• Configure SCKDV in OSPI_BAUDR for proper OSPI interface clock rate, SCKDV must be greater than 4

• Specify length of instruction, address, and data in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 960 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• Set wait cycles value to proper Latency in OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 register

• Set TDE to proper value(recommended value is 0,1,2) in OSPI_DDR_DRIVE_EDGE register

• Enable Specified OSPI interrupts if needed

• Set OSPI_EN bit in OSPI_OSPIEN register.

• Set XIPEN0 bit or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

• Once XIP read is finished, disable OSPI and unset XIPEN0 it or XIPEN1 bit in SYSCFG0_OSPIC register

26.3.10 Decryption function

OSPI provides secure communication path between OSPI master and external FLASH memories,

shown in the following figure. After user sets FLASH read instruction to OSPI_ENC_CFG.INSTR_RD and

enable OSPI decryption , The OSPI will decrypt the ciphertext read from external FLASH memories

using the key in OSPI_KEY0/OSPI_KEY1/OSPI_KEY2/OSPI_KEY3 registers and the initial vector in

OSPI_IV0/OSPI_IV1 registers.





        OSPI

                          read instruction,
        plaintext         address
    ARM        Decryption — FLASH
    CORE

                          ciphertext






    Figure 26-10 secure communication between OSPI master and external FLASH

    A typical scenario is that user writes the software-encrypted data to FLASH, and then sets up OSPI

    decryption function for further reading FLASH operation. Of course, software encryption function

    uses same key，same initial vector and same cryptographic algorithm as OSPI decryption does.

    The programming procedure is as follows:

    • Write software-encrypted data to FLASH

    • Configure key and initial vector stored in OSPI_KEYx registers and OSPI_IVx registers

    • Set read instruction to OSPI_ENC_CFG.INSTR_RD

    • Set OSPI_ENC_CFG.ENC_EN

    • Read data from external FLASH


    {dango  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    961 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
26.3.11 Sleep flag

OSPI HCLK can be disabled for low power consumption. We should check SYSCFG0_OSPIC.SLEEP0 and

SYSCFG0_OSPIC.SLEEP1 before disabling OSPI0 HCLK and OSPI1 HCLK.

    26.4.1 OSPI_CTRLR0

    Offset: 0x0000

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 26-4 OSPI_CTRLR0 Register Description

    Bits Symbol           TypeDescription

    31:25Rsvd             -    Reserved

24 SPI_HYPERBUS_EN rw SPI Hyperbus Frame format enable

                               Selects if data frame format for Transmitting/Receiving the data is in

                               Hyperbus mode. This field is effective only when OSPI_CTRLR0.FRF is set

                               to SPI frame format.

                               0x0: Disable Hyperbus Format

                               0x1: Enable Hyperbus Format

                          rw   SPI Frame Format

                               Selects data frame format for Transmitting/ Receiving the data.
    23:22SPI_FRF
                               0x0: Standard SPI Format

                               0x1: Dual SPI Format

    {dango                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        963 / 1446

                                                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   0x2: Quad SPI Format

                   0x3: Octal SPI Format

21:12Rsvd     -    Reserved

11:10TMOD     rw   Transfer Mode

                   In eeprom-read mode, receive data is not valid while control data is

                   being transmit- ted. When all control data is sent to the EEPROM,

                   receive data becomes valid and transmit data becomes invalid. All data

                   in the transmit FIFO is considered control data in this mode. This

                   transfer mode is only valid when the SPI is configured as master device.

                   0x0: Transmit and receive;

                   0x1: Transmit only mode or Write

                   0x2: Receive only mode or Read

                   0x3: EEPROM Read mode

9   SCPOL     rw   Serial Clock Polarity

                   Valid when the frame format (FRF) is set to Motorola SPI. Used to select

                   the polarity of the inactive serial clock, which is held inactive when the

                   SPI master is not actively transferring data on the serial bus.

                   0x0: Inactive state of serial clock is low

                   0x1: Inactive state of serial clock is high

8   SCPH      rw   Serial Clock Phase

                   Valid when the frame format (FRF) is set to Motorola SPI.

                   The serial clock phase selects the relation- ship of the serial clock with

                   the slave select signal. When SCPH = 0, data are captured on the first

                   edge of the serial clock. When SCPH = 1, the serial clock starts toggling

                   one cycle after the slave select line is activated, and data are captured

                   on the second edge of the serial clock.

                   0x0: Serial clock toggles in middle of first data bit

                   0x1: Serial clock toggles at start of first data bit

7:6  FRF     rw    Frame Format

                   Selects which serial protocol transfers the data.

                   0x0: Motorola SPI Frame Format

                   0x1: Texas Instruments SSP Frame Format
    {dango         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               964 / 1446

                                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                              0x2: National Microwire Frame Format

                              0x3: Reserved value

5      Rsvd          -        Reserved

4:0    DFS           rw       Data Frame Size in 32-bit transfer size mode

                              DFS value should be multiple of 2 if SPI_FRF = 0x01, DFS value should

                              be multiple of 4 if SPI_FRF = 0x10, DFS value should be multiple of 8 if

                              SPI_FRF = 0x11.

                              0x3: 4-bit serial data transfer

                              ......

                              0x1E: 31-bit serial data transfer

                              0x1F: 32-bit serial data transfer

26.4.2 OSPI_CTRLR1

Offset: 0x0004

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-5 OSPI_CTRLR1 Register Description

Bits   Symbol TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd      r    Reserved

15:0   NDF     rw   Number of Data Frames

                    When TMOD = 10 or TMOD = 11, this register field sets the number of data frames

                    to be continuously received by the SPI. The SPI continues to receive serial data until

                    the number of data frames received is equal to this register value plus 1, which

                    enables you to receive up to 64KB of data in a continuous transfer.

                    When the SPI is configured as a serial slave, the transfer continues for as long as

                    the slave is selected.

                    Therefore, this register serves no purpose and is not present when the SPI is

                    configured as a serial slave.

26.4.3 OSPI_OSPIEN

Offset: 0x0008

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-6 OSPI_OSPIEN Register Description

Bits Symbol    TypeDescription

{dango                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 965 / 1446

                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

31:1Rsvd   -     Reserved

0   OSPI_ENrw    OSPI Enable

                 Enables and disables all OSPI operations. When disabled, all serial transfers are

                 halted immediately. Transmit and receive FIFO buffers are cleared when the device

                 is disabled. It is impossible to program some of the SPI control registers when

                 enabled.

                 0x0: Disables Serial Transfer

                 0x1: Enables Serial Transfer

26.4.4 OSPI_MWCR

Offset: 0x000C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-7 OSPI_MWCR Register Description

Bits Symbol TypeDescription

31:3Rsvd   -    Reserved

2   MHS    rw   Microwire Handshaking

                Relevant only when the SPI is configured as a serial-master device. When configured

                as a serial slave, this bit field has no functionality. Used to enable and disable the

                busy/ready handshaking interface for the Microwire protocol.

                When enabled, the SPI checks for a ready status from the target slave, after the

                transfer of the last data/control bit, before clearing the BUSY status in the SR

                register.

                0x0: Handshaking interface is disabled

                0x1: Handshaking interface is enabled

1   MDD    rw   Microwire Control

                Defines the direction of the data word when the Microwire serial protocol is used.

                When this bit is set to 0, the data word is received by the SPI MacroCell from the

                external serial device. When this bit is set to 1, the data word is transmitted from

                the SPI MacroCell to the external serial device.

                0x0: OSPI receives data

                0x1: OSPI transmits data

0   MWMODrw     Microwire Transfer Mode

                Defines whether the Microwire transfer is sequential or non-sequential. When
& "dango             Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        966 / 1446

                                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  sequential mode is used, only one control word is needed to transmit or receive a

                  block of data words.

                  When non-sequential mode is used, there must be a control word for each data word

                  that is transmitted or received.

                  0x0: Non-Sequential Microwire Transfer

                  0x1: Sequential Microwire Transfer

26.4.5 OSPI_SER

Offset: 0x0010

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-8 OSPI_SER Register Description

Bits Symbol TypeDescription

31:4Rsvd   -     Reserved

3:0  SER   rw    Slave Select Enable Flag

                 Each bit in this register corresponds to a slave select line from the SPI master. When

                 a bit in this register is set (1), the corresponding slave select line from the master is

                 activated when a serial transfer begins. It should be noted that setting or clearing bits

                 in this register have no effect on the corresponding slave select outputs until a

                 transfer is started. Before beginning a transfer, you should enable the bit in this

                 register that corresponds to the slave device with which the master wants to

                 communicate. When not operating in broadcast mode, only one bit in this field should

                 be set.

                 0x0: No slave selected

                 0x1: Slave is selected

26.4.6 OSPI_BAUDR

Offset: 0x0014

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-9 OSPI_BAUDR Register Description

Bits Symbol TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd     -   Reserved

15:1 SCKDV rw     OSPI Clock Divider

                  The LSB for this field is always set to 0 and is unaffected by a write operation, which

{dango                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                         967 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  ensures an even value is held in this register. If the value is 0, the serial output

                  clock is disabled. The frequency of the output clock is derived from the following

                  equation:

                  Fclk_out = Fsclk /SCKDV

                  where SCKDV is any even value between 2 and 65534. For example:

                  for Fsclk = 3.6864 MHz and SCKDV =2

                  Fclk_out = 3.6864/2 = 1.8432MHz

0   Rsvd      -   Reserved

26.4.7 OSPI_TXFTLR

Offset: 0x0018

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-10 OSPI_TXFTLR Register Description

Bits Symbol TypeDescription

31:5Rsvd   -     Reserved

4:0 TFT    rw    Transmit FIFO Threshold

                 Controls the level of entries (or below) at which the transmit FIFO controller triggers

                 an interrupt. The FIFO depth is configurable in the range 2-256; this register is sized

                 to the number of address bits needed to access the FIFO. If you attempt to set this

                 value greater than or equal to the depth of the FIFO, this field is not written and

                 retains its current value.

                 When the number of transmit FIFO entries is less than or equal to this value, the

                 transmit FIFO empty interrupt is triggered.

26.4.8 OSPI_RXFTLR

Offset: 0x001C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-11 OSPI_RXFTLR Register Description

Bit Symbol TypeDescription

31:5Rsvd   -     Reserved

4:0 RFT    rw    Receive FIFO Threshold

                 Controls the level of entries (or above) at which the receive FIFO controller triggers

                 an interrupt. The FIFO depth is configurable in the range 2-256. This register is sized

{dango                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                       968 / 1446

                                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   to the number of address bits needed to access the FIFO. If you attempt to set this

                   value greater than the depth of the FIFO, this field is not written and retains its

                   current value. When the number of receive FIFO entries is greater than or equal to

                   this value + 1, the receive FIFO full interrupt is triggered.

26.4.9 OSPI_TXFLR

Offset: 0x0020

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-12 OSPI_TXFLR Register Description

Bit   Symbol    Type  Description

31:5  Rsvd      -     Reserved

4:0   TXTFL     r     Transmit FIFO Level

                      Contains the number of valid data entries in the transmit FIFO.

26.4.10         OSPI_RXFLR

Offset: 0x0024

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-13 OSPI_RXFLR Register Description

Bit   Symbol    Type  Description

31:5  Rsvd      -     Reserved

4:0   RXTFL     r     Receive FIFO Level

                      Contains the number of valid data entries in the receive FIFO.

26.4.11         OSPI_SR

Offset: 0x0028

Reset: 0x00000006

Table 26-14 OSPI_SR Register Description

Bit  Symbol TypeDescription

31:7Rsvd     -     Reserved

6    DCOL    r     Data Collision Error

                   Relevant only when the SPI is configured as a master device. This bit will be set if

                   ss_in_n input is asserted by other master, when the SPI master is in the middle of

                   the transfer. This informs the processor that the last data transfer was halted before

                   completion. This bit is cleared when read.
    dango                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                    969 / 1446

                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

               0x0: No Error

               0x1: Transmit Data Collision Error

5   TXE    r   Transmission Error

               Set if the transmit FIFO is empty when a transfer is started. This bit can be set only

               when the OSPI is configured as a slave device. Data from the previous transmission is

               resent on the txd line. This bit is cleared when read.

               0x1: Transmission Error

               0x0: No Error

4   RFF    r   Receive FIFO Full

               When the receive FIFO is completely full, this bit is set.

               When the receive FIFO contains one or more empty location, this bit is cleared.

               0x0: Receive FIFO is not full

               0x1: Receive FIFO is full

3   RFNE   r   Receive FIFO Not Empty

               Set when the receive FIFO contains one or more entries and is cleared when the

               receive FIFO is empty. This bit can be polled by software to completely empty the

               receive FIFO.

               0x0: Receive FIFO is empty

               0x1: Receive FIFO is not empty

2   TFE    r   Transmit FIFO Empty

               When the transmit FIFO is completely empty, this bit is set.

               When the transmit FIFO contains one or more valid entries, this bit is cleared. This bit

               field does not request an interrupt.

               0x0: Transmit FIFO is not empty

               0x1: Transmit FIFO is empty

1   TFNF   r   Transmit FIFO Not Full

               Set when the transmit FIFO contains one or more empty locations, and is cleared

               when the FIFO is full.

               0x0: Transmit FIFO is full

               0x1: Transmit FIFO is not Full

0   BUSY   r   OSPI Busy Flag

               When set, indicates that a serial transfer is in progress; when cleared indicates that

                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        970 / 1446

                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

               the SPI is idle or disabled.

               0x0: SPI is idle or disabled

               0x1: SPI is actively transferring data

Offset: 0x004C

Table 26-22 OSPI_DMACR Register Description

Bit   Symbol   Type  Description

31:2  Rsvd     r     Reserved

1     TDMAE    rw    Transmit DMA Enable

                     This bit enables/disables the transmit FIFO DMA channel.

                     0x0: Transmit DMA disabled

                     0x1: Transmit DMA enabled

                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                    974 / 1446

                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

0   RDMAE      rw   Receive DMA Enable

                    This bit enables/disables the receive FIFO DMA channel

                    0x0: Receive DMA disabled

26.4.20        OSPI_DMATDLR

Offset: 0x0050

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-23 OSPI_DMATDLR Register Description

Bit Symbol TypeDescription

31:5Rsvd    r   Reserved

4:0 DMATDLrw    Transmit Data Level

                This bit field controls the level at which a DMA request is made by the transmit logic.

                It is equal to the watermark level; that is, the dma_tx_req signal is generated when

                the number of valid data entries in the transmit FIFO is equal to or below this field

                value.

                dma_tx_req is asserted when DMATDL or less data entries are present in the

                transmit FIFO

26.4.21        OSPI_DMARDLR

Offset: 0x0054

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 26-24 OSPI_DMARDLR Register Description

Bit Symbol  TypeDescription

31:5Rsvd    r   Reserved

4:0 DMARDLrw    Receive Data Level

                This bit field controls the level at which a DMA request is made by the receive logic.

                The watermark level = DMARDL+1; that is, dma_rx_req is generated when the

                number of valid data entries in the receive FIFO is equal to or above this field value

                + 1.

                dma_rx_req is asserted when DMARDL or more valid data entries are present in the

                receive FIFO.



{dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    975 / 1446

                                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    26.4.22          OSPI_DRx(0~35)

    Offset: 0x0060 + 0x04*x

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Writing to any of these address locations has the same effect as pushing the data from into the transmit

    FIFO. Reading from any of these locations has the same effect as popping data from the receive FIFO.

    The FIFO buffers on the SPI are not addressable.

    Table 26-25 OSPI_DRx Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

    31:0DR     rw     Data Register

                      When writing to this register, you must right justify the data. Read data are

                      automatically right justified.

                      Read = Receive FIFO buffer

                      Write = Transmit FIFO buffer.

    26.4.23          OSPI_RX_SAMPLE_DLY

    Offset: 0x00F0

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 26-26 OSPI_RX_SAMPLE_DLY Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

    31:8Rsvd     r    Reserved

    7:0 RSD      rw   Rxd Sample Delay

                      This register is used to delay the sample of the rxd input port. Each value represents

                      a single sclk delay on the sample of rxd.

                      Note: If this register is programmed with a value that exceeds the depth of the

                      internal shift registers zero delay will be applied to the rxd sample.

    26.4.24          OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0

    Offset: 0x00F4

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 26-27 OSPI_SPI_CTRLR0 Register Description

    Bit Symbol             TypeDescription

    31:26Rsvd              r   Reserved

25 SPI_RXDS_SIG_ENrw Enable rxds signaling during address and command phase of Hypebus

                                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                              976 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                         transfer.

                         This bit enables rxds signaling by Hyperbus slave devices during

                         Command-Address (CA) phase. If the rxds signal is set to 1 during the

                         CA phase of transfer, OSPI transmits (2*SPI_CTRLR0.WAIT_CYCLES-1)

                         wait cycles after the address phase is complete.

24    SPI_DM_EN     rw   SPI data mask enable bit

                         When this bit is enabled, the txd_dmsignal is used to mask the data on

                         the txd data line.

23:21Rsvd           r    Reserved

20    XIP_INST_EN   rw   XIP instruction enable bit

                         If this bit is set to 1 then XIP transfers will also have instruction phase.

19    XIP_DFS_HC    rw   Fix DFS for XIP transfers

                         If this bit is set to 1 then data frame size for XIP transfers will be fixed

                         to the programmed value in OSPI_CTRLR0.DFS.

18    SPI_RXDS_EN   rw   Read data strobe enable bit

    Once this bit is set to 1 OSPI will use Read data strobe (rxds) to capture

                         read data in DDR mode.

                         Only valid in Hyperbus mode.

17    Rsvd          r    Reserved

16    SPI_DDR_EN    rw   SPI DDR Enable bit

                         This will enable Dual-data rate transfers in Dual/Quad/Octal frame

                         formats of SPI.

                         Only valid in Hyperbus mode.

15:11WAIT_CYCLES    rw   Wait cycles in Dual/Quad/Octal mode between control frames transmit

                         and data reception. Specified as number of SPI clock cycles.

10    Rsvd          r    Reserved

9:8   INST_L        rw   Dual/Quad/Octal mode instruction length in bits.

                         0x0: No Instruction

                         0x1: 4 bit Instruction length

                         0x2: 8 bit Instruction length

                         0x3: 16 bit Instruction length

7:6   Rsvd          r    Reserved

                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 977 / 1446

                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

5:2 ADDR_L      rw     This bit defines Length of Address to be transmitted. Only after this much

                       bits are programmed in to the FIFO the transfer can begin.

                       0x0: No Address

                       0x1: 4 bit Address length

                       0x2: 8 bit Address length

                       0x3: 12 bit Address length

                       0x4: 16 bit Address length

                       0x5: 20 bit Address length

                       0x6: 24 bit Address length

                       0x7: 28 bit Address length

                       0x8: 32 bit Address length

                       0x9: 36 bit Address length

                       0xA: 40 bit Address length

                       0xB: 44 bit Address length

                       0xC: 48 bit Address length

                       0xD: 52 bit Address length

                       0xE: 56 bit Address length

                       0xF: 60 bit Address length

1:0 TRANS_TYPE    rw   Address and instruction transfer format.

                       Selects whether OSPI will transmit instruction/address either in Standard

                       SPI mode or the SPI mode selected in OSPI_CTRLR0.SPI_FRF field.

                       0x0: Instruction and Address will be sent in Standard PI Mode.

                       0x1: Instruction will be sent in Standard SPI Mode and Address will be

                       sent in the mode specified by OSPI_CTRLR0.SPI_FRF.

                       0x2: Both Instruction and Address will be sent in the mode specified by

                       SPI_FRF.

                       0x3: Reserved.

26.4.25     OSPI_DDR_DRIVE_EDGE

Offset: 0x00F8

Reset: 0x0000_0000

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   27 Secure Digital Input and Output
    (SDIO)

   27.1 Overview


This document contains the User Specification of the SD Host core compatible with SD Host Controller

Standard Specification version 4.00. The host controller serves devices compatible with SD Memory

version 4.00 (SD, SDHC, SDXC), SDIO version 4.00, MMC/eMMC version 5.1. This host controller does

not support the UHS-II Interface which was introduced in version 4.00 as optional. The document

provides the core architecture description, register set description and sub-modules specification.


   27.2 Features


    • The SDIO provides an interface between the bus and eMMC, SD memory cards, SDIO cards.

    SD Host Controller Standard Specification version 4.00

    SD Physical Layer Specification version 4.00

    SDIO Specification version 4.00

    eMMC Specification 5.1

    Three data bus modes: 1-bit, 4-bit, 8-bit

    SD cards support for two data bus modes: 1-bit, 4-bit

    Data and command output
