27.3.1 SDIO_INFO

Offset: 0x0000

Reset: 0x0001_0000

Table 27-2 SDIO_INFO Register Description

Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31:24Rsvd    r    Reserved

23:16SAV     r    Slot Available

                  This field provides a number of implemented slots. Each bit of the field is assigned to

                  one of the slots (slot 0 is assigned to bit 16 of INFO register, slot 1 to bit 17, etc.). If

                  given slot is implemented, the bit is read as 1. Otherwise, if given slot in

                  unavailable, corresponding bit is 0. The slot 0 is always available. If slot n is

                  implemented, the slot n-1 also is implemented. This configuration supports one slot

                  and this field is equal to 0x01.

15:1  Rsvd   r    Reserved

0     SWR    rw   Software Reset

                  When set to 1, the entire core is reset. After reset operation complete, SWR bit is

                  automatically cleared. It takes some time to complete the requested reset

                  operation, so the software should always poll SWR bit status, and continue the other

                  operations only when SWR is cleared to 0.

                  There is no difference between SWR and SDIO_CTRL2.SRFA software resets. Both

                  resets the same flip-flops.

27.3.2 SDIO_DEBOUNCE

Offset: 0x0004

Reset: 0x0000_0032

Table 27-3 SDIO_DEBOUNCE Register Description

Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31:24Rsvd   r     Reserved





fi                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    987 / 1446

                                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

23:0  DP     rw    De-bounce Period

                   Defines the number of system (clk) clock cycles used by the de-bounce logic, which

                   detects card insertion and removal events. The de-bounce period is equal to DP *

                   tclk, where tclk is the period of clock. Typically, DP value should be chosen to obtain

                   the period of 20ms.

27.3.3 SDIO_CTRL0

Offset: 0x0008

Reset: 0x0003_0000

Table 27-4 SDIO_CTRL0 Register Description

Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31:18Rsvd     r    Reserved

17:16OTN      rw   Number of Outstanding Transfers

                   Specifies number of outstanding transfers on DMA (Master) interface. The number of

                   out-standings is (OTN + 1), where OTN can be defined in range 0 to 3.

15:4  Rsvd    r    Reserved

3:0   PBL     rw   Programmable Burst Length

                   This field defines a maximum number of beats in DMA burst.

                   The value can be changed when no active transfer.

                   0001b: 1 beat in burst

                   0010b: 2 beats in burst

                   0011b: 4 beats in burst

                   0100b: 8 bits in burst

                   Other: 16 beats in burst

27.3.4 SDIO_PHY

Offset: 0x0010

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-5 SDIO_PHY Register Description

Bit   Symbol       TypeDescription

31:27Rsvd          - Reserved

fi                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                        988 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

26    ACK         r    PHY Transaction Acknowledge

                       This bit is set 1 when UIS_WR or UIS_RD is confirmed, so either read data is

                       available in UIS_RDATA, or written data is already updated PHY register.

25    RD          rw   PHY Read Transaction Request

                       This bit requests read transaction from PHY Register.

24    WR          rw   PHY Write Transaction Requests

                       This bit requests write transaction to PHY Register.

23:16RDATA        r    PHY Read Data

                       This field stories data read from PHY Register.

15:8  WDATA       rw   PHY Write Data

                       This field stories data to be written to PHY Register.

7:6   Rsvd        -    Reserved

5:0   ADDR        rw   PHY Register Address

                       This filed stories address of PHY Register which is to be read of written. Refer to

                       the Integration Manual (cdns_sd4hc_integration_manual.pdf), Table:

                       Remapping register address to address of registers described in SD/eMMC PHY

                       User Guide (cdns_sd_emmc_dll_phy_user_guide.pdf).

27.3.5 SDIO_MODE

Offset: 0x0018

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-6 SDIO_MODE Register Description

Bit  Symbol TypeDescription

31:3Rsvd    -    Reserved

2:0  EMM    rw   Mode select

                 This field sets mode. The mode should reflect to the device setting. If the SD card is

                 in use, this field needs to be 0000b.

                 0000b: SD Card in use

                 0010b: SDR

                 0011b: DDR

                 0100b: HS200

{dango                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 989 / 1446

                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

              0101b: HS400

              0110b: HS400 Enhanced Strobe

              Others: Legacy

27.3.6 SDIO_IODELAY

Offset: 0x001C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-7 SDIO_IODELAY Register Description

Bit  Symbol    TypeDescription

31:21Rsvd      -    Reserved

20:16ODELAY    rw   Output delay value for IO.

                    Designer should update this register with delay value of IO with appropriate

                    output delay.

                    Delay is count in half of period of sdmclk. If sdmclk is working at 200MHz

                    frequency, then 1 is 2.5 ns.

                    This value will be used to compensate delay of DAT line when controller is

                    READ WAIT. The sum value of IDELAY_VAL, ODELAY_VAL should be less equal

                    5’d20 for controller to work properly.

15:5  Rsvd     -    Reserved

4:0   IDELAY   rw   Input delay value for IO.

                    Designer should update this register with delay value of IO with appropriate

                    input delay.

                    Delay is count in half of period of sdmclk. If sdmclk is working at 200MHz

                    frequency, then 1 is 2.5 ns.

                    This value will be used to compensate delay of DAT line when controller is

                    driving READ WAIT.

                    The sum value of IDELAY_VAL, ODELAY_VAL should be less equal 5’d20 for

                    controller to work properly.

27.3.7 SDIO_INFO1

Offset: 0x0078


fi                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    990 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Reset: 0x0000_0001

Table 27-8 SDIO_INFO1 Register Description

Bit     Symbol               Type   Description

31:2    Rsvd                 -      Reserved

1       HS400_ESS            r      High Speed 400 Enhance Strobe supported

                                    1: Supported

                                    0: Not supported

0       CQS                  r      Command Queuing supported

                                    1: Supported

                                    0: Not supported

27.3.8 SDIO_HCV

Offset: 0x007C

Reset: 0x0411_0000

Table 27-9 SDIO_HCV Register Description

Bit      Symbol       Type  Description

31:28    Rsvd         -     Reserved

27:16    HCV          r     Host Controller Version

                            Release number of the Host Controller

15:8     Rsvd         -     Reserved

7:0      FVN          r     Fix Version Number

                            Number of the fix related to the Host Controller Version.

27.3.9 SDIO_FSMM

Offset: 0x0080

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-10 SDIO_FSMM Register Description

Bit    Symbol TypeDescription

31     LOAD     rw   FSM monitor update request

                     Setting this bit to 1 starts internal FSM monitor to load value from selected FSM.

fi                               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                           991 / 1446

                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 After finishing this bit will be automatically cleared by hardware and FSM status can

                 be read.

30:16ADDR    w   FSM address

                 This field selects which FSM status will be read. All available status machines are

                 listed in Debug section of User Guide.

15:0  DATA   r   FSM status

                 This register contains read FSM status. Before reading it user should select FSM

                 address (ADDR), set LOAD bit and wait until hardware clears it.

27.3.10         SDIO_STATUS0

Offset: 0x0084

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-11 SDIO_STATUS0 Register Description

Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31:0STAT0 r     Tune status 0

                After invoking UHS-I tuning procedure each bit of this register represents status of

                one tuning step.

                This field correspond to tuning steps 31-0.

                0: Step failed

                1: Step passed

27.3.11         SDIO_STATUS1

Offset: 0x0088

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Bit    Symbol     Type   Description

31:8   Rsvd       -      Reserved

7:0    STAT1      r      Tune status 1

                         This field is continuation of STAT0 field.

                         Its value represents status of tuning steps 39-32.


fi                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    992 / 1446

                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    27.3.12           SDIO_TUNE_REQ

    Offset: 0x008C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 27-13 SDIO_TUNE_REQ Register Description

    Bit    Symbol TypeDescription

    31     TFR    rw     Tune Force Request

                         Setting this bit 1, the tune value is updated by TFV. This bit is set 1 until the

                         requested value is applied. The tuning command request is to be set after this bit is

                         automatically clear to 0.

    30:22Rsvd     -      Reserved

    21:16TFV      rw     Tune Force Value

                         This register contains value which can update the tune value and in consequence

                         sample point.

                         The new tune value becomes valid when the TFR transits from 1 to 0.

    15:6   Rsvd   -      Reserved

    5:0    TVAL   r      Tune Current Value

                         This field represents selected value of UHS-I delay.

    27.3.13           SDIO_BOOTERR

    Offset: 0x0090

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Bit   Symbol      TypeDescription

    31:6Rsvd          -   Reserved

    5     BOOTE_EBE r     Boot Error - End Bit Error

    4     BOOTE_DCEr      Boot Error - Data CRC Error

    3     BOOTE_DTE r     Boot Error - Data Timeout Error

2 BOOTE_IAE r Boot Error - Invalid Acknowledge Error

1 BOOTE_ATE r Boot Error - Acknowledge Timeout Error


    fi                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    993 / 1446

27.3.14     SDIO_SPEED

Offset: 0x0094

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-15 SDIO_SPEED Register Description

Bit   Symbol      TypeDescription

31:6Rsvd          -    Reserved

5:0   RGB_COEFF_IFMrw  Read Block Gap Coefficient

                       This register selects a target Read Block Coefficient.

                       The Host Controller has a set of Read Block Coefficient registers. Each SD

                       and eMMC speed mode has one dedicated register. While RGBC is updated

                       to set a Coefficient value, this register selects a target Read Block

                       Coefficient. Following list shows the addresses to speed modes relation:

                       000000b: Default Speed (DS)

                       000001b: High Speed (HS)

                       001000b: UHS-I SDR12

                       001001b: UHS-I SDR25

                       001010b: UHS-I SDR50

                       001100b: UHS-I DDR50

                       100000b: eMMC Legacy

                       100001b: eMMC SDR

                       100010b: eMMC DDR

27.3.15     SDIO_RBGC

Offset: 0x0098

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-16 SDIO_RBGC Register Description

Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

fi                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            994 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

31:4Rsvd    -     Reserved

3:0  RBGC    rw   Read block gap coefficient.

                  Read block gap timing can be variable based on pad timing when the clock is stopped

                  for flow control or stop at block gap purposes. When stopping the clock the host

                  controller has default settings for each interface mode that ensures read block gap

                  timing is met with pad delays up to 5ns (input and output path). If pad delays are

                  longer than 5ns then the default settings can changed (increased) to ensure read

                  block gap timing is not violated.

                  RBGC has a register for each interface mode and the interface mode is selected by

                  using RBGC.

27.3.16     SDIO_HCVSIS

Offset: 0x00FC

Reset: 0x0003_0000

Table 27-17 SDIO_HCVSIS Register Description

Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31:24Rsvd     r    Reserved

23:16SVN      r    Specification Version Number.

                   This field identifies the Host Controller Specification Version.

                   0h: Version 1.00

                   1h: Version 2.00

                   2h: Version 3.00

                   3h: Version 4.00

                   4h-FFh: Reserved

                   The host is compatible with SD Host Specification Version 4.00.

                   HWINIT Field - Note this field is hardware initialized after reset and the value read

                   back will match the IP configuration.

15:8  Rsvd    r    Reserved

7:0   ISES    r    Interrupt Signal For Each Slot.

                   This field allows to determine which slot is currently signaling Interrupt/Wakeup

                   signals. Each bit is logical OR of all Interrupts Signal and Wakeup Signal

{dango                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                      995 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                         implemented in given slot.

                         Bit 0: Slot 1

                         Bit 1: Slot 2

                         ...

                         Bit 7: Slot 8

    27.3.17       SDIO_ADDR

    Offset: 0x0200

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 27-18 SDIO_ADDR Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31:0SAAR rw System Address / Argument 2 This field is used as:

                      -   SDMA system memory address
                      -   Auto CMD23 Argument System Address:

                      This register is used as base address when SDMA engine is selected

                      (SDIO_CMD.DMAE=1 and SDIO_CTRL1.DMASEL=0) and SDIO_ACES.HV4E=0. When

                      the SDMA has stopped at the SDMA block gap, writing to SAAR[31:24] resumes

                      SDMA transfer. If the SDMA engine is stopped, value of the SAAR[31:24] register is

                      set to the next contiguous system address of the next data position Auto CMD23

                      Argument:

                      This mode is used when non-DMA or ADMA2 engine is selected or

                      SDIO_ACES.HV4E=1. When the block count is enabled, software will set value from

                      range 00000000h to 0000FFFFh.

    27.3.18       SDIO_BLOCK_COUNT

    Offset: 0x0204

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 27-19 SDIO_BLOCK_COUNT Register Description

    Bit   Symbol  TypeDescription

    31:16BCCT     rw      Block Count For Current Transfer

                          With this field, the number of data blocks can be de- fined for next transfer.

    fi                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               996 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 This register is used when SDIO_CMD.BCE is set 1, other- wise it will be ignored.

                 The value is decremented after each block transfer. When this field is set 0, no

                 data blocks will be transferred.

                 During data transfer read operation may return invalid value, and write operations

                 are ignored.

                 0000h: no block transfer

                 0001h...FFFF (h): 1...65535 block(s) transfer.

15   Rsvd -      Reserved

14:12SDMABB rw   SDMA Buffer Boundary

                 In this field, the system address boundary can be set for SDMA engine.

                 The SDMA transfer stops crossing the address boundary and generates the DMA

                 Interrupt (SDIO_STATUS3.DMAINT).

                 After the DMA Interrupt, when the SDIO_ACES.HV4E is 0, the software should

                 write new SDMA System Address (SDIO_ADDR.SAAR / SDIO_ACES.DMASA1) in

                 order to resume the SDMA transaction.

                 0: 4k bytes address boundary

                 1: 8k bytes address boundary

                 2: 16k bytes address boundary

                 3: 32k bytes address boundary

                 4: 64k bytes address boundary

                 5: 128k bytes address boundary

                 6: 256k bytes address boundary

                 7: 512k bytes address boundary

11:0 TBS  rw     Transfer Block Size

                 This field defines block size for block data transfers. During data transfer, read

                 operations may return an invalid value, and write operations are ignored.

                 The software will not set value that exceeds the physically implemented internal

                 FIFO buffer size. The buffer size is equal to 2 FIFODEPTH, where FI- FODEPTH is

                 the generic parameter of the core.

                 The SD/MMC (memory) uses block size up to 512 bytes.

                 The SDIO can use up to 2048 bytes.

                 000h: not used
    {dango           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   997 / 1446

                                                                  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  001h: 1 data block

                  002h: 2 data blocks

                  003h: 3 data blocks

                  ...

                  1FFh: 511 data blocks

                  200h: 512 data blocks

                  ...

                  800h: 2048 data blocks

                  Others: not used.

                  Note: It is recommended for the software to use native data block size (512B) in

                  case of multiple data block transfer (CTM.MSBS==1). Using smaller block may

                  cause unexpected response error when flow control is activated (i.e. SDCLK is

                  disabled) during response transfer.

27.3.19         SDIO_ARG

Offset: 0x0208

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-20 SDIO_ARG Register Description

Bit   Symbol Type Description

31:0 ARG1  rw    Command Argument 1

                 This field contains 32-bits argument for command issued by CTM.CIDX file write.

27.3.20         SDIO_CMD

Offset: 0x020c

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-21 SDIO_CMD Register Description

Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31:30Rsvd   -    Reserved

29:24CIDX   rw   Command Index

                 This field contains a command number (index) of the command to be sent.

                 The index can be defined in range 00-63, which means all commands (CMD00-

fi                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 998 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 CMD63 and ACMD00- ACMD63) defined in related specifications are supported.

                 Writing this filed triggers the actual command transfer. This field is to be written only

    when Command Inhibit CMD bit is 0 in Present State Register (PRS.CICMD). To

                 check the list of available commands, refer to the appropriate card/device

                 specifications.

23:22CT    rw    Command Type

                 This field defines specific type of command.

                 Normal Command (00b): Used by default when other command types are not

                 intended to be used

                 Suspend Command (01b): Not used

                 Resume Command (10b): Not used

                 Abort Command (11b): Used when the software wants to stop the current data

                 transfer (read or write data transfer).

                 The read transfer ends by stopping transfer to the internal buffer. The write transfer

                 ends with releasing DAT line to High-Z state. Then, after sending an Abort Command,

                 the software will issue the software reset.

                 The Suspend and Resume Mechanism is not supported by the SD Host version 4.00

                 and later, and the Suspend and Resume Commands will not be used.

21   DPS    rw   Data Present Select

                 Set to 1 for commands which transfer data (i.e. read or write data using DAT line).

                 Set to 0 for all other commands, including:

                 - Commands using only CMD line
                 - Commands with busy signalized on DAT[0] line (CTM.RTS=11b)

20   CICE   rw   Command Index Check Enable

                 When set to 1, the host checks if the Command Index field in the response is equal

                 to the CTM.CIDX value. When 0, the check is not performed and Command Index

                 field of the response is ignored.

                 Recommended settings depends on response type, see following table for details:

                 CTM.RTS=00: 0 - No Response

                 CTM.RTS=01: 0 - R2

                 CTM.RTS=10: 0 - R3, R4

                 CTM.RTS=10: 1 - R1, R5, R6, R7

                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                999 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  CTM.RTS=11: 1 - R1b, R5b

19    CRCCE rw    Command CRC Check Enable

                  When set to 1, the host checks if the CRC field of the response is valid.

                  When 0, the CRC check is disabled and the CRC field of the response is ignored.

                  The CRC check should be disabled for responses which do not contain an actual CRC

                  value (some responses contain all 1s in place of the CRC field), and enabled for all

                  other kinds of responses.

                  Recommended settings depends on response type, see following table for details:

                  CTM.RTS=00: 0 - No Response

                  CTM.RTS=01: 0 - R2

                  CTM.RTS=10: 0 - R3, R4

                  CTM.RTS=10: 1 - R1, R5, R6, R7

                  CTM.RTS=11: 1 - R1b, R5b

18    Rsvd   -    Reserved

17:16RTS     rw   Response Type Select

                  Defines the expected response length.

                  00b: no response

                  01b: 136-bit response

                  10b: 48-bit response

                  11b: 48-bit response with BUSY

                  Every command implies one of the response types listed above. To check the

                  response type corresponding to a given command, please refer to the appropriate

                  card/device specifications.

15:9  Rsvd   -    Reserved

8     RID    rw   Response Interrupt Disable

                  When set to 1, the Command Complete Interrupt (ENIS.CC) will be disabled. The

                  host will ignore the ENSE.CC_SE and behave as the ENSE.CC_SE would be 0.

                  When set to 0, the ENIS.CC will be enabled or disabled depend on the ENSE.CC_SE

                  bit only.

7     RECE   rw   Response Error Check Enable

                  When set 1, the host will look after R1/R5 responses. If any error will be detected in

                  the response, the ENIS.ERSP bit is set to 1.
{dango                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                      1000 / 1446

                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 The software will set this bit only when R1/R5 response is expected.

                 The software will set CTM.RID and RECE bits to 1 when the host checks R1/R5

                 errors. And both bits will be clear to 0, when the Software Driver will checks R1/R5

                 errors. On response error, the ENIS.ERSP bit (in Interrupt Status) is set 1.

6   RECT rw      Response Type R1/R5

                 Select R1 or R5 response type for the response content checker. Listed below error

                 bits will be evaluated.

                 RECT = 0, Response Type - R1 (SD Memory):

                 bit 31 OUT_OF_RANGE

                 bit 30 ADDRESS_ERROR

                 bit 29 BLOCK_LEN_ERROR

                 bit 26 WP_VIOLATION

                 bit 25 CARD_IS_LOCKED

                 bit 23 COM_CRC_ERROR

                 bit 21 CARD_ECC_FAILED

                 bit 20 CC_ERROR

                 bit 19 ERRORRECT = 1

                 RECT = 1, Response Type - R5 (SDIO):

                 bit 7 COM_CRC_ERROR

                 bit 3 ERROR

                 bit 1 FUNCTION_NUMBER

                 bit 0 OUT_OF_RANGE

                 This field is ignored when CTM.RECE=0.

5   MSBS   rw    Multi/Single Block Select

                 Multi-block or single-block data transfer can be selected with this field.

                 0: Single-block

                 1: Multi-block

                 This field is hardware-protected by Command Inhibit DAT bit in SDIO_STATUS2

                 Register (SDIO_STATUS2.CIDAT). When CIDAT=1, all writes to this field are ignored.

4   DTDS   rw    Data Transfer Direction Select

                 Selects direction of data transfer for commands with DPS=1.

                 0: Write
       {dango        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1001 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

        1: Read

        For commands with CTM.DPS=0, this field is ignored. This field is hardware-

        protected by Command Inhibit DAT bit in SDIO_STATUS2 Register

        (SDIO_STATUS2.CIDAT). When CIDAT=1, all writes to this field are ignored.

3:2 ACE rw Auto CMD Enable

        The field allows to send one additional command to the card/device when the

        command is issued.

        00b: No auto command

        01b: Auto CMD12

        10b: Auto CMD23

        11b: Reserved

        If Auto CMD disable (00b) is set, the host does not send any additional command.

        This setting will be used when auto command is not required or not intended.

        If Auto CMD12 (01b) is set, the host sends CMD12 (Abort) automatically when last

        block of multi-block transfer is completed.

        If Auto CMD23 (10b) is set, the host sends CMD23 (Set Block

        Count) automatically before issued transfer data command.

        An argument of this command can be set in SDMA. On any error the issued

        command will not be sent. This field is hardware-protected by Command Inhibit DAT

        bit in SDIO_STATUS2 Register (SDIO_STATUS2.CIDAT). When PRS.CIDAT=1, all

        writes to this field are ignored.

1 BCE rw Block Count Enable

        When set to 1, the BSBC.BCCT Block Count is enabled.

        Each transferred block automatically decrements the counter value. The multi -block

        transfer will end, when the counter reaches 0. So the finite transfer can not be

        performed with this setting.

        When 0, block counting is disabled, and BSBC.BCCT retains its value.

        The transfer will be infinite in non-DMA and SDMA modes. For ADMA mode the

        transfer can be infinite or finite. The finite transfer ends on reading the descriptor

        with END status (so the transfer length is designated by the table of descriptors). In

        case of infinite transfer, the software will explicitly set ABORT command type to stop

        transfer.
{dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1002 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  This field is hardware-protected by Command Inhibit DAT bit in SDIO_STATUS2

                  Register (SDIO_STATUS2.CIDAT). When CIDAT=1, all writes to this field are ignored.

0   DMAE     rw   DMA Enable

                  When set to 1, it enables DMA functionality. DMA can be enabled only if it is

                  supported as indicated in the DMA Support in the CAP1.DMAS register. If DMA is not

                  supported (due to host configuration), this bit is ignored.

                  This field is hardware-protected by Command Inhibit DAT bit in SDIO_STATUS2

                  Register (SDIO_STATUS2.CIDAT).

                  When SDIO_STATUS2.CIDAT=1, all writes to this field are ignored.

                  Note: The ADMA2 mode uses only the finite transfer mode, i.e. this bit is to be set

                  1.

27.3.21     SDIO_RESP1

Offset: 0x0210

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-22 SDIO_RESP1 Register Description

Bit Symbol TypeDescription

31:0RESP1 r      The RESP1 - RESP4 registers store the response returned by the card.

                 The mapping of the actual device response and the RESP1 - RESP4 contents depends

                 on the type of response. The type of response is determined by the RTS field

                 (Response Type) for all user-defined commands.

                 The separate cases are the Auto-CMD12 response (called R1b in the SD Memory

                 Specification) and Auto-CMD23 response (called R1 in the SD Memory Specification).

                 Auto-CMD12 and Auto-CMD23 responses are handled by the core automatically and

                 goes to the RESP4 register regardless of the RTS value.

                 RESP1-RESP4 relation to received response field:

                 Auto-CMD12 resp: Response field R[39:8] - RESP3[31:0]

                 Auto-CMD23 resp: Response field R[39:8] - RESP3[31:0]

                 No response: RTS=00b

                 136-bit: RTS=01b, Response field R[127:8]

                 - RESP3[23:0], RESP2[31:0], RESP1[31:0], RESP0[31:0]

{dango                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   1003 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

              48-bit:RTS=10b, Response field R[39:8] - RESP1[31:0] 48-bit with BUSY:RTS=11b,

              Response field R[39:8] - RESP1[31:0]

              Implementation note: Registers value are undefined after reset, and will be valid after

              response is received.

27.3.22     SDIO_RESP2

Offset: 0x0214

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-23 SDIO_RESP2 Register Description

Bit    Symbol     Type   Description

31:0   RESP2      r      RESP2 - Response Register #2

27.3.23     SDIO_RESP3

Offset: 0x0218

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-24 SDIO_RESP3 Register Description

Bit    Symbol     Type   Description

31:0   RESP3      r      RESP3 - Response Register #3

27.3.24     SDIO_RESP4

Offset: 0x021c

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-25 SDIO_RESP4 Register Description

Bit    Symbol     Type   Description

31:0   RESP4      r      RESP4 - Response Register #4

27.3.25     SDIO_DATA

Offset: 0x0220

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-26 SDIO_DATA Register Description

fi                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   1004 / 1446

                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Bit Symbol TypeDescription

31:0BDP rw    Buffer Data Port

              The field is to access the internal buffer (data block) in non DMA transfer mode. 8-bit,

              16-bit, or 32-bit access to BDP is possible with the following restrictions:

              -  Only sequential contiguous access in Little Endian mode is possible. For example,

              if the software accesses BDP[7:0], then the next transfer will access BDP[15:8]. No

              byte skipping is allowed.

    - Each new block will start at the least significant byte of BDP, which is BDP[7:0].
    - If the block size is not a multiple of 32-bits, and the soft- ware accesses BDP using

              32-bit words, then the excess bytes of the last word are ignored. This allows the soft-

              ware driver to use only 32-bit data transfers regardless of the block size.

              -    Access to the register with precaution - the FIFO pointers can be damaged when

              buffer is not ready or when number of accesses exceed the transfer block size

              (BSBC.TBS).

              Following shows all transfers (byte enable variations) that are allowed on BDP:

              Transfer width = 32-bit:

              be[3:0] = ’b1111 -> BDP[31:0]

              Transfer width = 16-bit:

              be[3:0] = ’b0011 -> BDP[15:0]

              be[3:0] = ’b1100 -> BDP[31:16]

              Transfer width = 8-bit:

              be[3:0] = ’b0001 -> BDP[7:0]

              be[3:0] = ’b0010 -> BDP[15:8]

              be[3:0] = ’b0100 -> BDP[23:16]

              be[3:0] = ’b1000 -> BDP[31:24]

27.3.26     SDIO_STATUS2

Offset: 0x0224

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-27 SDIO_STATUS2 Register Description

Bit Symbol TypeDescription

{dango               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1005 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

31:25Rsvd  -    Reserved

24   CMDSL r    CMD Line Signal Level (SD Mode only)

                The value is equal to the actual signal level on CMD line of the SD interface.

                Is useful for debugging purposes.

23:20DATSL1 r   DAT[3:0] Line Signal Level (SD Mode only)

                The value is equal to the actual signal level on DAT pins of the SD interface:

                PRS.23: dat_i[3] pin level

                PRS.22: dat_i[2] pin level

                PRS.21: dat_i[1] pin level

                PRS.20: dat_i[0] pin level

19   WPSL  r    Write Protect Switch Pin Level

                The value is equal to the actual signal level on Write Protect pin of the SD interface.

                1: Means that the write operation is enabled

                0: Means that the write operations is disabled

18   CDSL  r    Card Detect Pin Level

                The value is equal to the inverted signal level on Card Detect pin of the SD

                interface.

                1: Means that the card is inserted

                0: Means no card is inside the slot

                De-bouncing is not performed on CDSL, therefore the use of Card Inserted (CI) bit

                is recommended during normal work.

                CDSL bit is useful only for debugging purposes.

17   CSS   r    Card State Stable

                Indicates if Card Detect Pin Level (CDSL) is stable.

                1: Means that the CDSL value is stable

                0: Means that the CDSL is not stable (during card insertion/removal or during the

                reset)

                Field is useful for debugging purposes.

16   CI    r    Card Inserted

                Indicates if the card is inserted inside the slot.

                0: No card in slot

                1: Card is inserted

                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                       1006 / 1446

                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                Unlike PRS.CDSL, value of PRS.CI bit is guaranteed to be stable (i.e. de-bouncing is

                performed on this bit). Use of this bit is recommended during the normal operation

                of host.

15:12Rsvd   -   Reserved

11   BRE    r   Buffer Read Enable

                This field represents data buffer (BDP.BDP) state for read transfer in non-DMA

                mode.

                1: Valid data can be read from the data buffer

                0: No valid data inside the data buffer

                After reading the entire data block, this bit changes to 0.

10   BWE    r   Buffer Write Enable

                This bit represents data buffer (BDP.BDP) state for write transfer in non-DMA mode.

                1: Data can be written to the data buffer

                0: Data cannot be written

                After reading the entire data block, this changes to 0. This bit will be cleared in case

                of SBGR at non-DMA write transfer (even if the internal buffer is ready). The buffer

                must not be written after the SBGR. If the BWR was set, the only action from the

                S/W is to clear the interrupt status.

9   RTA     r   Read Transfer Active (SD Mode only)

                Indicates the status of the read data transfer.

                0: No data read transfer in progress

                1: Data read transfer in progress

                Bit is set 1 after sending the read command, or after restarting the read transfer by

                the Continue Request (SDIO_CTRL1.CREQ).

                Bit is set 0 by the hardware after the last block of the read transfer, or after

                stopping the read transfer by the Stop at Block Gap Request (SDIO_CTRL1.SBGR).

                In both cases, the entire data is to be read by the system from the internal data

                buffer before setting this bit to 0. In other words, SDIO_STATUS2.RTA=0 means that

                the entire data is already transferred to the system, and internal data buffer is

                empty.

8   WTA     r   Write Transfer Active (SD Mode only)

                Indicates the status of the write data transfer.
{dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1007 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 0: No data write transfer in progress

                 1: Data write transfer in progress

                 Bit is set 1 after sending the write command, or after restarting the write transfer

                 by the Continue Request (HC1.CREQ). Bit is set 0 by the hardware after the last

                 block of the write transfer, or after stopping the write transfer by the Stop at Block

                 Gap Request (SDIO_CTRL1.SBGR).

                 In both cases, the entire data has to be transferred to the card from the internal data

                 buffer before setting this bit to 0.

                 In other words, WTA=0 means that the entire data is already transferred to the

                 card, and CRC response for the last data block is already received.

7:4   DATSL2 r   DAT[7:4] Line Signal Level (SD Mode only)

                 Field can be used to monitor DAT lines in range 7-4. The value is equal to the actual

                 signal level on DAT pins of the SD interface:

                 PRS.7: dat7_i_pin level

                 PRS.6: dat6_i_pin level

                 PRS.5: dat5_i_pin level

                 PRS.4: dat4_i_pin level

3     Rsvd   -   Reserved

2     DLA    r   DAT Line Active (SD Mode only)

                 Indicates if the DAT lines of SD interface are currently in use.

                 1: DAT lines are active (in use)

                 0: DAT lines are released (not in use)

                 This bit set to 1, when Read or Write Transfer bits are active

                 (SDIO_STATUS2.RTA=1 or SDIO_STATUS2.WTA=1), or if the card indicates busy

                 state on the DAT lines. The card can become busy immediately after the write

                 operation, or after command which requires response with busy.

                 Falling edge of this bit (change from 1 to 0) directly triggers Transfer Complete

                 Interrupt (ENIS.TC).

1    CIDAT   r   Command Inhibit DAT (SD Mode only)

                 Indicates if the host can issue a command which uses DAT line. Commands which

                 use DAT line include write and read data commands and commands with busy

                 response.
{dango               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                       1008 / 1446

                                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    1: Command using DAT line cannot be sent

                    0: Command using DAT line can be sent

                    When CIDAT=1 then the CTM[15:0] is write- protected. The software can write

                    CTM[15:0] only when CIDAT=0.

    0    CICMD r    Command Inhibit CMD

                    Indicates if the host can issue a command.

                    0: Command can be sent

                    1: Command cannot be sent

                    If this bit is 0, indicates the CMD line is not in use and the Host Controller can issue

                    an SD command using the CMD line.

                    This bit is set immediately after the CI is written, indicating start of command

                    transmission.

                    This bit is cleared when the command response is received.

                    Even if the Command Inhibit DAT is set to 1, commands using only the CMD line

                    can be issued if the Command Inhibit CMD is 0.

                    Change from 1 to 0 directly triggers Command Complete Interrupt (ENIS.CC).

    27.3.27        SDIO_CTRL1

    Offset: 0x0228

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 27-28 SDIO_CTRL1 Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:27Rsvd   -   Reserved

26 WORM rw Wakeup Event Enable On SD Card Removal

                    When set to 1, enables wake-up event via Card Removal assertion in the ENIS.CR

                    register.

25 WOIS rw Wake-Up Event Enable On Card Inserted

                    When set to 1, enables wake-up event via Card Insertion assertion in the ENIS.CIN

                    register.

24 WOIQ rw Wakeup Event Enable On Card Interrupt

                    When set to 1, enables wake-up event via Card Interrupt assertion in the

    {dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                        1009 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 ENIS.CINT

23:20Rsvd   -    Reserved

19   IBG    rw   Interrupt At Block Gap (SD Mode only)

                 When set to 1, enables interrupt detection at the block gap for a multiple block

                 transfer.

                 This bit is valid only in SD4 mode.

                 If the SD card cannot signal an interrupt during a multiple block transfer, this bit

                 should be set to 0.

                 This field is ignored in UHS-II mode.

18   RWC    rw   Read-Wait Control (SD Mode only)

                 When set to 1, enables Read Wait control. The Read Wait function is optional for

                 SDIO cards.

                 If the card does not support read wait, this bit would never be set to 1; otherwise,

                 DAT line conflict may occur.

                 This field is ignored in UHS-II mode.

17   CR     rw   Continue Request

                 When set to 1, restarts the transfer previously stopped using the Stop At Block

                 Gap.

                 The software will set HC1.SBGR (Stop At Block Gap) bit to 0 before setting the (CR)

                 Continue Request.

                 When HC1.SBGR=1, then all write operations to Continue Request are ignored.

                 Clearing HC1.SBGR can be done before or simultaneously with writing the CR.

                 Continue Request bit is cleared automatically by the host when PRS.DLA (Data Line

                 Active) changes from 0 to 1, indicating the actual restart of the transfer.

16  SBGR   rw    Stop At Block Gap Request

                 When set to 1, orders the stop executing read and write transaction at the next

                 possible block gap for non-DMA, SDMA and ADMA transfers. The soft- ware will

                 maintain SBGR=1 until the current transfer is complete (typically by waiting for -

                 Transfer Complete bit). After Transfer Complete event, the software will set SBGR

                 back to 0.

                 In case of the read transfer, the host stops after the next data block received from

                 the card. This uses the Read-Wait mechanism if it is enabled by HC1.RWC, or stops

                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                    1010 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  the card clock if Read-Wait is disabled.

                  In the case of the write transfer, host stops after the last block written to the data

                  buffer. The host sends all data already written to the internal data buffer be- fore

                  stopping the transfer.

                  In case of stopping non-DMA write transfer, the soft- ware will set this bit only at

                  block gap (block unit (SD mode)).

15:13BVS2    rw   SD Bus Voltage Select for VDD2

                  The field is used to configure VDD2 voltage level.

                  000b: VDD2 is not supported

                  001b-011b: Reserved

                  100b: Reserved for 1.2V

                  101b: 1.8V (1.8V VDD Support in Capability registers need to be set)

                  110b-111b: Not used

12    BP2    rw   SD Bus Power for VDD2

                  This bit is used to enable (1) or disable (0) VDD2 supply.

                  The field is ignored in legacy mode.

11:9  BVS    rw   SD Bus Voltage Select

                  This field is used to configure VDD1 voltage level.

                  000b-100b: Reserved

                  101b: 1.8V (typical) for embedded

                  110b: 3.0V (typical)

                  111b: 3.3V (typical)

                  others: Reserved.

8     BP     rw   SD Bus Power for VDD1

                  When set to 1, the VDD1 voltage is supplied to card/device.

                  Setting bit to 0 cause that host stops driving SDCLK, CMD/DAT lines. If the device is

                  connected to the host, lines go low before disabling VDD1.

                  The host will set this bit automatically to 0 when card is removed from the slot. This

                  is to provide the hot removal support.

7     CDSS   rw   Card Detect Signal Selection

                  A card detection mechanism will base on either port or register value.

                  0- normal mode
{dango            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                          1011 / 1446

                                                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  1- CDTL(HC1.6) bit (testing mode)

6     CDTL   rw   Card Detect Test Level

                  Designates card insertion status when HC1.CDSS=1. Bit provided for test purposes.

                  0- no card

                  1- card inserted

5     EDTW   rw   Extended Data Transfer Width

                  This bit is to enable/disable 8-bit DAT bus width mode.

                  0: Bus width selected by HC1.DTW

                  1: 8-bit mode enabled

4:3   DMASELrw    DMA Select

                  In this field the DMA mode can be selected. The field behavior depends on the Host

                  Controller Compatibility bit (SDIO_ACES.HV4E).

                  Host Controller version 3.00 compatible mode (SDIO_ACES.HV4E=0)

                  00b: SDMA mode

                  01b: Reserved

                  10b: ADMA2 (32-bit Address)

                  11b: ADMA2 (64-bit Address)

                  Host Controller version 4.00 compatibility mod (SDIO_ACES.HV4E=1)

                  00b: SDMA mode

                  01b: Not Used

                  10b: ADMA2 mode

                  11b: Reserved

                  The ADMA2 address bus width is configured by 64- bit Addressing bit in Host

                  Controller 2 register when SDIO_ACES.HV4E=1.

2     HSE    rw   High Speed Enable (SD mode only)

                  Selects operating mode to Default Speed (HSE=0) or High Speed (HSE=1).

                  The maximum SD clock frequency is defined as 0- 25MHz in the default speed

                  mode, and 0-50MHz in the High Speed mode.

1     DTW    rw   Data Transfer Width (SD mode only)

                  Bit used to configure DAT bus width to 1 or 4.

                  0: 1-bit mode

                  1: 4-bit mode
{dango                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  1012 / 1446

                                                                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    This bit is ignored when the HC1.EDTW is set 1 (8- bit mode selected).

    0   LEDC   rw   LED Control

                    State of this bit directly drives led port of the host in order to control the external

                    LED diode.

                    LEDC=1 will switch LED on, while LEDC=0 will switch it off.

                    The software will switch LED on to caution the user not to remove the card while

                    the transfer is in progress.

    27.3.28        SDIO_CTRL2

    Offset: 0x022C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 27-29 SDIO_CTRL2 Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

    31:27Rsvd   -   Reserved

26 SRDAT rw Software Reset For DAT Line (SD Mode only)

                    When set to 1, resets the logic related to the data path, including data buffers and

                    the DMA logic.

                    The following registers and bits are cleared: BDP register:

                    -   Buffer

                    PRS register:

                    -   Buffer Read Enable
                    -   Buffer Write Enable
                    -   Read Transfer Active
                    -   Write Transfer Active
                    -   DAT Line Active
                    -   Command Inhibit HC1 register:
                    -   Continue Request
                    -   Stop At Block Gap Request ENIS register:
                    -   Buffer Read Ready
                    -   Buffer Write Ready
                    -   DMA Interrupt

    {dango                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   1013 / 1446

                                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
                     -   Block Gap Event
                     -   Transfer Complete

                     After completing the reset operation, SDIO_CTRL2.SRDAT bit is automatically

                     cleared. It takes some time to complete the reset operation, so the software will

                     wait until SDIO_CTRL2.SRDAT=0, and continue the other operations only when

                     SDIO_CTRL2.SRDAT=0.

25 SRCMD rw Software Reset For CMD Line (SD Mode only)

                     When set to 1, resets the logic related to the command generation and response

                     checking.

                     The following registers and bits are cleared:

                     -   PRS register: Command Inhibit CMD
                     -   ENIS register: Command Complete

                     After completing the reset operation, SDIO_CTRL2.SRCMD bit is automatically

                     cleared. It takes some time to complete the reset operation, so the software will wait

                     until SRCMD=0, and continue the other operations only when

                     SDIO_CTRL2.SRCMD=0.

    24 SRFA    rw    Software Reset For All

                     When set to 1, the entire slot is reset. After completing the reset operation, SRFA bit

                     is automatically cleared. It takes some time to complete the reset operation, so the

                     software will wait until SRFA=0, and continue the other operations only when

                     SRFA=0.

                     Additionally, after SRFA, software should reset and reinitialize card inserted to the

                     slot.

                     SD Card Power may be enabled 1 ms after this bit is cleared to ensure SD Card has

                     been reset properly.

    23:20Rsvd   -    Reserved

    19:16DTCV   rw   Data Timeout Counter Value

                     This value determines the interval by which DAT line timeouts are detected.

                     The interval can be computed as below:

                     1111b: Reserved

                     1110b: t_sdmclk*2(27+2)

                     1101b: t_sdmclk*2(26+2)
    {dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   1014 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  ...

                  0001b: t_sdmclk*2(14+2)

                  0000b: t_sdmclk*2(13+2)

                  Where t_sdmclk is the sdmclk clock period Refer to the Data Timeout Error

                  (ENIS.EDT) register for information on factors which generate data timeouts.

15:8 SDCFSL rw    SDCLK Frequency Select (lower part)

                  This register and SDIO_CTRL2.SDCFSH are used to calculate frequency of SDCLK

                  clock.

                  The SDCLK frequency is calculated with following expressions:

                  -   sdclk = sdmclk; when (N=0)
                  -   sdclk = sdmclk/2N; when (N>0)

                  Variable N is concatenation of SDIO_CTRL2.SDCFSH and SDIO_CTRL2.SDCFSL.

                  The value of SDCFSL, SDCFSH registers can be changed only when

                  SDIO_CTRL2.SDCE (SD Clock Enable) =0.

7:6   SDCFSHrw    SDCLK Frequency Select (higher part)

                  This register is an extension to SDCFSL.

5:3   Rsvd   -    Reserved

2     SDCE   rw   SD Clock Enable

                  When set to 1, SDCLK clock is enabled. When cleared to 0, SDCLK clock is stopped.

                  The host clears SDCE automatically when card is removed from the slot

                  The SDCLK clock should be stopped by the soft- ware when changing the clock

                  divider (i.e. SDCE bit will be cleared before writing SDIO_CTRL2.SDCFSL,

                  SDIO_CTRL2.SDCFSH).

1     ICS    r    Internal Clock Stable

                  When read as 1, indicates that the clock on sdmclk pin of the host is stable after

                  setting ICE to 1.

                  When read as 0, indicates that the clock is not stable yet (for example the external

                  PLL that generates the clock is not yet locked).

                  The value of ICS is equal to the actual signal level on ics pin of the host. The user

                  will connect ics to the external PLL if required. Otherwise, ics should be connected

                  directly to the ice output of the host.

0      ICE  rw    Internal Clock Enable
      {dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 1015 / 1446

                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  This field is designated to controls (enable/disable) external clock generator (e.g.

                  PLL). The ICE bits of every slot are logically OR-ed together and then drive the ice

                  pin. It means, the ice pin is 0 only when ICE in 0 for every slot implemented inside

                  the host.

                  The ice pin is 1 if at least one of the ICE bits is set to 1.

                  When set to 0, the clock on sdmclk pin can be stopped externally.

                  If the sdmclk is stopped, then host goes to a very low power state. The host

                  registers are still operable (read and written operation are valid) even if the clock

                  on sdmclk is stopped.

                  Setting of the ICE bit does not affect card detection. It means, the card detection

                  works even if the clock on sdmclk is stopped.

27.3.29         SDIO_STATUS3

Offset: 0x0230

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-30 SDIO_STATUS3 Register Description

Bit   Symbol Type  Description

31:28Rsvd    -     Reserved

27    ERSP   r_w1cResponse Error (SD Mode only)

                   Generated on error detection inside R1 or R5 response.

                   Errors will be checked only if RECE is set 1.

26    Rsvd   -     Reserved

25    EADMA r_w1cADMA Error

                   Generated when an error occurs during ADMA read or write transfer.

                   To resolve the cause of the error, the state of the ADMA engine at error occurrence

                   is saved in ADMA Error Status register, and the address of the descriptor

                   processed at error occurrence is provided in ADMA System Address register.

24    EAC    r_w1cGenerated when an error occurs during Auto

                   CMD12/Auto CMD23 command transmission. It indicates one of the following

                   conditions:

                   - one of the bits in SDIO_ACES register has changed from 0 to 1,


                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1016 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
                    - Auto CMD12 is not executed due to the previous command error.

    23   ECL    r_w1cCurrent Limit Error

                    Generated when host is not supplying power to SD card due to some failure.

                    The value is equal to the actual signal level on cle pin of the host.

    22   EDEB   r_w1cData End Bit Error (SD mode only)

        When set to 1, indicates detecting 0 at the end bit position of read data transfer

                    which uses the DAT line, or at the end bit position of the Write CRC Status.

    21   EDCRC r_w1cData CRC Error (SD mode only)

                    When set to 1, indicates detecting CRC error when transferring read data which

                    uses the DAT line, or when detecting the Write CRC status having a value of other

                    than 010.

                    This bit will be set to 1 immediately when conflict on CMD line detected.

                    The conflict is signalized by setting this bit and ENIS.EDT to 1.

    20   EDT    r_w1cData Timeout Error (SD mode only)

                    When set to 1, indicates detecting one of the follow- ing timeout conditions:

                    1 Busy timeout for the response with busy.

                    2 Busy timeout after Write CRC status.

                    3 Write CRC Status timeout.

                    4 Read data timeout.

                    This bit will be set to 1 immediately when conflict on CMD line conflict detected.

    19   ECI    r_w1cCommand Index Error (SD mode only)

                    When set to 1, indicates that Index error occurs in the command response.

18 ECEB r_w1cCommand End Bit Error (SD mode only)

                    When set to 1, indicates detecting that the end bit of a command response is 0.

    17   ECCRC  r_w1cCommand CRC Error (SD mode only)

                    When set to 1, indicates that command CRC error has occurred.

    16   ECT    r_w1cCommand Timeout Error (SD mode only)

                    When set to 1, indicates that no response was re- turned within 64 SDCLK cycles

                    from the end bit of the command.

    15   EINT   r   Error Interrupt

                    This bit is set if: - any of bits in range ENIS[31:16] is set;

                    The software can check for an error by reading this single bit first.
    dango               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  1017 / 1446

                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

14    CQINT  r   Command Queuing Interrupt

                 This interrupt is asserted when at least one of the bits in CQIS register is set. This

                 interrupt is cleared only by clearing the source interrupt in CQIS register.

13:9  Rsvd   -   Reserved

8     CINT   r   Card Interrupt

                 Indicates the card interrupt. CINT is not sampled by the card clock, so the

                 interrupt can be detected even with SD clock stopped (SDIO_CTRL2.SDCE=0).

                 Also, CINT is not cleared by writing 1. Instead, the software will clear the source of

                 an interrupt inside the card. After detecting the Card Interrupt, the software will

                 stop further interrupt detection by clearing ENSE.CINT_- SE to 0. Then, the

                 software will clear the interrupt source inside the card by using the appropriate

                 commands. For the details, please refer to the SDIO Card Specification.

                 After clearing the interrupt source, the card will stop to drive the interrupt signal to

                 the host. Finally, when the interrupt service routine is finished, the interrupt

                 detection can be enabled by setting ENSE.CINT_SE back to 1.

7     CR     r_w1cCard Removal

                 Generated when the PRS.CI bit changes from 1 to 0, indicating card removal event.

                 When read as 1, indicates that the card was removed from the slot.

                 When read as 0, indicates that the card state is stable (still inserted or removed) or

                 that the de-bouncing is in progress.

6     CIN    r_w1cCard Insertion

                 Generated when the PRS.CI bit changes from 0 to 1, indicating card insertion.

                 When read as 1, indicates that the card was inserted to the slot.

                 When read as 0, indicates that the card state is stable (still inserted or removed) or

                 that the de-bouncing is in progress.

5     BRR    r_w1cBuffer Read Ready

                 Generated when the BRE changes from 0 to 1, indicating that the data buffer can

                 be read by the software.

4     BWR    r_w1cBuffer Write Ready

                 Generated when the BWE changes from 0 to 1, indicating that the data buffer can

                 be written by the soft- ware.

3     DMAINTr_w1cDMA Interrupt

                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1018 / 1446

                                                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                In SDMA mode, DMA interrupt is generated when the host controller detects the

                Host SDMA Buffer boundary.

                In ADMA mode, DMA interrupt is generated when the INT flag is set in a currently

                serviced ADMA descriptor.

2     BGE    r_w1cBlock Gap Event

                Generated when the read/write transaction is stopped at a block gap as the result

                of setting HC1.SBGR to 1.

1     TC     r_w1cTransfer Complete

                SD Mode:

                Generated when the transfer which uses the DAT line is complete. Transfers which

                use the DAT line include the read/write transfers and commands with a busy

                response.

                In case of the read transfer, TC indicates that the entire data was transferred from

                the card to the host system (i.e. the host FIFO is empty after reading the last data

                block).

                In case of the write transfer, TC indicates that the entire data was transferred

                from the host to the card (i.e. the host FIFO is empty after writing the last data

                block), and the card accepted the data (busy signal released after the last block).

                In the case of the command with a busy response, TC indicates that the busy signal

                is released after the response.

0     CC     r_w1cCommand Complete

                Generated when the end bit of the response is received, except the response for

                Auto-CMD12 command. Auto-CMD12 command does not generate CC.

27.3.32           SDIO_ACES

Offset: 0x023c

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 27-33 SDIO_ACES Register Description

Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31    PVE     rw   Preset Value Enable

                   Setting this bit to 1 triggers an automatically update of SDIO_CTRL2.SDCFSL,

                   SDIO_CTRL2.SDCFSH, SDIO_CTRL2.CGS, SDIO_ACES.DSS registers by the host.

                   Values for an update are taken from PV1 - PV4 and depends on SDIO_ACES.UMS.

30    Rsvd    -    Reserved

29    A64B    rw   64-bit Addressing

                   Specifies the addressing mode for DMA ending. This field is ignored when

                   SDIO_ACES.HV4E=0.

                   0: 32-bit addressing

                   1: 64-bit addressing

28    HV4E    rw   Host Version 4.00 Enable

                   Selects backward (SD Host 3.00 Version) compatibility mode or SD Host 4.00

                   Version mode.

{dango                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                       1024 / 1446

                                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 0: Version 3.00

                 1: Version 4.00

                 The software can select system address register SDMA (when this bit is 0) or

                 DMAADDR1 / DMAADDR2 (when this bit is 1) for the SDMA engine.

27:24Rsvd   -    Reserved

23   SCS    rw   Sampling Clock Select (UHS-I only)

                 The host updates this bit when the tuning procedure is finished. If this bit is set to 1,

                 this means that the tuning procedure is successfully completed.

                 Otherwise it means that procedure failed and clock tuning logic is disabled.

                 This bit is valid only after the procedure is finished. Writing 1 will be ignored.

                 Writing 0 will reset and disable tuning block.

22   EXTNG rw    Execute Tuning (UHS-I only)

                 This register controls tuning procedure. The procedure starts when the bit is set 1.

                 The procedure can be aborted when the bit is cleared. The bit is read 1 while the

                 procedure is in progress, and 0 when the procedure is finished.

                 SCS = 0, EXTNG = 0 - Reset and disable clock tuning logic

                 SCS = 0, EXTNG = 1 - Reset and restart tuning process

                 SCS = 1, EXTNG = 0 - Stop tuning procedure

                 SCS = 1, EXTNG = 1 - Start retuning (without clock tuning logic reset)

21:20DSS    rw   Driver Strength Select (UHS-I only)

                 This bit controls the electric parameters of I/O driver. Up to 4 configurations of I/O

                 driver settings can be implemented:

                 00: Driver Type B (default)

                 01: Driver Type A

                 10: Driver Type C

                 11: Driver Type D

                 The bit is ignored when the V18SE is cleared.

19  V18SE rw     1.8V Signaling Enable (UHS-I only)

                 This bit controls I/O signaling voltage level.

                 If the bit is 0 or 1, the I/O uses the 3.3V or 1.8V signaling, respectively.

                 The SW driver will set this bit 1 when UHS-I mode. Depend on the selected SD

                 interface mode, the software will set this field as follows:

                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                         1025 / 1446

                                                                  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 0: For Default Speed, High Speed mode

                 1: For UHS-I mode

18:16UMS    rw   UHS Mode Select

                 Used to select one of UHS-I modes.

                 000b: SDR12

                 001b: SDR25

                 010b: SDR50

                 011b: SDR104

                 100b: DDR50

                 101b: Reserved

                 110b: Reserved

                 111b: Reserved

                 The selected UHS-I mode (when value is in range 000b-100b) will be ignored when

                 V18SE is 0.

15:8  Rsvd  -    Reserved

7     CNIACEr    Command Not Issued By Auto CMD12 Error

                 When read as 1, the command was not executed by the Host due to the previous

                 Auto CMD12 error.

                 When Host detects any error during Auto-CMD12, then all further command

                 generation attempts are blocked. The software reset sequence is needed for

                 recovery.

                 Bit is set to 0, when Auto CMD23 Error is detected (any of bits SDIO_ACES[4:1] is

                 set).

6     Rsvd  -    Reserved

5     ACRE  r    Auto CMD Response Error

                 When read as 1, means an error is detected in response to Auto Command.

4     ACIE  r    Auto CMD Index Error

                 When read as 1, means that Command Index error occurred in the Auto CMD

                 response.

3     ACEBE r    Auto CMD End Bit Error

                 When read as 1, indicates that the end bit of the Auto-CMD response is 0.

2     ACCE  r    Auto CMD CRC Error

                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 1026 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  When read as 1, indicates a CRC error was detected in the Auto CMD response or

                  conflict on the CMD lines is detected:

                  ACCE(SDIO_ACES.2) = 0, ACTE(SDIO_ACES.1) = 0

                  -   No error

                  ACCE(SDIO_ACES.2) = 0, ACTE(SDIO_ACES.1) = 1

                  -   Auto CMD Timeout error detected ACCE(SDIO_ACES.2) = 1, ACTE(SDIO_ACES.1)

                  = 0

                  -   Auto CMD CRC error detected ACCE(SDIO_ACES.2) = 1, ACTE(SDIO_ACES.1) = 1
                  -   Conflict on the CMD line detected

1   ACTE    r     Auto CMD Timeout Error

                  When read as 1, indicates that there was no response within 64 SDCLK clock cycles

                  from the end bit of the Auto CMD or conflict on the CMD lines is detected (see table

                  in SDIO_ACES.ACCE field description). If this bit is set to 1, the other error status

                  bits (SDIO_ACES[4:2]) are meaningless.

0   ACNE    r     Auto CMD12 Not Executed

                  When set to 1, means the host cannot issue Auto CMD12 due to some error. If this

                  bit is set to 1, other error status bits (SDIO_ACES[4:1]) are meaningless. Bit is

                  updated with 0, when Auto CMD23 Error is detected (any of bits SDIO_ACES[4:1] is

                  set).

27.3.33        SDIO_CAP1

Offset: 0x0240

Reset: 0x176a_08b2

Table 27-34 SDIO_CAP1 Register Description

Bit Symbol     TypeDescription

31:30SLT       r      Slot Type

                      These bits inform what type of slot is provided.

                      00: Removable Card Slot

                      01: Embedded Slot for One Device

                      10: Shared Bus Slot

                      11: Reserved

{dango                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                 1027 / 1446

                                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

29   AIS     r   Asynchronous Interrupt Support

                 0: Not supported

                 1: Supported

28   A64S    r   64-bit System Addressing Support

                 0: Not supported

                 1: Supported

27   Rsvd    r   Reserved

26   VS18    r   Voltage Support 1.8V

                 0: Not supported

                 1: Supported

25   VS30    r   Voltage Support 3.0V

                 0: Not supported

                 1: Supported

24   VS33    r   Voltage Support 3.3V

                 0: Not supported

                 1: Supported

23   SRS     r   Suspend / Resume Support

                 0: Not supported

                 1: Supported

                 The host controller does not support Suspend / Resume mechanism.

22   DMAS    r   SDMA Support

                 0: Not supported

                 1: Supported

                 This bit defines whether the SDMA is supported in the slot.

21   HSS     r   High Speed Support

                 0: Not supported

                 1: Supported

20   ADMA1S  r   ADMA1 Support

                 0: Not supported

                 1: Supported

19   ADMA2S  r   ADMA2 Support

                 0: Not supported

                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                1028 / 1446

                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  1: Supported

18    EDS8  r     8-bit Embedded Device Support

                  0: Not supported

                  1: Supported

                  If this bit is 0, the HC1.EDTW register is not implemented.

17:16CAP1_MBLr    Max Block Length

                  This value indicates the maximum block size that can be transferred by the host.

                  Three sizes can be defined as indicated below:

                  00b: 512 Bytes

                  01b: 1024 Bytes

                  10b: 2048 Bytes

                  11b: Reserved

                  The physical FIFO buffer size is defined by the separate FIFODEPTH generic

                  parameter, and the physical buffer size is equal to 2 FIFODEPTH * 8 bytes.

                  Therefore, the Maximum Block Size de- fined by MBL will always be less or equal

                  to the physical buffer size.

15:8  BCSDCLK r   Base Clock Frequency for SD Clock

                  Field defines the base clock frequency for the SD Clock in 1MHz units. The base

                  clock is the clock sourced to sdmclk pin of the host. The maximum clock

                  frequency supported is between 10MHz to 255MHz.

                  If BCSDCLK = 0, the Host System has to obtain the clock information via another

                  method (i.e. not defined by the specification).

7     TCU    r    Timeout Clock Unit

                  Field defines the frequency unit for the CAP1.TCF.

                  0: KHz

                  1: MHz

6     Rsvd   r    Reserved

5:0   TCF    rw   Timeout Clock Frequency

                  Defines the base clock frequency used to detect Data Timeout Error. The

                  CAP1.TCU bit deter- mines the unit used.

                  111111b: 63kHz(CAP1.TCU=0) or 63MHz(CAP1.TCU=1)

                  111110b: 63kHz(CAP1.TCU=0)   or 63MHz(CAP1.TCU=1)

                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1029 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   ...

                   000001b-1kHz(CAP1.TCU=0)or 1MHz(CAP1.TCU=1)

                   000000b: Host System has to obtain the clock information via another method

                   (i.e. not defined by the spec).

27.3.34       SDIO_CAP2

Offset: 0x0244

Reset: 0x1000_0075

Table 27-35 SDIO_CAP2 Register Description

Bit  Symbol   TypeDescription

31:29Rsvd     r   Reserved

28    VDD2S   r   VDD2 Supported

                  Information about support or lack of support of optional VDD2.

27:24Rsvd     r   Reserved

23:16CLKMPR   r   Clock Multiplier

                  This field is to be 0 (fixed), as the Clock Multiplier is not supported.

15:14RTNGM    r   Re-Tuning Modes

                  Depending on the retuning method, the some restrictions are assumed for the data

                  length between re-tunings.

                  The core can work with supporting one of the three method:

                  0 - Mode1: The software driver will use timer to calculate when the re-tuning is

                  to be rerun. The data length between operations is limited to the 4MB.

    1 - Mode2: The driver will use either the retuning request or timer to predict

                  when next retuning should be per formed. The data length between operations is

                  limited to the 4MB.

                  2 - Mode3: This mode is similar to the mode2 with one exception. The core is

                  able to per- form auto retuning during the transmission, so data length limitation

                  is not exists. Mode 3 is currently not supported. The driver can configure the timer

                  by getting the RTNGCNT. This field is to be 0 or 1, because the mode 3 is not

                  supported.

13   UTSM50   r   Use Tuning for SDR50

{dango                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   1030 / 1446

                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 1: tuning operation is necessary in SDR50 mode

                 0: tuning operation is not necessary in SDR50 mode

12    Rsvd   r   Reserved

11:8  RTNGCNTr   Timer Count for Re-Tuning

                 These bits contain initial value for timer used to starting periodically Re-Tuning

                 Operation.

                 0h: Re-Tuning Timer disabled

                 1h: 1 second

                 ...

                 n - 2(n-1) seconds

                 ...

                 Bh: 1024 seconds

                 Eh-Ch: Reserved

                 Fh: Obtain this info in other way

7     Rsvd   r   Reserved

6     DRVD   r   1.8V Line Driver Type D Supported

                 1: Driver Type D supported

                 0: Driver Type D not supported

5     DRVC   r   1.8V Line Driver Type C Supported

                 1: Driver Type C supported

                 0: Driver Type C not supported

4     DRVA   r   1.8V Line Driver Type A Supported

                 1: Driver Type A supported

                 0: Driver Type A not supported

3     UHSII  r   UHS-II Supported

                 1: UHS-II supported

                 0: UHS-II not supported

2     DDR50  r   DDR50 Supported

                 1: DDR50 mode supported

                 0: DDR50 mode not supported

1     SDR104 r   SDR104 Supported

                 1: SDR104 mode supported

    dango            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  1031 / 1446

                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  0: SDR104 mode not supported

0     SDR50   r   SDR50 Supported

                  1: SDR50 mode supported

                  0: SDR50 mode not supported

    27.3.38          SDIO_ADMASTATUS

    Offset: 0x0254
