30.6.30     I2C_TXABTSRC

Offset: 0x80

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 30-32 I2C_TXABTSRC Register Description

 Bit    Symbol                     Type  Description

 31:23  TX_FLUSH_CNT               r     This field indicates the number of Tx FIFO Data

                                         Commands which are flushed due to TX_ABRT interrupt. It

                                         is cleared whenever I2C is disabled.

                                         Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter or Slave-Transmitter

 dango                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            1133 / 1446

                                                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

22:17  Rsvd                   -   Reserved.

16     ABRT_USER_ABRT         r   This is a master-mode-only bit. Master has detected the

                                  transfer abort (I2C_ENABLE[1])

                                  Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter

                                  0x1: Transfer abort detected by master

                                  0x0: Transfer abort detected by master- scenario not

                                  present

15     ABRT_SLVRD_INTX        r   1: When the processor side responds to a slave mode

                                  request for data to be transmitted to a remote master and

                                  user writes a 1 in CMD (bit 8) of I2C_DATACMD register.

                                  Role of I2C: Slave-Transmitter

                                  0x1: Slave trying to transmit to remote master in read

                                  mode

                                  0x0: Slave trying to transmit to remote master in read

                                  mode- scenario not present

14    ABRT_SLV_ARBLOST  r         This field indicates that a Slave has lost the bus while

                                  transmitting data to a remote master. I2C_TXABTSRC[12]

                                  is set at the same time.

                                  Note: Even though the slave never 'owns' the bus,

                                  something could go wrong on the bus. This is a fail safe

                                  check. For instance, during a data transmission at the

                                  low-to-high transition of SCL, if what is on the data bus is

                                  not what is supposed to be transmitted, then I2C no

                                  longer own the bus.

                                  Role of I2C: Slave-Transmitter

                                  0x1: Slave lost arbitration to remote master

                                  0x0: Slave lost arbitration to remote master- scenario not

                                  present

13     ABRT_SLVFLUSH_TXFIFO | r   This field specifies that the Slave has received a read

                                  command and some data exists in the TX FIFO, so the

                                  slave issues a TX_ABRT interrupt to flush old data in TX

                                  FIFO.
{dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                      1134 / 1446

                                                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                              Role of I2C: Slave-Transmitter

                              0x1: Slave flushes existing data in TX-FIFO upon getting

                              read command

                              0x0: Slave flushes existing data in TX-FIFO upon getting

                              read command- scenario not present

12   ARB_LOST             r   This field specifies that the Master has lost arbitration, or

                              if I2C_TXABTSRC[14] is also set, then the slave

                              transmitter has lost arbitration.

                              Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter or Slave-Transmitter

                              0x1: Master or Slave-Transmitter lost arbitration

                              0x0: Master or Slave-Transmitter lost arbitration- scenario

                              not present

11   ABRT_MASTER_DIS      r   This field indicates that the User tries to initiate a Master

                              operation with the Master mode disabled.

                              Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter or Master-Receiver

                              0x1: User intitating master operation when MASTER

                              disabled

                              0x0: User initiating master operation when MASTER

                              disabled- scenario not present

10   ABRT_10B_RD_NORSTRT  r   This field indicates that the restart is disabled

                              (I2C_RESTART_EN bit (I2C_CON[5]) =0) and the master

                              sends a read command in 10-bit addressing mode.

                              Role of I2C: Master-Receiver

                              0x1: Master trying to read in 10Bit addressing mode when

                              RESTART disabled

                              0x0: Master not trying to read in 10Bit addressing mode

                              when RESTART disabled

9   ABRT_SBYTE_NORSTRT    r   To clear Bit 9, the source of the ABRT_SBYTE_NORSTRT

                              must be fixed first; restart must be enabled

                              (I2C_CON[5]=1), the SPECIAL bit must be cleared

                              (I2C_TAR[11]), or the GC_OR_START bit must be cleared

                              (I2C_TAR[10]). Once the source of the
{dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   1135 / 1446

                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                            ABRT_SBYTE_NORSTRT is fixed, then this bit can be

                            cleared in the same manner as other bits in this register.

                            If the source of the ABRT_SBYTE_NORSTRT is not fixed

                            before attempting to clear this bit, bit 9 clears for one

                            cycle and then gets reasserted. When this field is set to 1,

                            the restart is disabled (I2C_RESTART_EN bit

                            (I2C_CON[5]) =0) and the user is trying to send a START

                            Byte.

                            Role of I2C: Master

                            0x1: User trying to send START byte when RESTART

                            disabled

                            0x0: User trying to send START byte when RESTART

                            disabled- scenario not present

8  ABRT_HS_NORSTRT      r   This field indicates that the restart is disabled

                            (I2C_RESTART_EN bit (I2C_CON[5]) =0) and the user is

                            trying to use the master to transfer data in High Speed

                            mode.

                            Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter or Master-Receiver

                            0x1: User trying to switch Master to HS mode when

                            RESTART disabled

                            0x0: User trying to switch Master to HS mode when

                            RESTART disabled- scenario not present

7   ABRT_SBYTE_ACKDET   r   This field indicates that the Master has sent a START Byte

                            and the START Byte was acknowledged (wrong behavior).

                            Role of I2C: Master

                            0x1: ACK detected for START byte

                            0x0: ACK detected for START byte-scenario not present

6   ABRT_HS_ACKDET      r   This field indicates that the Master is in High Speed mode

                            and the High Speed Master code was acknowledged

                            (wrong behavior).

                            Role of I2C: Master

                            0x1: HS Master code ACKed in HS Mode
{dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                1136 / 1446

                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                             0x0: HS Master code ACKed in HS Mode- scenario not

                             present

5  ABRT_GCALL_READ       r   This field indicates that I2C in the master mode has sent a

                             General Call but the user programmed the byte following

                             the General Call to be a read from the bus

                             (I2C_DATACMD[9] is set to 1).

                             Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter

                             0x1: GCALL is followed by read from bus

                             0x0: GCALL is followed by read from bus-scenario not

                             present

4   ABRT_GCALL_NOACK     r   This field indicates that I2C in master mode has sent a

                             General Call and no slave on the bus acknowledged the

                             General Call.

                             Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter

                             0x1: GCALL not ACKed by any slave

                             0x0: GCALL not ACKed by any slave-scenario not present

3   ABRT_TXDATA_NOACK    r   This field indicates the master-mode only bit. When the

                             master receives an acknowledgement for the address, but

                             when it sends data byte(s) following the address, it did

                             not receive an acknowledge from the remote slave(s).

                             Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter

                             0x1: Transmitted data not ACKed by addressed slave

                             0x0: Transmitted data non-ACKed by addressed slave-

                             scenario not present

2   ABRT_10ADDR2_NOACK   r   This field indicates that the Master is in 10-bit address

                             mode and that the second address byte of the 10-bit

                             address was not acknowledged by any slave.

                             Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter or Master-Receiver

                             0x1: Byte 2 of 10Bit Address not ACKed by any slave

                             0x0: This abort is not generated

1   ABRT_10ADDR1_NOACK   r   This field indicates that the Master is in 10-bit address

                             mode and the first 10-bit address byte was not

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                    1137 / 1446

                                  V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                                       acknowledged by any slave.

                                       Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter or Master-Receiver

                                       0x1: Byte 1 of 10Bit Address not ACKed by any slave

                                       0x0: This abort is not generated

0      ABRT_7B_ADDR_NOACK     r        This field indicates that the Master is in 7-bit addressing

                                       mode and the address sent was not acknowledged by any

                                       slave.

                                       Role of I2C: Master-Transmitter or Master-Receiver

                                       0x1: This abort is generated because of NOACK for 7-bit

                                       address

                                       0x0: This abort is not generated

30.6.31     I2C_SDASETUP

Offset: 0x94

Reset: 0x0000_0064

Table 30-33 I2C_SDASETUP Register Description

 Bit   Symbol     Type     Description

 31:8  Rsvd       -        Reserved.

 7:0  | SDA_SETUP | rw     SDA Setup. It is recommended that if the required delay is 1000ns, then

                           for an ic_clk frequency of 10 MHz, I2C_SDA_SETUP should be

                           programmed to a value of 11. I2C_SDA_SETUP must be programmed

                           with a minimum value of 2.

30.6.32               I2C_ACKGC

Offset: 0x98

Reset: 0x0000_0001

Table 30-34 I2C_ACKGC Register Description

 Bit   Symbol           Type  Description

 31:1  Rsvd             -     Reserved.

 0     ACK_GEN_CALL | rw      ACK General Call. When set to 1, I2C responds with a ACK (by

                              asserting ic_data_oe) when it receives a General Call. Otherwise, I2C

                              responds with a NACK (by negating ic_data_oe).

                              0x1: Generate ACK for a General Call


                           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1138 / 1446

V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

0x0: Generate NACK for General Call





30.6.33     I2C_ENSTS

Offset: 0x9C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 30-35 I2C_ENSTS Register Description

 Bit   Symbol        Type                 Description
 31:3  Rsvd                  | -          Reserved.
 2     SLV_RX_DATA_LOST  r                Slave Received Data Lost. This bit indicates if a Slave-

                                          Receiver operation has been aborted with at least one

                                          data byte received from an I2C transfer due to the

                                          setting bit 0 of I2C_ENABLE from 1 to 0. When read as

                                          1, I2C is deemed to have been actively engaged in an

                                          aborted I2C transfer (with matching address) and the

                                          data phase of the I2C transfer has been entered, even

                                          though a data byte has been responded with a NACK.

                                          Note: If the remote I2C master terminates the transfer

                                          with a STOP condition before the I2C has a chance to

                                          NACK a transfer, and I2C_ENABLE[0] has been set to

                                          0, then this bit is also set to 1.

                                          When read as 0, I2C is deemed to have been disabled

                                          without being actively involved in the data phase of a

                                          Slave-Receiver transfer.

                                          Note: The CPU can safely read this bit when I2C_EN

                                          (bit 0) is read as 0.

                                          0x1: Slave RX Data is lost

                                          0x0: Slave RX Data is not lost

1      SLV_DISABLED_WHILE_BUSY r          Slave Disabled While Busy (Transmit, Receive). This bit

                                          indicates if a potential or active Slave operation has

                                          been aborted due to the setting bit 0 of the

                                          I2C_ENABLE register from 1 to 0. This bit is set when

           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1139 / 1446

                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                the CPU writes a 0 to the I2C_ENABLE register while:

                (a) I2C is receiving the address byte of the Slave-

                Transmitter operation from a remote master;

                OR,

                (b) address and data bytes of the Slave-Receiver

                operation from a remote master.

                When read as 1, I2C is deemed to have forced a NACK

                during any part of an I2C transfer, irrespective of

                whether the I2C address matches the slave address set

                in I2C (I2C_SAR register) OR if the transfer is

                completed before I2C_ENABLE is set to 0 but has not

                taken effect.

                Note: If the remote I2C master terminates the transfer

                with a STOP condition before the I2C has a chance to

                NACK a transfer, and I2C_ENABLE[0] has been set to

                0, then this bit will also be set to 1.

                When read as 0, I2C is deemed to have been disabled

                when there is master activity, or when the I2C bus is

                idle.

                Note: The CPU can safely read this bit when I2C_EN

                (bit 0) is read as 0.

                0x1: Slave is disabled when it is active

                0x0: Slave is disabled when it is idle

0 I2C_EN    r   ic_en Status. This bit always reflects the value driven

                on the output port ic_en.

                ■When read as 1, I2C is deemed to be in an enabled

                state.

                ■When read as 0, I2C is deemed completely inactive.

                Note: The CPU can safely read this bit anytime. When

                this bit is read as 0, the CPU can safely read

                SLV_RX_DATA_LOST (bit 2) and

                SLV_DISABLED_WHILE_BUSY (bit 1).
{dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.               1140 / 1446

                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                                           0x1: I2C enabled

                                           0x0: I2C disabled

30.6.34       I2C_SPKLEN

Offset: 0xA0

Reset: 0x0000_0001

Table 30-36 I2C_SPKLEN Register Description

 Bit   Symbol     Type     Description
 31:8  Rsvd     | -        Reserved.
 7:0  | FS_SPKLEN rw       This register must be set before any I2C bus transaction can take

                           place to ensure stable operation. This register sets the duration,

                           measured in ic_clk cycles, of the longest spike in the SCL or SDA

                           lines that will be filtered out by the spike suppression logic. This

                           register can be written only when the I2C interface is disabled which

                           corresponds to the I2C_ENABLE[0] register being set to 0. Writes at

                           other times have no effect. The minimum valid value is 1; hardware

                           prevents values less than this being written, and if attempted results

                           in 1 being set. or more information, refer to "Spike Suppression".

30.6.35     I2C_RESDETCLR

Offset: 0xA8

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 30-37 I2C_RESDETCLR Register Description

 Bit   Symbol           Type  Description

 31:1  Rsvd             -     Reserved.

 0     CLR_RESTART_DET  r     Read this register to clear the RESTART_DET interrupt (bit 12) of

                              I2C_RAWINTSTS register.










{dango                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1141 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   31 Universal Asynchronous Receiver
    Transmitter (UART)

   31.1 Introduction


The UART controller is used to transmit/ receive data via UART protocol. Each UART controller can be

program individually and has its own interrupt to CPU. The UART controller can support 8 bits transfer

without parity or 9-bit transfer with even or odd parity. One FIFO and one shift register are used for

transmit engine, and another set of FIFO and shift buffer are used for receive engine. For integration in

systems where Infrared SIR serial data format is required, the UART can be configured for a software-

programmable IrDA SIR Mode. If this mode is not selected, only the UART (RS232 standard) serial

data format is available.


   31.2 Features


    • 9-bit serial data support

    • False start bit detection

    • Programmable fractional baud rate support

    • Programmable serial data baud rate as calculated by the following:

    baud rate = (serial clock frequency)/(16×divisor)

    • Support IrDA SIR Mode

    • Support CTS RTS










    fi    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1142 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   31.3 Block Diagram




    Peripheral Bus(APB)



   TX    RX
 Buffer    Buffer


   TX     Baud  RX
 Engine   Rate  Engine
    Generator


   TX     RX
 Shift    Shift
Register    Register


    UART TX    UART RX

    Figure 31-1 Functional Block Diagram of UART Controller










    fi    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1143 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   31.4 Flow




  Write to MCR to
program SIR mode or
        not


     Write 1 to
  LCR[7](DLAB) bit


Write to DLL, DLH to set
up divisor for required
       baud rate


     Write 0 to LCR[7]
         (DLAB) bit

   Write to LCR to set up
  transfer characteristics
    such as data length,
number of stop bits, parity
      bits, and so on

   Write to FCR to enable
   FIFOs and set Transmit
    FIFO threshold level


   Write to IER to enable
    required interrupts


   Write characters to be
  transmitted to transmit
   FIFO by writing to THR

     Wait for Transmit
   Holding Register empty
    interrupt
     (IIR[3:0]=4'b0010)

        Y
        More data to
         transmit?

        N
      Clear THR empty
  interrupt by reading IIR
    register


    Figure 31-2 UART configuration flow

    fis    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1144 / 1446

   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
  31.5 UART Registers


   Table 31-1 UART Registers Map

   Register Name       Offset   Type   Reset Value   Description

   UART_RBR            0x0000   r      0x0000_0000   Receive buffer register.

   UART_DLL            0x0000   rw     0x0000_0000   Divisor latch low register.

   UART_THR            0x0000   w      0x0000_0000   Transmit holding register.

   UART_DLH            0x0004   rw     0x0000_0000   Divisor latch high register.

   UART_IER            0x0004   rw     0x0000_0000   Interrupt enable register.

   UART_FCR            0x0008   w      0x0000_0000   FIFO control register.

   UART_IIR            0x0008   rw     0x0000_0000   Interrupt identification register.

   UART_LCR            0x000C   rw     0x0000_0000   Line control register.

   UART_MCR            0x0010   w      0x0000_0000   Modem control register.

   UART_LSR            0x0014   r      0x0000_0060   Line status register.

   UART_USR            0x007C   r      0x0000_0000   UART status register.

   UART_DLF            0x00C0   rw     0x0000_0000   Divisor latch fraction register.

   31.5.1 UART_RBR

   Offset: 0x0000

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 31-2 UART_RBR Register Description

   Bit Symbol Type                         Description

   31:8 Rsvd   -      Reserved.

7:0 RBR r This register contains the data byte received on the serial input port (sin) in UART

                      mode, If in non-FIFO mode, the data in the RBR must be read before the next data

                      arrives, otherwise it will be overwritten, resulting in an over-run error. If in FIFO

                      mode, this register accesses the head of the receive FIFO. If the receive FIFO is full

                      and this register is not read before the next data character arrives, then the data

                      already in the FIFO will be preserved but any incoming data will be lost and an over-

                      run error occurs.

   31.5.2 UART_DLL

   Offset: 0x0000

   {dango                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1145 / 1446

                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 31-3 UART_DLL Register Description

    Bit Symbol Type Description

    31:8 Rsvd   -    Reserved.

    7:0 DLL     rw   Divisor latch (low).

                     This register makes up the lower 8-bits of a 16-bit, read/write, Divisor Latch

                     register that contains the baud rate divisor for the UART. Once the DLL is set, at

                     least 8 clock cycles of the slowest uart clock should be allowed to pass before

                     transmitting or receiving data.

                     Note: DLH and DLL cannot be 0 at the same time.

    31.5.3 UART_THR

    Offset: 0x0000

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 31-4 UART_THR Register Description

    Bit Symbol Type Description

    31:8 Rsvd   -    Reserved.

    7:0 THR     w    Transmit holding register.

                     This register contains data to be transmitted on the serial output port in UART mode

                     or the serial infrared output in infrared mode. Data should only be written to the

                     THR when the THR Empty (THRE) bit (LSR[5]) is set. If in FIFO mode, 16 number of

                     characters of data may be written to the THR before the FIFO is full. Any attempt to

                     write data when the FIFO is full results in the write data being lost.

    31.5.4 UART_DLH

    Offset: 0x0004

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 31-5 UART_DLH Register Description

    Bit Symbol Type Description

    31:8 Rsvd   -    Reserved.

7:0 DLH rw Upper 8-bits of a 16-bit, read/write, Divisor Latch register that contains the baud

                     rate divisor for the UART.

                     Baud rate = (serial clock freq) / (16 * divisor).


                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1146 / 1446

                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   Once the DLH is set, at least 8 clock cycles of the slowest uart clock should be

                   allowed to pass before transmitting or receiving data.

                   Note: DLH and DLL cannot be 0 at the same time.

   31.5.5 UART_IER

   Offset: 0x0004

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 31-6 UART_IER Register Description

   Bit   Symbol Type Description

   31:4 Rsvd   -   Reserve.

3 EDSSI rw This is used to enable/disable the generation of Modem Status Interrupt

                   0x0: Disable Modem Status Interrupt

                   0x1: Enable Modem Status Interrupt

2 ELSI rw This is used to enable/disable the generation of Receiver Line Status Interrupt.

                   0x0: Disable Receiver Line Status Interrupt

                   0x1: Enable Receiver Line Status Interrupt

1 ETBEI rw Enable Transmit Holding Register Empty Interrupt. This is used to enable/disable the

                   generation of Transmitter Holding Register Empty Interrupt.

                   0x0: Disable Transmit empty interrupt

                   0x1: Enable Transmit empty interrupt Value

0 ERBFI rw Enable Received Data Available Interrupt. This is used to enable/disable the

                   generation of Received Data Available Interrupt and the Character Timeout

                   Interrupt.

                   0x0: Disable Receive data Interrupt

                   0x1: Enable Receive data Interrupt

   31.5.6 UART_FCR

   Offset: 0x0008

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 31-7 UART_FCR Register Description

   Bit   Symbol Type Description

   31:8 Rsvd   -   Reserved.

   7:6   RT    w   This is used to select the trigger level in the receiver FIFO at which the Received

   {dango              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1147 / 1446

                                                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                    Data Available Interrupt will be generated.

                    0x0: 1 character in FIFO

                    0x1: FIFO 1/4 full

                    0x2: FIFO 1/2 full

                    0x3: FIFO 2 less than full

   5:3   Rsvd   -   Reserved.

2 XFIFOR w This resets the control portion of the transmit FIFO and treats the FIFO as empty.

                    0x1: Transmit FIFO reset.

                    Note that this bit is 'self-clearing' and it is not necessary to clear this bit.

1 RFIFOR w This resets the control portion of the receive FIFO and treats the FIFO as empty.

                    0x1: Receive FIFO reset.

                    Note that this bit is 'self-clearing' and it is not necessary to clear this bit.

0 FIFOE w This bit enables/disables the transmit and receive FIFOs. Whenever the value of this

                    bit is changed both the transmit and receive controllers portion of FIFOs is reset.

                    0x0: FIFO disabled

                    0x1: FIFO enabled

   31.5.7 UART_IIR

   Offset: 0x0008

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 31-8 UART_IIR Register Description

   Bit   Symbol Type Description

   31:8 Rsvd    -   Reserved.

   7:6   FIFOSE r   This is used to indicate whether the FIFOs are enabled or disabled.

                    0x0: FIFOs are disabled

                    0x3: FIFOs are enabled

   5:4   Rsvd   -   Reserved.

   3:0   IID    w   This indicates the highest priority pending interrupt which can be one of the

                    following types specified in Values.

                    0x0: Modem status

                    0x1: No interrupt pending

                    0x2: THR empty

   {dango               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   1148 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  0x4: Received data available

                  0x6: Receiver line status

                  0x7: Busy detect

                  0xC: Character timeout

31.5.8 UART_LCR

Offset: 0x000C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 31-9 UART_LCR Register Description

Bit   Symbol Type Description

31:8 Rsvd    -    Reserved.

7     DLAB   rw   Divisor Latch Access Bit.

                  Writeable only when uart is not busy (USR[0] is zero). This bit is used to enable

                  reading and writing of the Divisor Latch register (DLL and DLH) to set the baud rate

                  of the UART. This bit must be cleared after initial baud rate setup in order to access

                  other registers.

                  0x0: Divisor Latch register is writable only when UART Not BUSY

                  0x1: Divisor Latch register is always readable and writable

6     Rsvd   -    Reserved.

5     SP     rw   Stick Parity.

                  This bit is used to force parity value. When PEN, EPS and Stick Parity are set to 1,

                  the parity bit is transmitted and checked as logic 0. If PEN and Stick Parity are set

                  to 1 and EPS is a logic 0, then parity bit is transmitted and checked as a logic 1. If

                  this bit is set to 0, Stick Parity is disabled.

                  0x0: Stick parity disabled

                  0x1: Stick parity enabled

4     EPS    rw   Even Parity Select.

                  This is used to select between even and odd parity, when parity is enabled (PEN set

                  to one). If set to one, an even number of logic '1's is transmitted or checked. If set

                  to zero, an odd number of logic '1's is transmitted or checked.

                  0x0: An odd parity is transmitted or checked

                  0x1: An even parity is transmitted or checked

{dango                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                      1149 / 1446

                                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

   3     PEN    rw   Parity Enable.

                     This bit is used to enable and disable parity generation and detection in transmitted

                     and received serial character respectively.

                     0x0: Disable parity

                     0x1: Enable parity

   2     STOP   rw   Number of stop bits.

                     0x0: 1 stop bit

                     0x1: 1.5 stop bits when DLS (LCR[1:0]) is zero, else 2 stop bit

   1:0   DLS    rw   Data Length Select.

                     This register is used to select the number of data bits per character that the

                     peripheral will transmit and receive.

                     0x0: 5 data bits per character

                     0x1: 6 data bits per character

                     0x2: 7 data bits per character

                     0x3: 8 data bits per character

   31.5.9 UART_MCR

   Offset: 0x0010

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 31-10 UART_MCR Register Description

   Bit   Symbol Type Description

   31:7 Rsvd    -    Reserved.

6 SIRE w This is used to enable/ disable the IrDA SIR Mode

                     0x0: IrDA SIR Mode disabled

                     0x1: IrDA SIR Mode enabled

                     Note: To enable SIR mode, write the appropriate value to the MCR register before

                     writing to the LCR register.

   5:0   Rsvd   -    Reserved.

   31.5.10          UART_LSR

   Offset: 0x0014

   Reset: 0x0000_0060

   Table 31-11 UART_LSR Register Description

   {dango                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                     1150 / 1446

                                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Bit   Symbol Type Description

31:8 Rsvd   -   Reserved.

7     RFE   w   Receiver FIFO Error bit.

                This bit is only relevant when FIFO's are enabled (FCR[0] set to one). This is used to

                indicate if there is at least one parity error, framing error, or break indication in the

                FIFO.

                This bit is cleared when the LSR is read and the character with the error is at the

                top of the receiver FIFO and there are no subsequent errors in the FIFO.

                0x0: No error in RX FIFO

                0x1: Error in RX FIFO

6     TEMT  r   Transmitter Empty bit.

                If FIFO's enabled (FCR[0] set to one), this bit is set whenever the Transmitter Shift

                Register and the FIFO are both empty.

                If FIFO's are disabled, this bit is set whenever the Transmitter Holding Register and

                the Transmitter Shift Register are both empty.

                0x0: Transmitter not empty

                0x1: Transmitter empty

5     THRE  r   Transmit Holding Register Empty bit.

                This bit is set whenever data is transferred from the THR or TX FIFO to the

                transmitter shift register and no new data has been written to the THR or TX FIFO.

                0x0: THRE interrupt control is disabled

                0x1: THRE interrupt control is enabled Value

4     BI    w   Break Interrupt bit.

                This is used to indicate the detection of a break sequence on the serial input data. It

                is set whenever the serial input, sin, is held in a logic '0' state for longer than the

                sum of start time + data bits + parity + stop bits.

                Reading the LSR or RBR clears the BI bit

                0x0: No break sequence detected

                0x1: Break sequence detected

3     FE    w   Framing Error bit.

                This is used to indicate the occurrence of a framing error in the receiver. A framing

                error occurs when the receiver does not detect a valid STOP bit in the received data.

                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                         1151 / 1446

                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                Reading the LSR or RBR clears the FE bit

                0x0: FIFO disabled

                0x1: FIFO enabled

2     PE    r   Parity Error bit.

                This is used to indicate the occurrence of a parity error in the receiver if the Parity

                Enable (PEN) bit (LCR[3]) is set. Since the parity error is associated with a character

                received, it is revealed when the character with the parity error arrives at the top of

                the FIFO. Reading the LSR or RBR clears the PE bit

                0x0: No parity error

                0x1: Parity error

1     OE    r   Overrun error bit.

                An overrun error occurs when the FIFO is full and a new character arrives at the

                receiver. The data in the FIFO is retained and the data in the receive shift register is

                lost. Reading the LSR or RBR clears the OE bit

                0x0: No overrun error

                0x1: Overrun error

0     DR    r   Data Ready bit.

                This is used to indicate that the receiver contains at least one character in the

                receiver FIFO. This bit is cleared when the receiver FIFO is empty.

                0x0: Data not ready

                0x1: Data ready

31.5.11        UART_USR

Offset: 0x007C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 31-12 UART_USR Register Description

Bit   Symbol Type Description

31:1 Rsvd   -   Reserved.

0     BUSY  r   UART Busy.

                This indicates that a serial transfer is in progress, when cleared indicates that the

                uart is idle or inactive.

                This bit will be set to 1 (busy) under any of the following conditions:

                - Transmission in progress on serial interface
& "dango            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1152 / 1446

                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                 - Transmit data present in THR and the baud divisor is non-zero ({DLH,DLL} does

                 not equal 0) when the divisor latch access bit is 0 (LCR.DLAB = 0)

                 - Reception in progress on the interface

                 - Receive data present in RBR

                 Note: It is possible for the UART Busy bit to be cleared even though a new character

                 may have been sent from another device. That is, if the uart has no data in the THR

                 and RBR and there is no transmission in progress and a start bit of a new character

                 has just reached the uart. This is due to the fact that a valid start is not seen until

                 the middle of the bit period and this duration is dependent on the baud divisor that

                 has been programmed. If a second system clock has been implemented, the

                 assertion of this bit will also be delayed by several cycles of the slower clock.

                 0x0: Uart is idle or inactive

                 0x1: Uart is busy (actively transferring data)

31.5.12         UART_DLF

Offset: 0x00C0

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 31-13 UART_DLF Register Description

Bit Symbol Type Description

31:6 Rsvd   -    Reserved.

5:0 DLF     rw   Fractional part of divisor.

                 The fractional value is added to integer value set by DLH, DLL. Fractional value is

                 determined by (Divisor Fraction value)/ (2^6).










                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1153 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
32 ISO7816 Controller (ISO7816)

32.1 Introduction


The ISO7816 controller is an enhance UART protocol which is able to do half-duplex communication on

the 2-wires bus. There are totally 4 ISO7816 controllers in V32F20x/ V32H20x, each ISO7816

controller can be program individually and has its own interrupt to CPU. A clock divider is also included

inside the IP which is capable to generate a 1~5 MHz clock for ISO7816 device. The setting in ISO7816

controller will be reset after wake-up from sleep or deep-sleep mode, programmer should restore the

setting manually after wake-up from these two states.


   32.2 Features


     Programmable Baud rate generator

     Support automatically retry and retry count control.

     Support automatically parity check and ACK generation during receive mode.

     Support half-duplex transmit/receive.

     Support clock divider.


    32.3 Block Diagram



    Peripheral Bus(APB)



   TX            RX
 Buffer    Buffer
   IT    IT
   TX    Baud    RX  Clock
 Engine  Rate  Engine Divider
      Generator

   TX            RX
 Shift         Shift
Register      Register


    ISO7816 IO    ISO7816 CLK



    Figure 32-1 Functional Block Diagram of ISO7816 Controller

    fi    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1154 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   32.4 ISO7816 Registers


    Table 32-1 ISO7816 Registers Map

    Register Name           Offset     Type  Reset Value    Description

ISO7816_BAUDDIVL 0x0004 rw 0x0000_0000 ISO78160 baud-rate low byte register.

ISO7816_BAUDDIVH 0x0008 rw 0x0000_0000 ISO78160 baud-rate high byte register.

    ISO7816_DATA            0x000C     rw    0x0000_0000    ISO78160 data register.

    ISO7816_INFO            0x0010     r     0x0000_0000    ISO78160 information register.

    ISO7816_CFG             0x0014     rw    0x0000_0000    ISO78160 control register.

    ISO7816_CLK             0x0018     rw    0x0000_0000    ISO78160 clock divider control register.

    32.4.1 ISO7816_BAUDDIVL

    Offset: 0x0004

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 32-2 ISO7816_BAUDDIVL Register Description

    Bit        Symbol               Type      Description

    31:8       Rsvd                 -         Reserved.

    7:0        BAUDDIVL             rw        Low byte of baud-rate divider.

    32.4.2 ISO7816_BAUDDIVH

    Offset: 0x0008

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 32-3 ISO7816_BAUDDIVH Register Description

    Bit   Symbol       Type Description

    31:8 Rsvd          -   Reserved.

7:0 BAUDDIVH rw High byte of baud-rate divider. The baud-rate counter is count from BAUDDIV to

                           0xFFFF, so the setting should be.

                           BAUDDIV = 0x10000 – (APBCLK/Baud-rate).

                           For example, when APBCLK is 6.5536MHz and baud-rate is 9600, then 0x10000-

                           (6553600/9600) = 0xFD56 should be written into this register.

    32.4.3 ISO7816_DATA

    Offset: 0x000C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

                                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1155 / 1446

                                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

   Table 32-4 ISO7816_DATA Register Description

   Bit  Symbol Type Description

   31:8 Rsvd   -   Reserved.

7:0 DATA rw Read: Receive data, when receive data is not read by programmer and another data

                   has been received, the OVIF flag will be set.

                   Write: Transmit data, a write to this port will trigger a transmit event on the

                   ISO7816 bus.

   32.4.4 ISO7816_INFO

   Offset: 0x0010

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 32-5 ISO7816_INFO Register Description

   Bit   Symbol     Type  Description

   31:10 Rsvd       -     Reserved.

   9     SDSTOP     rc_w1 Transmit finish flag.

                          This flag will be set when data is transmitted. It must wait until DMA

                          transmit finish to read this flag.

   8     TXRTYERRIF rc_w1 Transmit Retry counter reach flag.

                          This bit will be set when retry count reach the limit setting in TXRTYCNT

                          register. When this bit is set to 1, it is necessary to re-initialize the whole

                          ISO7816 module by disabling EN bit in ISO7816x_CFG register.

   7     RXOVIF     rc_w1 Receive overflow flag.

                          This bit will be set when a data is received by not been read by CPU but

                          another receive data is coming in.

   6     TXIF       rc_w1 Transmit interrupt flag.

                          This bit will set when a byte has been transmitted and ACK has been

                          received. This bit will be set no matter the received ACK is 0 or 1 when

                          AUTOTXRTY is 0. When AUTOTXRTY is 1, this bit will be set only when

                          receive ACK is 1 or the retry count reach the setting in the RTYCNT.

   5     RXIF       rc_w1 Receive interrupt flag.

                          This bit will set when a byte has been received and ACK has been

                          transmitted no matter the check sum result is correct or not. Programmer

   {dango                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               1156 / 1446

                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                         should take care of the error handling during receive mode.

   4     -         -     Reserved.

   3     TXERRIF   rc_w1 When AUTOTXRTY is 0, this bit will be set to 1 when a transfer is completed

                         and received ACK is 0. When AUTOTXRTY is 1, this bit will be set to 1 when

                         the retry count has reach RTYCNT and the final received ACK is 0.

   2     RXERRIF   rc_w1 When received data have check sum error, this bit will be set to 1.

   1     CHKSUM    r     The received data’s check sum bit. This bit can be read/ write by CPU, by

                         the original message will be lost.

   0     RXACK     r     The received ACK at the end of transmit. This bit can be read/ write by CPU,

                         by the original message will be lost.

   32.4.5 ISO7816_CFG

   Offset: 0x0014

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 32-6 ISO7816_CFG Register Description

   Bit   Symbol    Type Description

   31:17 Rsvd      -    Reserved.

   16    RXACKSET  rw   RXACK length.

                        0: 2 bits

                        1: 1 bit.

15:12 TXRTYCNT rw Transmit retry number control register.

                        This is register is valid only when AUTOTXRTY is set to 1.

                        0: No retry (DMA will stop immediately as long as receive ACK is 0).

                        1: Retry one time.

                        2: Retry two times.

                        ….

                        15: Retry 15 times.

                        As long as the retry times is reach but still receive error ACK, the TXRTYERRIF

                        bit will be set to 1. And also no more DMA request will be asserted to DMA

                        controller. This is useful when using DMA to transfer data, programmer can

                        use this interrupt to determine if the transmit has been terminated.

   11    LSB       rw   MSB/LSB transmit order control register.

   {dango                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1157 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        0: Transmit MSB first.

                        1: Transmit LSB first.

10 AUTOTXRTY rw Automatic transmit retry when receive ACK is 0.

                        0: Disable automatic re-transmit mode.

                        1: Enable automatic re-transmit mode.

                        The retry count is controlled by the TXRTYCNT. When this bit is set to 0, the

                        DMA transfer will be continued even if the received ACK is 0. When this bit is

                        set to 1, the DMA transfer will be terminated when the receive ACK is 0 and

                        retry count reach the setting in TXRTYCNT.

    9   AUTORXACK rw    Automatic response ACK as 0 when receive an error data to let transmitter

                        re-send the data.

                        0: Disable automatic re-receive mode.

                        1: Enable automatic re-receive mode.

    8   TXRTYERRIE rw   Transmit retry error interrupt enable register.

    7   RXOVIE    rw    Receive overrun interrupt enable register.

    6   TXIE      rw    Transmit interrupt enable register.

    5   RXIE      rw    Receive interrupt enable register.

    4   ACKLEN    rw    ACK low period when receive an error data.

                        0: 1 bit

                        1: 2 bits.

    3   TXERRIE   rw    Transmitted data error interrupt enable register.

    2   RXERRIE   rw    Received data error interrupt enable register.

    1   CHKP      rw    Parity mode control register.

                        0: Even parity

                        1: Odd parity.

    0   EN        rw    ISO7816 enable register.

                        0: Disable ISO7816 function.

                        1: Enable ISO7816 function.

    32.4.6 ISO7816_CLK

    Offset: 0x0018

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 32-7 ISO7816_CLK Register Description
                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  1158 / 1446

                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Bit          Symbol        Type     Description

31:8         Rsvd          -        Reserved.

7            CLKEN         rw       ISO7816 clock output enable.

                                    0: Disable clock output.

                                    1: Enable clock output.

6:0          CLKDIV        rw       The ISO7816 clock divider ratio.

                                    0: APBCLK/1

                                    1: APBCLK /2

                                    2: APBCLK /3

                                    …

                                    127: APBCLK /128

Note: In ISO7816’s protocol, the re-transmit should be less or equal to 3 times, this part should be

take care by software to disable the auto transmit or receive function after two times of failure retry.

The following table shows the ISO7816’s package format.

Table 32-8 Package format of ISO7816

 bit           0      1~8        9      10      11                  12   0           …

 Transmit      TransmitterReceiver      ReceiverTransmitter         --   TransmitterReceiver

 Direction

 Definition    Start  | Data     Parity | ACK*                      Wait | Start     …

               bit               bit                                     bit

 Value         0      MSBLSB     P      ACK*                        1    0           …

 TX            0      MSBLSB     P      1                                0           …

 RX            1      1          1      ACK*                        1    1           …

 Description   10 bits data             3 bits guarding time             Next Frame

*If ACK is 0, then the RX will be reset from 1 to 0 at the middle of 10ᵗʰ bit, and set to 1 at 11ᵗʰ or 12ᵗʰ

bit, which is controlled by ACKLEN in ISO7816_CFG.

The transmit package contains totally 10 bits including a start bit and 8 bits data and 1 parity bit. And

then in a 3-bit guarding time, the receiver will check the parity correctness response 1 or 2 bits ACK to

transmitter. At bit 12, both transmitter and receiver should be kept at high state before next package is

ready to be transmitted.




{dango                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.             1159 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
33 CRYPT Controller (CRYPT)

33.1 Introduction


The CRYPT controller is used to accelerate the ECC’s sign and verify process speed. The major feature

of CRYPT controller is the VLI (variable length integer) multiply, add, sub, and shift. It can achieve

overall about 4 times faster than pure software process. Every encryption algorithm use VLI can use

this hardware to accelerate the process speed. The CRYPT controller can access internal SRAM4

(address: 0x21000000 ~ 0x21003FFF), it can also generate the carry or borrow bit automatically.


   33.2 Features


     Support VLI from 32 bits to 512 bits.

     VLI multiplier.

     VLI add and carry bit generation.

     VLI substrate and borrow bit generation.

     VLI right shift 1 bit.

     Internal SRAM0 direct access.


    33.3 Block Diagram


    SYSTEM BUS(APB)



    APB Interface





    CRYPT    Internal
    Engine     SRAM






    Figure 33-1 Functional Block Diagram of CRYPT Controller




    fis    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1160 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   33.4 CRYPT Registers


    Table 33-1 CRYPT Registers Map

    Register Name           Offset   Type   Reset Value   Description

    CRYPT_CTRL               0x00    rw     0x0000_0000   CRYPT control register.

    CRYPT_PTRA               0x04    rw     0x0000_0000   CRYPT pointer A register.

    CRYPT_PTRB               0x08    rw     0x0000_0000   CRYPT pointer B register.

    CRYPT_PTRO               0x0C    rw     0x0000_0000   CRYPT pointer O register.

    CRYPT_CARRY              0x10    r      0x0000_0000   CRYPT carry/borrow bit register.

    33.4.1 CRYPT_CTRL

    Offset: 0x00

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 33-2 CRYPT_CTRL Register Description

    Bit Symbol         Type Description

    31:16 Rsvd     -   Reserved.

15 NOSTOP rw This register is used to control if the CPU will be stop by CRYPT engine when the

                       CRYPT engine is busy and CPU read or write CRYPT engine register.

                       0: Stop CPU when CRYPT engine is busy.

                       1: No stop CPU when CRYPT engine is busy.

                       This register only affect the behavior when CPU access the CRYPT register, if CPU

                       didn’t access the crypt register, this bit has no effect.

    14:12 Rsvd     -   Reserved.

11:8 LENGTH rw This bit is used to control the VLI length of current operation. Programmer can set

                       this register together with write 1 to ACT bit, but should not change this register

                       when ACT is 1.

                       0: 32 bits VLI

                       1: 64 bits VLI

                       2: 96 bits VLI

                       ……

                       15: 512 bits VLI

    7   Rsvd       -   Reserved.


    {dango                           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1161 / 1446

                                                                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

6:4 MODE rw This register controls the operation mode of crypt engine. Programmer can set

                      this register together with write 1 to ACT bit, but should not change this register

                      when ACT is 1.

                      0: Multiply mode, *PTr = *PTRA x *PTRB

                      1: Add mode, *PTr = *PTRA + *PTRB

                      2: Sub mode, *PTr = *PTRA - *PTRB

                      3: RSHIFT1 mode, *PTr = (*PTRA>>1)

                      4~7: Reserved.

   3:1   Rsvd    -    Reserved.

   0     ACT     rw   Write 1 to this bit will start an operation specified in the MODE register. And this

                      bit will be cleared automatically after the operation is done. Write 0 to this bit has

                      no effect.

   33.4.2 CRYPT_PTRA

   Offset: 0x04

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 33-3 CRYPT_PTRA Register Description

   Bit   Symbol Type Description

   31:16 Rsvd   -    Reserved.

15:0 PTRA rw This is the PTRA register of CRYPT controller. The value here defines an address in

                     the SRAM0 (byte unit). The data in this address will be read out to do the CRYPT

                     calculation. Since the CRYPT control only support 32 bits process, the PTRA[1:0]

                     should be 0 for all the time.

   33.4.3 CRYPT_PTRB

   Offset: 0x08

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 33-4 CRYPT_PTRB Register Description

   Bit   Symbol Type Description

   31:16 Rsvd   -    Reserved.

15:0 PTRB rw This is the PTRB register of CRYPT controller.

                     The value here defines an address in the SRAM0 (byte unit). The data in this

                     address will be read out to do the CRYPT calculation. Since the CRYPT control only

   {dango                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                      1162 / 1446

                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      support 32 bits process, the PTRB[1:0] should be 0 for all the time.

    33.4.4 CRYPT_PTRO

    Offset: 0x0C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 33-5 CRYPT_PTRO Register Description

    Bit Symbol Type Description

    31:16 Rsvd    -   Reserved.

15:0 PTRO rw This is the PTRO register of CRYPT controller.

                      The value here defines an address in the SRAM0 (byte unit). The CRYPT engine will

                      write calculation result into this address. Since the CRYPT control only support 32

                      bits process, the PTRO[1:0] should be 0 for all the time.

    33.4.5 CRYPT_CARRY

    Offset: 0x10

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 33-6 CRYPT_CARRY Register Description

    Bit Symbol Type Description

    31:1 Rsvd  -     Reserved.

    0    CARRY r     This bit represent the carry bit after add operation is done.

                     The bit represent borrow bit after sub operation is done. Programmer can read this

                     register immediately after the ACT bit is clear to 0.


    33.5     Application Note

    33.5.1 Data Format

    The VLI is a positive integer with 32~512 bits. So all the operations here are unsigned processes. The

    following table shows the data sequence in the SRAM.

    Table 33-7 the data sequence in the SRAM

     Address                                Data

     PTR + 0                                VLI[31:0]

     PTR + 4                                VLI[63:32]

     PTR + 8                                VLI[95:64]

    {dango            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1163 / 1446

                                                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

 PTR + 12                                   VLI[127:96]

 ……                                         ……

 PTR + 60                                   VLI[511:480]

When different kind of LENGTH mode is selected, only the selected range of VLI will be used as input

data. The following table shows the valid VLI bits under different LENGTH setting.

Table 33-8 the valid VLI bits under different LENGTH setting

 LENGTH                                      Data

 0                                           VLI[31:0]

 1                                           VLI[63:0]

 2                                           VLI[95:0]

 3                                           VLI[127:0]

 ……                                          ……

 15                                          VLI[511:0]

33.5.2 Operation Detail

For each operation, they will have different behavior on many ways, like the process cycles and the

output width. The following tables shows detail of each mode.

                 MULT             ADD                        SUB                  RSHIFT1

                 *PTr =           *PTr =                     *PTr =               *PTr =
 Operation
                 *PTRA x *PTRB    *PTRA + *PTRB              *PTRA - *PTRB        (*PTRA>>1)

 Output length   2*input length   input length               input length         input length

                                  0: Output not overflow.    0: *PTRA > *PTRB
 CARRY bit       X                                                                X
                                  1: Output overflow         1: *PTRA < *PTRB

 LENGTH          Operation Cycles

 0               8                6                          6                    6

 1               17               8                          8                    8

 2               32               12                         12                   10

 3               53               14                         14                   12

 4               80               18                         18                   14

 5               113              20                         20                   16

 6               152              24                         24                   18

 7               197              26                         26                   20

{dango               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                   1164 / 1446

                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

     8        248     30     30     22

     9        305     32     32     24

     10       368     36     36     26

     11       437     38     38     28

     12       512     42     42     30

     13       593     44     44     32

     14       680     48     48     34

     15       773     50     50     36

    By default, during the operation cycle, if the CPU accesses any of the register of CRYPT engine, the

    CPU will be halted until the operation is done. This is to prevent the configuration change during the

    operation. So if there is any high priority task in the system, it may block until the CPU is released by

    CRYPT engine. Set NOSTOP bit in the CRYPT_CTRL register to prevent this kind of situation. Under this

    condition, programmer should take care not to change the CRYPT configuration during the operation

    busy time.

    Since the CRYPT only supports operations in the SRAM0, when one of the input is not in the SRAM0

    (ex. In SRAM1 or SRAM2 or FLASH or rM), it is necessary to copy it into SRAM0 before do any

    operation.


    34 HFM Coprocessor (HFM)

    34.1 Introduction


HFM Coprocessor is a dedicated module for processing the harmonic voltage and current in application.

This engine is dedicated for system, offloading the calculation overload on CPU core. It has AHB slave

interface that the system could configure and submit the command through this slave interface. It has

an AHB Master for data fetch and processing. The main architecture is as shown below. The T2F Engine

could start fetching the waveform sampling and do the FFT calculation.


    34.2 Feature


 Support U + IA/ U + IA + IB/ UA + UB + UC + IA + IB + IC mode.

 Support 3 energy buckets.

{dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1165 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Support the calculation of the 1st to 21st harmonics.

     Support power and energy anti-submarine

HFM_PW_RMS0_1             0x1CC rw   0x0000_0000 The RMS power of phase B of energy bucket 0

HFM_PW_RMS0_2             0x1D0 rw   0x0000_0000 The RMS power of phase C of energy bucket 0

HFM_PW_RMS1_0             0x1D4 rw   0x0000_0000 The RMS power of phase A of energy bucket 1

HFM_PW_RMS1_1             0x1D8 rw   0x0000_0000 The RMS power of phase B of energy bucket 1

HFM_PW_RMS1_2             0x1DC rw   0x0000_0000 The RMS power of phase C of energy bucket 1

HFM_PW_RMS2_0             0x1E0 rw   0x0000_0000 The RMS power of phase A of energy bucket 2

HFM_PW_RMS2_1             0x1E4 rw   0x0000_0000 The RMS power of phase B of energy bucket 2

HFM_PW_RMS2_2             0x1E8 rw   0x0000_0000 The RMS power of phase C of energy bucket 2

34.4.1 HFM_DEVID

Offset: 0x000

Reset: 0xA000_0B0C

Table 34-2 HFM_DEVID Register Description

Bit          Symbol            Type      Description

31:0         DEVID             r         HFM device ID value.

34.4.2 HFM_MODE

Offset: 0x004

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 34-3 HFM_MODE Register Description

Bit     Symbol         Type   Description

31:29   Rsvd           -      Reserved.

28:24   NUM0           rw     Energy 0 bucket Harmonic number selection.

                              0: Fundamental wave

                              1~20: Second ~ twenty one harmonic

23:21   Rsvd           --     Reserved

20:16   NUM1           rw     Energy 1 bucket Harmonic number selection.

                              0: Fundamental wave

                              1~20: Second ~ twenty one harmonic

15:13   Rsvd           --     Reserved.

{dango                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                       1168 / 1446

                                                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

12:8   NUM2         rw   Energy 2 bucket Harmonic number selection.

                         0: Fundamental wave

                         1~20: Second ~ twenty one harmonic

7:2    Rsvd         -    Reserved.

1:0    MODE         rw   HFM mode configuration.

                         00: U + IA

                         01: U + IA + IB

                         10: UA + UB + UC + IA + IB + IC

                         11: Reserved

34.4.3 HFM_STS

Offset: 0x008

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 34-4 HFM_STS Register Description

Bit     Symbol                  Type    Description

31      REQVLD                  r       Valid Request asserted.

30      SATERR                  r       Saturation Error Occur.

29:6    Rsvd                    -       Reserved.

5       DSPBUSY                 r       The DSP is busy.

4       T2FBUSY                 r       The T2F is busy.

3       Rsvd                    -       Reserved.

2:0     FSM                     r       FFT Transform state.

34.4.4 HFM_EXCHADDR

Offset: 0x00C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 34-5 HFM_EXCHADDR Register Description

Bit   Symbol    Type Description

31:0 EXCHADDR rw   FFT calculation exchange buffer base address.

                   The base address should guarantee 512 bytes boundary. It will use 512 bytes

                   during space, software should keep 512B free up space.

34.4.5 HFM_WAVEADDR

Offset: 0x010
{dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                         1169 / 1446

                                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 34-6 HFM_WAVEADDR Register Description

   Bit   Symbol       Type  Description

31:0 WAVEADDR rw The source waveform & FFT Result buffer base address.

                            CFG_MODE:

                            ‘B00 (U+IA): Address keep 1KB boundary, free space of 1KB.

                            ‘B01(U+IA+IB): Address keep 2KB boundary, free space of 1.5KB

                            `B10 (3U+3I): Address keep 4KB boundary, free space of 3KB.

   34.4.6 HFM_DSPINSTRADDR

   Offset: 0x014

   Reset: 0x0008_0000

   Table 34-7 HFM_DSPINSTRADDR Register Description

   Bit  Symbol   Type Description

   31:0 INSTRADDR rw   The DSP instruction buffer base address.

                       CFG_MODE:

                       Address keep 16KB boundary.

                       Bit 31~14 is valid.

                       Eg. When DSP request the PC 0x4, the logic will convert to read INSTRADDR +

                       0x4<<2.

   34.4.7 HFM_DSPEXCHADDR

   Offset: 0x018

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 34-8 HFM_DSPEXCHADDR Register Description

   Bit  Symbol        Type Description

   31:0 DSPEXCHADDR rw      The DSP power value and exchange buffer base address. If the DSP request

                            address [10] is ‘1’, the access will be located at this 4kb space base on this

                            base address.

                            The value should keep 4KB boundary.

   34.4.8 HFM_CMD

   Offset: 0x020

   Reset: 0x0000_0000
   {dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  1170 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

   Table 34-9 HFM_CMD Register Description

   Bit   Symbol   Type   Description

   31:2 Rsvd      -      Reserved.

   1     DSPSTART r_w1c Start the DSP processing.

                         Write 1 to start the DSP.

                         When hardware finish the calculation, this bit will be cleared automatically.

0 T2FSTART r_w1c Start run Bit. Write 1 to start the engine.

                         When hardware finish the calculation, this bit will be cleared automatically.

   34.4.9 HFM_INTEN

   Offset: 0x024

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

                                 0: Disable

                                 1: Enable

                                 0: Disable

                                 1: Enable

   2      EGY0SPILL       rw     ENGY0 spill interrupt enable control.

                                 0: Disable

                                 1: Enable

   1      DSPDONE         rw     DSP process done interrupt enable control.

                                 0: Disable

                                 1: Enable

   0      T2FDONE         rw     T2F command done interrupt enable control.

                                 0: Disable

                                 1: Enable

   Offset: 0x028

   {dango                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            1171 / 1446

                                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

   4       EGY2SPILL         rc_w1     ENGY2 spill interrupt status.

   3       EGY1SPILL         rc_w1     ENGY1 spill interrupt status.

   2       EGY0SPILL         rc_w1     ENGY0 spill interrupt status.

   34.4.11     HFM_EGYxCONST

   Offset: 0x030 ~ 0x038

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   34.4.12     HFM_DSPCTRL1

   Offset: 0x03C

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 34-13 HFM_DSPCTRL1 Register Description

   Bit   Symbol      Type Description

   31:24 Rsvd        -     Reserved.

   23    PWRCRPEN    rw    Power creeping enable control.

                           The creeping judgment adopts the comparison between the average value

                           and the threshold value for three consecutive times. If the value is lower than

                           the threshold value, the subduction state is considered at this time.

                           0: Disable

                           1: Enable

22 PWRTHSEL2 rw Energy 2 power threshold value selection.

                           0: Select threshold 0 (HFM_EGYPWRTH0)

                           1: Select threshold 0 (HFM_EGYPWRTH1)

21 PWRTHSEL1 rw Energy 1 power threshold value selection.

   {dango                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                              1172 / 1446

                                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                       0: Select threshold 0 (HFM_EGYPWRTH0)

                       1: Select threshold 0 (HFM_EGYPWRTH1)

20 PWRTHSEL0 rw Energy 0 power threshold value selection.

                       0: Select threshold 0 (HFM_EGYPWRTH0)

                       1: Select threshold 0 (HFM_EGYPWRTH1)

19:18 CF2OUTSEL rw CF2 output energy bucket selection.

                       00: CF2 output comes from energy bucket 0

                       01: CF2 output comes from energy bucket 1

                       10: CF2 output comes from energy bucket 2

                       11: CF2 output is always  0

17:16 CF1OUTSEL rw CF1 output energy bucket selection.

                       00: CF1 output comes from energy bucket 0

                       01: CF1 output comes from energy bucket 1

                       10: CF1 output comes from energy bucket 2

                       11: CF1 output is always  0

15:14 CF0OUTSEL rw CF0 output energy bucket selection.

                       00: CF0 output comes from energy bucket 0

                       01: CF0 output comes from energy bucket 1

                       10: CF0 output comes from energy bucket 2

                       11: CF0 output is always  0

13 CFOUTINV2 rw Polarity reversal of Energy Bucket 2.

                       0: output original polarity

                       1: output opposite polarity

12 CFOUTINV1 rw Polarity reversal of Energy Bucket 1.

                       0: output original polarity

                       1: output opposite polarity

11 CFOUTINV0 rw Polarity reversal of Energy Bucket 0.

                       0: output original polarity

                       1: output opposite polarity

    10   CFOUTEN2 rw   CF2 output enable control.

                       0: CF2 output 0

                       1: CF2 output enable

                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1173 / 1446

                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    9     CFOUTEN1     rw   CF1 output enable control.

                            0: CF1 output 0

                            1: CF1 output enable

    8     CFOUTEN0     rw   CF0 output enable control.

                            0: CF0 output 0

                            1: CF0 output enable

    7:6   CFPULSESEL rw     CF pulse period selection.

                            00: 80ms

                            01: 40ms

                            10: 20ms

                            11: 10ms

5:4 CFFASTEN rw CF output frequency select through changing the threshold value.

                            00: The energy value is compared with {threshold *16384}, CF output

                            frequency x1

                            01: The energy value is compared with {threshold *16384/4}, CF output

                            frequency x4

                            10: The energy value is compared with {threshold *16384/8}, CF output

                            frequency x8

                            11: The energy value is compared with {threshold *16384/16}, CF output

                            frequency x16

    3     EGY2EN       rw   Energy bucket 2 switch (including CF)

    2     EGY1EN       rw   Energy bucket 1 switch (including CF)

    1     EGY0EN       rw   Energy bucket 0 switch (including CF)

    0     CRPEN        rw   Energy bucket to prevent creeping switch control.

                            0: Disable

                            1: Enable

                      1: Enable

                      1: Enable

                      1: Enable

                      Energy bucket 2 power accumulation mode select

                      0: Add the positive numbers

26:25 PWRMODE2   rw   1: Add the negative numbers

                      2: Add the original values

                      3: Add the absolute values

                      Energy barrel 2 Power accumulation source selection.

                      0: Add the active power

                      1: Add the reactive power

                      When the accumulation source selects the effective value, the bit
24   TYPESEL2    rw
                      represents the choice of combining mode of the effective value of

                      energy barrel 2:

                      0: RMSA+RMSB+RMSC

                      1: RMSA-RMSB-RMSC

                      Energy barrel 2 Accumulation source selection.

                      00: Add the power value
22:21 INMODE2    rw
                      01: Add the effective current values

                      10~11: Add the constants

                      Energy barrel 2 Energy accumulation mode selection.

20   EGYMODE2    rw   0: Add the original value

                      1: Add the absolute value

                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                     1175 / 1446

                      1: Enable

                      1: Enable

                      A channel of energy bucket 1 enable control.

17   CHAEN1      rw   0: Disable

                      1: Enable

                      Energy bucket 1 power accumulation mode select

                      0: Add the positive numbers

16:15 PWRMODE1   rw   1: Add the negative numbers

                      2: Add the original values

                      3: Add the absolute values

                      Energy barrel 1 Power accumulation source selection.

                      0: Add the active power

                      1: Add the reactive power

                      When the accumulation source selects the effective value, the bit
14   TYPESEL1    rw
                      represents the choice of combining mode of the effective value of

                      energy barrel 1:

                      0: RMSA+RMSB+RMSC

                      1: RMSA-RMSB-RMSC

13   Rsvd        -    Reserved.

                      Energy barrel 1 Accumulation source selection.

                      00: Add the power value
12:11 INMODE1    rw
                      01: Add the effective current values

                      10~11: Add the constants

                      Energy barrel 1 Energy accumulation mode selection.

10   EGYMODE1    rw   0: Add the original value

                      1: Add the absolute value

                      C channel of energy bucket 0 enable control.

9    CHCEN0      rw   0: Disable

                      1: Enable

                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                     1176 / 1446

                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                       B channel of energy bucket 0 enable control.

8     CHBEN0      rw   0: Disable

                       1: Enable

                       A channel of energy bucket 0 enable control.

7     CHAEN0      rw   0: Disable

                       1: Enable

                       Energy bucket 0 power accumulation mode select

                       0: Add the positive numbers

6:5   PWRMODE0    rw   1: Add the negative numbers

                       2: Add the original values

                       3: Add the absolute values

    34.4.17       HFM_EGYCFDIVTH

    Offset: 0x050

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 34-18 HFM_EGYCFDIVTH Register Description

    Bit   Symbol  Type Description

    31:16 Rsvd    -     Reserved.

15:0 CFDIVTH rw Energy bucket overflow counting threshold

                        Counting threshold overflow every two times, generate a CF pulse. If the interval

                        time between two overflows is less than the setting pulse width, the pulse will be

                        output with 50% duty cycle; otherwise, CF will be output according to the set

                        pulse width.

                        That is to say, CF frequency is the frequency division multiple relation of energy

                        overflow frequency. Configurable 2/ 4/ 6/... only Even frequency division.

                        0/ 1/ 2:2 frequency division.

                        When the value N is 3, 5, 7, 9... For these odd numbers, CF pulse is n-1

                        frequency divider of overflow frequency.


    {dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1178 / 1446

                                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

0      EGYOUTCLR0      r_w1c   Cumulative energy value clear for energy bucket 0.
