34.4.23     HFM_EGYCRPSTS

Offset: 0x090

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 34-24 HFM_EGYCRPSTS Register Description

Bit     Symbol    Type     Description

31:21   Rsvd      -        Reserved.

20:12   QOV       r        Reactive power creeping status of each channel.

                           [12]: Q0_0, A phase reactive power of energy bucket 0

                           [13]: Q0_1, B phase reactive power of energy bucket 0

                           [14]: Q0_2, C phase reactive power of energy bucket 0

                           [15]: Q1_0, A phase reactive power of energy bucket 1

                           [16]: Q1_1, B phase reactive power of energy bucket 1

                           [17]: Q1_2, C phase reactive power of energy bucket 1

                           [18]: Q2_0, A phase reactive power of energy bucket 2

                           [19]: Q2_1, B phase reactive power of energy bucket 2

                           [20]: Q2_2, C phase reactive power of energy bucket 2

                           0: Startup status.

                           1: Creep state.

11:3    POV      r         Active power creeping status of each channel.

                           [3]: P0_0, the A phase active power of energy bucket 0

                           [4]: P0_1, the B phase active power of energy bucket 0

                           [5]: P0_2, the C phase active power of energy bucket 0


                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1180 / 1446

                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                   [6]: P1_0, the A phase active power of energy bucket 1

                   [7]: P1_1, the B phase active power of energy bucket 1

                   [8]: P1_2, the C phase active power of energy bucket 1

                   [9]: P2_0, the A phase active power of energy bucket 2

                   [10]: P2_1, the B phase active power of energy bucket 2

                   [11]: P2_2, the C phase active power of energy bucket 2

                   0: Startup status

                   1: Creeping state

2:0   EGYCRP   r   The flag of creeping status.

                   [0]:creep status of energy bucket 0

                   [1]:creep status of energy bucket 1

                   [2]:creep status of energy bucket 2

                   0: Startup status

                   1: Creeping state

34.5     Application Note

34.5.1 DATA Format

The following table shows the data sequence in the SRAM.

U+IA Mode waveform data in SRAM:

 Offset Address                  Data
 0                               IA.0[31:0]                  U.0[31:0]     |
 8                               IA.1[31:0]                  U.1[31:0]

 16                              IA.2[31:0]                  U.2[31:0]

 24                              IA.3[31:0]                  U.3[31:0]

 ……                              ……                          ……

 1016                            IA.127[31:0]                U.127[31:0]

U+IA Mode FFT result in SRAM:

 Offset Address     Data

 0                  IA.0.Imaginary[31:0]                U.0.Imaginary[31:0]

 8                  IA.0.Real[31:0]                     U.0.Real[31:0]

 16                 IA.1.Imaginary[31:0]                U.1.Imaginary[31:0]

 24                 IA.1.Real[31:0]                     U.1.Real[31:0]

 ……                 ……                                  ……

 1016               IA.63.Real[31:0]                    U.63.Real[31:0]

U+IA+IB Mode waveform data in SRAM:

 Offset Address                 Data

 0                              IA.0[31:0]                U.0[31:0]

 8                              U.1[31:0]                   IB.0[31:0]

 16                             IB.1[31:0]                  IA.1[31:0]
§                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1189 / 1446

                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

 24                             IA.2[31:0]        U.2[31:0]

 ……                             ……                ……

 1524                           IB.127[31:0]      IA.127[31:0]

U+IA+IB Mode FFT result in SRAM:

 Offset Address      Data

 0                   IA.0.Imaginary[31:0]       U.0.Imaginary[31:0]

 8                   U.0.Real[31:0]             IB.0.Imaginary[31:0]

 16                  IB.0.Real[31:0]            IA.0.Real[31:0]

 24                  IA.1.Imaginary[31:0]       U.1.Imaginary[31:0]

 ……                  ……                         ……

 1524                IB.63.Real[31:0]           IA.63.Real[31:0]

U1+U2+U3+I1+I2+I3 Mode waveform data in SRAM:

 Offset Address                 Data

 0                              U2.0[31:0]        U1.0[31:0]

 8                              I1.0[31:0]        U3.0[31:0]

 16                             I3.0[31:0]        I2.0[31:0]

 24                             U2.1[31:0]        U2.1[31:0]

 ……                             ……                ……

 3048                           I3.127[31:0]      I2.127[31:0]

U+IA+IB Mode FFT result in SRAM:

 Offset Address     Data

 0                  U2.0.Imaginary[31:0]        U1.0.Imaginary[31:0]

 8                  I1.0.Imaginary[31:0]        U3.0.Imaginary[31:0]

 16                 U2.0.Real[31:0]             U1.0.Real[31:0]

 24                 I1.0.Real[31:0]             U3.0.Real[31:0]

 ……                 ……                          ……

 3048               I3.63.Real[31:0]            I2.63.Real[31:0]

34.5.2 DSP

34.5.2.1     DSP Access Space Map

The DSP request address is 11-bits width, the address is word address, so the total access space is 8KB.

fi                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1190 / 1446

                                                                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    DSP could fetch the waveform and the T2F result through this bus, also it could update the energer

    register through this bus.

The space is consistent of two blocks, the address 0x0 ~ 0x2FF is map to the AHB ram space, the

    actual physical address is req_addr + waver_baddr.

    The address 0x300 ~ 0x3FF is mapping to APB slave register. So the DSP could update the energer of

    the calculation result.

    The address from 0x400 ~0x4FF is mapping to DSP data ram (results and calc buf), the results mapping

    shows as below. Its base addr is DSP_EXCH_BADDR.

    The results address mapped as below:

    Table 34-55 Result Address Map

    Name                      DSP_ADDR(%d)Soc_offset (base on      Harm Num

                                        DSP_EXCH_BADDR)

    DSP_RAM_DAT_PA            1024      0                          1–21

    DSP_RAM_DAT_QA            1045      21                         1–21

    DSP_RAM_DAT_PB            1066      42                         1–21

    DSP_RAM_DAT_QB            1087      63                         1–21

    DSP_RAM_DAT_PC            1108      84                         1–21

    DSP_RAM_DAT_QC            1129      105                        1–21

    DSP_RAM_DAT_UA_RMS        1150      126                        1–21

    DSP_RAM_DAT_UB_RMS        1192      168                        1–21

    DSP_RAM_IA_HR             1297      273                        data

    DSP_RAM_UB_HR             1318      294

    DSP_RAM_IB_HR             1339      315

    DSP_RAM_UC_HR             1360      336

    DSP_RAM_IC_HR             1381      357

DSP_RAM_DAT_Q_ACC   1404                    380

DSP_RAM_DAT_U_HR_ACC_H1406                  382

34.5.2.2     DSP program guide

1.   Configure DSPINSTRADDR (keep 1KB boundary), Write DSP instruction code to SRAM according

DSP instructions base address.

2.   Configure DSPEXCHADDR (keep 4KB boundary), DSP will write all fund and harmonic (1-21)

results to this address area.

3.   Load ratio calibration configure to DSPCALICFGADDR. (these value will be recommend after FPGA

test)

4.   T2F result to address WAVEADDR .DSP fetch data from WAVEADDR after DSP start.

5.   Configure CMD.DSPSTART to 1, start DSP.

6.   Waiting CMD.DSPSTART to become 0, check INTSTS. DSPDONE bit.

7.   DSP load results needed by energy accumulation to power value registers according to

MODE.NUMx.
{dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                          1192 / 1446

             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    34.5.3 EGY

    34.5.3.1 EGY program note

    1.   Enable EGY by configure DSPCTRL1. EGYxEN bit to 1.

    2.   Enable energy anti-creep by configure DSPCTRL1.CRPEN bit to 1.

    3.   Enable power anti-creep by configure DSPCTRL1. PWRCRPEN bit to 1.

    4.   CF output mapped as CF0–>IOD2, CF1–>IOD3, CF2->IOD4.

    5.   Configure SYSCFG1_ENGYCFEN.ENGYCFEN and DSPCTRL1. CFOUTENx to 1, enable CF output to IO

    port.

    6.   EGY accumulate using power value register, includes P /Q /RMS of 3-phase selected by ENGYCFG0

    register.

    7.   MODE.NUMx make DSP choose which harmonic write to power value.

    8.   EGY accumulation frequency controlled by register ENGYCLKDIV. Accumulation frequency is divided

    by ENGYCLKDIV of HCLK.

    9.   Support energy overflow interrupt.

    10. CF pulse frequency is divided by EGYCFDIVTH. Only even division factor is supported.

    35 Waver Controller (WAVER)

    35.1     Introduction


Vango metering chip transmits waveform data via SPI, WAVER must receive the data through BSPI0. It

processes the received waveform data into the 698 protocol, and sends 698 protocol data through

BSPI1. The received data is filtered and compensated by CIC for FFT analysis.

WAVER locks the synchronization byte first, and then obtains U, IA and IB according to the configured

transmission mode.

When WAVER receives data, only the frame header and the data that have passed the check are

considered valid data. WAVER data must be received through DMA, and the received data is placed in

SRAM. The length of the DMA received data is related to the configuration. For example, WAVE_LEN =

0 (256), CICEN = 0, MODE = 1 (U + IA + IB), set the DMA received data length to 256 * 3 = 768

(words), where the frame length is 256, the packet length is 3, and the receiving mode is word. After

the DMA receives a cycle, configure the HFM module to extract data from the SRAM for T2F calculation.


    {dango  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1193 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   35.2 Features


     Support locking frame header data, data verification and integrity check.

     Support waveform sampling input mode UA + IA, U + IA + IB, UA + UB + UC + IA + IB + IC.

     Support 698 protocol to upload data.

     Support SPI0_1 to upload data using big endian/little endian.

     Support 2/3/6 Channels parallel CIC Filtering

     Support 2/3/6 Channels parallel compensation.

     Support down sampling from 256 samples to 128 per-cycles.

     Support CIC and Compensation bypass.


    35.3 Block Diagram


    The main architecture is as follow:

        CFG                                                MODE

                                                       |                               ||
    V92XXX    SPI0                 FIFO                |  FIFOx  CICx  COMPx           |
    SPI1      698 protocol  Little/Big Endian  IB_FSM  Backup FIFOx    x=6(channels)   |||
                                                       |                               |
              CM33

        Figure 35-1 WAVER Main Architecture


    35.4 Waveform In-bound Format


The waveform data is serially transmitted to WAVER in bytes. The first byte is the synchronization byte,

which is used to synchronize the start of the transmission. The synchronization byte is 0x7D.

The last byte of each group of data is the check value. According to the MODE configuration, there are

3 transmission modes. Detailed information refer to 35.6.2.

CKSUM is the sum of each group of data, and then the value obtained by adding 0x33. When MODE=2,

the CHSUM algorithm is as follows:

CKSUM = 0x33 + ~ (U_Byte0 + U_Byte1 + U_Byte2 + IA_Byte0 + IA_Byte1 + IA_Byte2 + IB_Byte0

fi Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1194 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    + IB_Byte1 + IB_Byte2);

    Transfer U( 24 bits)+IA( 24bits)+IB( 24bits):


HEAD[7:0] U[23:16] U[15:8] U[7:0] IA[23:16] IA[15:8] IA[7:0]
   0x7D

    nn ————



    IB[23:16]   IB[15:8]     IB[7:0] CKSUM[7:0]          IDLE

                                               LLL ————
        Figure 35-2 SPI Transfer structure of U+IA+IB

    Transfer of U+IA:

    HEAD[7:0]     U[23:16] U[15:8]   U[7:0] IA[23:16]  IA[15:8] IA[7:0] CKSUM[7:0]
       0x7D

        ————        ——

        Figure 35-3 SPI transfer structure of U+IA

    Transfer of UA+UB+UC+IA+IB+IC:

    HEAD[7:0]  UA[23:16]     UA[15:8] UA[7:0] UB[23:16]  UB[15:8]     UB[7:0]
       0x7D

    Mn


    UC[23:16] UC[15:8] UC[7:0] IA[23:16] IA[15:8] IA[7:0] IB[23:16]

    nn ————



   IB[15:8] IB[7:0] IC[23:16] IC[15:8] IC[7:0] CKSUM[7:0]

nn ————

        Figure 35-4 SPI transfer structure of UA + UB + UC + IA + IB + IC


    35.5 Waver upload frame format


    Table 35-1 Waver upload frame format

    Num  Code          Description

    1    68H           Start of frame.

    2    31H/33H/34H   Command symbol:


    i                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1195 / 1446

                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        31H: For single-phase voltage and current sampling;

                        33H: For three-phase voltage and current sampling;

                        34H: Single-phase voltage, live line current and zero line current

                        sampling;

     L0 L1              Length field (2 bytes) (frame bytes except start symbol and end
 3
                        symbol).

 4   Frame number       Frame number（1 Byte）.

     Voltage sampling   Data field:

     value 1            Voltage sampling value  1 (3 bytes, hex complement code);
 5
     Current sample     Current sampling value  1 (3 bytes, hex complement code);

     value 1

 6   ......             Data field.

     Voltage sampling   Data field:

     value n            Voltage sampling value n (3 bytes, hex complement code);
 7
     Current sample     Current sampling value n (3 bytes, hex complement code);

     value n

     Cumulative sum     Cumulative sum. (from the command character to the data field,
 8
                        including the cumulative sum of the data field, 4 bytes)

 9   16H                End of frame.

Note 1: the transmission sequence adopts little endian.

Note 2: when the command character is 31H, the transmission sequence of voltage sampling value and

current sampling value is: 3 byte voltage sampling value and 3 byte current sampling value; when the

command character is 33H, the transmission sequence of voltage sampling value and current sampling

value is: 3 byte A-phase voltage sampling value, 3-byte B-phase voltage sampling value, 3-byte C-

phase voltage sampling value, 3-byte A-phase current sampling value, 3-byte B-phase current

sampling value, 3-byte C-phase current sampling value; when the command character is 34h, the

transmission sequence of voltage sampling value and current sampling value is: 3-byte voltage

sampling value, 3-byte current sampling value, and 3-byte zero line current sampling value.

Note 3: the serial number of data frame is used to judge whether the data has packet loss.








                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1196 / 1446

   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
  35.6 WAVER Registers


   The register field could be accessed through the AHB bus. The core could set the configuration and

   enable the Waver through the register configuration.

   Table 35-2 WAVER Registers Map

   Register Name       Offset   Type  Reset Value      Description

   WAVER_CFG           0x20     rw    0x0000_0000      WAVER configuration register

   WAVER_MODE          0x30     rw    0x0000_0000      WAVER mode configuration register

   WAVER_DATA          0x40     rw    0x0000_0000      WAVER data register

   WAVER_INFO          0x4C     rw    0x0000_0000      WAVER information register

   WAVER_STATE         0x50     rw    0x0000_0000      WAVER state register

   35.6.1 WAVER_CFG

   Offset: 0x20

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 35-3 WAVER_CFG Register Description

   Bit   Symbol   Type Description

   31    EN       rw   WAVER module enable control.

                       0: Disable

                       1: Enable

   30:9 Rsvd      -    Reserved.

   8     ENDIAN   rw   Endian configuration.

                       0: Little Endian

                       1: Big Endian

   7     BAKEN    rw   The backup waveform for CM0 processing control.

                       0: Disable

                       1: Enable

   6:5   LEN      rw   The number of samples per-cycle.

                       00: 256 samples per-cycle

                       01: 128 samples per-cycle

                       10~11: Reserved

4 TRANSEN rw Transport the upload Waveform to CM33 system enable control.

   {dango                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1197 / 1446

                                                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        0: Disable the transport

                        1: Enable the transport

3     UPLOADEN  rw      Waveform sample upload enable control.

                        0: Disable

                        1: Enable

2     CICEN     rw      CIC Down sample enable.

                        0: Down sample disabled.

                        1: Down sample enable.

1     CICSEL    rw      Down sample rate selection.

                        0: Down sample to 128 points per-cycle. 128 points contain one cycle.

                        1: Down sample to 64 points per-cycle. 128 points contain two cycles.

0     COMPEN    rw      Compensation enable control.

                        0: Disable

                        1: Enable

35.6.2 WAVER_MODE

Offset: 0x30

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 35-4 WAVER_MODE Register Description

Bit     Symbol       Type   Description

31:2    Rsvd         -      Reserved.

1:0     MODE         rw     Waver mode configuration.

                            00: UA + IA (31H)

                            01: U + IA + IB (34H)

                            10: UA + UB + UC + IA + IB + IC (33H)

                            11: Reserved

35.6.3 WAVER_DATA

Offset: 0x40

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 35-5 WAVER_DATA Register Description

Bit    Symbol     Type     Description

31:0   DATA       r        Sample data buffer candidate for FFT analysis.

{dango                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                      1198 / 1446

                                                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Note: The data from sample data buffer is 20 valid bits with 1 signed bit. The first sample from data

buffer is U, then IA and IB if enabled.

35.6.4 WAVER_INFO

Offset: 0x4C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 35-6 WAVER_INFO Register Description

Bit    Symbol            Type   Description

31:18  Rsvd              -      Reserved.

17     LOCKERR           rc_w1  A Lock Error has been occur between two cycles wave.

16:9   LOCKERRCNT        r      Flame count of Lock Error cycles.

                                When clear LOCKERR, auto clear LOCKERRCNT to 0

8      INTGRITYERR       rc_w1  An integrity error occurred while receiving data.

7:0    INTGRITYERRCNT    r      Flame count of Integrity Error cycles.

                                When clear INTGRITYERR, auto clear INTGRITYERRCNT to 0.

35.6.5 WAVER_STATE

Offset: 0x50

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 35-7 WAVER_STATE Register Description

Bit    Symbol       Type Description

31:29  Rsvd         -   Reserved.

28:21  FCNT         r   Current flame count.

20:12  OPCNT        r   Current operation count (max 256).

11:0   IBOUNDSTS    r   Integrity Error occur on IB SPI0 interface.

                        12’h1: IDLE

                        12’h2: SYNC_LOCK(waiting to download 7D from SPI0)

                        12’h4: FETCH_UA(waiting to download UA data from SPI0)

                        12’h8: FETCH_UB (waiting to download UB data from SPI0)

                        12’h10: FETCH_UC (waiting to download UC data from SPI0)

                        12’h20: FETCH_IA (waiting to download IA data from SPI0)

                        12’h40: FETCH_IB (waiting to download IB data from SPI0)

                        12’h80: FETCH_IC (waiting to download IC data from SPI0)

{dango                      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                             1199 / 1446

V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

12'h100: Checksum (waiting to download checksum data from SPI0)

12’h200: PUT_EOF (upload state grid frame header to SPI1)

12’h400: PUT_Data (upload data to SPI1)

12’h800: PUT_EOF (upload end of CKSUM frame to SPI1)

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   36 Basic Direct Memory Access Controller
    (BDMA)

   36.1 Introduction


The BDMA controller is used to transfer data from memory to memory or transfer data from memory to

IO or from IO to memory. There are total 4 channels integrated in V32F20x/ V32H20x and each

channel’s priority is round-robin.


   36.2 Features


     Memory to Memory Transfer

     Memory to IO Transfer

     IO to Memory Transfer

     Software mode or Hardware DMA request mode.

     Support Fix, Package Round or Frame Round Address mode.

     Support Package/Frame/Data-abort IRQ generation.

     Support detection of data abort on bus.

     Integrated with AHB to APB Bridge.

     Support AES 128/192/256 encode/decode

     KEY register access protection.










    fi    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1201 / 1446

    36.5.3 BDMA_CTLx

    Offset: 0x0010
    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 36-5 shows the bit assignment of BDMA_CTLx register, the data in this register will be latched

    into DMA channel after the transfer is start, so any modification to this register won’t affect the on-

    going DMA transfer. Only STOP bit can stop the DMA transfer immediately.

    Offset: 0x0010

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 36-5 BDMA_CTLx Register Description

    Bit   Symbol Type Description

    31:24 FLEN   rw   Frame length

                      Actual transfer frame length is (FLEN + 1).

    23:16 PLEN   rw   Package length

                      Actual transfer package length is (PLEN + 1).

    15    STOP   rw   Force stop DMA transfer

                      Write 1 to this bit will force the DMA channel back to idle state, programmer

                      should clear this bit to 0 if another DMA transfer need to be assigned into the

                      same channel.

14 AESEN rw Enable AES encrypt/decrypt function of DMA channel

                      Only channel 3 supports AES function. When AES function is enabled, the SIZE

                      should be set to 32 bits mode and SMODE and TMODE should be set to 1 or 2. And

                      package size should be multipy of 4.

    13    CONT   rw   Continuous mode


                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1206 / 1446

                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                  DMA transfer will not stop until STOP bit is set to 1. Every time DMA complete all

                  package and frame transfer, it will start from beginning and do the transfer

                  continuously.

12    TMODE rw    Transfer mode selection

                  0: One DMA request only transfers one single data.

                  1: One DMA request transfers one package data.

11:7  Rsvd  -     Reserved

6:5   DMODE rw    Destination address mode

                  0: Fix.

                  1: Incremental but rounded at package end.

                  2: Incremental but rounded at frame end.

                  3: Reserved.

4:3   SMODE rw    Source address mode

                  0: Fix.

                  1: Incremental but rounded at package end.

                  2: Incremental but rounded at frame end.

                  3: Reserved.

2:1   SIZE   rw   Transfer size mode

                  0: Byte (8 bits).

                  1: Half-word (16 bits)

                  2: Word (32 bits)

                  3: Reserved.

0     EN     rw   DMA channel enable

                  0: Disable DMA channel (no effect when CONT is 1)

                  1: Enable DMA channel.

                  This bit will be cleared automatically by hardware when a DMA transfer is done at

                  CONT is 0.

Reset: 0x0000_0000

36.5.9 BDMA_AESKEYx

Offset: 0x00A0

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 36-12 BDMA_AESKEYx Register Description (x = 0 ~ 7)

Bit Symbol Type Description

31:0 KEYx rw   AES KEY register.

               KEY0: Bit 31~0

               KEY1: Bit 63~32

               KEY2: Bit 95~64

               KEY3: Bit 127~96

               KEY4: Bit 159~128

               KEY5: Bit 191~160

               KEY6: Bit 223~192

               KEY7: Bit 255~224.

               When mode is AES128, only bit 127~0 is used. When mode is AES192, only bit

               191~0 is used.

               When mode is AES256, bit 255~0 is used.

This register stores the AES key for the program code or data encryption and decryption. The key is a

confidential info to the chip, so these key registers should always only be accessed by secure and

privilege APP or logic. Non-secure APP could not read or write to it.










               Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1210 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   37 Basic Serial Peripheral Interface
    (BSPI)

   37.1 Introduction


A Basic Serial Peripheral Interface (BSPI) controller is built in V32F20x/ V32H20x to facilitate

communicating with other devices and components. The setting in BSPI controller will be reset after

wake up from sleep or deep-sleep mode, programmer should restore the setting manually after wake

up from these two states. The BSPICLK range supported by BSPI host mode is APBCLK/ 2/ 4/ 8/ 16/

32/ 64/ 128. The maximum BSPICLK clock supported by BSPI slave mode is APBCLK /2.


   37.2 Features


     Support both master and slave mode.

     Support single byte and consecutive bytes transferring.

     Support receive overrun error indication.

     Support transmitting / receiving interrupt request.

     Programmable phase and polarity of master clock.

     Selectable data sampling time (end or middle of clock period).

     Programmable master SCK clock frequency: APB Clock / 2, / 4, /8, /16, /32, /64, /128.

     Built-in 8-depth 8-bits FIFO in both transmit and receive direction, the interrupt level of these two

    FIFOs is both programmable.










    i    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1211 / 1446

 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
37.3 Block Diagram


         Peripheral Bus（APB）

             Read

         RX FIFO

 MOSI

 MISO    Shift register
                       Communication
                         controller

         TX FIFO

                  Write

 CLK           Baudrate  SCLSEL
              generator


 CSN         CSN
             logic

     Figure 37-1 BSPI Controller Block Diagram

 Usually, the SPI is connected to the external device by 4 pins.

 MOSI: The output pin in master mode/the input pin in slave mode. In general, MOSI is used to send

 data in master mode and receive data in slave mode.

 MISO: The input pin in master mode/the output pin in slave mode. In general, MISO is used to receive

 data in master mode and send data in slave mode.

 CLK: BSPI serial clock pin, and controlled by master.

 CSN: Slave chip select pin, According to the setting of BSPI and CSN, CSN can be used as following:

 --Select a slave to communication.

 --Synchronous data frame.

 Note: The maximum BSPICLK clock supported by BSPI slave mode is 1/2 APBCLK.

37.4.1 BSPI_CTRL

Offset: 0x0000

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 37-2 BSPI_CTRL Register Description

Bit   Symbol  Type  Description

30:16 Rsvd    -     Reserved.

15    BSPIEN  rw    BSPI enable.

                    If this bit is set to “1”, the corresponding GPIO become the BSPI interface.

                    These pins cannot be used as GPIO. Therefore, any further corresponding

                    setting on the selected GPIOs will be no effect.

                    0: Disable

                    1: Enable

14:13 Rsvd    -     Reserved.

12    LSBF    rw    BSPI LSB control.

                    Data bit transfer control register

                    0: Most significant bit

                    1: Least significant bit

11    BSPIRST r_w1c BSPI software reset.

                    If this bit is written by “1”, the state machine of BSPI controller and FIFO

                    pointer will return to the original value.

10    CSGPIO  rw    The control owner of BSPICS.

9     SWAP    rw    BSPI MISO/MOSI swap control.

                    0: No swap

                    1: Swap

8     MOD     rw    BSPI Mode Selection register.

                    0: Master

                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            1213 / 1446

                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                      1: Slave

7:6   Rsvd    -       Reserved

5     SCKPHA rw       BSPI clock phase.

                      Refer to timing scheme on previous section

4     SCKPOL  rw      BSPI clock polarity.

                      Refer to timing scheme on previous section

3     Rsvd    -       Reserved.

2:0   SCKSEL  rw      Master mode clock selection.

                      000: APBCLK / 2

                      001: APBCLK / 4

                      010: APBCLK / 8

                      010: APBCLK / 16

                      100: APBCLK / 32

                      101: APBCLK / 64

                      110: APBCLK / 128

                      111: Reserved

37.4.2 BSPI_TXSTS

Offset: 0x0004

Reset: 0x0000_8200

Table 37-3 BSPI_TXSTS Register Description

Bit   Symbol      Type  Description

31:16 WMASK       w     Write mask bit. The mask bit value ‘1’ would make the corresponding bit

                        writeable.

                        Bit[16] = ‘1’, means bit[0] writeable.

                        …

                        Bit[31] = ‘1’, means bit[15] writeable.

15    BSPITXIF    rw    BSPI Transmit Interrupt flag.

                        This bit is set to “1” by hardware when the transmit FIFO level is lower then

                        the value set by the user. When SMART is set in BSPI_MISC register, the bit

                        will be cleared as long as the transmit FIFO level is high than interrupt level,

                        else you should write “1” to this control bit to clear this flag.


                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1214 / 1446

                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                       Read 0= Not Occurred

                       Read 1= Occurred

                       Write 0= No effect

                       Write 1= Clear the flag

14    BSPITXIEN   rw   BSPI Transmit Interrupt Enable.

                       If this bit is set to “1”, and BSPI interrupt (when 8-bit TX FIFO level in lower

                       then interrupt level) occurs, hardware will issue an IRQ3 or FIQ to CPU. If

                       this bit is cleared to “0”, this interrupt will be masked.

                       0: Disabled

                       1: Enabled

13:10 Rsvd        -    Reserved.

9     TXEMPTY     r    Transmot FIFO empty register.

                       This bit will be set by hardware when the transmit FIFO is empty. And will

                       be clear by hardware when the transmit FIFO is not empty.

                       0: Not empty

                       1: Emoty

8     TXFUR       r    Transmit FIFO under run register.

                       This will be set when transmit FIFO is empty and more data is requested by

                       external BSPI master, this bit will be set only at BSPI slave mode. This bit

                       will be cleared when programmer write 1 to BSPITXIF flag.

7     Rsvd        -    Reserved.

6:4   TXFLEV      rw   Transmit FIFO interrupt level register.

                       This register is used to indicated how much empty slots is required when

                       issued interrupt. The larger value is set, the lower interrupt penalty you

                       have. Since you can write more data in one interrupt.

                       FIFO Full Interrupt issue timing

                       000: Data no. in FIFO < 1,  8 write is  allowed.

                       001: Data no. in FIFO < 7,  1 write is  allowed.

                       010: Data no. in FIFO < 6,  2 write is  allowed.

                       011: Data no. in FIFO < 5,  3 write is  allowed.

                       100: Data no. in FIFO < 4,  4 write is  allowed.


                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.       1215 / 1446

                                                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        101: Data no. in FIFO < 3,  5 write is  allowed.

                        110: Data no. in FIFO < 2,  6 write is  allowed.

                        111: Data no. in FIFO < 1,  7 write is  allowed

   3     DMATXDONE rc_w1 Transfer done flag, will auto set 1 after transmit all the data. Write 1 to

                        clear.

   2:0   TXFFLAG    r   Transmit FIFO Data Level.

                        The register is used to indicate how much data is still in the FIFO.

                        000: No data in FIFO or 8 bytes in FIFO.

                        001: 1 byte in FIFO.

                        010: 2 bytes in FIFO.

                        011: 3 bytes in FIFO.

                        100: 4 bytes in FIFO.

                        101: 5 bytes in FIFO.

                        110: 6 bytes in FIFO.

                        111: 7 bytes in FIFO.

   37.4.4 BSPI_RXSTS

   Offset: 0x000C

   Reset: 0x0000_0000

   Table 37-5 BSPI_RXSTS Register Description

   Bit   Symbol     Type Description

31:16 WMASK w Write mask bit. The mask bit value ‘1’ would make the corresponding bit

                       writeable.

                       Bit[16] = ‘1’, means bit[0] writeable.
   dango                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            1216 / 1446

                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     …

                     Bit[31] = ‘1’, means bit[15] writeable.

15    BSPIRXIF r     BSPI Transmit Interrupt flag.

                     This bit is set to “1” by hardware when the receive FIFO level is higher then the

                     value set by the user. When SMART is set in BSPI_MISC register, the bit will be

                     cleared as long as the receive FIFO level is lower than interrupt level, else you

                     should write “1” to this control bit to clear this flag.

                     Read 0= Not Occurred

                     Read 1= Occurred

                     Write 0= No effect

                     Write 1= Clear the flag

14    BSPIRXIEN rw   BSPI Receive Interrupt Enable.

                     If this bit is set to “1”, and BSPI interrupt (when 8-bit RX FIFO level in higher

                     then interrupt level) occurs, hardware will issue an IRQ3 or FIQ to CPU. If this

                     bit is cleared to “0”, this interrupt will be masked.

                     0: Disable

                     1: Enable

13:10 Rsvd     -     Reserved.

9     RXFULL   r     Receive FIFO full register.

                     This bit will be set by hardware when the receive FIFO is full. And will be clear

                     by hardware when the receive FIFO is not full.

                     0: Not Full

                     1: Full

8     RXFOV    r     Receive FIFO over run register.

                     This will be set when receive FIFO is full and more data is received on the BSPI

                     data bus. This bit will be cleared when write 1 to BSPIRXIF.

7     Rsvd     -     Reserved.

6:4   RXFLEV   rw    Receive FIFO interrupt level register.

                     This register is used to indicated how much bytes is stored in receive FIFO

                     when issued interrupt. The larger value is set, the lower interrupt penalty you

                     have. Since you can read more data in one interrupt.


                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1217 / 1446

                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                     FIFO Full Interrupt issue timing

                     000: Data no. in FIFO >= 1,  1 read is  allowed.

                     001: Data no. in FIFO >= 2,  2 read is  allowed.

                     010: Data no. in FIFO >= 3,  3 read is  allowed.

                     011: Data no. in FIFO >= 4,  4 read is  allowed.

                     100: Data no. in FIFO >= 5,  5 read is  allowed.

                     101: Data no. in FIFO >= 6 , 6 read is allowed.

                     110: Data no. in FIFO >= 7,  7 read is  allowed.

                     111: Data no. in FIFO >= 8 , 8 read is allowed.

3     Rsvd       -   Reserved.

2:0   RXFFLAG    r   Receive FIFO Data Level.

                     The register is used to indicate how much data is still in the FIFO.

                     000: No data in FIFO or 8 bytes in FIFO

                     001: 1 byte in FIFO.

                     010: 2 bytes in FIFO.

                     011: 3 bytes in FIFO.

                     100: 4 bytes in FIFO.

                     101: 5 bytes in FIFO.

                     110: 6 bytes in FIFO.

                     111: 7 bytes in FIFO.

Offset: 0x0010

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Bit    Symbol         Type    Description

31:8   Rsvd           -       Reserved.

7:0    BSPIRXDATA     r       Read data from BSPI Receive Buffer.

37.4.6 BSPI_MISC

Offset: 0x0014

Reset: 0x0000_0003

Table 37-7 BSPI_MISC Register Description

                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                    1218 / 1446

                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Bit   Symbol Type Description

31:10 Rsvd   -    Reserved.

9     OVER   rw   BSPI FIFO over write mode enable control.

                  This register is used to control the data will be overwrite or skipped when TX/RX

                  FIFO is full.

                  0: The further write to the full FIFO will be skipped.

                  1: The further write to the full FIFO will overwrite the last written data in the FIFO

8     SMART rw    BSPI FIFO smart mode control.

                  When this bit is set to “1”, programmer don’t need to clear the transmit/receive

                  interrupt flag when the FIFO status is reach, programmer only needs write-

                  to/read-from transmit/receive FIFO and let the FIFO level lower/higher than the

                  interrupt level, and the interrupt flag will clear automatically.

                  0: Normal interrupt clear mode

                  1: Smart interrupt clear mode

7:5   Rsvd   -    Reserved.

4     BSY    r    BSPI Controller busy flag.

                  This bit is used to indicate if the BSPI controller is busy or not.

                  0: Idle

                  1: Busy

3     RFF    r    Receive FIFO full flag.

                  This bit is used to indicate if the BSPI controller is real full or not. If the receive

                  FIFO is full, that means any data read from BSPI bus can’t be written into the

                  FIFO, and the RFOV bit will be set in this situation.

                  0: Receive FIFO not full

                  1: Receive FIFO full, not empty slot.

2     RNE    r    Receive FIFO Not Empty Flag.

                  This bit is used to indicated if there is any data currently in the receive FIFO.

                  0: Receive FIFO is empty

                  1: Receive FIFO is not empty

1     TNF    r    Transmit FIFO Not Full Flag.

                  This bit is used to indicated if there is any empty slots in the transmit FIFO.


                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1219 / 1446

              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

              0: Transmit FIFO is full, you can’t write any more data into it.

              1: Transmit FIFO is not full.

    0 TFE r   Transmit FIFO empty flag.

              This bit is used to indicated if the transmit FIFO is empty or not.

              0: Transmit FIFO is not empty.

              1: Transmit FIFO is empty.


37.5 Application Note

37.5.1 Master Mode

As in master mode, the shifting clock (BSPICLK) is generated by chip. There are two control bits to

control the clock phase and polarity. The transmission starts immediately from a register writes to the

BSPITXD control register. As long as there is data in the FIFO, the transmition will start automatically

when one byte is transferred.

The BSPI shifts the data from MSB to LSB through the SDO pin. The 8-bit data is shifted out after 8

SCK cycles. At the same time, the data is also shifted in through slave device SDI pin. When the

transmit FIFO level is lower then the interrupt trigger level, the BSPITXIF flag bit will be set; besides, a

BSPI interrupt will be generated if the BSPITXIRQEN bit is set. When the receive FIFO level is higher

then the interrupt trigger level, the BSPIRXIF flag bit will be set; besides, a BSPI interrupt will be

generated if the BSPIRXIRQEN bit is set. Programmer can read BSPI data from BSPIRXD control

register.

The following diagram depicts the timing scheme on BSPI master mode for different operation types

(polarity control bit equals “1” or “0”, phase control bit equals “1” or “0”, and sample strobe control bit

equals “1” or “0”).

37.5.2 Slave Mode

About BSPI Slave mode, BSPICLK and BSPICSN will be generated by external Host. The Slave mode of

BSPI supports CLK polarity and phase configuration. BSPI_TXDAT and BSPI_RXDAT registers can be

used in data transmission or receiving. The Slave mode of BSPI supports the biggest BSPICLK is

APBCLK/2. When it receiving CLK frequency is faster, we suggest using DMA to transmit data.


    i    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1222 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
37.5.3 Consecutive Bytes Transfer

Consecutive bytes transfer is available in master and slave modes. In transmission, software is able to

send the data consecutively as long as the TNF bit is 1. In reception, software will check for overrun

error to monitor if there is any missing data due to the polling rate is too low.










    i    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1223 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
38 Power Management Unit (PMU)

38.1 Overview


The PMU controller is used to control the chip to go to low power mode, such as sleep and deep sleep

mode. There are 32 IOs integrated inside the PMU controller which can wake-up the CM0 or Dual-CM33

from sleep or deep sleep mode.

The CM0 deep sleep mode will power off the digital section of CM0 and all peripherals. The CM33 deep

sleep mode will power off the digital section of CM33 and all peripherals. But the retention SRAM, TIMER,

RTC included in always on domain will still be power on and keep alive.


   38.2 Features


    • Deep sleep/sleep mode entry/exit control.

    • CM0 and CM33 can controlled independently.

    • Password protection to avoid entry deep sleep mode in unexpected event.

    • 32 IOs with wake-up and interrupt function.

    • Automatic clock gating during sleep and deep sleep mode.

    • Global reset control.

    • Two Crystal absent detector and interrupt.

    • Two Crystal absent issue recover.

    • Embedded 256 bytes retention SRAM

    • LPAURT 0/1 Control.

    • LPUART receiver buffer management.


    38.3 PMU Registers


    Table 38-1 PMU Registers Map

    Register Name     Offset    Type Reset Value   Description

    PMU_CM0DSLPEN     0x0000    rw   0x0000_0000   CM0 deep sleep enable register

    PMU_CM0DSLPPSWD   0x0004    rw   0x0000_0000   CM0 deep sleep password register

    PMU_CM0SLPEN      0x0008    rw   0x0000_0000   CM0 sleep enable register


    i                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1224 / 1446

    PMU_CM33DSLPEN          0x000C  rw   0x0000_0000   CM33 deep sleep enable register

PMU_CM33DSLPPSWD 0x0010 rw 0x0000_0000 CM33 deep sleep password register

    PMU_CM33SLPEN           0x0014  rw   0x0000_0000   CM33 sleep enable register

    PMU_CTRL                0x0018  rw   0x0000_0000   PMU control register

    PMU_STS                 0x001C  rw   0x0000_0000   PMU status register

    PMU_CM0PWRSTS           0x0020  rw   0x0000_0000   CM0 power status register

    PMU_CM33PWRSTS          0x0024  rw   0x0000_0000   CM33 power status register

    PMU_WKUINT              0x0028  rw   0x0000_0000   Wakeup int register

    PMU_WAITCYCLE           0x002c  rw   0x0002_2222   PMU_WAITCYCLE

    PMU_IO_WKU_EN           0x0030  rw   0x0000_0000   IO_WKU_EN Abstract

PMU_UART32K_0_CFG 0x0034 rw 0x0000_0000 UART32K_0_CFG Abstract

PMU_UART32K_1_CFG 0x0038 rw 0x0000_0000 UART32K_1_CFG Abstract

    PMU_RETRAM              0x003C  rw   0x0000_0000   Retention RAM configuration register.

    PMU_RETRAMSTS           0x0040  rw   0x0000_0000   Retention RAM status register

    38.3.1 PMU_CM0DSLPEN

    Offset: 0x0000

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 38-2 PMU_CM0DSLPEN Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31 WKU r Current wake-up signal, this bit reflects the CM0 wake-up status receive by PMU

                    controller, programmer must make sure this bit is 0 before entering deep sleep mode,

                    otherwise the system will wake up immediately because of non-clear wake up event

    30:1Rsvd    -   Reserved

0 DSLEEPr This bit indicates the CM0 deep sleep mode has been entry.

                    To enable the CM0 deep sleep mode, programmer should write correct password into

                    PMU_DSLPPSWD then write 0x55AA_AA55 to this register.

    38.3.2 PMU_CM0DSLPPSWD

    Offset: 0x0004

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 38-3 PMU_CM0DSLPPSWD Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

                                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            1225 / 1446

                                                                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    31:1Rsvd     -   Reserved

0 UNLOCKr This bit indicates the CM0 deep-sleep mode has unlocked and is ready to entry deep

                     sleep mode. To unlock the deep sleep mode, programmer should write 0xAA55_55AA

                     to this register. This bit will be cleared immediately after any register read or write to

                     any PMU register, including ICE read/write, so programmer should set correct

                     password to PMU_CM0DSLPEN immediately, otherwise the UNLOCK procedure should

                     start again.

    38.3.3 PMU_CM0SLPEN

    Offset: 0x0008

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 38-4 PMU_CM0SLPEN Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

31 WKU r Current wake up signal, this bit reflects the CM0 wake up status receive by PMU

                    controller, programmer must make sure this bit is 0 before entering sleep mode,

                    otherwise the system will wake up immediately because of non-clear wake up event.

    30:1Rsvd   -    Reserved

0 SLEEP r This bit indicates the CM0 sleep mode has unlocked and is ready to entry sleep mode.

                    To enable the CM0 sleep mode, programmer should write correct password

                    0x55AA_AA55 into this register.

    38.3.4 PMU_CM33DSLPEN

    Offset: 0x000C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 38-5 PMU_CM33DSLPEN Register Description

31 WKU r Current wake up signal, this bit reflects the CM33 wake up status receive by PMU

                    controller, programmer must make sure this bit is 0 before entering deep sleep mode,

                    otherwise the system will wake up immediately because of non-clear wake up event.

    30:1Rsvd   -    Reserved

0 DSLEEPr This bit indicates CM0 control the CM33 enter deep sleep mode.

                    To enable the CM33 deep sleep mode, programmer should write correct password

                    into PMU_DSLPPSWD then write 0x55AA_AA55 to this register.

    31:12Rsvd          -    Reserved

11 WDTRST_CM33_0rw This bit indicated if the last interrupt is cause by watch dog timer. When

10 WDTRST_CM33_1rw This bit indicated if the last interrupt is cause by watch dog timer. When

                            this bit is 1, it means the last reset is coming from watch dog timer.

                            When this bit is 1, it means the last reset is coming from external reset

38.3.14     PMU_UART32K_0_CFG

Offset: 0x0034

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 38-15 PMU_UART32K_0_CFG Register Description

Bit   Symbol      Type  Description

31    U32K0_EN    rw    UART 32K controller enable register. When this bit is set to 1, the UART

                        32K controller will start to receive data after two 32768 Hz clock cycles. So

                        use must set correct parameter and control register before set this register

                        to 1.

30    U32K0_INTSTS r_w1cUart32K0 interrupt status.

                        Write 1 to clear.
dango                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                  1236 / 1446

                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    29   Rsvd        -    Reserved

    28   U32K0_PES   r_w1cThis bit is set when the parity or stop bit is error. Write 1 to clear.

27 U32K0_WKUEN rw Enable the UART32K0 wakeup to PMU CTRL

26 U32K0_ACO rw Auto-calibration off control register. In order to correct receive UART data

                          under 32K Hz frequency, there is a hardware auto calibration mechanism

                          inside the controller. By default, this mode is turned on, if programmer can

                          set 1 to this bit to turn off this function.

    25:24U32K0_DBS   rw   De-bounce control register

                          0: No de-bounce

                          1: 2 32768Hz clocks de-bounce

                          2: 3 32768Hz clocks de-bounce

                          3: 4 32768Hz clocks de-bounce

                          This register is used when the UART bus is dirty, enable the de-bounce

                          function can re- duce the noise on the receive data. But a too large de-

                          bounce cycles may result in loss of receive data.

23 U32K0_MSB rw UART receive order control register.

                          0: LSB receive first.

                          1: MSB receive first.

    22   U32K0_PE    rw   UART parity enable register.

                          0: 1+8+1 mode

                          1: 1+8+1+1 mode, the parity bit is con- trolled by PMODE register.

21:20U32K0_PMOD rw Parity mode control register, this register is valid only when MODE is set

                          to 1. 0: Even parity

                          1: Odd parity

                          2: Always 0 at parity bit.

                          3: Always 1 at parity bit.

19 U32K0_WKU_PPrw Only received a good data with parity check pass, then the module will try

                          to wake up system.

    18:16U32K0_RXS   rw   Receive data select

                          0: From UART14 RX (IOB7)

                          1: From UART15 RX (IOD14)


                          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1237 / 1446

                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                            2: From UART16 RX (IOG2)

                            3: From UART17 RX (IOG8)

                            4: From UART18 RX (IOD2)

                            5: From UART19 RX (IOD4)

                            Others : Reserved

    15:0 U32K0_BPS    rw    Baud rate divider register, this register must be set before enable UART

                            32K engine.

                            PHASE = 65536*Baud-rate/32768.

                            For example, when baud-rate is 9600, fill 65536*9600/32768 = 19200 in

                            this register. The value in this register should not larger than 0x8000,

                            which means maximum baud rate is 16384.

    38.3.15     PMU_UART32K_1_CFG

    Offset: 0x0038

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 38-16 PMU_UART32K_1_CFG Register Description

    Bit   Symbol      Type  Description

    31    U32K1_EN    rw    UART 32K controller enable register. When this bit is set to 1, the UART

                            32K controller will start to receive data after two 32768 Hz clock cycles. So

                            use must set correct parameter and control register before set this register

                            to 1.

    30    U32K1_INTSTS r_w1cUart32K1 interrupt status.

                            Write 1 to clear.

    29    Rsvd        -     Reserved

    28    U32K1_PES   r_w1cThis bit is set when the parity or stop bit is error. Write 1 to clear.

27 U32K1_WKUEN rw Enable the UART32K1 wakeup to PMU CTRL

26 U32K1_ACO rw Auto-calibration off control register. In order to correct receive UART data

                            under 32K Hz frequency, there is a hardware auto calibration mechanism

                            inside the controller. By default, this mode is turned on, if programmer can

                            set 1 to this bit to turn off this function.

    25:24U32K1_DBS    rw    De-bounce control register

                            0: No de-bounce


                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1238 / 1446

                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                         1: 2 32768Hz clocks de-bounce

                         2: 3 32768Hz clocks de-bounce

                         3: 4 32768Hz clocks de-bounce

                         This register is used when the UART bus is dirty, enable the de-bounce

                         function can reduce the noise on the receive data. But a too large de-

                         bounce cycles may result in loss of receive data.

23 U32K1_MSB rw UART receive order control register.

                         0: LSB receive first.

                         1: MSB receive first.

    22   U32K1_PE   rw   UART parity enable register.

                         0: 1+8+1 mode

                         1: 1+8+1+1 mode, the parity bit is controlled by PMODE register.

21:20U32K1_PMOD rw Parity mode control register, this register is valid only when MODE is set

                         to 1.

                         0: Even parity

                         1: Odd parity

                         2: Always 0 at parity bit.

                         3: Always 1 at parity bit.

19 U32K1_WKU_PPrw Only received a good data with parity check pass, then the module will try

                         to wake up system.

    18:16U32K1_RXS  rw   Receive data select

                         0: From UART14 RX (IOB7)

                         1: From UART15 RX (IOD14)

                         2: From UART16 RX (IOG2)

                         3: From UART17 RX (IOG8)

                         4: From UART18 RX (IOD2)

                         5: From UART19 RX (IOD4)

                         Others : Reserved

    15:0 U32K1_BPS  rw   Baud rate divider register, this register must be set before enable UART

                         32K engine.

                         PHASE = 65536*Baud-rate/32768.


                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1239 / 1446

                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                            For example, when baud-rate is 9600, fill 65536*9600/32768 = 19200 in

                            this register. The value in this register should not larger than 0x8000,

                            which means maximum baud rate is 16384.

    38.3.16     PMU_RETRAM

    Offset: 0x003C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 38-17 PMU_RETRAM Register Description

    Bit   Symbol      TypeDescription

    31    MODESWT     rw   Retention RAM mode switch.

                           0: Normal RAM mode

                           1: UART32K buffer mode

    30:21Rsvd         -    Reserved

    20:16U32K1_WKUCNTrw    Receive data counts to generate wakeup source.

                           0: Receive 1 data

                           1: Receive 2 data

                           ...

                           32-1: Receive 32 data

    15:13Rsvd         -    Reserved

12:8 U32K0_WKUCNTrw Receive data counts to generate wakeup source.

                           0: Receive 1 data

                           1: Receive 2 data

                           …

                           32-1: Receive 32 data

    7:6   Rsvd        -    Reserved

    5:0   BUFADDR     rw   LPUART RX buffer base address configuration of uart32k0.

                           00: Start at address 0x0 in RET_RAM

                           01: Start at address 0x4 in RET_RAM

                           .........

                           The base address for uart32k1 is 32 Bytes bias to the base address of

                           uart32k0.

                           for example, when BUFADDR = 00, then the base address of uart32k0 is


                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1240 / 1446

                                V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

               0x0, and the base address of uart32k1 is 0x0+0x32=0x32.

    Offset: 0x0040

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

39 UART32K Controller (UART32K)

The UART32K Controller is used to receive frame data from external in low power mode. UART32k and

retram form a data transmission system in low power mode.


   39.1 Overview


The UART_32K controller is used to detect the active of UART14 ~ 19 bus and receive data via UART

protocol. There is two UART_32K controllers in V32F20x/ V32H20x, which can select input from

UART14~19 RX. UART_32K controller can operate at 32K RC or crystal frequency, so it can work under

sleep or deep sleep mode. The maximum baud rate UART32K can support is 9600. The UART32K

Controller can support 8 bit transfer without parity or 9bits transfer with even or odd parity. One data

buffer and one shift register are used for receive engine.


   39.2 Features


    • Operated under sleep or deep-sleep mode.

    • Wake-up capability under sleep or deep-sleep mode.

    • Programmable baud rate generator.

    • 8 bits or 9 bits with odd/even parity transfer.

    • Parity error detection.

    • Receive data can buffer to retention ram.

    • Programmable receive data counts to generate wakeup source.


    39.3 UART32K Registers


    Please refer to the PMU register.


    39.4 Application


When the UART32K is enabled, the correspond UARTx’s RX will be mux off into constant value ’1’. This is

disable that UARTx’s RX channel. When the cfg_retram_mode_switch de-assert, the UART32K will try to

finalize the last transfer of the UART, if the last transfer finished. The UART32K will set the uart_mux_off

zero to disable the mux off. That is the UARTx’s RX channel will receive RX data again. The relationship

i Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1242 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

is as shown as the figure below.

Not: x = 14 ~ 19.



PMU_REGS

40 Input Output WRAP(IO_WRAP)

40.1 Overview


The IO_WRAP is the io control logic, include GPIO module, IO_CTRL module, EXTI module.

The GPIO_A/B/C/D/E/F/G/H/I groups are controlled by GPIO controller which can keep their state at

deep sleep mode.

Up to 16 software event/interrupt requests and wakeup sources of deep sleep and sleep mode.

The GPIO_D/E groups are high speed GPIO, the fastest turning frequency is half of AHB0 frequency. The

output data register is in CRC model. Please refer CRC chapter.

The IOC13/IOC14/IOC15/IOI8 are in RTC power domain, can wake up RTCOnly mode. Please refer RTC

Wrapper chapter. They are controlled by RTC Wrapper module, not by GPIO module.

The IOC13/IOI8 can be used as TAMPER1 detecting. Please refer RTC Wrapper chapter.




    40.2     General-Purpose I/Os (GPIO)

    40.2.1 GPIO functional description

    Each of the general-purpose I/O ports has 32-bit configuration register (GPIOx MODER), two 32-bit

    data registers (GPIOx_IDR, GPIOx_ODR), a 32-bit set/reset register (GPIOx_BSRR), a 16-bit reset

    register (GPIOx_BRR) and a 32-bit locking register (GPIOx_LCKR).

    Subject to the specific hardware characteristics of each I/O port listed in the datasheet, each port bit of

    the General Purpose IO (GPIO) Ports, can be individually configured by software in several modes:

    x  Input floating

    x  Input pull-up

    x  Input-pull-down

    x  Analog

    x  Output open-drain

    x  Output push-pull

    x  Alternate function push-pull

    x  Alternate function open-drain



       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1244 / 1446

                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Each I/O port bit is freely programmable, however the I/O port registers have to be accessed as 32-bit

    words (half-word or byte accesses are not allowed). The purpose of the GPIO_BSRR and GPIO_BRR

    registers is to allow atomic read/modify accesses to any of the GPIO registers. This way, there is no

    risk that an IRQ occurs between the read and the modify access.

    40.2.2 Block Diagram

                                                                          — — —
        To on-chip      Analog                                                    |
        peripheral      Alternate function input     |                            |
                                  2                  |         on/off             I
              Read                84                     <<                       |
                                  3
                     2            3                  ||   trigger                 |           Protection
     Write           H            g
                     2   H²                              oot]                         /\ Protection
                         8₃                          |    )    Output             ivSSᶠᵒᵘⁿ |  diode
            Read/write   3                           ||       control  VssN-Mos   ||  Ves
     From on-chip                                    |                Push-pull   |
     peripherali        Alternate function output        pen-drain or



        Figure 40-1 Basic structure of an I/O port bit
40.2.3 Alternate functions (AF)

It is necessary to program the Port Bit Configuration register before using a default alternate function.

x For alternate function inputs, the port must be configured in Input mode (floating, pull up or pull-

down) and the input pin must be driven externally.

Note: It is also possible to emulate the AFI input pin by software by programming the GPIO controller.

In this case, the port should be configured in Alternate Function Output mode.

And obviously, the corresponding port should not be driven externally as it will be driven by the software

using the GPIO controller.

x For alternate function outputs, the port must be configured in Alternate Function Output mode (Push-

Pull or Open-Drain).

x For bidirectional Alternate Functions, the port bit must be configured in Alternate Function Output

mode (Push-Pull or Open-Drain). In this case the input driver is configured in input floating mode

If a port bit is configured as Alternate Function Output, this disconnects the output register

and connects the pin to the output signal of an on-chip peripheral.
        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1245 / 1446

                                                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

if software configures a GPIO pin as Alternate Function Output, but peripheral is not activated, its output

is not specified.

40.2.4 Input configuration

When the I/O Port is programmed as Input:

x  The Output Buffer is disabled

x  The Schmitt Trigger Input is activated

x  The weak pull-up and pull-down resistors are activated or not depending on input configuration (pull-

up, pull-down or floating)

xA read access to the Input Data register obtains the I/O State.

40.2.5 Output configuration

When the I/O Port is programmed as Output:

x The Output Buffer is enabled:

– Open Drain Mode: A “0” in the Output register activates the N-MOS while a “1” in the Output register

leaves the port in Hi-Z (the P-MOS is never activated)

– Push-Pull Mode: A “0” in the Output register activates the N-MOS while a “1” in the Output register

activates the P-MOS

x The Schmitt Trigger Input is activated.

x The weak pull-up and pull-down resistors are disabled.

x A read access to the Input Data register gets the I/O state in open drain mode

x A read access to the Output Data register gets the last written value in Push-Pull mode

40.2.6 Alternate function configuration

When the I/O Port is programmed as Alternate Function:

x  The Output Buffer is turned on in Open Drain or Push-Pull configuration

x  The Output Buffer is driven by the signal coming from the peripheral (alternate function out)

x  The Schmitt Trigger Input is activated

x  The weak pull-up and pull-down resistors are disabled.

x  The data present on the I/O pin is sampled into the Input Data register every APB2 clock cycle

x  A read access to the Input Data register gets the I/O state in open drain mode

x  A read access to the Output Data register gets the last written value in Push-Pull mode

40.2.7 Analog configuration

When the I/O Port is programmed as Analog configuration:
& "dango        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                          1246 / 1446

                                                                          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    x   The Output Buffer is disabled.

    x   The Schmitt Trigger Input is de-activated providing zero consumption for every analog value of the

    I/O pin. The output of the Schmitt Trigger is forced to a constant value (0).

    x   The weak pull-up and pull-down resistors are disabled.

    x   Read access to the Input Data register gets the value “0”.

    40.2.8.8     GPIO_LCKR

    Offset: 0x001C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 40-10 GPIO_LCKR Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

    31:17Rsvd  -   Reserved

16 LCKK rw This bit can be read any time. It can only be modified using the lock key write

                   sequence.

                   0: Port configuration lock key not active


                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1262 / 1446

                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                1: Port configuration lock key active. The GPIOx_LCKR register is locked until an

                MCU reset or a peripheral reset occurs.

                LOCK key write sequence:

                WR LCKR[16] = 1 + LCKR[15:0] WR LCKR[16] = 0 + LCKR[15:0] WR LCKR[16] = 1

                + LCKR[15:0] RD LCKR

                RD LCKR[16] = 1 (this read operation is optional but it confirms that the lock is

                active)

                Note: During the LOCK key write sequence, the value of LCK[15:0] must not

                change.

                Any error in the lock sequence aborts the lock.

                After the first lock sequence on any bit of the port, any read access on the LCKK bit

                returns 1 until the next CPU reset.

15   LCK15 rw   Px15 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

14   LCK14 rw   Px14 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

13   LCK13 rw   Px13 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

12   LCK12 rw   Px12 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

11   LCK11 rw   Px11 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

10   LCK10 rw   Px10 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

9    LCK9 rw    Px9 configure lock signal


                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1263 / 1446

                                    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

8    LCK8  rw   Px8 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

7    LCK7  rw   Px7 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

6    LCK6  rw   Px6 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

5    LCK5  rw   Px5 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

4    LCK4  rw   Px4 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

3    LCK3  rw   Px3 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

2    LCK2  rw   Px2 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

1    LCK1  rw   Px1 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

0    LCK0  rw   Px0 configure lock signal

                1: Lock

                0: Un-lock

40.3 IO Control Registers


 Table 40-14 IOCTRL Registers Map

   40.4 External interrupt/ event controller (EXTI)


The external interrupt/event controller consists of up to 16 edge detectors. Each input line can be

independently configured to select the type (event or interrupt) and the corresponding trigger event

(rising or falling or both). Each line can also masked independently. A pending register maintains the

status line of the interrupt requests.

40.4.1 Main features

The EXTI controller main features are the following:

x Independent trigger and mask on each interrupt/event line
& "dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1346 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

x Dedicated status bit for each interrupt line

x Generation of up to 16 software event/interrupt requests

40.4.2 Wakeup event management
40.4.3 Functional description

To generate the interrupt, the interrupt line should be configured and enabled. This is done by

programming the two trigger registers with the desired edge detection and by enabling the interrupt

request by writing a ‘1’ to the corresponding bit in the interrupt mask register.

When the selected edge occurs on the external interrupt line, an interrupt request is generated. The

pending bit corresponding to the interrupt line is also set. This request is reset by writing a ‘1’ in the

pending register.

To generate the event, the event line should be configured and enabled. This is done by programming

the two trigger registers with the desired edge detection and by enabling the event request by writing

a ‘1’ to the corresponding bit in the event mask register. When the selected edge occurs on the event

line, an event pulse is generated. The pending bit corresponding to the event line is not set An

interrupt/ event request can also be generated by software by writing a ‘1’ in the software interrupt/

event register.

Hardware interrupt selection

To configure the 16 lines as interrupt sources, use the following procedure:

x Configure the mask bits of the 16 Interrupt lines (EXTI_IMR)

x Configure the Trigger Selection bits of the Interrupt lines (EXTI_MODE and EXTI_POLA)

x Configure the enable and mask bits that control the NVIC IRQ channel mapped to the External

Interrupt Controller (EXTI) so that an interrupt coming from one of the 16 lines can be correctly

acknowledged.

Hardware event selection

To configure the 16 lines as event sources, use the following procedure:

x Configure the mask bits of the 16 Event lines (EXTI_EMR)

x Configure the Trigger Selection bits of the Event lines (EXTI_MODE and EXTI_POLA)

Software interrupt/event selection

The 16 lines can be configured as software interrupt/event lines. The following is the procedure to

generate a software interrupt.

x Configure the mask bits of the 16 Interrupt/Event lines (EXTI_IMR, EXTI_EMR)

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1347 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

x Set the required bit of the software interrupt register (EXTI_SWIER)

15    SWIER15rw   Software Interrupt on line15

                  Writing a 1 to this bit when it is at 0 sets the corresponding pending bit in

41 Compare Module (COMP)

41.1 Features

• Can Compare voltages of VIP and VIN at the same time.

• Can configure speed and power of the comparator.

• Can wake up MCU in deep-sleep and sleep mode.

• Can generate interrupt.

• Can configure the filter of comparator.

• Can have hysteresis voltage of 25mV.

41.2 Block Diagram

         HYST                            FILTER_SAMPLEMODE FILTER BYPASS
     +     LOWPWR                          FILTER_CLKDIV
        cmp        Filtering                 CMP_OUT
     -      EN

                                         CMP_INT_CFG    Pulse Gen   INT_OUT
                                         CMP_WKU_CFG                WKU_OUT



     Figure 41-1 COMP Block Diagram


41.3 General description


The analog comparator can compare voltage levels on external pins.


41.4 Comparator modes


The analog comparator supports both Standard and Low Power mode. Comparator mode can be selected

i    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                              1370 / 1446

 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

 by COMP_SPDPWR configure.


41.5 Comparator outputs


 The comparator output can be routed to an external pin. The comparator can be used with the bus clock

 disabled, to save power if the control registers are not required to be written.


 41.6 Comparator output modes


 The comparator supports 3 types of output modes.

 • Raw output mode

 • Filtered output mode

 • Long pulse mode

 41.6.1 Raw output mode

 In raw output mode the output to the I/O will be directly from the comparator.

 Raw output mode can be selected from the control register by configuring its bits 17:16 to Bypass mode.

 41.6.2 Filtered Output mode

 In Filtered output mode unwanted glitches can be filtered out by selecting the filter clock and the

 sample mode.

 COMP_CR.CLKDIV will select the timed period of the filter clock and COMP_CR.SAMPLEMODE will select

 how much shorter outputs will be filtered as compared to the filter clock.

 41.6.3 Long pulse mode

 Long pulse mode can be selected by configuring the register long_pulse.Long pulse mode can be used

 with both raw output mode and filtered output mode. The parameter long_pulse_time will select the time

 period of the pulse. If the comparator output is shorter than the configured long_pulse_time, output on

 the I/O will be stretched to the configured long_pulse_time. If the output is longer than the configured

 long_pulse_time the comparator output or the raw output will be passed through.



 i    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1371 / 1446

 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
41.7 COMP Registers


 Table 41-1 COMP Registers Map

 Register Name        Offset          Type  Reset Value   Description

 COMP_CR              0x0000          rw    0x0000_0000   Control Register

 COMP_EN              0x0004          rw    0x0000_0000   Enable Register

 COMP_LONG_PULSE      0x0008          rw    0x0000_0000   Long pulse mode register

 COMP_INT_CTRL        0x000C          rw    0x0000_0000   Interrupt Control

 COMP_WKU_CTRL        0x0014          rw    0x0000_0000   Wake up control

 COMP_STATUS          0x0018          rw    0x0000_0000   Status Register

 41.7.1 COMP_CR

 Offset: 0x0000

 Reset: 0x0000_0000

 Table 41-2 COMP_CR Register Description

 Bit Symbol        TypeDescription

 31:21Rsvd         -          Reserved

 20:18CLKDIV       rw         Filter clock divider. Filter clock equals the Analog Comparator clock

                              divided.

                              0: Filter clock period duration equals 1 Analog Comparator clock

                              period.

                              1: Filter clock period duration equals 2 Analog Comparator clock

                              period.

                              2: Filter clock period duration equals 4 Analog Comparator clock

                              period.

                              3: Filter clock period duration equals 8 Analog Comparator clock

                              period.

                              4: Filter clock period duration equals 16 Analog Comparator clock

                              period.

                              5: Filter clock period duration equals 32 Analog Comparator clock

                              period.

                              6: Filter clock period duration equals 64 Analog Comparator clock

                              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1372 / 1446

                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                         period

                         7: Filter clock period duration equals 128 Analog Comparator clock

                         period

17:16SAMPLEMODE     rw   Control the filtering of the Analog Comparator output.

                         0: Bypass mode. Filtering is disabled. The raw Analog Comparator

                         output will be passed-through.

                         1: Filter 1 clock period. Any pulse duration shorter than one cycle of

                         the designated filter clock (see FILTERCGF_CLKDIV) will be filtered

                         out. Pulse widths up to two cycles long may be filtered.

                         2: Filter 2 clock period. Any pulse duration shorter than two cycles of

                         the designated filter clock will be filtered-out. Pulse widths up to three

                         cycles long may be filtered.

                         3: Filter 3 clock period. Any pulse duration shorter than three cycles of

                         the designated filter clock will be filtered-out. Pulse widths up to four

                         cycles long may be filtered.

15:4  Rsvd          -    Reserved

3:2   SPDPWR        rw   00: Low speed, Ultra low power, Power consumption 2.5ua

                         01: Low speed, Low power, Power consumption 3ua

                         10: Medium speed, Medium power, Power consumption 20ua

                         11: High speed, High power, Power consumption 100ua

1     HYST          rw   0: No Hysteresis Voltage

                         1: 25mV Hysteresis Voltage

0     Rsvd          -    Reserved

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Bit  Symbol       Type Description

                        1: When 1

                        if VIP is greater than VIN, out = 1 if VIP is less than VIN, out = 0

                        0: When 0

                        if VIP is greater than VIN, out = 0 if VIP is less than VIN, out = 1
