41.7.4 COMP_INT_CTRL

Offset: 0x000C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 41-5 COMP_INT_CTRL Register Description

Bit   Symbol          TypeDescription

31:6Rsvd              - Reserved



i                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1374 / 1446

                                                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

5     INTSOURCE   rw   Select which Analog comparator output (filtered or unfiltered) is used for

                       interrupt detection.

                       0: Select Analog Comparator filtered output as input for interrupt

                       detection.

                       1: Select Analog Comparator raw output (unfiltered) as input for

                       interrupt detection. Must be used when Analog comparator is used as

                       wake up source in Power down mode.

4:2   INTTYPE     rw   Comparator interrupt type selector.

                       0: The analog comparator interrupt edge sensitive is disabled.

                       2: Analog comparator interrupt is rising edge sensitive.

                       4: Analog comparator interrupt is falling edge sensitive.

                       6: Analog comparator interrupt is rising and falling edge sensitive.

                       1: The analog comparator interrupt level sensitive is disabled.

                       3: Analog comparator interrupt is high level sensitive.

                       5: Analog comparator interrupt is low level sensitive.

                       7: The analog comparator interrupt level sensitive is disabled.

1     INTCLR      rw   COMP interrupt clear.

                       0: No effect

                       1: Clear Interrupt

0     INTEN       rw   COMP interrupt enable.

                       1: Interrupt enable

                       0: Interrupt disable

41.7.5 COMP_WKU_CTRL

Offset: 0x0014

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 41-6 COMP_WKU_CTRL Register Description

Bit   Symbol       TypeDescription

31:3Rsvd           -    Reserved

4:2   WKUCTRL      rw   Comparator wake up type selector.

                        0: The analog comparator wake up edge sensitive is disabled.

                        2: Analog comparator wake up is rising edge sensitive.

                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                             1375 / 1446

                                                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                       4: Analog comparator wake up is falling edge sensitive.

                       6: Analog comparator wake up is rising and falling edge sensitive.

                       1: The analog comparator wake up level sensitive is disabled.

                       3: Analog comparator wake up is high level sensitive.

                       5: Analog comparator wake up is low level sensitive.

                       7: The analog comparator wake up level sensitive is disabled.

1     WKUSOURCE   rw   Select which Analog comparator output (filtered our unfiltered) is used

                       for wake up detection.

                       0: Select Analog Comparator filtered output as input for wake up

                       detection.

                       1: Select Analog Comparator raw output (unfiltered) as input for wake

                       up detection. Must be used when Analog comparator is used as wake

                       up source in Power down mode.

0     WKUEN       rw   1: Wake Up enable

                       0: Wake Up disable

Offset: 0x0018

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 41-7 COMP_STATUS Register Description

Bit    Symbol                    Type        Description

31:3   Rsvd                      -           Reserved

2      VAL                       r           Comparator Output

                                             1: Compare result is 1

                                             0: Compare result is 0

1      INTSTS                    rw          Interrupt Status

                                             1: Interrupt event occur

                                             0: No interrupt

                                             Write 1 to Clear

0      WKUSTS                    rw          Wake-Up Status

                                             1: Wake-Up event occur

                                             0: No Wake-Up

42 Software Define VGIO

42.1 Introduction


The VGIO is a Vango properity special function module. It is use to remap the IO through the

reconfiguration of registers. In V32F20x/ V32H20x, the VGIO is 32-32. That means in every 32 pins

group, any IO could reassign to any IO in this group. The configuration to the register should be aided

with a program to auto generated.


   42.2 VGIO Registers


The register in VGIO is used to configure the routing of IO. The relationship of connection and

    configuration is shown as below.


        Le]                                              eee
        RE                                                 a

                                                         wee

     BIE                                                wees
        EI                                           EEE see
        =] 0 GIG Ld                                      ese
        EEE                                             sees
    EE EB

        Figure 42-1 VGIO Architecture

    There are four sides in VGIO, and each side has its own register group including 32 registers for

    configuration:

43 RTC Wrapper(RTC_WRAP)

43.1 Overview


    The RTC Wrapper is the subwrapper module dedicated for real-time clock. Its power consumption is

    extremely low, so that battery can be used when main power is removed. It uses the external 32.768kHz

    XOL crystal oscillator as the main working clock to generate the 1Hz clock, and it provides calibration

    under different temperature every 30 seconds. In addition, It supplies uncovering detection to record

    tamper events and wakeup function in RTC only mode. The RTC wrapper block diagram is shown as

    follows:

                   RTC Wrapper

    APB     REGS   fer) RTC                  Clock
                          TIME               Source
                                             Select

                                    RTC            32768Hz XOL     RTC_SEC
        Uncover                     Clock                          RTC_MIN

    Detection      (=)    Second                       RTC_TIME
                           Pulse                   32768Hz RCL

     RTC COMP                       RTC
      Wakeup                        Clock
    RTC Timer      (of                                                 RTC ALM
      Wakeup              RTC Auto Calibration     RTC_PLLDIVAC
      RTC IO                   i
      Wakeup

     RTC Time              Temperature Measurement
      Wakeup
                           ADC
     RTC PMU       2       CTRL              ADC    ADC
                                                   Clock
                                                   6.5MHz
                                             Temperature
                                                Sensor





                       Figure 43-1 RTC Wrapper Block Diagram

    The function of main submodules in RTC wrapper is below:

    • RTC TIME

    - Supply calendar time and leap year detection

    • Second Pulse

     - Generate second pulse with auto-calibration
                           Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.       1380 / 1446

          V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    • ADC_CTRL

     - ADC controller which controls analog ADC

    • ADC

     - Measure temperature

    • RTC Auto-Calibration

      - Provide calibration under different temperature every 30 seconds

      - Generate PLL divider written into RTC_PLLDIVAC register, and RTC_PLLDIVAC value should be

         written to RTCALM_PLLDIV register by software for high-precision second pulse generation

    • REGS

     - RTC wrapper registers with APB interface

    • Uncover detection

     - Detect tamper events and record them

    • RTC only wakeup

     - Support RTC comparator wakeup

     - Support RTC timer wakeup

     - Support RTC IO wakeup

     - Support RTC time wakeup

    • RTC PMU

     - Control power state of RTC wrapper

     - Monitor the voltage of main power supply

     - Manage RTC only mode


    43.2 Features


• Support power supplied by main power when main power is ready.

• Support auto power supply switch of main power and battery supply.

• Support auto power isolation when main power is invalid.

• Contain RC32K clock as a backup RTC clock resource when 32.768 kHz XOL crystal oscillator is invalid.

• Calendar with subsecond, seconds, minutes, hours, days, week, month and year, in BCD (binary-coded

decimal) format.

• Wakeup function in RTC only mode, the wakeup source can be RTC time, RTC timer, RTC IO and RTC

comparator.

§ dango Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1381 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    • Support auto calibration of 1Hz pulse generation with temperature monitoring.

    • Support Uncovering detection to record tamper events.

    • Contain a ADC for temperature measurement.

    • Contain a 2-Channel single ended comparator.

    • Contain two groups of registers, RTC registers group and RTC analog registers group.


43.3 Functional description

43.3.1 register access protection

For the sake of access safety, some RTC registers are protected from reading or writing.

Calendar time/wakeup time/ auto-calibaraion registers write protection

RTC calendar time registers, wakeup time registers and auto-calibration registers are protected from

writing.

• Software can do following steps to disable write protection:

    - Write 0x5AA55AA5 to RTC_PWD register to enable the RTC_CE port’s access

    - Write 0xA55AA55B to RTC_CE register to disable write protection

    - Poll BSY bit in RTC_CE register to wait for completion of writing RTC_CE register

• Software can do following steps to enable write protection:

    - Write 0x5AA55AA5 to RTC_PWD register to enable the RTC_CE port’s access

    - Write 0xA55AA55A to RTC_CE register to enable write protection

    - Poll BSY bit in RTC_CE register to wait for completion of writing RTC_CE register

Calendar time registers read protection

RTC calendar time registers are protected from reading. Software can do following steps to disable read

protection:

• Read RTC_LOAD register

• Poll BSY bit in RTC_CE register to wait for completion of reading RTC_LOAD register

RTCANA_SWRST register write protection

RTCANA_SWRSTPSWD register provides write protection for RTCANA_SWRST. Writing 0xBEEF_BEEF to

RTCANA_SWRSTPSWD register can disable the write protection.

RTCANA_RTCCTRL register read/write protection

RTCANA_RTCCTRLPSWD register controls access permission of RTCANA_RTCCTRL register. Writing

0xAA55_55AA to RTCANA_RTCCTRLPSWD register can disable the read/write protection. Any read or

        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1382 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

write access to RTCANA_RTCCTRL register will enable read/write protection.

43.3.2 Calendar time

The RTC calendar time can be set and read through calendar time registers. Below is programming

sequences for setting calendar time and reading calendar time.

Set calendar time

• Poll BSY bit in RTC_CE register to wait for completion of the last operation.

• Write 0x5AA55AA5 to RTC_PWD register to enable the RTC_CE access

• Write 0xA55AA55B to RTC_CE register to disable write protection of calendar time registers

• Configure RTC calendar time registers

• Write 0x5AA55AA5 to RTC_PWD register to enable the RTC_CE access

• Write 0xA55AA55A to RTC_CE register to enable write protection of calendar time registers

• Poll BSY bit in RTC_CE register to wait for completion of writing RTC_CE register.

Read calendar time

• Poll BSY bit in RTC_CE register to wait for completion of the last operation.

• Read RTC_LOAD register to trigger loading calendar time to calendar time registers.

• Poll BSY bit in RTC_CE register to wait for completion of reading RTC_LOAD register

• Read calendar time registers

43.3.3 RTC second pulse output

Internal RTC second pulse can be output to PC14 GPIO pin for frequency precision check. If you need a

high-precision reference second pulse, please refer to clause 44.3.3.










    § dango  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1383 / 1446

V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual




RTC


                              30th second
29 seconds                    calibaration

Second Pulse    PC14    Mr ...

    output second pulse










    Figure 43-2 RTC second pulse output

Programming flow for outputting RTC second pulse is below:

• Set RTCANA_GPIOMODER.MODER1 to alternate function mode

• Set RTCANA_GPIOOSPEEDR.DR1

• Set RTCANA_GPIOOTYPER.OT1 to output push-pull type

• Set RTCANA_GPIOPUPDR.PUPDR1 to no pull-up and pull-down

• Set RTCANA_GPIOAFRH.AFRH1 to AF0

You can set RTCANA_GPIOMODER.MODER1 to analog mode to disable output. If you enable auto-

calibration, you can see second pulse width changes every 30 seconds.

43.3.4 Auto-calibration

Auto-calibration is also known as automatic temperature compensation, the programming flow chart is

below:

Auto-calibration related parameters are stored in FLASH INFO0 region, user needs to read FLASH INFO0

                             RTC_ACP2 register.

                             and the low 16 bits is abandoned.

                             a=PCLK/6553600.

    43.3.5 Uncovering detection

    The uncovering detection is used to detect and record tamper events. The module detects tamper

events from PI8 and PC13 GPIO pins, and has built-in 2 FIFOs with depth of 10 entries to record the

    time and type of tamper events. Software can read the FIFO through the APB registers to obtain the

    tamper event information.


                                              FIFO1
                                     IO1 event type  |
                                     current time
    PI8                                              >
                                                     1

                                              IO1 tamper record
                                                    [|
                      Detection Logic


                                       IO0 event type
                                       current time
        PC13
                                                            [I
                                                            1 IO0 tamper record
                                                            I
                                                            I
                                                            FIFO0

                      Figure 43-4 uncovering detection principle
    Programming sequence is as follows:

    • Set RTCANA_GPIOMODER.MODER0 to alternate function mode

    • Set RTCANA_GPIOMODER.MODER3 to alternate function mode

    • Set RTCANA_GPIOOSPEEDR.DR0

    i                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.         1388 / 1446

                                                                 V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• Set RTCANA_GPIOOSPEEDR.DR3

• Set RTCANA_GPIOOTYPER.OT0 to output push-pull type

• Set RTCANA_GPIOOTYPER.OT3 to output push-pull type

• Set RTCANA_GPIOPUPDR.PUPDR0 to no pull-up and pull-down

• Set RTCANA_GPIOPUPDR.PUPDR3 to no pull-up and pull-down

• Set RTCANA_GPIOAFRH.AFRH0 to AF0

• Set RTCANA_GPIOAFRH.AFRH3 to AF0

• Set RTC_IODECMODE

• Poll RTC_FIFOSTS, once the FIFO0 or FIFO1 is not empty, software can read RTC_DECDATAIO0 or

RTC_DECDATAIO1 register

43.3.6 Wakeup in RTC only mode

RTC only mode can be entered in active and passive way, and CPU will execute from boot ROM when

system is waked up from RTC only mode. The passive RTC only mode is entered when main power is

removed and exited when main power is supplied. There are several kinds of wakeup sources for active

RTC only mode, consists of RTC time wakeup, IO wakeup, timer wakeup, and comparator wakeup.

Programming procedure for each wakeup is given below:

RTC time wakeup

• Read current calendar time(refer to clause 43.3.2)

• Set wakeup time, for example, several seconds delayed from current calendar time

• Write wakeup time to RTC only wakeup time registers

• Unmask second time by setting RTC_WKUCTRL

• Set RTC_WKUCTRL.WKU_ENABLE to 1

• Write 0xAA55_55AA to RTCANA_RTCCTRLPSWD to disable read/write protection

• Set RTCANA_RTCCTRL.RTCONLY_EN to 1 to enable RTC only mode

• Put CM0 and CM33 cores into deep sleep mode

When wakeup time is reached, system will exit RTC only mode and boot from ROM.

IO wakeup

PI8, PC13, PC14 and PC15 can be used for RTC only wakeup IO sources, and we take PI8 for example.

• Set RTCANA_CLKEN.RTCIO_CLKEN to 1 to enable RTC timer clock

• Set RTCANA_GPIOMODER.MODER3 to input mode

• Set RTCANA_GPIOOSPEEDR.DR3 to proper speed

• Set RTCANA_GPIOOTYPER.OT3 to output open-drain
& "dango        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                     1389 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• Set RTCANA_GPIOPUPDR.PUPDR3 to no pull-up and pull-down

• Reset RTCANA_EXTIEMRIMR, RTCANA_EXTIMODE and RTCANA_EXTIPOLA registers

• Write 0xBEEF_BEEF to RTCANA_SWRSTPSWD register to disable write protection

• Set RTCANA_SWRST.IO to 1 to software reset RTC IO

• Set RTCANA_EXTIMODE.MODE3 to 3(1, 2 can also be selected)

• Set RTCANA_EXTIPOLA.POLA3

• Set RTCANA_EXTIEMRIMR.EMR3 to 1

• Write 0xAA55_55AA to RTCANA_RTCCTRLPSWD to disable read/write protection

• Set RTCANA_RTCCTRL.RTCONLY_EN to 1 to enable RTC only mode

• Put CM0 and CM33 cores into deep sleep mode

Input a pulse to PI8 , system will exit RTC only mode and boot from ROM.

Timer wakeup

• Set RTCANA_CLKEN.RTCTIMER_CLKEN to 1 to enable RTC timer clock

• Reset RTCANA_TMRCFG register

• Write 0xBEEF_BEEF to RTCANA_SWRSTPSWD register to disable write protection

• Set RTCANA_SWRST.TIMER to 1 to software reset RTC timer

• Set RTCANA_TMRCFG .RTCTIMER_EN to 1 to enable RTC timer

• Configure RTCANA_TMRCNT register

• Set RTCANA_TMRCFG .RTCTIMER_WKUEN to 1 to enable RTC timer wakeup function

• Write 0xAA55_55AA to RTCANA_RTCCTRLPSWD to disable read/write protection

• Set RTCANA_RTCCTRL.RTCONLY_EN to 1 to enable RTC only mode

• Put CM0 and CM33 cores into deep sleep mode

When the timer counts down to 0, system will exit RTC only mode and boot from ROM.

Comparator wakeup

PI8 and PC14 are used as RTC comparator inputs, and we take PI8 for example.

• Set RTCANA_CLKEN.RTCCMP_CLKEN to 1 to enable RTC comparator clock

• Set RTCANA_GPIOMODER.MODER3 to analog mode

• Set RTCANA_GPIOOSPEEDR.DR3 to proper speed

• Set RTCANA_GPIOOTYPER.OT3 to output push-pull

• Set RTCANA_GPIOPUPDR.PUPDR3 to no pull-up and pull-down

• Write 0xBEEF_BEEF to RTCANA_SWRSTPSWD register to disable write protection

• Set RTCANA_SWRST.COMP to 1 to software reset RTC comparator
§ Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1390 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• Set RTCANA_CMPCFG.LCMPA1_WKU_MODE to 1(it depends)

• Set RTCANA_CMPCFG.LCMPA_FIL_CLKDIV and RTCANA_CMPCFG.LCMPA_FIL_SAMPMODE

• Set RTCANA_CMPCFG. LCMPA1_WKU_EN to 1 to enable RTC comparator wakeup function

• Write 0xAA55_55AA to RTCANA_RTCCTRLPSWD to disable read/write protection

• Set RTCANA_RTCCTRL.RTCONLY_EN to 1 to enable RTC only mode

• Put CM0 and CM33 cores into deep sleep mode

When connect external high voltage(>0.7 v) to PI8 , system will exit RTC only mode and boot from

ROM.


   43.4 RTC interrupts


    RTC interrupts are given below:

    • Calibration done interrupt

    • Illegal write to CE register interrupt

    • Illegal time checking interrupt

    • PI8 interrupt

    • PC15 interrupt

    • PC14 interrupt

    • PC13 interrupt

    • RTC CMP1 interrupt

    • RTC CMP2 interrupt

    • RTC CMP1 wakeup interrupt

    • RTC CMP2 wakeup interrupt

    The following are the registers related to RTC Interrupts:

    • RTC_INTSTS

    • RTC_INTEN

    • RTCANA_EXTIEMRIMR

    • RTCANA_EXTIMODE

    • RTCANA_EXTICFG

    • RTCANA_CMPWKUINT





    § dango  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1391 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
   43.5 RTC Registers


    Table 43-2 RTC Registers Map

    Register Name   Offset TypeReset Value   Description

    RTC_SEC         0x0000 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC calendar time second register.

    RTC_MIN         0x0004 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC calendar time minute register.

    RTC_HOUR        0x0008 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC calendar time hour register.

    RTC_DAY         0x000C rw   0x0000_0000  RTC calendar time day register.

    RTC_WEEK        0x0010 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC calendar time week register.

    RTC_MON         0x0014 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC calendar time month register.

    RTC_YEAR        0x0018 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC calendar time year register.

    RTC_TIME        0x001C rw   0x0000_0000  RTC accurate subsecond time register.

    RTC_PLLDIVAC    0x0020 r    0x0000_0000  RTC PLL divider value after auto calibration register.

    RTC_CAL         0x0030 rw   0x0000_0001  RTC calibration register.

    RTC_CTRL        0x0038 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC control register.

    RTC_PWD         0x0044 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC_CE access protection register.

    RTC_CE          0x0048 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC time registers access protection register.

    RTC_LOAD        0x004C rw   0x0000_0000  RTC calendar time load register.

    RTC_INTSTS      0x0050 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC interrupt status register.

    RTC_INTEN       0x0054 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC interrupt enable register.

    RTC_WKUSEC      0x0058 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC only wakeup time second register .

    RTC_WKUMIN      0x005C rw   0x0000_0000  RTC only wakeup time minute register.

RTC_WKUHOUR 0x0060 rw 0x0000_0000 RTC only wakeup time hour register.

    RTC_WKUDAY      0x0064 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC only wakeup time day register.

RTC_WKUMONTH 0x0068 rw 0x0000_0000 RTC only wakeup time month register.

    RTC_WKUYEAR     0x006C rw   0x0000_0000  RTC only wakeup time year register.

RTC_WKU_CTRL 0x0070 rw 0x0000_0000 RTC only wakeup control register.

    RTC_ACCTRL      0x0080 rw   0x0000_0000  RTC calibration control register.

    RTC_ACTI        0x0084 rw   0x0000_1800  Auto-calibration center temperature control register.

    RTC_ACF200      0x0088 rw   0x0064_0000  Auto-calibration F200 control register.

    RTC_ACADCW      0x008C rw   0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration manual ADC value control register.

                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1392 / 1446

                                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    RTC_ACP0          0x0090 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration P0 register.

    RTC_ACP1          0x0094 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration P1 register.

    RTC_ACP2          0x0098 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration P2 register.

    RTC_ACP3          0x009C rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration P3 register.

    RTC_ACP4          0x00A0 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration P4 register.

    RTC_ACP5          0x00A4 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration P5 register.

    RTC_ACP6          0x00A8 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration P6 register.

    RTC_ACP7          0x00ACrw      0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration P7 register.

    RTC_ACK1          0x00B0 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration K1 register.

    RTC_ACK2          0x00B4 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration K2 register.

    RTC_ACK3          0x00B8 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration K3 register.

    RTC_ACK4          0x00BCrw      0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration K4 register.

    RTC_ACK5          0x00C0 rw     0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration K5 register.

    RTC_ACTEMP        0x00C4 r      0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration calculated temperature register.

    RTC_ACPPM         0x00C8 r      0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration calculated PPM register.

    RTC_ACKTEMP       0x00D0rw      0x0000_0000  Auto-calibration temperature section control register.

RTC_IODECMODE 0x00E8 rw 0x0000_0000 RTC uncovering detection mode register.

RTC_DECDATAIO0 0x00EC r 0x0000_0000 RTC uncovering detection FIFO 0 register.

RTC_DECDATAIO1 0x00F0 r 0x0000_0000 RTC uncovering detection FIFO 1 register.

    43.5.9 RTC_PLLDIVAC

    Offset: 0x0020

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-11 RTC_PLLDIVAC Register Description


    Bit Symbol    TypeDescription

    31:26Rsvd     -   Reserved

25:0 PLLDIVACr RTC PLL divider value after auto calibration compensation.

                      Note: User should read this register and write the value to PLLDIV field in

                      RTCALM_PLLDIV register to update the value of pll divider.

    43.5.10       RTC_CAL

    Offset: 0x0030

    Reset: 0x0000_0001

    Table 43-12 RTC_CAL Register Description


                                 Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1395 / 1446

                           V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:14Rsvd      -   Reserved

13:0 CAL rw RTC 32768 calibration, the RTC engine will do calibration for every 30 seconds, the

                       internal counter will count 32768 times during 1~29 second. At the 30 second, it will

                       count [32768- (CAL-1)] for a second, so it can let the average 1 second pulse in 30

                       seconds become an accurate 1 second pulse. The PPM resolution of the CAL field is

                       1.02ppm, and the adjustable range is 8332.3ppm. (12 minutes/day). When the auto-

                       calibration function is enabled, this field will be updated automatically by the auto-

                       calibration engine.

    43.5.11              RTC_CTRL

    Offset: 0x0038

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-13 RTC_CTRL Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:2Rsvd    -     Reserved

    1     TIMEENrw    RTC sub-second counter enable

                      1: Enable.

                      0: Disable.

    0     Rsvd  -     Reserved

    43.5.12               RTC_PWD

    Offset: 0x0044

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-14 RTC_PWD Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:1Rsvd    -     Reserved

0 PWDEN rw This field is used to protect the RTC_CE port access.

                      Before access the RTC_CE, programmer should write 0x5AA55AA5 to this port, and the

                      PWDEN will be set to 1. This bit will be cleared automatically after any write to RTC_CE

                      port. Which means programmer should write 0x5AA55AA5 to this port again before

                      next access to RTC_CE port.



                       Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1396 / 1446

                                                                                   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    43.5.13        RTC_CE

    Offset: 0x0048

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-15 RTC_CE Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:2Rsvd   -      Reserved

1 BSY r This flag indicates the RTC update procedure or RTC read procedure is ongoing. This

                      bit will be set immediately after the CE is cleared from 1 to 0 or when RTC_LOAD port

                      is read by CPU. This bit will cleared automatically after the read or write procedure is

                      done. Programmer can polling this bit to know if the RTC update is done or not. The

                      update or read procedure take around 3 32K period, which is around 100 us.

    0     CE   rw     This field is used to unlock the access to RTC calendar time registers.

                      This bit will be set to 1 when PWDEN is set to 1 and 0xA55AA55B is written to

                      RTC_CE. After this bit is set to 1, the RTC calendar time registers can be programmed,

                      but the actual update to RTC core will be start after this bit is cleared to 0. To clear this

                      bit, the PWDEN should be set to 1 and 0xA55AA55A should be written to RTC_CE.

    43.5.14        RTC_LOAD

    Offset: 0x004C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-16 RTC_LOAD Register Description

     Bit      Symbol  Type  Description

     31:0 | LOAD      rw    RTC calendar time load register.

                            When programmer read this port, the current calendar time will be latched and

                            programmer can read calendar time from RTC_SEC, RTC_MIN, RTC_HOUSR,

                            RTC_DAY, RTC_WEEK, RTC_MON, RTC_YEAR and RTC_TIME registers. Reading

                            RTC_LOAD will takes 3 clocks(clock frequency is 32768Hz), and programmer

                            can check the BSY bit in RTC_CE to confirm if the reading operation is done.

    43.5.15        RTC_INTSTS

    Offset: 0x0050

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

                                Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                                       1397 / 1446

                                     V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Table 43-17 RTC_INTSTS Register Description

    Bit   Symbol         Type        Description

    31:5Rsvd             -           Reserved

    4     DOACALU        r_w1c       When the current data needs to do auto-calibration, this bit will be

                                     asserted interrupt will not be asserted to CPU.

    3     AC_BUSY        r_w1c       Auto-calibration busy flag.

                                     Interrupt will not be asserted to CPU.

    2     AC_DONE        r_w1c       Calibration done interrupt.

1 ILLEGAL_CE_INTR r_w1c Illegal write to CE register interrupt.

0 ILLEGAL_TIME_INTRr_w1c Illegal time checking interrupt.

    43.5.16         RTC_INTEN

    Offset: 0x0054

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-18 RTC_INTEN Register Description

    Bit   Symbol            TypeDescription

    31:3Rsvd                -       Reserved

    2     AC_DONE_EN        rw      Auto calibration interrupt enable.

                                    1: Enable

                                    0: Disable

1 ILLEGAL_CE_INTR_EN rw Illegal write to CE register interrupt enable. when this bit is 1, the

                                    INTSTS[2] interrupt will be asserted to CPU,

0 ILLEGAL_TIME_INTR_ENrw Illegal time checking interrupt enable.

                                    When this bit is 1, the INTSTS[0] interrupt will be asserted to CPU

    43.5.23     RTC_WKUCTRL

    Offset: 0x0070

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-25 RTC_WKUCTRL Register Description

    Bit   Symbol             Type  Description

    31:7  Rsvd               -     Reserved

    6     WKUYEAR_MASK       rw    Mask year time of RTCOnly wakeup function

                                   1: Mask

                                   0: Unmask

    5     WKUMONTH_MASK      rw    Mask month time of RTCOnly wakeup function

                                   1: Mask

                                   0: Unmask

    4     WKUDAY_MASK        rw    Mask day time of RTCOnly wakeup function

                                   1: Mask

                                   0: Unmask

    3     WKUHOUR_MASK       rw    Mask hour time of RTCOnly wakeup function

                                   1: Mask

                                   0: Unmask

    2     WKUMIN_MASK        rw    Mask minute time of RTCOnly wakeup function

                                   1: Mask

                                   0: Unmask

    1     WKUSEC_MASK        rw    Mask second time of RTCOnly wakeup function

                                   1: Mask

                                   1: Enable

                            0: Disable

    43.5.24     RTC_ACCTRL

    Offset: 0x0080

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-26 RTC_ACCTRL Register Description

    Bit   Symbol   TypeDescription

    31:15Rsvd      -    Reserved

14 ADCJUDGErw ADC data judge enable. When this bit is set, ADC data received need to do auto-

                        calibration parameter calculation. If the absolute value of the result of the

                        subtraction of current data and last data less than or equal to 0x3, it will not do

                        ALU calculation. If this bit is 0, it will not do judgement and each sampled data

                        should do auto-calibration calculation.

                        1: Enable

                        0: Disable

    13:8  ACPER    rw   Auto trigger period control, the actual period is controlled by (ACPER +

                        1)*ACCLK. For example, when ACCLK is set to 2 (1 minute), and ACPER is set to

                        5, then the auto trigger period is (5+1)*1 minute = 6 minutes.

    7:6   Rsvd     -    Reserved

    5:4   ACCLK    rw   Auto-trigger clock source selection

                        0: Disable auto trigger function

                        1: Auto trigger count clock is 1second

                        2: Auto trigger count clock is  1 minute

                        3: Auto trigger count clock is  1 hour

    3     ADCSEL   rw   ADC source select

                        0: From ADC read data

                        1: From port RTC_ADCW

    2     Rsvd     -    Reserved

    1     MANU     rw   Auto-calibration manual trigger function

                        Write 1 to this field will trigger an auto-calibration procedure. This bit will be

                        cleared to 0 when the procedure is done




                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1401 / 1446

                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

0    ACEN       rw   Auto-calibration enable

                     0: Disable auto-calibration function

                     1: Enable auto-calibration function

43.5.25         RTC_ACTI

Offset: 0x0084

Reset: 0x0000_1800

Table 43-27 RTC_ACTI Register Description

Bit  Symbol TypeDescription

31:14Rsvd   -    Reserved

13:0 ACTI   rw   Auto-calibration Ti control.

                 This field is used to store the Ti value which is used as the center temperature during

                 calibration. This register is 8 bits integer and 8 bits fraction value. For example,

                 0x1880 means 24.5 degree. This register can be updated only when CE is 1, and

                 should be fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.26         RTC_ACF200

Offset: 0x0088

Reset: 0x0064_0000

Table 43-28 RTC_ACF200 Register Description

Bit  Symbol TypeDescription

31:26Rsvd   -    Reserved

25:0 F200   rw   Auto-calibration F200 control.

                 This field is used to store the F200 value which is used for the calculation for PLLDIV

                 value. This value equals Fpclk/2 or Fpll1/2 which depends on RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL

                 bit[3].

                 For example, when Fpclk is 48MHz, F200 = 48000000/2 = 0x16e3600. This register

                 can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.27         RTC_ACADCW

Offset: 0x008C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-29 RTC_ACADCW Register Description


                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1402 / 1446

                             V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Bit  Symbol         TypeDescription

31:12Rsvd           -    Reserved

11:0 ACADCW         rw   This field is used to store the manual ADC value which is used for the

                         calculation for temperature. By default, the auto-calibration engine will read

                         the ADC value automatically, but if programmer wishes to control the ADC

                         value manually, this register can be used to control the ADC value read by the

                         engine. This register is 12 bits integer. This field is valid only when ADCSEL is

                         set to 1.

43.5.28         RTC_ACP0

Offset: 0x0090

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-30 RTC_ACP0 Register Description

Bit    Symbol   TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd       -      Reserved

15:0 ACP0       rw     ACP0 is parameter p0 used for auto-calibration. P0 is quadratic term coefficient in

                       polynomial T=((P0*((X*X)>>16) + P1*X + P2)>>8, where T is temperature

                       value stored in RTC_ACTEMP register, X is ADCDATA value in RTCANA_ADCCTRL

                       register. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset. This register can

                       be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.29     RTC_ACP1

Offset: 0x0094

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-31 RTC_ACP1 Register Description

Bit    Symbol   TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd       -      Reserved

15:0 ACP1       rw     ACP1 is parameter P1 used for auto-calibration. P1 is first-order coefficient in

                       polynomial T=((P0*((X*X)>>16) + P1*X + P2)>>8, where T is temperature

                       value stored in RTC_ACTEMP register, X is ADCDATA value in RTCANA_ADCCTRL

                       register. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset.This register can

                       be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is 1.



                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1403 / 1446

                        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
43.5.30        RTC_ACP2

Offset: 0x0098

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-32 RTC_ACP2 Register Description

Bit  Symbol   TypeDescription

31:0ACP2      rw   ACP2 is parameter P2 used for auto-calibration. P2 is constant term coefficient in

                   polynomial T=((P0*((X*X)>>16) + P1*X + P2)>>8, where T is temperature value

                   stored in RTC_ACTEMP register, X is ADCDATA value in RTCANA_ADCCTRL

                   register. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset.This register can

                   be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.31       RTC_ACP3

Offset: 0x009C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-33 RTC_ACP3 Register Description

Bit  Symbol    TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd      -    Reserved

15:0 ACP3      rw   ACP3 is a 16-bit signed value, and ACP3 is the K coefficient of the crystal for the

                    current temperature. The hardware will select the K coefficient from

                    RTC_ACKx(x=1~5) according the actual temperature T stored in RTC_ACTEMP

                    register when RTC auto-calibration, and update the RTC_ACKx to the ACP3 . This

                    register can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN

                    is 1.

43.5.32       RTC_ACP4

Offset: 0x00A0

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-34 RTC_ACP4 Register Description

Bit  Symbol    TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd      -    Reserved

15:0  ACP4     rw   ACP4 is a 16-bit signed value, and it stores the crystal deviation in normal

                    temperature. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset.This register


                    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1404 / 1446

V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is 1.



43.5.33       RTC_ACP5

Offset: 0x00A4

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-35 RTC_ACP5 Register Description

Bit  Symbol   TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd     -    Reserved

15:0 ACP5     rw   ACP5 is a 16-bit signed value, and it is used to generate one-second pulse. Its

                   value can be calculated by the equation ACP5 = 65536/10/1.017=6444,

                   where 1.107ppm = 1000000/30/32768. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO

                   region after reset. This register can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be

                   fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.34     RTC_ACP6

Offset: 0x00A8

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-36 RTC_ACP6 Register Description

Bit  Symbol   TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd     -    Reserved

15:0 ACP6     rw   ACP6 is a 16-bit signed value, and it is used to generate one-second pulse output

                   by PLL clock. Its value can be calculated by the equation ACP6 = PCLK

                   frequence*4096/2/10/1000000. When PCLK frequence is 6.5536MHz, ACP6 =

                   1342. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset. This register can be

                   updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.35     RTC_ACP7

Offset: 0x00AC

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-37 RTC_ACP7 Register Description

Bit  Symbol   TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd     -    Reserved


                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1405 / 1446

                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

15:0 ACP7     rw   ACP7 is a 16-bit signed value, and it is used for frequency calibration. Its value

                   is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset. This register can be updated only

                   when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.36       RTC_ACK1

Offset: 0x00B0

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-38 RTC_ACK1 Register Description

Bit  Symbol   TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd     -    Reserved

15:0 ACK1     rw   ACK1 is 16-bit signed and used for auto-calibration. Hardware will automatically

                   select a suitable ACKx(x is 1,2,3,4,5) according to calculated temperature.

                   When T< KTEMP1, ACK1 value will be written to RTC_ACP3 by hardware. Its

                   value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset. This register can be updated

                   only when CE is 1, and should be a fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.37     RTC_ACK2

Offset: 0x00B4

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-39 RTC_ACK2 Register Description

Bit  Symbol   TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd     -    Reserved

15:0 ACK2     rw   ACK2 is 16-bit signed and used for auto-calibration. Hardware will automatically

                   select a suitable ACKx(x is 1,2,3,4,5) according to calculated temperature.

                   When KTEMP1≤T< KTEMP2, ACK2 value will be written to RTC_ACP3 by

                   hardware. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset. This register

                   can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be a fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.38       RTC_ACK3

Offset: 0x00B8

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-40 RTC_ACK3 Register Description

Bit Symbol    TypeDescription


                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1406 / 1446

                       V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

31:0ACK3     rw   ACK3 is 16-bit signed and used for auto-calibration. Hardware will automatically

                  select a suitable ACKx(x is 1,2,3,4,5) according to calculated temperature.

                  When KTEMP2≤T< KTEMP3, ACK3 value will be written to RTC_ACP3 by hardware.

                  Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset. This register can be

                  updated only when CE is 1, and should be a fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.39       RTC_ACK4

Offset: 0x00BC

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-41 RTC_ACK4 Register Description

Bit  Symbol   TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd     -    Reserved

15:0 ACK4     rw   ACK4 is 16-bit signed and used for auto-calibration. Hardware will automatically

                   select a suitable ACKx(x is 1,2,3,4,5) according to calculated temperature.

                   When KTEMP3≤T< KTEMP4, ACK4 value will be written to RTC_ACP3 by

                   hardware. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset. This register

                   can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be a fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.40     RTC_ACK5

Offset: 0x00C0

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-42 RTC_ACK5 Register Description

Bit  Symbol   TypeDescription

31:16Rsvd     -    Reserved

15:0 ACK5     rw   ACK5 is 16-bit signed and used for auto-calibration. Hardware will automatically

                   select a suitable ACKx(x is 1,2,3,4,5) according to calculated temperature.
                   When T≥KTEMP4, ACK5 value will be written to RTC_ACP3 by hardware. Its value
                   is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset. This register can be updated only
                   when CE is 1, and should be a fixed value when ACEN is 1.

43.5.41     RTC_ACTEMP

Offset: 0x00C4

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-43 RTC_ACTEMP Register Description


                   Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1407 / 1446

                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:16Rsvd   -   Reserved

15:0 TEMP r This field is used to store the calculated result of current temperature. This register

                    will be updated automatically after the auto-calibration procedure is done. The

                    format in this field is 8 bits integer and 8 bits fraction signed value. For example,

                    0x1880 means 24.5 degree.

    43.5.42        RTC_ACPPM

    Offset: 0x00C8

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-44 REGISTERS_RTC_ACPPM Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:16Rsvd   -   Reserved

15:0 PPM r This field is used to store the calculated PPM(part per million). This field will be

                    updated automatically after the auto-calibration procedure is done. This field is a 16

                    bits signed value and the unit of this field is 0.1 PPM.

    43.5.43        RTC_ACKTEMP

    Offset: 0x00D0

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-45 RTC_ACKTEMP Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:24KTEMP4rw    This field is used to control the section 3 temperature. This is a signed 8 bits value.

                     This field can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is

                     1. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset.

    23:16KTEMP3rw    This field is used to control the section 3 temperature. This is a signed 8 bits value.

                     This field can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is

                     1. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset.

15:8 KTEMP2rw This field is used to control the section 2 temperature. This is a signed 8 bits value.

                     This field can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is

                     1. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset.

7:0 KTEMP1rw This field is used to control the section 1 temperature. This is a signed 8 bits value.

                     This field can be updated only when CE is 1, and should be fixed value when ACEN is

                         Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1408 / 1446

    V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    1. Its value is loaded from FLASH INFO region after reset.


    43.5.44     RTC_IODECMODE

    Offset: 0x00E8

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    31:4 Rsvd          -   Reserved

3:2 TAMPER1_DEC_EN rw IOI8 used as TAMPER1 detecting.

                           0: Disable TAMPER1 detecting.

                           1: Rising edge trigger data detecting.

                           2: Falling edge trigger data detecting.

                           3: Both falling edge and rising edge of IOI8 detecting.

1:0 TAMPER0_DEC_EN rw IOC13 used as TAMPER0 detecting.

                           0: Disable TAMPER0 detecting.

                           1: Rising edge trigger data detecting.

                           2: Falling edge trigger data detecting.

                           3: Both falling edge and rising edge of IOC13 detecting.

                   1: Full;

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Bit  Symbol     TypeDescription

    31:1Rsvd        -   Reserved

                        Write 0xBEEF_BEEF as the password, and RTCANA_SWRST register can be

    43.6.2 RTCANA_XTAL

    Offset: 0x0008

    Reset: 0x0000_026f

    Table 43-52 RTCANA_XTAL Register Description

    Bit   Symbol                TypeDescription

    31:18Rsvd                   -   Reserved

    17    XTAL32K_EXIST         r   XTAL 32K current status:

                                    1: Exist

                                    0: Absent

    16    XTAL_32K_ABSENT       r   When high, indicate xtal 32k is absent once.

15 XTAL_32K_ABSENT_CLRrw Write 0 to clear XTAL_32K_ABSENT bit.

    14:11Rsvd                   -   Reserved


    § dango                     Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1413 / 1446

                                                                         V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

    10    XTAL_EN_SEL             rw   Enable of XTAL (XOL) selection

                                       0: Controlled by XTAL_EN_LV register

                                       1: Controlled automatically by hardware

    9     XTAL_BYP_LV             rw   External active crystal input selectable signal, high active. Only

                                       effective while XTAL_EN_LV = 0 and XIN as external active crystal

                                       input.

                                       0: Can’t support active crystal input.

                                       1: Can support active crystal input.

    8     XTAL_EN_LV              rw   Enable of XTAL LV:

                                       0: Disable the 32.768K XTAL

                                       1: Enable the 32.768K XTAL

    7:0   REG_XTAL_LV             rw   Register to control operating, AGC function and oscillate state

                                       check function of crystal OSC.

                                       [2:0]: Control operating current of crystal OSC.

                                       3’b100 (default)

                                       [3]: AGC function bypass signal, high active.

                                       1’b0: Don’t bypass AGC function.

                                       [5:4]: Internal RF resistor selection registers

                                       2’b10 (default)

                                       [7:6]: Control the AGC gain. 2’b01 (default)

43.6.5 RTCANA_ADCCTRL

Offset: 0x0038

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 43-55 RTCANA_ADCCTRL Register Description

Bit   Symbol     TypeDescription

31:16ADCDATA r        ADC conversion result for the analog request. It is used for temperature

                      calculation. See RTC_ACP0 register for more information.

15:8  Rsvd       -    Reserved

7:4   ADCCH      rw   ADC channel select

                      1010: temperature channel

                      Others: Reserved

3     Rsvd       -    Reserved

2     ADCNACK rw      ADC conversion nack flag

                      1: ADC conversion nack

                      When this bit is set, ADC conversion gets wrong, maybe the ADC is not power-up

                      correctly.

                          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                              1415 / 1446

V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Write 1 to clear.



1     ADCVALIDrw      1: ADC conversion is finished.

                      When this bit is set, you can read the result from ADCDATA. Write 1 to clear.

0     ADCREQ     rw   ADC analog request.

                      1: Request ADC ANA conversion write 1 to start ADC ANA conversion.

                      When request is ack or nack, this bit will clear by hardware.

                        10: Alternate function mode

                        00: Input

                        01: General purpose output mode

                        10: Alternate function mode

                        11: Analog mode(reset state)

1:0 MODER0 rw These bits are written by software to configure the PC13 direction mode.

                        00: Input

                        01: General purpose output mode 10: Alternate function mode

                        11: Analog mode(reset status)

                       0: Output push-pull (reset state)

                       0: Output push-pull (reset state)

                       0: Output push-pull (reset state)

                       1: Output open-drain

    43.6.18           RTCANA_GPIOODR

    Offset: 0x0090

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-68 RTCANA_GPIOODR Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:4Rsvd    -     Reserved

3 ODR3 rw This bit can be read and written by software for PI8.

                      Note: For atomic bit set/reset, the ODR bits can be individually set and reset by

                      writing to the GPIOx_BSRR register (x = A..I).

2 ODR2 rw These bits can be read and written by software for PC15.

                      Note: For atomic bit set/reset, the ODR bits can be individually set and reset by

                      writing to the GPIOx_BSRR register (x = A..I).

1 ODR1 rw These bits can be read and written by software for PC14.

                      Note: For atomic bit set/reset, the ODR bits can be individually set and reset by

                      writing to the GPIOx_BSRR register (x = A..I).

0 ODR0 rw These bits can be read and written by software for PC13.

                      Note: For atomic bit set/reset, the ODR bits can be individually set and reset by

                      writing to the GPIOx_BSRR register (x = A..I).

    43.6.19           RTCANA_GPIOBSRR

    Offset: 0x0094

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-69 RTCANA_GPIOBSRR Register Description

    Bit    Symbol  Type Description

    31:8 Rsvd      -   Reserved

    7:6    BS3     w   These bits are write-only. A read to these bits returns the value 0x0000.

                       0: No action on the corresponding PI8 ODR bit

                       1: Sets the corresponding PI8 ODR bit

    5:4    BS2     w   These bits are write-only. A read to these bits returns the value 0x0000.

                            Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                               1424 / 1446

                            V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        0: No action on the corresponding PC15 ODR bit

                        1: Sets the corresponding PC15 ODR bit

    3:2   BS1       w   These bits are write-only. A read to these bits returns the value 0x0000.

                        0: No action on the corresponding PC14 ODR bit

                        1: Sets the corresponding PC14 ODR bit

    1:0   BS0       w   These bits are write-only. A read to these bits returns the value 0x0000.

                        0: No action on the corresponding PC13 ODR bit

                        1: Sets the corresponding PC13 ODR bit

    43.6.20            RTCANA_GPIOLCKR

    Offset: 0x0098

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-70 RTCANA_GPIOLCKR Register Description

    Bit   Symbol TypeDescription

    31:17Rsvd    -     Reserved

16 LCKK rw This bit can be read in any time. It can only be modified using the lock key write

                       sequence.

                       0: Port configuration lock key not active

                       1: Port configuration lock key active. The GPIOx_LCKR register is locked until an

                       MCU reset or a peripheral reset occurs.

                       LOCK key write sequence:

                       WR LCKR[16] = 1 + LCKR[15:0] WR LCKR[16] = 0 + LCKR[15:0] WR LCKR[16] = 1

                       + LCKR[15:0] RD LCKR

                       RD LCKR[16] = 1 (this read operation is optional but it confirms that the lock is

                       active)

                       Note: During the LOCK key write sequence, the value of LCK[15:0] must not

                       change.

                       Any error in the lock sequence aborts the lock.

                       After the first lock sequence on any bit of the port, any read access on the LCKK bit

                       returns 1 until the next CPU reset.

    15:4  Rsvd   -     Reserved

    3     LCK3   rw    PI8 configure lock signal


                        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1425 / 1446

                               V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

                        1: Lock

                        0: Un-lock

    2     LCK2   rw     PC15 configure lock signal

                        1: Lock

                        0: Un-lock

    1     LCK1   rw     PC14 configure lock signal

                        1: Lock

                        0: Un-lock

    0     LCK0   rw     PC13 configure lock signal

                        1: Lock

                        0: Un-lock

    43.6.21          RTCANA_GPIOAFRH

    Offset: 0x009C

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 43-71 RTCANA_GPIOAFRH Register Description

    Bit    Symbol    Type  Description

    31:16  Rsvd      -     Reserved

15:12 AFRH3 rw PI8 Alternate function selection, refer to GPIO alternate function table for details

                           0000: AF0

                           0001: AF1

                           …

                           1111: AF15

11:8 AFRH2 rw PC15 Alternate function selection, refer to GPIO alternate function table for

                           details

                           0000: AF0

                           0001: AF1

                           …

                           1111: AF15

44 RTC Wakeup and Alarm (RTCALM)

44.1 Overview


The module implements the function of wake-up and alarm in low power mode. The module can get

real-time clock counter from RTC module, and set the time interval and alarm time. Once the setting

time expires, wakeup or alarm flags will be set, and interrupts will be generated.


   44.2 Feature


    • Programmable multiple wakeup sources in low-power mode.

    • Support alarm function.

    • Support second pulse generation from high-frequency clock divide.

    • Support reference second pulse capturing

    • Support interrupt generation.


44.3 Functional description

44.3.1 Wakeup in low-power mode

RTCALM supplies several kinds of wakeup functions in low-power mode(sleep and deep sleep), consists

of multi-second wakeup, multi-minute wakeup, multi-hour wakeup, clock counter wakeup, multi-

interval wakeup, multi-second-interval wakeup and alarm wakeup.

The programming sequence for each wakeup function is similar, so we just show the programming

sequence of multi-second wakeup below:

• Enable RTCALM PCLK by setting RTCALM bit in SYSCFGLP_PCLKEN register(if RTCALM PCLK is not

enabled before).

• Set a value in unit of second to RTCALM_WKUSEC register.

• Poll the CFG_BUSY bit in RTCALM_STS register to make sure the configuration above is finished.

• Write bit ‘1’ to RTCALM_CFGVALID register.

• Set INT_WKUSEC_STS bit in RTCALM_INTSTS register to clear multi-second wakeup interrupt.

• Set INT_WKUSEC_EN bit in RTCALM_INTEN register.

• Configure CM33_WKU_SRC_EB field in PMU_CTRL register to select RTCALM as wakeup source.

i Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc. 1433 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

• Write 0xAA55_55AA to PMU_CM33DSLPPSWD register and then write 0x55AA_AA55 to

PMU_CM33SLPEN register to enter deep sleep mode.

Once multi-second wakeup happens, CPU will execute the program following the deep sleep statement.

Software can let statements of checking INT_WKUSEC_STS bit in RTCALM_INTSTS register follow the

deep sleep statement, so that software can confirm the happening of multi-second wakeup.

44.3.2 Alarm function

Alarm function is used for wakeup in low-power mode or generating a notification.The procedure below

is given for generating an alarm notification:

• Enable RTCALM PCLK by setting RTCALM bit in SYSCFGLP_PCLKEN register(if RTCALM PCLK is not

enabled before).

• Set INT_ALARM_EN bit in RTCALM_INTSTS register to clear multi-second wakeup interrupt.

• Set INT_ALARM_EN bit in RTCALM_INTEN register.

• Program alarm registers to let alarm happen at a suitable time.

• Configure RTCALM_WKUALMCTRL to set alam masks and enable alarm function.

• Poll the CFG_BUSY bit in RTCALM_STS register to make sure the configuration above is finished.

• Write bit ‘1’ to RTCALM_CFGVALID register.

44.3.3 High-frequency clock divide

The goal of high-frequency clock divide is generating a high-precision second pulse. Figure below show

the principle of the generation.





    RTC ALARM



    APB clock ] 2*(PLLDIV+1)  PI0    J
    PLL1        output second pulse

    PLLCLK_SEL







        Figure 44-1 second pulse generated from high-frequency clock divide

    i        Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1434 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

As metioned above, RTC auto-calibration can generate PLL divider for generating a high-precision

second pulse,so we have two ways to generate second pulse:

Generate second pulse when RTC auto-calibration disabled

First of all, DIVCAP_MODE field in RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL register must be set to DIVIDE mode by

software. The PLLCLK_SEL bit in RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL register is used for selecting clock source, and

PLLDIV field in RTCALM_PLLDIV is used as divider factor. The programming flow is as follows:

• Enable RTCALM PCLK by setting RTCALM bit in SYSCFGLP_PCLKEN register(if RTCALM PCLK is not

enabled before).

• Set DIVCAP_MODE field in RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL register to DIVIDE mode

• Select APB clock or PLL1 as clock source.

• Set value of (clock source frequency/2 -1) to PLLDIV field in RTCALM_PLLDIV register.

• Configure alternate function of GPIO pin #0 for second pulse output.

RTCALM doesn’t supply disable flag to stop output of second pulse, you can change mode of GPIO pin

#0 to analog mode to stop output of second pulse.

Generate second pulse when RTC auto-calibration enabled(recommended)

• Read RTC_PLLDIVAC register and write its value to RTCALM_PLLDIV register

• Select the same PLL clock source as RTC_ACF200 register

• Set DIVCAP_MODE field in RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL register to DIVIDE mode

• Configure alternate function of GPIO pin #0 for second pulse output.

44.3.4 Reference second pulse capturing

RTCALM provides reference second pulse capturing mechanism to help improve precision of local second

pulse. RTCALM will calculate the PCLK count between two adjacent rising edges of input reference second

pulse, and then write its value to RTCALM_PLLDIV register. RTCALM will use the PLLDIV value in

RTCALM_PLLDIV register to generate local second pulse.










    Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1435 / 1446

   V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual








                     output
        PI0          second pulse
 RTC
ALARM   PI1    J     input reference
                     second pulse







       Figure 44-2 reference second pulse capturing

   The programming flow is given below:

   • Enable RTCALM PCLK by setting RTCALM bit in SYSCFGLP_PCLKEN register(if RTCALM PCLK is not

   enabled before).

   • Configure alternate functions of GPIO pin #0 and GPIO pin #1 for second pulse capturing.

   • Set DIVCAP_MODE field in RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL register to WAIT mode.

   • Select APB clock or PLL1 as clock source.

   • Wait for DIVCAP_MODE field in RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL register becoming IDLE mode

   • Set DIVCAP_MODE field in RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL register to DIVIDE mode to output local secon pulse.

   RTCALM doesn’t supply disable flag to stop output of second pulse, you can change mode of GPIO pin

   #0 to analog mode to stop output of second pulse.


   44.4 RTCALM interrupts


   RTCALM has 7 maskable interrupts, they are:

   • Alarm interrupt

   • Clock counter wakeup interrupt

   • Midnight wakeup interrupt

   • Multi-hour wakeup interrupt

   • Multi-minute wakeup interrupt

   • Multi-second wakeup interrupt

   • Multi-interval and multi-second-interval wakeup interrupt

   § dango      Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.        1436 / 1446

        V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Register RTCALM_INTEN is used for enabling or disabling interrupt generation, and register

RTCALM_INTSTS shows whether the specified interrupt happens or not. Once the specified interrupt

happens, the corresponding bit field in register RTCALM_INTSTS will be set, and software can write ‘1’

to corresponding bit field in register RTCALM_INTSTS to clear the specified interrupt.


   44.5 RTCALM Registers


    Table 44-1 RTCALM Registers Map

    Register Name     Offset       Type Reset Value   Description

    RTCALM_WKUSEC     0x0000       rw   0x0000_0000   Multi-second low-power wakeup or interrupt

                                                      register.

    RTCALM_WKUMIN     0x0004       rw   0x0000_0000   Multi-minute low-power wakeup or interrupt

                                                      register.

    RTCALM_WKUHOUR    0x0008       rw   0x0000_0000   Multi-hour low-power wakeup or interrupt

                                                      register.

    RTCALM_WKUCNT     0x000C       rw   0x0000_0000   Clock counter low-power wakeup or interrupt

                                                      register.

    RTCALM_ITV        0x0010       rw   0x0000_0000   Multi-interval low-power wakeup or interrupt

                                                      control register

    RTCALM_SITV       0x0014       rw   0x0000_0000   Multi-second-interval low-power wakeup or

                                                      interrupt control register.

    RTCALM_ALARMSEC   0x001C       rw   0x0000_0000   Inaccurate second alarm control register.

    RTCALM_ALARMMIN   0x0020       rw   0x0000_0000   Minute alarm control register.

RTCALM_ALARMHOUR 0x0024 rw 0x0000_0000 Hour alarm control register.

RTCALM_WKUALMCTRL 0x0034 rw 0x0000_0002 Alarm and wakeup control register.

    RTCALM_INTEN      0x0038       rw   0x0000_0000   Interrupt enable register.

    RTCALM_INTSTS     0x003C       rw   0x0000_0000   Interrupt status register.

    44.5.5 RTCALM_ITV

    Offset: 0x0010

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 44-6 RTCALM_ITV Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

    31:3Rsvd   -     Reserved

2:0 ITV rw Control multi-interval wakeup and interrupt. This register operates independently of

                     RTCALM_WKUSEC, RTCALM_WKUMIN and RTCALM_WKUHOUR.

                     0: Every 1 second

                     1: Every 1 minute

                     2: Every 1 hour

                     3: Every 1 day (at 00:00)

                     4: Every 500 ms

                     5: Every 250 ms

                     6: Every 125 ms

                     7: Every 62.5 ms (when SITV is 0 or SITVEN is 0)

                     7: Every SITV second (when SITV is not 0 and SITVEN is 1)

                                        0: Multi-interval and multi-second-interval wakeup

                                        interrupt is not active

                                        1: Multi-interval and multi-second-interval wakeup

                                        interrupt is active

44.5.16     RTCALM_WKUCNTR

Offset: 0x0040

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 44-17 RTCALM_WKUCNTR Register Description

Bit    Symbol        Type  Description

31:24  Rsvd          -     Reserved

23:0   WKUCNTR       r     Current WKUCNT value.

44.5.17     RTCALM_CFGVALID

Offset: 0x0044

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 44-18 RTCALM_CFGVALID Register Description

Bit     Symbol              Type       Description

31:1    Rsvd                -          Reserved

0       CFG_VALID           r_w1c      Configuration information really valid.

44.5.18     RTCALM_STS

Offset: 0x0048

Reset: 0x0000_0000
§                              Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.                            1444 / 1446

                              V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual

Table 44-19 RTCALM_STS Register Description

Bit   Symbol    Type Description

31:1 Rsvd       -   Reserved

0     CFG_BUSY r    Configuration busy flag.

                    When this flag is set, it is not allowed for next write operation.

                    It controls the following registers:

                    RTCALM_WKUSEC, RTCALM_WKUMIN, RTCALM_WKUHOUR, RTCALM_WKUCNT,

                    RTCALM_ITV, RTCALM_SITV, RTCALM_ALARMSEC, RTCALM_ALARMMIN,

                    RTCALM_ALARMHOUR, RTCALM_ALARMDAY, RTCALM_ALARMMONTH,

                    RTCALM_ALARMYEAR.

44.5.19     RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL

Offset: 0x004C

Reset: 0x0000_0000

Table 44-20 RTCALM_DIVCAPCTRL Register Description

Bit   Symbol         TypeDescription

31:4Rsvd             -    Reserved

3     PLLCLK_SEL     rw   Select the source of rtc pll divide clock

                          0: APB clock

                          1: PLL1

2     Rsvd           -    Reserved

1:0   DIVCAP_MODE    rw   This field is used to control the divide/capture mode.

                          0: High-frequency clock DIVIDE mode, count from 0 to 2*(PLLDIV+1). A

                          pulse will be generated when the counter reached.

                          1: WAIT mode, the counter will be idle until a first rising edge of

                          external second pulse input, then the mode will be switched to CAPTURE

                          mode automatically.

                          2: CAPTURE mode, the counter will start count automatically and count

                          until another rising pulse of external second pulse. Then the MODE will

                          be switched to IDLE mode automatically.

                          3: IDLE mode, the counter value is stored and programmer can read the

                          RTCALM_PLLDIV register for the capture counter value.


                          Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1445 / 1446

                      V32F20x_V32H20x Reference Manual
    44.5.20     RTCALM_PLLDIV

    Offset: 0x0050

    Reset: 0x0000_0000

    Table 44-21 RTCALM_PLLDIV Register Description

    Bit Symbol TypeDescription

    31:26Rsvd -   Reserved

25:0 PLLDIV rw This field has three fuctions, the first function is storing PLL divider value in capture

                  mode, the second function is storing value of RTC_PLLDIVAC register, and the last

                  is also used as clock divider in high frequency divide mode. The actual clock divider

                  value is 2*(PLLDIV+1).

                  When used for storing value of RTC_PLLDIVAC register, software is responsible for

                  writing value of RTC_PLLDIVAC register to this field after periodic auto-calibration is

                  done.










                  Hangzhou Vango Technologies, Inc.    1446 / 1446
